Please refer to this<a href="https://drive.google.com/file/d/14E-cuIgPhwmVeVg3UdhzqhOYqeu-pTEU/view?usp=sharing"> </a><a href="https://www.omadahealth.com/hubfs/Hiring%20Documents/Omada_Salary_Zones.pdf">resource</a> for detail on zone designations.&nbsp;&nbsp;</p> <p>Zone 1: $142,600 - $178,300</p> <p>Zone 2: $136,400 - $170,500</p> <p>Zone 3: $124,000 - $155,000</p> <p>Please note that zones may be updated as market data changes but we will honor the range applicable to your zone (which is determined by your location) as of your application date.</p> <p>*The actual offer, including the compensation package, will be within the posted range for the relevant zone and will be determined based on multiple factors, such as the candidate's skills and experience, and other business considerations such as internal equity.</p> <p>Please&nbsp;<a href="https://www.omadahealth.com/applicant-specific-notice">click here </a>for more information on our Candidate Privacy Notice.</p>

title: Senior Software Engineer, Data Engineering
full_description: <p>Omada Health is on a mission to bend the curve of chronic disease.</p> <p><strong>Job overview:</strong><strong><br></strong></p> <p>We are dedicated to leveraging data to drive strategic decision-making and operational efficiency. Our team is passionate about harnessing the power of data to solve complex problems and deliver impactful insights.</p> <p>We are seeking a highly skilled and motivated Data Engineer to join our team. The ideal candidate will be responsible for designing, building, and maintaining robust data architectures and engineering data models and pipelines. This role will play a critical part in ensuring the integrity, scalability, and performance of our data processing and products.</p> <p><strong>Key Responsibilities:</strong></p> <ul> <li>Data Architecture: Design, develop, and implement scalable, secure, and efficient data solutions that meet the needs of the organization.</li> <li>Data Modeling: Create and maintain logical and physical data models to support business intelligence, analytics, and reporting requirements.</li> <li>Pipeline Engineering: Design, build, and optimize ETL (Extract, Transform, Load) processes and data pipelines to ensure smooth and efficient data flow from various sources.</li> <li>Data Integration: Integrate diverse data sources, including APIs, databases, and third-party data, into a unified data platform.</li> <li>Performance Optimization: Monitor and optimize the performance of data systems and pipelines to ensure low latency and high throughput.</li> <li>Data Quality and Governance: Implement data quality checks, validation processes, and governance frameworks to ensure the accuracy and reliability of data.</li> <li>Collaboration: Partner closely with data scientists, analysts, and other stakeholders to understand data requirements and deliver solutions that meet their needs.</li> <li>Documentation: Maintain comprehensive documentation of data architectures, models, and pipelines for ongoing maintenance and knowledge sharing.</li> <li>Training: You'll train and collaborate with teammates effectively in data engineering best practices</li> <li>Technical Influence/Leadership: Recommends policy changes and establishes department-wide procedures. Uses extensive experience and knowledge to resolve complex problems.&nbsp;</li> <li>Monitor and manage production environment to deliver data within defined SLAs&nbsp;</li> </ul> <p><strong>How you can make an impact:</strong></p> <ul> <li>You’ll evaluate, benchmark, and improve the scalability, robustness, and performance of our data platform and applications</li> <li>You'll make significant contributions to the architecture and design of our data processing platform</li> <li>You’ll implement scalable, fault tolerant, and accurate ETLs frameworks.</li> <li>You’ll gather and process raw data at scale from diverse sources</li> <li>You’ll collaborate with product management, data scientists, analysts, and other engineers on technical vision, design, and planning</li> <li>You’ll implement and maintain a high level of data quality monitoring in our analytics &amp; ML ecosystem</li> <li>You’ll train and collaborate with teammates effectively in data engineering best practices</li> <li>You will be responsible for leading, documenting, and collaborating across teams for technical projects.</li> </ul> <p><strong>You will love this job if you:</strong></p> <ul> <li>You are passionate about building data-driven systems to enable Data Scientist, Data Analysts and AI/ML Engineers.</li> <li>You want to make a difference to empower digital healthcare through Data-driven decision making.</li> <li>You would like to learn how to build scalable, performant and reliable data pipelines.</li> </ul> <p><strong>About you:&nbsp;</strong></p> <p><strong>Experience:</strong></p> <ul> <li>5+ years of experience building, maintaining, and orchestrating scalable data pipelines.&nbsp;</li> <li>3+ years of experience as a data engineer developing or maintaining integration with software such as Airflow or any Python-based data pipeline codebase.</li> <li>Experience applying a variety of integration patterns for different use cases.</li> <li>Experience in backend software development to contribute to distributed computing development and data technologies, with broad experience across systems, contexts, and ideas.</li> <li>Experience implementing data pipelines and improving the performance of ETL processes and related SQL queries.</li> <li>Experience in data modeling for OLTP and OLAP applications</li> <li>Experience with Cloud platforms such as Amazon AWS</li> <li>Familiarity with workflow management tools (Airflow preferred)</li> <li>Familiarity with cloud-based data warehouses (Amazon Redshift preferred)</li> <li>Exceptional Problem solving and analytical skills</li> <li>Experience working with sensitive data i.e. PHI / PII &amp; security best practices</li> <li>Familiarity with data governance practices and principles<strong>.</strong></li> </ul> <p><strong>Technical Skills:</strong></p> <ul> <li>Proficiency in SQL and experience with relational databases (e.g., MySQL, PostgreSQL).</li> <li>Proficiency in Analytical SQL (e.g. Analytics Queries, Distributed database queries) and experience working with massive parallel processing (MPP) databases (e.g., Redshift, BigQuery, Snowflake).</li> <li>Proficiency in programming languages such as Python, Java, or Scala.</li> <li>Knowledge of data modeling techniques (3NF)&nbsp; and tools (e.g., ER/Studio, ERwin).</li> <li>Software Engineering Mindset: Apply best practices to write elegant, maintainable code and understand automated testing concepts.</li> <li>Familiarity with business intelligence tools and environments.</li> <li>Familiarity with big data technologies (e.g., Lambda, Iceberg or Delta Lake, Spark, or Kafka)</li> <li>Software engineering mindset and an ability to write elegant, maintainable code while following engineering best practices&nbsp;</li> </ul> <p><strong>Communication Skills:</strong> Excellent communication and collaboration skills, both written and verbal, with the ability to convey complex technical concepts to non-technical stakeholders.</p> <p><strong>Self-Directed:</strong> Leading projects and tasks effectively with cross functional stakeholders minimal guidance. You care about writing quality software and recognize that there are often many right answers. Ability to lead tasks and projects effectively.</p> <p><strong>Education:</strong> Bachelor’s degree in Computer Science or a similar discipline preferred.</p> <p><strong>Technologies we use:</strong> Ruby on Rails, Redshift, Athena, Postgres, SQL, Python, Apache Airflow, Appflow, S3, SNS, SQS, Kafka, Docker, Kubernetes, AWS infrastructure, Lambda, Serverless, Tableau, Bugsnag, Datadog, GitLabCI, Cursor, OpenMetadata, Databricks</p> <p><strong>Bonus Points for:</strong></p> <ul> <li>Experience with NoSQL databases (e.g., document and graph databases)</li> </ul> <p><strong>Experience building internal frameworks or development productivity tools would be a big plus.</strong></p> <ul> <li>Experience building data infrastructure, frameworks and automation is a big plus.&nbsp;</li> <li>Experience using Generative AI tools or technologies to improve engineering workflows, automation, analytics, or data platform capabilities.</li> </ul> <p><strong>Benefits:</strong></p> <ul> <li>Competitive salary with generous annual cash bonus</li> <li>Equity grants</li> <li>Remote first work from home culture</li> <li>Flexible Time Off to help you rest, recharge, and connect with loved ones</li> <li>Generous parental leave</li> <li>Health, dental, and vision insurance (and above market employer contributions)</li> <li>401k retirement savings plan</li> <li>Lifestyle Spending Account (LSA)</li> <li>Mental Health Support Solutions</li> <li>...and more!</li> </ul> <p>It takes a village to change health care. As we build together toward our mission, we strive to embody the following values in our day-to-day work. We hope these hold meaning for you as well as you consider Omada!</p> <ul> <li><strong>Cultivate Trust.</strong> We listen closely and we operate with kindness. We provide respectful and candid feedback to each other.</li> <li><strong>Seek Context. </strong>We ask to understand and we build connections. We do our research up front to move faster down the road.</li> <li><strong>Act Boldly.</strong> We innovate daily to solve problems, improve processes, and find new opportunities for our members and customers.</li> <li><strong>Deliver Results. </strong>We reward impact above output. We set a high bar, we’re not afraid to fail, and we take pride in our work.</li> <li><strong>Succeed Together. </strong>We prioritize Omada’s progress above team or individual. We have fun as we get stuff done, and we celebrate together.&nbsp;</li> <li><strong>Remember Why We’re Here.</strong> We push through the challenges of changing health care because we know the destination is worth it.</li> </ul> <p><strong>About Omada Health:</strong> Omada Health (Nasdaq: OMDA) is reverse engineering the way healthcare is delivered in America, putting the space between doctor visits–where health is won or lost–at the center of care. Today's healthcare system poorly serves chronic conditions that require ongoing support outside of the exam room, like obesity, diabetes, hypertension, cholesterol, and musculoskeletal conditions. Omada’s virtual-first model combines human-led care teams, connected devices, and AI-enabled technology to deliver personalized care at scale, including support for GLP-1 therapy. Omada has served more than two million members since launch across 2,000+ employers, health plans, pharmacy benefit managers, and health systems. Learn more at omadahealth.com.</p> <p>Omada is thrilled to share that we’ve been certified as a Great Place to Work! Please&nbsp;<a href="https://www.greatplacetowork.com/certified-company/7022924">click here</a> for more information.</p> <p>We carefully hire the best talent we can find, which means actively seeking diversity of beliefs, backgrounds, education, and ways of thinking. We strive to build an inclusive culture where differences are celebrated and leveraged to inform better design and business decisions. Omada is proud to be an equal opportunity workplace and affirmative action employer. We are committed to equal opportunity regardless of race, color, religion, sex, gender identity, national origin, ancestry, citizenship, age, physical or mental disability, legally protected medical condition, family care status, military or veteran status, marital status, domestic partner status, sexual orientation, or any other basis protected by local, state, or federal laws.</p> <p>Below is a summary of salary ranges, by geographic zone, for this role*. These ranges represent a good faith estimate of the minimum and maximum base salary for the position in that zone at the time of posting. Please refer to this<a href="https://drive.google.com/file/d/14E-cuIgPhwmVeVg3UdhzqhOYqeu-pTEU/view?usp=sharing"> </a><a href="https://www.omadahealth.com/hubfs/Hiring%20Documents/Omada_Salary_Zones.pdf">resource</a> for detail on zone designations.&nbsp;&nbsp;</p> <p>Zone 1: $179,400 - $224,300</p> <p>Zone 2: $171,600 - $214,500</p> <p>Zone 3: $156,000 - $195,000</p> <p>Please note that zones may be updated as market data changes but we will honor the range applicable to your zone (which is determined by your location) as of your application date.</p> <p>*The actual offer, including the compensation package, will be within the posted range for the relevant zone and will be determined based on multiple factors, such as the candidate's skills and experience, and other business considerations such as internal equity.</p> <p>Please&nbsp;<a href="https://www.omadahealth.com/applicant-specific-notice">click here </a>for more information on our Candidate Privacy Notice.</p>

title: Forward Deployed Engineer
full_description: <p>Canopy Connect is building the infrastructure that powers best-in-class insurance experiences. We empower consumers to gain value and convenience from their own data, and we enable businesses to securely and quickly support their customers.</p> <p>We're growing quickly and are scaling up. As&nbsp;<strong>Forward Deployed Engineer, </strong>you'll ensure that Canopy Connect’s API customers launch with our product quickly and successfully. This position requires strong technical and communication skills. You will work with companies that range from small startups to large technology unicorns.&nbsp;</p> <p><strong>Responsibilities</strong></p> <ul> <li>Lead end-to-end API onboarding and implementation for new customers, guiding technical teams through setup, testing, and go-live.</li> <li>Serve as primary post-sale contact for strategic enterprise accounts, managing cross-functional relationships across product, engineering, and executive teams.</li> <li>Act as an internal advocate for customer needs, providing structured feedback to product and engineering to inform roadmap priorities.</li> <li>Analyze usage data to identify expansion opportunities and ensure customers realize measurable value from Canopy Connect’s platform.</li> <li>Support contract renewals and growth initiatives in partnership with Sales, Engineering and Leadership.</li> </ul> <p><strong>About you</strong></p> <ul> <li>3-5 years of software engineering experience, preferably with&nbsp;our tech stack: <ul> <li>Node.js + Express</li> <li>React + TailwindCSS + Next.js + React Aria</li> <li>PostgreSQL</li> <li>Design skills in Figma, Adobe XD, Photoshop or similar</li> <li>Amazon Web Services</li> </ul> </li> <li>Proven success managing large or strategic enterprise accounts with complex integration needs.</li> <li>Demonstrated ability to design and operationalize customer success processes in early-stage or scaling organizations.</li> <li>Strong technical acumen and ability to troubleshoot issues related to APIs, data integrations, and authentication workflows.</li> <li>Excellent communication skills with the ability to translate technical concepts for non-technical stakeholders.</li> <li>Experience collaborating closely with engineering and product teams to deliver customer-driven solutions.</li> <li>Understanding of UX principles, workflow optimization, and data security protocols</li> </ul> <p>Please note that we are only accepting candidates who are authorized to work in the United States or Canada.</p> <p>The expected base salary range for this position is: $125k - $150k depending on experience and skills. We may go above this range commensurate with experience.</p><div class="content-conclusion"><hr> <p><strong>Our Benefits</strong></p> <ul> <li><strong>Healthcare</strong>: We offer company-sponsored health, dental and vision insurance for employees and dependents.</li> <li><strong>Equity</strong>: We are a team of owners and we provide our team with generous equity grants.</li> <li><strong>Time to Recharge</strong>: We offer unlimited paid time off. Unplug, recharge, and come back refreshed.</li> <li><strong>Remote Friendly</strong>: We are a fully distributed team. We all work remotely. We offer a $1,000 stipend to equip you with a home office that is comfortable and personalized to your needs.</li> <li><strong>Friday Reimbursed Lunch</strong>: Order lunch on Fridays, and we'll cover it!</li> <li><strong>401K</strong>: We offer a 3.5% match.&nbsp;</li> </ul> <p><strong>Our Core Values</strong></p> <ul> <li><strong>Be Worthy of Trust | </strong>We are building critical infrastructure that processes sensitive data. We earn trust every day by being transparent, honest and clear to all stakeholders.</li> <li><strong>Find a Way |</strong> We seize opportunities and iterate quickly. We refuse to get blocked. We are relentlessly optimistic about possibilities. Tenacious and creative, we find a way.</li> <li><strong>Own It | </strong>We are owners of the product and the company, and we own our successes as well as our failures. We value people who embody extreme ownership.</li> <li><strong>Think “We” Not “I” | </strong>We prioritize the success of the team over individual contributions. The best decisions are made through triangulating the opinions of the team. Productive disagreement leads to good decisions. We don’t tolerate brilliant jerks.</li> <li><strong>Raise the Bar</strong> <strong>|</strong> Our standard is to be the best in the world at what we do. We redefine excellence each day.</li> </ul> <hr> <p>To protect the interests of all parties, Canopy Connect will not accept unsolicited resumes from any source other than directly from a candidate. Any unsolicited resumes sent to Canopy Connect will be considered Canopy Connect property. Canopy Connect will not pay a fee for any placement resulting from the receipt of an unsolicited resume. Canopy Connect will consider any candidate for whom an Agency has submitted an unsolicited resume to have been referred by the Agency free of any charges or fees.</p> <p>&nbsp;</p></div>

title: Forward Deployed Engineer, Data & Analytics
location: Atlanta; Boston; Charlotte; Chicago; Dallas; Los Angeles; New York; San Francisco
full_description: <h3><strong>Company Overview</strong></h3> <p>We are the better way to work in finance. As private equity’s value creation partner, we sit at the heart of PE—where sponsors and CFOs meet. Through financial consulting rooted in data, technology, and AI, we help clients drive value—supporting the office of the CFO to drive end-to-end value creation.</p> <p>If you crave challenging work and are looking to&nbsp;grow, come solve&nbsp;complex&nbsp;issues alongside&nbsp;1,600+ finance &amp; technology experts in&nbsp;a supportive, collaborative&nbsp;environment.&nbsp;</p> <p>Backed&nbsp;by premier private equity firms and headquartered&nbsp;in New York with 11&nbsp;offices&nbsp;around&nbsp;the&nbsp;globe, we are a high-growth, entrepreneurial firm&nbsp;looking&nbsp;for&nbsp;people&nbsp;who&nbsp;want&nbsp;to be part of building&nbsp;something&nbsp;great. Come make your mark.&nbsp;</p> <h3><strong>Data &amp; Analytics</strong></h3> <p>Accordion's Data &amp; Analytics and Engineering team offers cutting-edge, intelligent solutions to a global clientele, leveraging a blend of domain knowledge, sophisticated technology tools, and deep analytics capabilities to tackle complex business challenges.</p> <p>We partner with Private Equity clients and their Portfolio Companies across diverse sectors, including Retail, CPG, Healthcare, Media &amp; Entertainment, Technology, and Logistics.</p> <p>We deliver agentic workflows, and solutions designed to streamline processes and enhance business insights across vast and complex data sets ranging from Sales, Operations, Marketing, Pricing, Customer Strategies, and more.</p> <p>This role can be based in any of our US office locations and has the potential for client travel to be onsite. Ideal candidates should be local to the desired location.</p> <p>This position is not eligible for immigration sponsorship.</p> <h3><strong>About the Role and You</strong></h3> <p>We're looking for a rare combination: a data scientist who thinks like a strategist and a strategist who speaks fluent data. You fall in love with problems and quantify complex situations using math. This isn't a role in building models in isolation—it's about transforming how a business makes decisions, identifying opportunities, and driving growth. You'll be an equal parts analyst, advisor, and architect of our strategic direction.</p> <h3><strong>What You’ll Do</strong></h3> <p>You'll sit at the intersection of data and strategy, turning complex business challenges into quantifiable opportunities. This means diving deep into our numbers to surface insights that others miss, then translating those insights into actionable growth strategies. You'll work directly with leadership to shape major business decisions, from market expansion and pricing strategy to resource allocation and competitive positioning.</p> <p>Your day-to-day will involve identifying the right questions to ask before anyone else thinks of asking them, building the analytical frameworks to answer those questions, and presenting recommendations that fundamentally shift how we operate. You'll own strategic initiatives from conception through execution, measuring impact and iterating as you go. You’ll travel to client sites as needed, leading AI assessment and Strategy Engagements.</p> <h3><strong>What We're Looking For</strong></h3> <p><strong>The Strategic Mindset:</strong> You naturally think several moves ahead. You understand business models, competitive dynamics, and growth levers. You can walk into a room with executives and hold your own in discussions about market strategy, unit economics, and long-term planning. You've seen how businesses succeed and fail, and you know how to spot the difference.</p> <p><strong>The Technical Depth:</strong> You're genuinely strong in quantitative methods—statistics, predictive modeling, optimization, and experimental design. You can wrangle messy data, build robust analyses, and know when a sophisticated model is needed versus when a simple calculation will do. Python, R, and SQL are still in your wheelhouse.</p> <p><strong>The Translation Layer: </strong>You can explain complex analytical concepts to non-technical stakeholders without dumbing them down. You know how to build conviction around data-driven recommendations and can defend your methodology when challenged. You make the quantitative accessible and actionable.</p> <p><strong>The Business Acumen:</strong> You understand P&amp;Ls, growth metrics, customer economics, and operational levers. You've worked closely enough with business teams to know what keeps them up at night and how your analyses can help them sleep better.</p> <h3><strong>Requirements</strong></h3> <ul> <li>5+ years of experience in data science, engineering or data engineering roles with increasing strategic responsibility</li> <li>Strong foundation in data platforms, data science, statistics, machine learning, and quantitative analysis</li> <li>Additional considering will be given to engineers who have a background in data but have yet to work deeply with LLM enabled workflows</li> <li>Experience working directly with business leaders to convey options and tradeoffs to define solution design and scope.</li> <li>Demonstrated experience influencing business strategy through data</li> <li>Track record of identifying and driving growth opportunities</li> <li>Excellent communication skills with technical and executive audiences</li> <li>Proficiency in modern data science tools and languages</li> <li>Understanding of business finance and growth frameworks</li> </ul> <h3><strong>Ideal Background</strong></h3> <p>You might come from management consulting or technical consulting with deep analytical chops, a curiosity for working on business problems, strategic roles at high-growth companies, corporate strategy teams with hands-on data work, or data science roles where you've grown into strategic advisory. What matters most is that you've successfully used data to change how businesses operate and grow.</p> <h3><strong>What Success Looks Like</strong></h3> <p>In your first year, you'll have identified three major strategic opportunities we weren't seeing before, built the analytical infrastructure to evaluate them rigorously, and driven at least one to implementation with measurable business impact. You'll have become a trusted advisor to leadership and the person they turn to when facing the company's thorniest strategic questions.</p> <p>The annual salary for this role ranges from: $145,000 to $195,000 USD + benefits + bonus + <em>overtime eligible (only added for applicable roles</em>) -remove this if not an overtime eligible role. Actual compensation packages are determined by evaluating a wide array of factors unique to each candidate, including but not limited to geographic location, skill set, years and depth of experience, education, certifications, cost of labor and internal equity.</p> <p>Accordion is an Equal Opportunity Employer. We are committed to building a team that represents a variety of backgrounds, perspectives, and skills.&nbsp; We do not discriminate on the basis of race, color, religion, marital status, age, national origin, ancestry, physical or mental disability, medical​​​ condition, pregnancy, genetic information, gender, sexual orientation, gender identity or ​expression, veteran status, or any other status protected under federal, state, or local law.</p> <p>Please note that as per Accordion policy, we do not accept unsolicited resumes from third-party recruiters unless such recruiters are engaged to provide candidates for a specified opening and in alignment with our inclusive diversity values. Any employment agency, person or entity that submits an unsolicited resume does so with the understanding that Accordion will have the right to hire that applicant at its discretion without any fee owed to the submitting employment agency, person or entity.</p> <p><span style="color: rgb(255, 255, 255);">#LI-NG1</span></p>

title: Senior Data Engineer
full_description: <div class="content-intro"><p><strong>We Breathe Life Into Data</strong></p> <p><span style="font-weight: 400;">At Komodo Health, our mission is to reduce the global burden of disease. And we believe that smarter use of data is essential to this mission. That’s why we built the <a href="https://www.komodohealth.com/technology/healthcare-map?hsLang=en" target="_blank">Healthcare Map</a> — the industry’s largest, most complete, precise view of the U.S. healthcare system — by combining de-identified, real-world patient data with innovative algorithms and decades of clinical experience. The Healthcare Map serves as our foundation for a powerful suite of software applications, helping us answer healthcare’s most complex questions for our partners. Across the healthcare ecosystem, we’re helping our clients unlock critical insights to track detailed patient behaviors and treatment patterns, identify gaps in care, address unmet patient needs, and reduce the global burden of disease.&nbsp;</span></p> <p><span style="font-weight: 400;">As we pursue these goals, it remains essential to us that we stay grounded in our values: be awesome, seek growth, deliver “wow,” and enjoy the ride. At Komodo, you will be joining a team of ambitious, supportive Dragons with diverse backgrounds but a shared passion to deliver on our mission to reduce the burden of disease —&nbsp;and enjoy the journey along the way. </span></p></div><p><strong>We Breathe Life Into Data</strong></p> <p>At Komodo Health, our mission is to reduce the global burden of disease. And we believe that smarter use of data is essential to this mission. That’s why we built the <a href="https://www.komodohealth.com/technology/healthcare-map?hsLang=en">Healthcare Map</a> — the industry’s largest, most complete, precise view of the U.S. healthcare system — by combining de-identified, real-world patient data with innovative algorithms and decades of clinical experience. The Healthcare Map serves as our foundation for a powerful suite of software applications, helping us answer healthcare’s most complex questions for our partners. Across the healthcare ecosystem, we’re helping our clients unlock critical insights to track detailed patient behaviors and treatment patterns, identify gaps in care, address unmet patient needs, and reduce the global burden of disease.&nbsp;</p> <p>As we pursue these goals, it remains essential to us that we stay grounded in our values: be awesome, seek growth, deliver “wow,” and enjoy the ride. At Komodo, you will be joining a team of ambitious, supportive Dragons with diverse backgrounds but a shared passion to deliver on our mission to reduce the burden of disease — and enjoy the journey along the way.</p> <p><strong>The Opportunity at Komodo Health</strong></p> <p data-start="1314" data-end="1719">Join Komodo Health's Data Foundations team and play a critical role in shaping the core data products that fuel our Healthcare Map. This role is essential for transforming massive, complex healthcare datasets into performant, trustworthy, and usable data assets that directly power both customer-facing applications and internal product innovation. By building and scaling our foundational data systems, you will directly enable the transparency and efficiency required to drive better health outcomes across the industry.</p> <p><strong>Mission</strong></p> <p>The Senior Data Engineer will design, build, operate, and improve large-scale data pipelines and foundational data products that power Komodo’s Healthcare Map, analytics products, and downstream AI/ML-enabled use cases. This is a hands-on engineering role focused on processing complex healthcare data at scale, improving reliability and performance, and contributing to the technical direction of core data systems.</p> <p><strong>Looking back on your first 12 months at Komodo Health, you will have…</strong></p> <ul> <li><strong>Architectural Advancement:</strong> Deliver high-impact technical initiatives that improve pipeline performance, scalability, and system efficiency.</li> <li><strong>Platform Hardening:</strong> Improve the reliability, observability, and cost-efficiency of core Data Foundations systems.</li> <li><strong>Healthcare Data Innovation:</strong> Develop deep domain expertise and contribute novel approaches to challenges such as patient journey mapping and identity resolution.</li> <li><strong>Cross-Functional Delivery:</strong> Partner with Data Product and Engineering teams to ship scalable, production-grade data solutions.</li> <li><strong>Partner on architecture:&nbsp;</strong>Raise the bar across the team through mentorship, design reviews, and engineering best practices.</li> </ul> <p><strong>Key Responsibilities</strong></p> <ul> <li data-section-id="2gkp8u" data-start="1290" data-end="1447">Build, operate, and optimize large-scale production data pipelines using Python, SQL, Airflow, cloud infrastructure, and distributed processing frameworks.</li> <li data-section-id="1f97rl8" data-start="1448" data-end="1599">Transform massive healthcare claims, EHR, and reference datasets into trusted, performant Healthcare Map data products and serving-ready data assets.</li> <li data-section-id="1qwig7r" data-start="1600" data-end="1724">Strengthen pipeline reliability through data quality checks, validation, lineage, observability, monitoring, and alerting.</li> <li data-section-id="1rqfa2o" data-start="1725" data-end="1821">Debug complex data, system, and performance issues across computationally intensive workflows.</li> <li data-section-id="1ej7f07" data-start="1822" data-end="1969">Partner with Data Product Quality, Product, Platform, and Engineering teams to translate healthcare data needs into scalable technical solutions.</li> <li data-section-id="glj1sc" data-start="1970" data-end="2094">Contribute to system design, architecture, code quality, testing, documentation, CI/CD, and rotational production support.</li> <li data-section-id="1533p9t" data-start="2095" data-end="2205">Enable downstream analytics, product, and AI/ML use cases through high-quality, well-modeled, reliable data.</li> </ul> <p><strong>What you bring to Komodo Health:</strong></p> <ul> <li>Healthcare data experience across claims, clinical, RWE, provider, patient, or life sciences datasets, including coding systems such as ICD-10, CPT, NDC, or NPI.</li> <li data-section-id="9cpdi" data-start="1018" data-end="1123">Strong hands-on experience building, operating, and debugging production-grade data pipelines at scale.</li> <li data-section-id="2fmimj" data-start="1124" data-end="1225">Advanced Python and SQL skills, with experience in Airflow or similar workflow orchestration tools.</li> <li data-section-id="10v9o2b" data-start="1226" data-end="1303">Experience with Spark or comparable distributed data processing frameworks.</li> <li data-section-id="1fl1uk4" data-start="1304" data-end="1370">Proven experience designing and operating data solutions in AWS.</li> <li data-section-id="15o1h7o" data-start="1371" data-end="1473">Strong instincts for data quality, reliability, root-cause analysis, and production troubleshooting.</li> <li data-section-id="hrrb9" data-start="1474" data-end="1589">Ability to communicate technical trade-offs clearly and collaborate with engineering, product, and data partners.</li> <li data-section-id="12hi9bh" data-start="1590" data-end="1706">Comfort using AI-assisted engineering tools for productivity, debugging, documentation, and technical exploration.</li> </ul> <p data-section-id="1emn294" data-start="4785" data-end="4825"><strong>AI-Augmented Engineering Expectations:</strong></p> <ul> <li>You will be expected to leverage AI-augmented engineering tools, such as ChatGPT, Gemini, or Claude, to improve productivity and technical decision-making. This may include using AI to generate and refine code, accelerate documentation, automate test case creation, debug complex issues, explore unfamiliar technical concepts, and assess architectural trade-offs and risks.</li> </ul> <p><strong>Additional skills and experience we’d prioritize (nice to have)…</strong></p> <ul> <li data-section-id="17jtyuh" data-start="1118" data-end="1243">Experience delivering external-facing data products through customers, APIs, serving layers, or production access patterns.</li> <li data-section-id="e01i3n" data-start="1244" data-end="1363">Ability to optimize high-scale data architectures for performance, cost, versioning, and large-volume productization.</li> <li data-section-id="1ol2g4e" data-start="1364" data-end="1464">Experience applying AI or agentic workflows to engineering, data quality, delivery, or operations.</li> <li data-section-id="1o613s4" data-start="1465" data-end="1572">Success in high-growth or ambiguous environments that require balancing architecture, speed, and quality.</li> </ul> <p>Open to US remote, OR SF/NYC hybrid</p> <p><span style="font-weight: 400;">#LIRemote</span></p><div class="content-pay-transparency"><div class="pay-input"><div class="description"><p>The pay range for each job posting reflects a minimum and maximum range of annual base pay that we reasonably expect to pay for this position within the US. We carefully consider multiple business-related factors when determining compensation, including job-related skills, work experience, geographic work location, relevant training and certifications, business needs and market demands.</p> <p>&nbsp;</p> <p>The starting annual base pay for this role is listed below. This position may be eligible for performance-based bonuses as determined in the Company’s sole discretion and in accordance with a written agreement or plan. This role may also be eligible for equity awards. In addition, this role is eligible for benefits including, but not limited to, comprehensive health, dental, and vision insurance; flexible time off and holidays; 401(k) with company match; disability insurance and life insurance; and leaves of absence in accordance with applicable state and local laws and regulations and company policy.&nbsp;</p></div><div class="title">San Francisco Bay Area and New York City:</div><div class="pay-range"><span>$207,000</span><span class="divider">&mdash;</span><span>$238,000 USD</span></div></div><div class="pay-input"><div class="title">All Other US Locations: </div><div class="pay-range"><span>$180,000</span><span class="divider">&mdash;</span><span>$207,000 USD</span></div></div></div><div class="content-conclusion"><p data-start="513" data-end="581"><strong>Komodo's AI Standard<br></strong></p> <p data-sourcepos="9:1-9:347">At Komodo, we're not just witnessing the AI revolution – we're leading it. This is a pivotal moment in time, where being first to market with AI transforms industries and sets the bar. We've already established industry leadership in leveraging AI to revolutionize healthcare, and we expect every team member to contribute.&nbsp;AI here isn't optional; it's foundational. We expect you to integrate AI into your daily work – from summarizing documents to automating workflows and uncovering insights. This isn't just about efficiency; it's about making every moment more meaningful, building on trust in AI, and driving our collective success.</p> <p><strong><em>Join us in shaping the future of healthcare intelligence.</em><br><br>Where You’ll Work</strong></p> <p>Komodo Health has a hybrid work model with hubs in San Francisco, New York City, and Chicago. Roles vary — some can be performed from anywhere in the country, others are scoped to a specific region, and some are based near one of our hubs. For hub-based Dragons, we're building intentional in-office rhythms alongside the flexibility that's core to how we work. Whatever your setup, expectations will always be clear before you join.</p> <p><strong>Equal Opportunity Statement</strong></p> <p><span style="font-weight: 400;">Komodo Health provides equal employment opportunities to all applicants and employees. We prohibit discrimination and harassment of any type with regard to race, color, religion, age, sex, national origin, disability status, genetics, protected veteran status, sexual orientation, gender identity or expression, or any other characteristic protected by federal, state, or local laws.&nbsp;</span></p> <p data-start="279" data-end="506">By submitting your application, you acknowledge that you have read and understand Komodo Health’s&nbsp;<a class="decorated-link" href="https://www.komodohealth.com/privacy-notice-for-employees-and-contractors/" target="_new" data-start="381" data-end="503">Privacy Notice for Employees and Contractors</a>.</p> <p data-start="513" data-end="581"><em>This notice explains how we collect, use, and retain applicant data.</em></p></div>

title: Senior Forward Deployed Engineer, AI
full_description: <div class="content-intro"><p><strong>We Breathe Life Into Data</strong></p> <p><span style="font-weight: 400;">At Komodo Health, our mission is to reduce the global burden of disease. And we believe that smarter use of data is essential to this mission. That’s why we built the <a href="https://www.komodohealth.com/technology/healthcare-map?hsLang=en" target="_blank">Healthcare Map</a> — the industry’s largest, most complete, precise view of the U.S. healthcare system — by combining de-identified, real-world patient data with innovative algorithms and decades of clinical experience. The Healthcare Map serves as our foundation for a powerful suite of software applications, helping us answer healthcare’s most complex questions for our partners. Across the healthcare ecosystem, we’re helping our clients unlock critical insights to track detailed patient behaviors and treatment patterns, identify gaps in care, address unmet patient needs, and reduce the global burden of disease.&nbsp;</span></p> <p><span style="font-weight: 400;">As we pursue these goals, it remains essential to us that we stay grounded in our values: be awesome, seek growth, deliver “wow,” and enjoy the ride. At Komodo, you will be joining a team of ambitious, supportive Dragons with diverse backgrounds but a shared passion to deliver on our mission to reduce the burden of disease —&nbsp;and enjoy the journey along the way. </span></p></div><p><strong>The Opportunity at Komodo Health</strong></p> <p data-start="841" data-end="1268">Komodo’s Labs team builds Marmot — our AI-native product, powered by an MCP Gateway architecture that connects our data platform to the tools customers need. The Forward Deployed Engineering team is where Marmot meets the real world: we deploy AI-native solutions into complex enterprise healthcare environments, including customer cloud infrastructure, strict compliance requirements, and integrations that vary by engagement.</p> <p data-start="1270" data-end="1448">We sit at the intersection of Engineering, Product, and Revenue. We build, deploy, and own production outcomes for some of Komodo’s most complex and visible customer engagements</p> <p><strong>About the role</strong></p> <p data-start="107" data-end="375">As a Senior Forward Deployed Engineer, AI, you will own end-to-end delivery for some of Komodo’s most technically demanding customer engagements — from solution architecture through deployment inside a customer’s cloud, data infrastructure, and compliance environment.</p> <p data-start="377" data-end="679">This is a deeply technical engineering role with direct customer context. You will write production code, design cloud-native deployment patterns, build integrations, develop MCP servers that extend the Komodo platform into a customer’s stack, and debug issues in environments you do not fully control.</p> <p data-start="681" data-end="1025">The Forward Deployed Engineering team operates with a clear principle: own your work end to end. You will be expected to translate customer requirements into scalable, product-adjacent solutions, communicate clearly with technical and non-technical stakeholders, surface risks early, and make sound tradeoffs between speed and production rigor.</p> <p data-start="1027" data-end="1331">This role is best suited for someone who enjoys ambiguity, takes initiative, and is energized by solving complex problems in close partnership with customers. You’ll work on some of Komodo’s most complex and visible deployments — and see your work run in production.</p> <p><strong>In your first 90 days, you will have:</strong></p> <ul> <li>Learned the Marmot platform end to end, including the agent loop, MCP Gateway integration surface, and deployment toolchain that allows FDE to stand up isolated customer environments independently.</li> <li>Shipped your first customer-facing deliverable, such as a new MCP server, Databricks/Delta Lake integration, or agentic workflow — and seen it run in a real customer environment.</li> <li>Built strong working relationships with Sales, Solutions Engineering, and Product partners, with a clear understanding of who owns what and where you create leverage.</li> <li>Mapped the technical gaps in your first customer deployment and proposed a concrete plan to close them.</li> </ul> <p><strong>By the end of your first year, you will have:</strong></p> <ul> <li>Led end-to-end delivery of at least one high-complexity customer deployment, standing up AI-native solutions inside a customer’s own cloud environment and delivering outcomes with measurable business impact.</li> <li>Built and shipped production-grade agentic workflows and MCP servers that solve customer-specific problems.</li> <li>Established yourself as a trusted technical partner to senior customer stakeholders by leading architecture reviews, navigating compliance constraints, and helping convert pilots into broader programs.</li> <li>Contributed reusable deployment patterns to the FDE playbook, creating frameworks the team can rely on for future customer engagements.</li> <li>Participated in Komodo’s architecture governance, influencing platform decisions based on what you have seen and built in the field.</li> <li>Partnered cross-functionally with Sales, Product, Data, and Core Engineering to close gaps and shape the roadmap.</li> </ul> <p><strong>These are the essential job duties you will be responsible for …</strong></p> <ul> <li data-section-id="uo0uhx" data-start="123" data-end="390">Design, build, and deploy AI-native solutions inside customer environments, including MCP servers, agentic workflows, and custom integrations that adapt Komodo’s platform to each customer’s cloud infrastructure, systems, data contracts, and compliance requirements.</li> <li data-section-id="153nckv" data-start="392" data-end="567">Own the infrastructure layer for customer deployments, including Terraform-managed AWS environments, VPC networking, IAM, security controls, and data isolation requirements.</li> <li data-section-id="3uv6jt" data-start="569" data-end="763">Work with data at scale across tools like Databricks, Delta Lake, Snowflake, and S3 — debugging pipeline failures, optimizing performance, and building integrations that hold up in production.</li> <li data-section-id="1lhwxau" data-start="765" data-end="947">Drive day-to-day technical engagement with customer stakeholders by scoping work, communicating progress, explaining tradeoffs, surfacing risks early, and building trust over time.</li> <li data-section-id="ahe9mc" data-start="949" data-end="1109" data-is-last-node="">Turn field learnings into reusable FDE patterns, deployment templates, engineering standards, and roadmap input for Core Platform and architecture governance.</li> </ul> <p><strong>What you bring to Komodo Health (required):</strong></p> <p data-start="204" data-end="439">We know strong candidates may not have every tool listed below. We’re most interested in engineers who have shipped production systems, learn quickly, and can operate across AI, infrastructure, data, and customer-facing technical work.</p> <ul data-start="441" data-end="2212"> <li data-section-id="1p49v8k" data-start="441" data-end="601">Production engineering experience: 7+ years of software engineering experience, with a track record of building and owning systems that run in production.</li> <li data-section-id="weczrg" data-start="603" data-end="821">Agentic AI experience: Hands-on experience building LLM-powered applications, agentic workflows, or AI tools beyond prototypes. Experience with frameworks such as LangGraph, Strands, CrewAI, or similar is a plus.</li> <li data-section-id="f70tr3" data-start="823" data-end="1012">Cloud infrastructure ownership: Strong AWS and Terraform experience, including VPC networking, IAM, security controls, and standing up reliable customer or single-tenant environments.</li> <li data-section-id="q2yt4" data-start="1014" data-end="1194">Application engineering depth: Strong Python skills, experience with APIs, async service patterns, and building software that other teams, customers, or businesses depend on.</li> <li data-section-id="1xpseix" data-start="1196" data-end="1416">Data platform experience: Experience working with large-scale data systems such as Databricks, Delta Lake, Snowflake, S3, or similar platforms, including debugging failures and improving performance or reliability.</li> <li data-section-id="1i9zmhz" data-start="1418" data-end="1625">Security and enterprise judgment: Comfort designing for data isolation, customer security requirements, and regulated environments with constraints such as SOC 2, BAA, HIPAA, or similar considerations.</li> <li data-section-id="1nevqbv" data-start="1627" data-end="1796">Integration mindset: Experience building integration surfaces for AI systems, including MCP servers or equivalent patterns that connect tools, data, and workflows.</li> <li data-section-id="r71yoo" data-start="1798" data-end="2028">Customer-facing technical leadership: Ability to lead technical conversations with customer engineers and senior stakeholders, explain tradeoffs clearly, drive alignment, and build trust without handing off the conversation.</li> <li data-section-id="1m4wl30" data-start="2030" data-end="2212">Comfort with ambiguity: Ability to work across infrastructure, application, data, and AI layers, find a path when one does not already exist, and deliver on what you commit to.</li> </ul> <p><strong>Nice to have:</strong></p> <ul> <li>Experience with LLM evaluation frameworks in production, such as LangSmith, Braintrust, Ragas, or equivalent tools.</li> <li>Familiarity with healthcare or life sciences data, including IQVIA data structures, pharma customer workflows, payer data contracts, or similar data environments.</li> <li>Experience with API gateway or service mesh patterns used for AI tool integration.</li> <li>Contributions to shared platform infrastructure, including code committed to shared repos, participation in architecture reviews, or tooling that other engineers depend on.</li> <li>Experience deploying in regulated environments with data residency, audit logging, or multi-party data access constraints.</li> </ul> <p><strong>A Note On Travel:</strong></p> <div ambiguity: Ability to work across infrastructure, application, data, and AI layers, find a path when one does not already exist, and deliver on what you commit to.</li> </ul> <p><strong>Nice to have:</strong></p> <ul> <li>Experience with LLM evaluation frameworks in production, such as LangSmith, Braintrust, Ragas, or equivalent tools.</li> <li>Familiarity with healthcare or life sciences data, including IQVIA data structures, pharma customer workflows, payer data contracts, or similar data environments.</li> <li>Experience with API gateway or service mesh patterns used for AI tool integration.</li> <li>Contributions to shared platform infrastructure, including code committed to shared repos, participation in architecture reviews, or tooling that other engineers depend on.</li> This isn't just about efficiency; it's about making every moment more meaningful, building on trust in AI, and driving our collective success.</p> <p><strong><em>Join us in shaping the future of healthcare intelligence.</em><br><br>Where You’ll Work</strong></p> <p>Komodo Health has a hybrid work model with hubs in San Francisco, New York City, and Chicago.
