title: Staff Data Engineer
full_description: <div class="content-intro"><p><strong>We Breathe Life Into Data</strong></p> <p><span style="font-weight: 400;">At Komodo Health, our mission is to reduce the global burden of disease. And we believe that smarter use of data is essential to this mission. That’s why we built the <a href="https://www.komodohealth.com/technology/healthcare-map?hsLang=en" target="_blank">Healthcare Map</a> — the industry’s largest, most complete, precise view of the U.S. healthcare system — by combining de-identified, real-world patient data with innovative algorithms and decades of clinical experience. The Healthcare Map serves as our foundation for a powerful suite of software applications, helping us answer healthcare’s most complex questions for our partners. Across the healthcare ecosystem, we’re helping our clients unlock critical insights to track detailed patient behaviors and treatment patterns, identify gaps in care, address unmet patient needs, and reduce the global burden of disease.&nbsp;</span></p> <p><span style="font-weight: 400;">As we pursue these goals, it remains essential to us that we stay grounded in our values: be awesome, seek growth, deliver “wow,” and enjoy the ride. At Komodo, you will be joining a team of ambitious, supportive Dragons with diverse backgrounds but a shared passion to deliver on our mission to reduce the burden of disease —&nbsp;and enjoy the journey along the way. </span></p></div><p><strong>The Opportunity at Komodo Health:</strong>&nbsp;</p> <p><strong>Team:</strong> Data Foundations owns the core data products that power Komodo’s Healthcare Map. We design and deliver the foundational data assets, models, and serving capabilities that make healthcare data usable, performant, and trustworthy for customers and internal product teams. Our work reaches customers directly and also powers consuming applications through APIs, analytics layers, and other data delivery surfaces.</p> <p><strong>Mission:</strong>This role exists to architect and deliver the foundational data products and serving capabilities that turn complex healthcare data into trusted, production-ready assets for customers and applications. As a Staff-level IC, you will set technical direction across data modeling, productization, analytics enablement, and scalable delivery patterns, partnering with Product, Data Science, and consuming application teams to make Healthcare Map data usable at enterprise scale.</p> <p>Komodo Health leverages the latest engineering technology to tackle healthcare’s biggest challenges by transforming extraordinary amounts of data into meaningful insights. As a Staff Data Engineer, you will drive the development of state-of-the-art foundational products that unlock the potential of our Healthcare Map, fostering a culture of technical excellence and transparency across the organization.</p> <p><strong>Looking back on your first 12 months at Komodo Health, you will have accomplished…</strong></p> <ul> <li><strong>Architectural Advancement:</strong> Managed and delivered high-impact projects that introduced new technical and data services to market, significantly improving pipeline performance.</li> <li><strong>Innovation in Healthcare Data:</strong> Developed a deep understanding of domain-specific challenges and introduced innovative algorithms that address complex patient journey mapping.</li> <li><strong>Collaborative Roadmap Delivery:</strong> Partnered across Data Product and Engineering to define technical requirements and deliver scalable, production-grade solutions that meet business needs.</li> <li><strong>Platform Hardening:</strong> Built and hardened core Data Foundations components, measurable through improved reliability, observability, and cost-efficiency of our Healthcare Map products.</li> <li><strong>Technical Multiplication:</strong> Mentored and up-leveled the engineering team, raising the bar for code quality and system design through coaching and rigorous design reviews.</li> </ul> <p><strong>These are the essential job duties you will be responsible for…</strong></p> <ul> <li><strong>Data Product Development &amp; Delivery:</strong> Architect, build, and deliver scalable Healthcare Map data products that power direct customer use cases, APIs, analytics surfaces, serving layers, and internal applications.</li> <li><strong>Scalable Data Systems:</strong> Design and implement high-performance data processing and serving patterns across large-scale healthcare datasets, using the right tools for the problem across SQL, Python, Spark, Rust, C++, and emerging AI-enabled engineering workflows.</li> <li><strong>Foundational Data Models: </strong>Create shared data models, productized datasets, reusable libraries, and technical standards that become the foundation for downstream product, analytics, and application teams.</li> <li><strong>Serving &amp; Consumption Enablement: </strong>Build data products that are easy to consume through APIs, serving layers, exports, analytics environments, and customer-facing delivery mechanisms.</li> <li><strong>Cross-Functional Product Delivery: </strong>Partner with Product, Data Science, Quality, Platform, and application teams to translate complex healthcare use cases into production-grade technical designs and execution plans.</li> <li><strong>Technical Leadership:</strong> Lead complex, multi-quarter initiatives, making clear trade-offs across performance, scalability, maintainability, cost, reliability, and time-to-market.</li> <li><strong>Quality, Observability &amp; Trust: </strong>Define and implement data quality checks, validation frameworks, observability, lineage, monitoring, and alerting to ensure Healthcare Map products are accurate, explainable, and reliable.</li> <li><strong>Engineering Excellence:</strong> Raise the bar for system design, code quality, documentation, testing, CI/CD, and operational readiness across the team.</li> <li><strong>Engineering Leverage:</strong> Mentor engineers through design reviews, technical deep dives, pairing, and architectural guidance, helping the team make better decisions at scale.</li> </ul> <p><strong>What you bring to Komodo Health (required):</strong></p> <ul> <li><strong>Data Product Engineering Expertise:</strong> Extensive experience building production-grade, large-scale data products, services, and analytical systems that serve real customer and business use cases.</li> <li><strong>Modern Data Systems Depth:</strong> Strong technical depth across SQL, distributed data processing, cloud data platforms, MPP databases, and high-scale compute frameworks such as Spark, Python, Rust, C++, or equivalent technologies.</li> <li><strong>Architecture &amp; Systems Design:</strong> Demonstrated ability to design data models, serving patterns, platform components, and system architectures for complex, high-volume data environments.</li> <li><strong>Healthcare Data Product Judgment:</strong> Ability to reason through data quality, identity, longitudinal patient journeys, claims or clinical data complexity, and downstream consumption needs.</li> <li><strong>AI &amp; ML Enablement:</strong> Experience designing data workflows, feature pipelines, evaluation datasets, or infrastructure that supports AI/ML training, inference, experimentation, and monitoring.</li> <li><strong>Analytical &amp; Statistical Rigor:</strong> Strong ability to use data analysis, statistical reasoning, hypothesis testing, and experimental design to validate product quality and business impact.</li> <li><strong>Technical Communication:</strong> Ability to explain technical decisions, trade-offs, risks, and delivery status clearly to engineers, product partners, data scientists, and senior stakeholders.</li> <li><strong>AI-Augmented Engineering:</strong> Ability to use AI tools such as ChatGPT, Gemini, Cursor, Claude, or similar systems to improve engineering productivity, design quality, testing, documentation, and decision-making.</li> </ul> <p><strong>Additional skills and experience we’d prioritize (nice to have):</strong></p> <ul> <li><strong>Healthcare Data Experience:</strong> Experience with claims, clinical, RWE, provider, patient, or life sciences data, including familiarity with coding systems such as ICD-10, CPT, NDC, RxNorm, NPI, or taxonomy data.</li> <li><strong>Data Product Delivery:</strong> Experience building and operating data products that are consumed by customers, analytics users, APIs, applications, or serving layers.</li> <li><strong>High-Scale Data Architecture:</strong> Experience designing systems for large-volume data processing, productization, versioning, delivery, performance optimization, and cost efficiency.</li> <li><strong>Applied AI / Agentic Workflows:</strong> Experience using, designing, or integrating AI-enabled workflows to improve engineering productivity, data quality, extraction, curation, testing, or product delivery.</li> <li><strong>Fast-Growth Execution:</strong> Experience operating in high-growth or ambiguous environments where technical leaders must balance architecture, delivery, quality, and speed.</li> </ul> <p>&nbsp;</p><div class="content-pay-transparency"><div class="pay-input"><div class="description"><p>The pay range for each job posting reflects a minimum and maximum range of annual base pay that we reasonably expect to pay for this position within the US. We carefully consider multiple business-related factors when determining compensation, including job-related skills, work experience, geographic work location, relevant training and certifications, business needs and market demands.</p> <p>&nbsp;</p> <p>The starting annual base pay for this role is listed below. This position may be eligible for performance-based bonuses as determined in the Company’s sole discretion and in accordance with a written agreement or plan. This role may also be eligible for equity awards. In addition, this role is eligible for benefits including, but not limited to, comprehensive health, dental, and vision insurance; flexible time off and holidays; 401(k) with company match; disability insurance and life insurance; and leaves of absence in accordance with applicable state and local laws and regulations and company policy.&nbsp;</p></div><div class="title">San Francisco Bay Area and New York City:</div><div class="pay-range"><span>$208,000</span><span class="divider">&mdash;</span><span>$282,000 USD</span></div></div><div class="pay-input"><div class="title">All Other US Locations: </div><div class="pay-range"><span>$181,000</span><span class="divider">&mdash;</span><span>$245,000 USD</span></div></div></div><div class="content-conclusion"><p data-start="513" data-end="581"><strong>Komodo's AI Standard<br></strong></p> <p data-sourcepos="9:1-9:347">At Komodo, we're not just witnessing the AI revolution – we're leading it. This is a pivotal moment in time, where being first to market with AI transforms industries and sets the bar. We've already established industry leadership in leveraging AI to revolutionize healthcare, and we expect every team member to contribute.&nbsp;AI here isn't optional; it's foundational. We expect you to integrate AI into your daily work – from summarizing documents to automating workflows and uncovering insights. This isn't just about efficiency; it's about making every moment more meaningful, building on trust in AI, and driving our collective success.</p> <p><strong><em>Join us in shaping the future of healthcare intelligence.</em><br><br>Where You’ll Work</strong></p> <p>Komodo Health has a hybrid work model with hubs in San Francisco, New York City, and Chicago. Roles vary — some can be performed from anywhere in the country, others are scoped to a specific region, and some are based near one of our hubs. For hub-based Dragons, we're building intentional in-office rhythms alongside the flexibility that's core to how we work. Whatever your setup, expectations will always be clear before you join.</p> <p><strong>Equal Opportunity Statement</strong></p> <p><span style="font-weight: 400;">Komodo Health provides equal employment opportunities to all applicants and employees. We prohibit discrimination and harassment of any type with regard to race, color, religion, age, sex, national origin, disability status, genetics, protected veteran status, sexual orientation, gender identity or expression, or any other characteristic protected by federal, state, or local laws.&nbsp;</span></p> <p data-start="279" data-end="506">By submitting your application, you acknowledge that you have read and understand Komodo Health’s&nbsp;<a class="decorated-link" href="https://www.komodohealth.com/privacy-notice-for-employees-and-contractors/" target="_new" data-start="381" data-end="503">Privacy Notice for Employees and Contractors</a>.</p> <p data-start="513" data-end="581"><em>This notice explains how we collect, use, and retain applicant data.</em></p></div>

title: Senior Analytics Engineer (AI Insurance SaaS)
full_description: <p><strong>About Us: </strong>EvolutionIQ’s mission is to improve the lives of injured and disabled workers and enable them to return to the workforce, saving billions of dollars in avoidable costs and lost productivity to the US and global economies and make insurance more affordable for everyone. We are currently experiencing massive growth and to accomplish our goals, we are hiring world-class talent who want to help build and scale internally, and transform the insurance space. Our team is our #1 priority, and we have been named one of <a href="https://www.evolutioniq.com/resources/evolutioniqs-award-winning-culture-three-years-running-as-an-inc-best-workplace">Inc.’s Best Workplaces</a> 3 years in a row!</p> <p><strong>The Adventure: </strong>We're the leading AI Guidance Platform in the insurance industry today, working with some of the largest insurance carriers in the US and expanding globally. We are seeking a highly skilled and motivated <strong>Senior</strong> <strong>Analytics Engineer</strong> to play a pivotal role in building and scaling our next-generation analytical data platform. This role will be instrumental in shaping our analytics strategy, designing robust data models, and ensuring the availability, accuracy, and performance of our business intelligence data assets. As a key member of our team, you will collaborate closely with data scientists, analysts, and other engineers to deliver data-driven insights that drive critical business decisions.</p> <h4><strong>Responsibilities:</strong></h4> <p><strong>Platform Design and Development:</strong></p> <ul> <li>Lead the design, development, and implementation of a scalable, reliable, and efficient analytical data platform.</li> <li>Influence decisions on technology and tool selection for data ingestion, storage, transformation, and analysis for the analytics platform.</li> <li>Contribute to the overall data architecture and strategy, ensuring alignment with business needs and best practices.</li> </ul> <p><strong>Data Pipeline Engineering:</strong></p> <ul> <li>Build and maintain robust ETL/ELT pipelines to ingest, transform, and load data from various sources into the data warehouse (e.g., cloud storage, databases, APIs).</li> <li>Develop and optimize data models for analytical use cases, ensuring data quality, consistency, and accessibility.</li> </ul> <p><strong>Data Quality and Governance:</strong></p> <ul> <li>Establish and enforce data quality standards and processes to ensure data accuracy and integrity.</li> <li>Implement data governance policies and procedures to manage data access, security, and compliance for analytics use cases</li> <li>Proactively identify and address data quality issues, working with stakeholders to resolve root causes.</li> </ul> <p><strong>Collaboration and Communication:</strong></p> <ul> <li>Partner with data scientists, analysts, and other engineers to understand their data needs and provide solutions.</li> <li>Effectively communicate technical concepts and designs to both technical and non-technical audiences.</li> <li>Mentor and guide data engineers, fostering a culture of learning and collaboration.</li> </ul> <p><strong>Innovation and Continuous Improvement:</strong></p> <ul> <li>Stay up-to-date on the latest trends and technologies in data engineering and analytics.</li> <li>Identify opportunities to improve existing data processes and tools.</li> <li>Proactively propose and implement innovative solutions to address data challenges.</li> </ul> <h4><strong>Required Qualifications &amp; Skills:</strong></h4> <ul> <li>Bachelor's or Master's degree in Computer Science, Engineering, or a related field.</li> <li>5+ years of experience in data engineering, with a focus on building analytical data platforms.</li> <li>Demonstrated experience with cloud data warehousing solutions (e.g. BigQuery, Snowflake, Redshift).</li> <li>Strong proficiency in SQL and experience with data modeling techniques (e.g., star schema, dimensional modeling).</li> <li>Experience building and maintaining ETL/ELT pipelines using tools like Dagster, Apache Airflow, dbt, or similar.</li> <li>Experience with programming languages such as Python, Java, or Scala.</li> <li>Experience with data governance and data quality tools and processes is highly desirable.</li> <li>Excellent problem-solving and analytical skills, along with passion for data and a commitment to data quality.</li> </ul> <p><strong>Work-life, Culture &amp; Perks:&nbsp;</strong></p> <ul> <li><strong>Compensation: </strong>The base salary range is <strong>$200-225K,</strong> with flexibility depending on a candidate’s background and experience. An annual bonus plan and company equity plan (RSUs) are also included in our compensation package.</li> <li><strong>Well-Being: </strong>Medical, dental, vision, short &amp; long-term disability, life insurance and AD&amp;D, and 401k matching. Additional family, wellness, and pet benefits.&nbsp;</li> <li><strong>Home &amp; Family:</strong> Paid time off and sick leave, 100% paid parental leave (16 weeks for primary caregivers and 12 weeks for secondary caregivers). We offer a flexible schedule for new parents returning to work.&nbsp;</li> <li><strong>Office Life: </strong>Catered lunches, happy hours, pet-friendly spaces, and monthly technology stipend.</li> <li><strong>Growth &amp; Training: </strong>$1,000/year for each employee for professional development, as well opportunities for tuition reimbursement.</li> <li><strong>Sponsorship: </strong>We are open to sponsoring candidates currently in the U.S. who need to transfer their active visa. Please check with our Recruiting team if your visa is applicable for transfer.</li> </ul> <p>&nbsp;</p> <p><em>EvolutionIQ appreciates your interest in our company as a place of employment. EvolutionIQ is an equal opportunity employer. We celebrate diversity and are committed to creating an inclusive environment for all employees.</em></p>

title: Senior Software Engineer (AI Data Engineering)
full_description: <p><strong>About Us: </strong>EvolutionIQ’s mission is to deliver state of the art technology that helps insurance claims teams make claims handling more accurate, fair, and efficient, so that more people impacted by injury or illness can continue their lives with dignity and stability. We are currently experiencing massive growth and to accomplish our goals, we are hiring world-class talent who want to help build and scale internally, and transform the insurance space. Our team is our #1 priority, and we have been named one of<a href="https://www.evolutioniq.com/resources/evolutioniqs-award-winning-culture-three-years-running-as-an-inc-best-workplace"> Inc.’s Best Workplaces</a> 3 years in a row and Built In’s Best Places to work in 2025 and 2026!</p> <p><strong>About You:&nbsp;</strong>An ambitious Software Engineer using agentic workflows as a massive lever for engineering velocity. While you use modern AI tools to accelerate code generation, your core strengths lie where LLMs fall short: system architecture, rigorous code quality, security, and complex debugging. You will be in the driver’s seat as we design and deliver the entire systems for our growing line of AI solutions. You thrive on fast, secure, consistent deployments and have a data driven mindset.</p> <p><strong>In this Role You Will:</strong></p> <ul> <li><strong>Orchestrate High-Velocity Workflows:</strong> Leverage advanced agentic coding tools (e.g., Cursor, multi-agent environments) to dramatically accelerate feature prototyping, code generation, and test coverage.&nbsp;</li> <li><strong>Own the Guardrails &amp; Quality:</strong> Act as the ultimate reviewer and architect; define the specifications, establish repo-context guardrails, and review AI-accelerated output for hidden security risks, scale bottlenecks, and architectural alignment.&nbsp;</li> <li><strong>Build Scalable Application and Data Layers:</strong> Design, build, and maintain our data pipelines and application to service our hundreds of users.</li> <li><strong>Bridging the Data:</strong> Partner closely with product, client services, and data science teams to transform raw pipeline outputs into meaning.&nbsp;</li> <li><strong>Drive Greenfield Engineering:</strong> Take complete ownership of designing, building, and launching enterprise-grade applications, moving fluidly between rapid prototyping and bulletproof production systems.</li> </ul> <p><strong>Skills Requirements:</strong></p> <ul> <li>Extensive experience going through the full feature development life cycle: Working with product managers, deciding scope, designing data models and pipeline architectures</li> <li>Proven fluency in using agentic coding tools like Cursor AI and/or Claude</li> <li>5+ years of engineering experience, an expert in Python and SQL Development</li> <li>Experience designing, building and launching backend architectures that support multi-tenant, multi-user scenarios through pipeline performance and data quality checks</li> <li>Ability to remain agile and move between rapid prototyping and stable production development</li> <li>Ability to write technical design documents, perform code reviews, and maintain state of the art engineering practices</li> <li>You know the critical questions to ask in order to understand a client’s business problem and can show the business impact of your technical solutions through data</li> <li>You have crisp written and verbal communication skills and the ability to effectively correspond between technical and non-technical audiences</li> <li>Excellent collaboration skills and are open to giving and receiving critical feedback across teams</li> <li>You have an eye towards analytics and helping to enable data driven decision making</li> </ul> <p><strong>Work-life, Culture &amp; Perks:&nbsp;</strong></p> <ul> <li><strong>Compensation: </strong>The base salary range is <strong>$190-225K,</strong> with flexibility depending on a candidate’s background and experience. An annual bonus plan and company equity plan (RSUs) are also included in our compensation package.</li> <li><strong>Well-Being: </strong>Medical, dental, vision, short &amp; long-term disability, life insurance and AD&amp;D, and 401k matching. Additional family, wellness, and pet benefits.&nbsp;</li> <li><strong>Home &amp; Family:</strong> Paid time off and sick leave, 100% paid parental leave (16 weeks for primary caregivers and 12 weeks for secondary caregivers). We offer a flexible schedule for new parents returning to work.&nbsp;</li> <li><strong>Office Life: </strong>Catered lunches, happy hours, pet-friendly spaces, and monthly technology stipend.</li> <li><strong>Growth &amp; Training: </strong>$1,000/year for each employee for professional development, as well opportunities for tuition reimbursement.</li> <li><strong>Sponsorship: </strong>We are open to sponsoring candidates currently in the U.S. who need to transfer their active visa. Please check with our Recruiting team if your visa is applicable for transfer.</li> </ul> <p>&nbsp;</p> <p><em>EvolutionIQ appreciates your interest in our company as a place of employment. EvolutionIQ is an equal opportunity employer. We celebrate diversity and are committed to creating an inclusive environment for all employees.</em></p>

title: Senior Data Engineer
full_description: <p><strong>What the Role Is</strong></p> <p>The Data team at Babylist powers decision-making across the entire business — from product growth to operations to AI. This is a senior individual contributor role on the Data Engineering team, sitting at the intersection of platform thinking and AI-native tooling. You'll own the architecture and systems that make data engineering itself more scalable — not just the pipelines, but the harnesses and agentic scaffolding that generate, test, and maintain them. You'll work cross-functionally with analysts, data scientists, and product teams to keep Babylist's data infrastructure reliable and ahead of where the business is going.</p> <p><strong>Who You Are</strong></p> <ul> <li>Experienced building production AI/LLM systems — RAG pipelines, agentic workflows, tool integrations (e.g., MCP servers) that real users depend on, not just prototypes</li> <li>Platform-minded — you see repetitive data engineering work as a system design problem and build the scaffolding to automate it</li> <li>Deeply fluent in Python and production-grade data engineering, with 7+ years building systems that hold up at scale</li> <li>Proficient with Airflow and dbt — you understand data modeling and ETL principles, not just the tooling</li> <li>Comfortable in AWS — you've provisioned and managed cloud data resources across EC2, S3, Lambda, and EKS</li> <li>Familiar with Snowflake or comparable modern cloud data warehouses</li> <li>Able to work cross-functionally with analysts and data scientists — translating their needs into infrastructure that actually serves them</li> <li>You naturally reach for AI in your work — at Babylist, every team uses AI daily. You're already using it to move faster and improve your output, and you stay curious about what's coming next.</li> <li><strong>You're genuinely excited about what AI can do -&nbsp;</strong>not just as a concept, but as something you want to get your hands on. At Babylist, every team uses AI daily, and we're looking for people who lean in.</li> </ul> <p><strong>How You Will Make An Impact</strong></p> <ul> <li>Build and scale data pipelines for ingestion into Snowflake — with a focus on reliability and performance across a $750M+ e-commerce business</li> <li>Design and ship the agentic systems that perform data engineering work — pipeline generation, testing, and maintenance as automated systems, not one-off builds</li> <li>Develop and maintain ML pipelines that help data scientists operationalize models and integrate them with our data infrastructure</li> <li>Implement and improve data monitoring across complex, multi-system user journeys</li> <li>Collaborate with Analytics Engineers on data modeling and the reliability of shared data assets</li> <li>Partner with product, analyst, and ML teams to deliver end-to-end data solutions — from ingestion to advanced analytics and AI</li> </ul> <p><strong>Why This Role</strong></p> <ul> <li>You're building the meta-layer — not just pipelines, but the systems that build pipelines; it's a rare scope for a data engineering role</li> <li>The data infrastructure is solid; you're extending and automating something that works, not inheriting a mess</li> <li>AI is core to the mandate here, not a future aspiration — agentic data engineering is a real part of the roadmap, not a stretch goal</li> <li>You'll have direct influence on how a 9M+ user platform scales its data systems through a pre-IPO growth moment.</li> </ul> <p><strong>About Compensation</strong></p> <p>We use a market-based approach to compensation. The starting salary range for this role is:</p> <p><span style="font-weight: 400;"><strong>$186,086.000 - $221,800 + target 15% annual bonus</strong></span></p> <p>Your starting salary will be based on your location, experience, and qualifications, with increases over time tied to performance, role growth, and internal pay equity.</p> <p><strong>Who We Are</strong></p> <p>Babylist is the leading platform for expecting and new families. More than 10 million people shop with Babylist every year, making it the go-to destination for seamless purchasing, guidance, and expert recommendations. As a modern, AI-forward tech company, Babylist has expanded from a universal registry into a full ecosystem — the Babylist Shop, Babylist Health, Babylist Money, NYC and LA showrooms, branded content, and more — generating $750M in revenue in 2025. Building the generational brand in baby, Babylist is reshaping the $235B kids and baby market and helping parents feel confident, connected, and cared for at every step.</p> <p><strong>Our Ways of Working</strong></p> <p>Babylist is remote-first with team members across the U.S. and Canada who move fast, think smart, and use AI as part of how they work every day — not as an experiment, as an expectation. We come together twice a year to build the relationships behind the work, and we hire people who are genuinely excited about what's possible and prove it through how they show up.</p> <p><strong>Why You Will Love Working At Babylist</strong></p> <p><strong>Our Culture</strong></p> <ul> <li>We work with focus and intention, then step away to recharge</li> <li>We believe in exceptional management, and we invest in the tools and the time to connect with colleagues</li> <li>We build products that positively impact millions of people's lives</li> <li>AI is part of how we work, not a side experiment. You'll have leading tools like Claude and ChatGPT from day one, with no waiting on access, so the busywork gets handled and your judgment goes where it counts</li> </ul> <p><strong>Growth &amp; Development</strong></p> <ul> <li>Competitive pay and meaningful opportunities for career advancement</li> <li>We believe technology and data can solve hard problems</li> <li>We're committed to career progression and performance-based advancement</li> </ul> <p><strong>Benefits</strong></p> <ul> <li>Company-paid medical, dental, and vision insurance</li> <li>Retirement savings plan with company matching and flexible spending accounts</li> <li>Generous paid parental leave and PTO</li> <li>Winter Wonder Week: the whole company takes a paid week off at the end of the year, whether or not you celebrate anything, so everyone's out at once and comes back refreshed</li> <li>Remote work stipend to set up your office</li> <li>Perks for physical, mental, and emotional health, parenting, childcare, and financial planning</li> </ul> <h3><strong>Important Notices</strong></h3> <p><strong>Recorded Interviews. </strong>Babylist uses an interview recording tool to record and transcribe interviews for evaluation purposes in accordance with applicable privacy laws. By participating in an interview, you consent to this recording and transcription.</p> <p><strong>Interview Integrity. </strong>AI is part of how we work at Babylist — we expect you to use it too. Your application and interviews should still reflect you and your own thinking. We'll tell you when AI is encouraged. Misrepresentation at any stage may result in removal from consideration for this and future roles.</p> <p><strong>How We Review Applications </strong>We use AI throughout our hiring process to help review, screen, and assess applications. These tools support our team's review; they do not replace it, and every hiring decision is made by a person.</p> <p><strong>Connections at Babylist. </strong>If you have a family member or close personal relationship with a current Babylist employee, please let your recruiter know. This helps us keep our process fair and transparent for everyone.</p> <p><strong>Protect Yourself from Scams. </strong>All official outreach comes from the Babylist Talent Team via @babylist.com email addresses only. We will never ask for payment or personal financial information. If you receive outreach via WhatsApp, Telegram, or a non-Babylist email — it's not us. Verify open roles at babylist.com/careers.</p>

title: Senior Data Engineer

title: Senior Analytics Engineer, GTM
full_description: <div class="content-intro"><p>We’re in an unbelievably exciting area of tech and are fundamentally reshaping the data storage industry. Here, you lead with innovative thinking, grow along with us, and join the smartest team in the industry.</p> <p>This type of work—work that changes the world—is what the tech industry was founded on. So, if you're ready to seize the endless opportunities and leave your mark, come join us.</p></div><p><strong>THE ROLE</strong></p> <h2>SHOULD YOU ACCEPT THIS CHALLENGE…</h2> <p>The GTM BI team at Pure Storage supports Revenue Operations across pipeline, forecasting, seller performance, and executive reporting — and we are at an inflection point.</p> <p>We are midway through a strategic migration to dbt as our transformation standard. Beyond that, our roadmap includes scalable automation frameworks, semantic business intelligence, and AI-enabled workflows that help surface insights with less manual effort.</p> <p>As a Senior Analytics Engineer, you will help drive that journey end-to-end. This role is grounded in modernization first: building scalable dbt foundations, improving quality and engineering standards, and enabling the team to operate more efficiently before scaling broader AI-enabled capabilities.</p> <p>You will partner closely with BI analysts, Sales Operations, Systems teams, and backend engineering partners to modernize how analytics is engineered, governed, and consumed across the organization. This role is for someone who wants to build — not maintain.</p> <h2>WHAT YOU’LL DO…</h2> <ul> <li>Lead and contribute hands-on to the team’s dbt and Snowflake modernization initiatives</li> <li>Build scalable semantic and metrics layers supporting GTM domains including pipeline, forecasting, quota, attainment, and seller performance</li> <li>Design and maintain robust ELT/ETL pipelines, orchestration workflows, and reusable business-facing datasets</li> <li>Build CI/CD, testing, observability, lineage, and data quality frameworks for analytics engineering workflows</li> <li>Ingest and transform data from APIs and GTM systems into curated canonical datasets with strong quality standards</li> <li>Drive architecture decisions and establish modern analytics engineering best practices across the team</li> <li>Partner closely with BIAs, RevOps, and Systems teams to translate business problems into scalable data products</li> <li>Help automate manual reporting and operational workflows through configurable and scalable frameworks</li> <li>Apply AI capabilities to workflow automation, operational efficiency, and insight generation, while helping lay the foundation for future conversational analytics initiatives</li> </ul> <h2>WHAT YOU’LL NEED TO BRING TO THIS ROLE…</h2> <ul> <li>7+ years of experience in analytics engineering, data engineering, or business intelligence</li> <li>Strong expertise in SQL, Python, Snowflake, and modern ELT development practices</li> <li>Hands-on experience with dbt, orchestration workflows, and scalable data modeling</li> <li>Experience implementing CI/CD, automated testing, observability, and data quality frameworks</li> <li>Strong understanding of semantic modeling, metrics layers, governed metrics, and reusable analytics datasets</li> <li>Experience using AI to build workflows, automate processes, improve operational efficiency, or support business intelligence use cases</li> <li>Ability to influence technical direction while remaining hands-on</li> <li>Strong communication and collaboration skills across technical and business teams</li> <li>Pragmatic interest in applying AI capabilities to real business workflows over time</li> <li>Passion for modernization, reporting trust, and scalable analytics engineering</li> </ul> <h2>PREFERRED EXPERIENCE</h2> <ul> <li>GTM, Sales, Revenue Operations, or forecasting analytics domains</li> <li>Salesforce, Clari, Anaplan, Workday, or related enterprise systems</li> <li>Analytics engineering modernization initiatives</li> <li>Self-service analytics ecosystems and semantic layers</li> <li>Conversational analytics, AI copilots, or workflow automation concepts</li> <li>Experience migrating legacy SQL or ETL frameworks into modern dbt-based transformation workflows</li> <li>Tableau or modern BI visualization platforms</li> </ul> <p>#LI-ONSITE<br><br></p><div class="content-conclusion"><p><strong>WHAT YOU CAN EXPECT FROM US:</strong></p> <ul> <li><strong>Innovation</strong>: We celebrate those who think critically, like a challenge, and aspire to be trailblazers.</li> <li><strong>Growth</strong>: We give you the space and support to grow along with us and to contribute to something meaningful. We have been named Fortune's Best Workplaces in Technology™, Fortune's Best Workplaces in the Bay Area™, and certified as a Great Place to Work®!</li> <li><strong>Team</strong>: We build each other up and set aside ego for the greater good.</li> </ul> <p>And because we understand the value of bringing your full and best self to work, we offer a variety of perks to manage a healthy balance, including flexible time off, wellness resources, and company-sponsored team events. Check out <a href="http://purebenefits.com">purebenefits.com</a> for more information.</p> <p><strong>ACCOMMODATIONS AND ACCESSIBILITY:</strong></p> <p>Candidates with disabilities may request accommodations for all aspects of our hiring process. For more on this, contact us at <a href="mailto:TA-Ops@purestorage.com">TA-Ops@purestorage.com</a> if you’re invited to an interview.</p> <p><strong>OUR COMMITMENT TO A STRONG AND INCLUSIVE TEAM:</strong></p> <p>We’re forging a future where everyone finds their rightful place and where every voice matters. Where uniqueness isn’t just accepted but embraced. That’s why we are committed to fostering the growth and development of every person, cultivating a sense of community through our Employee Resource Groups and advocating for inclusive leadership.</p> <p>Everpure is proud to be an equal opportunity employer. We do not discriminate based upon race, religion, color, national origin, sex (including pregnancy, childbirth, or related medical conditions), sexual orientation, gender, gender identity, gender expression, transgender status, sexual stereotypes, age, status as a protected veteran, status as an individual with a disability, or any other characteristic legally protected by the laws of the jurisdiction in which you are being considered for hire.</p> <p style="text-align: center;"><strong>Join us and bring your best.</strong></p> <p style="text-align: center;"><strong>Bring your bold.</strong></p> <p style="text-align: center;"><strong>Pure and simple.</strong></p></div>

title: Senior Data Engineering Manager -  Conversational AI & Finance Control Tower
full_description: <div class="content-intro"><p>We’re in an unbelievably exciting area of tech and are fundamentally reshaping the data storage industry. So, if you're ready to seize the endless opportunities and leave your mark, come join us.</p></div><p><strong>THE ROLE</strong></p> <p>The Data &amp; Analytics team at Ever Pure is looking for a Senior Manager for designing and building an <strong>AI-ready data infrastructure powering conversational AI and chatbot experiences f</strong>or Finance.<br>This role will lead the end-to-end build, defining the target state, identifying capability gaps, and delivering a phased roadmap with measurable outcomes. A core focus is enabling a conversational, AI-driven Finance experience, allowing stakeholders to access insights through natural language and copilots.</p> <p><strong>WHAT YOU'LL DO</strong></p> <p><strong>Finance Control Tower &amp; Data Foundation</strong></p> <ul> <li>Lead the design and build of the Finance Control Tower</li> <li>Establish a scalable, AI-ready data platform for reporting, analytics, and AI</li> <li>Deliver unified, trusted views across Finance (revenue, billing, forecasting)</li> </ul> <p><strong>Data Architecture, Semantic Layer &amp; Observability</strong></p> <ul> <li>Define data architecture and unified models across systems (Salesforce, SAP, NetSuite, Zuora)</li> <li>Build a semantic / metrics layer to standardize business definitions</li> <li>Implement data management and observability (quality, lineage, reliability)</li> </ul> <p><strong>Conversational AI &amp; AI Enablement</strong></p> <ul> <li>Enable natural language access to Finance data via chatbots and copilots</li> <li>Build RAG-based architectures using structured and unstructured data</li> <li>Develop embeddings and semantic context layers for business-aware insights</li> <li>Leverage platforms like Snowflake Cortex (or similar) for search, summarization, and classification</li> </ul> <p><strong>Data Engineering &amp; AI Integration</strong></p> <ul> <li>Build and scale data pipelines and AI-ready datasets in Snowflake (or similar)</li> <li>Integrate enterprise data sources (SFDC, NetSuite, Zuora)</li> <li>Enable LLM-driven use cases (forecasting, anomaly detection, variance analysis)</li> <li>Ensure outputs are accurate, explainable, and traceable</li> </ul> <p>We are primarily an in-office environment and therefore, you will be expected to work from the Bangalore office in compliance with Everpure's policies, unless you are on PTO, or work travel, or other approved leave.</p> <p><strong>WHAT YOU BRING</strong></p> <ul> <li>15+ years in data engineering, analytics, or related roles</li> <li>Experience building modern data platforms / data products at scale</li> <li>Strong expertise in:</li> <ul> <li>SQL, data modeling, ETL/ELT</li> <li>Semantic / metrics layer design</li> <li>Data governance, quality, and observability</li> <li>Experience with Snowflake (or similar) and Python</li> </ul> <li>Exposure to:</li> <ul> <li>Conversational AI / chatbot systems</li> <li>RAG, embeddings, vector search</li> <li>LLM frameworks and prompt design</li> </ul> <li>Understanding of Finance data (ARR, MRR, revenue) preferred</li> <li>Strong execution, stakeholder management, and communication skills</li> </ul> <p>#LI-ONSITE</p><div class="content-conclusion"><p><strong>WHAT YOU CAN EXPECT FROM US:</strong></p> <ul> <li><strong>Innovation</strong>: We celebrate those who think critically, like a challenge, and aspire to be trailblazers.</li> <li><strong>Growth</strong>: We give you the space and support to grow along with us and to contribute to something meaningful. We have been named Fortune's Best Workplaces in Technology™, Fortune's Best Workplaces in the Bay Area™, and certified as a Great Place to Work®!</li> <li><strong>Team</strong>: We build each other up and set aside ego for the greater good.</li> </ul> <p>And because we understand the value of bringing your full and best self to work, we offer a variety of perks to manage a healthy balance, including flexible time off, wellness resources, and company-sponsored team events. Check out <a href="http://purebenefits.com">purebenefits.com</a> for more information.</p> <p><strong>ACCOMMODATIONS AND ACCESSIBILITY:</strong></p> <p>Candidates with disabilities may request accommodations for all aspects of our hiring process. For more on this, contact us at <a href="mailto:TA-Ops@purestorage.com">TA-Ops@purestorage.com</a> if you’re invited to an interview.</p> <p><strong>OUR COMMITMENT TO A STRONG AND INCLUSIVE TEAM:</strong></p> <p>We’re forging a future where everyone finds their rightful place and where every voice matters. title: Senior Data Engineer
full_description: <p><strong>About SecurityScorecard:</strong></p> <p><span style="font-weight: 400;">SecurityScorecard is the global leader in cybersecurity ratings, with over 12 million companies continuously rated, operating in 64 countries. Founded in 2013 by security and risk experts Dr. Alex Yampolskiy and Sam Kassoumeh and funded by world-class investors, SecurityScorecard’s patented rating technology is used by over 25,000 organizations for self-monitoring, third-party risk management, board reporting, and cyber insurance underwriting; making all organizations more resilient by allowing them to easily find and fix cybersecurity risks across their digital footprint.&nbsp;</span></p> <p><span style="font-weight: 400;">Headquartered in New York City, our culture has been recognized by Inc Magazine as a "Best Workplace,” by Crain’s NY as a "Best Places to Work in NYC," and as one of the 10 hottest SaaS startups in New York for two years in a row. Most recently, SecurityScorecard was named to Fast Company’s annual list of the</span><a href="https://securityscorecard.com/blog/blog-top-innovators-of-the-year/"><span style="font-weight: 400;"> </span><span style="font-weight: 400;">World’s Most Innovative Companies for 2023</span></a><span style="font-weight: 400;"> and to the Achievers 50 Most Engaged Workplaces in 2023 award recognizing “forward-thinking employers for their unwavering commitment to employee engagement.”&nbsp; SecurityScorecard is proud to be funded by world-class investors including Silver Lake Waterman, Moody’s, Sequoia Capital, GV and Riverwood Capital.</span></p> <p><strong>About the Team:<br><br></strong>Be part of an exciting and growing Technology team focused on product development. Our team emphasis is collaboration and shared responsibilities, you will be hands-on involved in the entire Software Development Life Cycle. Our team is growing and so will you! Check out our tech blog below and let us know if you are our next star.<br><br><a href="https://techblog.securityscorecard.com/">https://techblog.securityscorecard.com/</a></p> <p><strong><br>About the Role:<br></strong></p> <p><strong>What you will do</strong></p> <p>We are looking for a Senior Data Engineer who thrives on building commercial products and plays a key role in delivering reliable, scalable, and maintainable applications. You will own projects end-to-end, collaborating with Product Managers to define requirements, implement solutions, and ensure ongoing success post-deployment. You'll focus on simplicity while contributing technical insights to enhance the system's performance, stability, and user experience.</p> <ul> <li>Participate in all stages of the software development life cycle, from design and development to deployment and maintenance.</li> <li>Contribute to the product and technical roadmap by providing data-driven insights and articulating trade-offs between technical options and business goals.</li> <li>Support and mentor junior engineers on the team through code reviews, pairing, and technical guidance.</li> <li>Follow clean code principles, using best practices to write secure, testable, and well-documented software.</li> <li>Work closely with a global team, including engineers and product managers, to build a stable, high-performance platform.</li> <li>Implement comprehensive testing and performance tuning to ensure system stability and uptime.</li> </ul> <p><strong><br>Responsibilities:</strong></p> <ul> <li>Translate requirements, estimate effort, and address or escalate blockers as needed.</li> <li>Write clean, maintainable code that adheres to best practices in readability, design patterns, reusability, and testing.</li> <li>Own end-to-end projects, understanding and contributing to all aspects (infrastructure, application tiers, and data tiers).</li> <li>Continuously monitor performance metrics and recommend improvements or refactors.</li> </ul> <p><strong><br>Our Tech Stack:</strong></p> <ul> <li>Scala, Apache Spark, Python</li> <li>Airflow</li> <li>ScyllaDB, PostgreSQL, ClickHouse, Hive, Redis, Kafka, Kafka Connect</li> <li>Docker, Jenkins, Terraform, AWS</li> </ul> <p><strong><br>Required Qualifications:</strong></p> <ul> <li>5+ years of experience working in the Data Engineering space, and with building and maintaining big data pipelines&nbsp;</li> <li>3+ years of experience working in agile environments (ideally Scrum), collaborating with cross-functional teams (engineering, design, product).</li> <li>Proficient in Spark</li> <li>Experience with strongly-typed languages (Java / Scala preferred)</li> <li>Experience designing, building, and maintaining RESTful APIs and integrating with external services.</li> <li>Participate in code reviews to ensure best practices, maintainability, and continuous improvement of the codebase.</li> <li>Ability to write and maintain unit and integration tests based on acceptance criteria, ensuring code quality and reliability.</li> <li>Proficiency with version control tools, particularly Git, for collaborative development and code management.</li> </ul> <p><strong><br>Preferred Qualifications:</strong></p> <ul> <li>Strong understanding of building scalable and high-performance back-end systems, optimizing for low-latency and high-throughput.</li> <li>Worked with a variety of data (structured/unstructured), data formats (flat files, XML, JSON, relational, parquet).</li> <li>Experience in functional programming languages (Scala preferred)</li> <li>Experience in Cyber Security</li> </ul> <p><strong><br>Communication and Collaboration:</strong></p> <ul> <li>Strong attention to documentation and maintaining standards across projects.</li> <li>Ability to present and defend technical decisions with confidence.</li> <li>Skilled at estimating project complexity based on early documentation and discussions.</li> <li>Excellent written and verbal communication skills, with the ability to articulate technical challenges and solutions clearly.</li> </ul> <p><span data-sheets-value="{&quot;1&quot;:2,&quot;2&quot;:&quot;SecurityScorecard is an industry-leading cybersecurity company backed by Google, Sequoia, and Riverwood. Our mission is to make the world a safer place. We measure your and your vendors' cyber-health by assigning a security rating of \&quot;A\&quot; through \&quot;F\&quot; based on outside-in, non-intrusive data. title: Senior Data Engineer
full_description: <p><strong>About SecurityScorecard:</strong></p> <p><span style="font-weight: 400;">SecurityScorecard is the global leader in cybersecurity ratings, with over 12 million companies continuously rated, operating in 64 countries. Founded in 2013 by security and risk experts Dr. Alex Yampolskiy and Sam Kassoumeh and funded by world-class investors, SecurityScorecard’s patented rating technology is used by over 25,000 organizations for self-monitoring, third-party risk management, board reporting, and cyber insurance underwriting; making all organizations more resilient by allowing them to easily find and fix cybersecurity risks across their digital footprint.&nbsp;</span></p> <p><span style="font-weight: 400;">Headquartered in New York City, our culture has been recognized by Inc Magazine as a "Best Workplace,” by Crain’s NY as a "Best Places to Work in NYC," and as one of the 10 hottest SaaS startups in New York for two years in a row. Most recently, SecurityScorecard was named to Fast Company’s annual list of the</span><a href="https://securityscorecard.com/blog/blog-top-innovators-of-the-year/"><span style="font-weight: 400;"> </span><span style="font-weight: 400;">World’s Most Innovative Companies for 2023</span></a><span style="font-weight: 400;"> and to the Achievers 50 Most Engaged Workplaces in 2023 award recognizing “forward-thinking employers for their unwavering commitment to employee engagement.”&nbsp; SecurityScorecard is proud to be funded by world-class investors including Silver Lake Waterman, Moody’s, Sequoia Capital, GV and Riverwood Capital.</span></p> <p><strong>About the Team:<br><br></strong>Be part of an exciting and growing Technology team focused on product development. Our team emphasis is collaboration and shared responsibilities, you will be hands-on involved in the entire Software Development Life Cycle. Our team is growing and so will you! Check out our tech blog below and let us know if you are our next star.<br><br><a href="https://techblog.securityscorecard.com/">https://techblog.securityscorecard.com/</a></p> <p><strong><br>About the Role:<br></strong></p> <p><strong>What you will do</strong></p> <p>We are looking for a Senior Data Engineer who thrives on building commercial products and plays a key role in delivering reliable, scalable, and maintainable applications. You will own projects end-to-end, collaborating with Product Managers to define requirements, implement solutions, and ensure ongoing success post-deployment. You'll focus on simplicity while contributing technical insights to enhance the system's performance, stability, and user experience.</p> <ul> <li>Participate in all stages of the software development life cycle, from design and development to deployment and maintenance.</li> <li>Contribute to the product and technical roadmap by providing data-driven insights and articulating trade-offs between technical options and business goals.</li> <li>Support and mentor junior engineers on the team through code reviews, pairing, and technical guidance.</li> <li>Follow clean code principles, using best practices to write secure, testable, and well-documented software.</li> <li>Work closely with a global team, including engineers and product managers, to build a stable, high-performance platform.</li> <li>Implement comprehensive testing and performance tuning to ensure system stability and uptime.</li> </ul> <p><strong><br>Responsibilities:</strong></p> <ul> <li>Translate requirements, estimate effort, and address or escalate blockers as needed.</li> <li>Write clean, maintainable code that adheres to best practices in readability, design patterns, reusability, and testing.</li> <li>Own end-to-end projects, understanding and contributing to all aspects (infrastructure, application tiers, and data tiers).</li> <li>Continuously monitor performance metrics and recommend improvements or refactors.</li> </ul> <p><strong><br>Our Tech Stack:</strong></p> <ul> <li>Scala, Apache Spark, Python</li> <li>Airflow</li> <li>ScyllaDB, PostgreSQL, ClickHouse, Hive, Redis, Kafka, Kafka Connect</li> <li>Docker, Jenkins, Terraform, AWS</li> </ul> <p><strong><br>Required Qualifications:</strong></p> <ul> <li>5+ years of experience working in the Data Engineering space, and with building and maintaining big data pipelines&nbsp;</li> <li>3+ years of experience working in agile environments (ideally Scrum), collaborating with cross-functional teams (engineering, design, product).</li> <li>Proficient in Spark</li> <li>Experience with strongly-typed languages (Java / Scala preferred)</li> <li>Experience designing, building, and maintaining RESTful APIs and integrating with external services.</li> <li>Participate in code reviews to ensure best practices, maintainability, and continuous improvement of the codebase.</li> <li>Ability to write and maintain unit and integration tests based on acceptance criteria, ensuring code quality and reliability.</li> <li>Proficiency with version control tools, particularly Git, for collaborative development and code management.</li> </ul> <p><strong><br>Preferred Qualifications:</strong></p> <ul> <li>Strong understanding of building scalable and high-performance back-end systems, optimizing for low-latency and high-throughput.</li> <li>Worked with a variety of data (structured/unstructured), data formats (flat files, XML, JSON, relational, parquet).</li> <li>Experience in functional programming languages (Scala preferred)</li> <li>Experience in Cyber Security</li> </ul> <p><strong><br>Communication and Collaboration:</strong></p> <ul> <li>Strong attention to documentation and maintaining standards across projects.</li> <li>Ability to present and defend technical decisions with confidence.</li> <li>Skilled at estimating project complexity based on early documentation and discussions.</li> <li>Excellent written and verbal communication skills, with the ability to articulate technical challenges and solutions clearly.</li> </ul> <p><span title: Data Engineering Manager
full_description: <p>Doximity is transforming the healthcare industry. Join our mission to help every physician be more productive and provide better care for their patients. As medicine's largest network in the United States, there's an elevated level of responsibility in everything we do. We don't take that responsibility lightly and are committed to building diverse teams with an inclusive culture that can make a direct impact on the healthcare system.</p> <p>One of Doximity's<a href="https://work.doximity.com/"> core values</a> is stretching ourselves. Even if you don't check off all the boxes below we encourage you to apply. Doximity is full of exceptional people who bring their own unique experiences to work everyday and make us all better for it!</p> <p><em>This role can be filled in our San Francisco headquarters OR remotely in the US or Canada.</em></p> <p><strong><em>About you</em></strong></p> <ul> <li><strong><em>Foremost an engineer</em></strong><em>.</em> You exemplify high code quality and guide others, and have at least <strong>5 years</strong> of industry experience as a data engineer or similar role.</li> <ul> <li>You are experienced in creating automated testing, applying design patterns, and other engineering best practices.</li> <li>You are an expert in Python and possess fluency in SQL and have developed maintainable data pipelines with each.</li> </ul> <li><strong><em>Experienced People Manager.</em></strong> You have experience managing 3 or more people whereby you were accountable for their growth and success as individuals and team members.</li> <li><strong><em>Communication Extraordinaire.</em></strong> You excel at communicating to both technical and non-technical people, with an ability to explain complex concepts simply.</li> <li><strong><em>Player Coach</em></strong><em>.</em> You are able to contribute individually, outside of your reports or team, balancing priorities between being a player (i.e. individual contributor) and a coach (i.e. manager or mentor).</li> <li><strong><em>See a project from end-to-end</em></strong><em>.</em> You have led several projects, your own ideas and other's. This means from idea generation to planning, execution, through delivering.</li> <li><strong><em>ML/AI–aware. </em></strong>You have working knowledge of how ML/AI applications are deployed and monitored, and you’re motivated to use this awareness to advance data-driven initiatives.</li> </ul> <p><strong><em>Here's How You Will Make an Impact</em></strong></p> <ul> <li><em>Collaborate with product managers, data analysts, and machine learning engineers to develop pipelines and ETL tasks in order to facilitate the extraction of insights.</em></li> <li><em>Establish data architecture processes and practices that can be scheduled, automated, replicated and serve as standards for other teams to leverage.</em><em>Inform data team strategy by working with the product leaders and managers. </em></li> <li><em>Actively participate in execution and some planning of organizational data team strategy. </em></li> <li><em>Collaborate with Doximity’s Data Leadership team to ensure alignment and success across the entire Data Organization.</em></li> <li><em>Manage individual Data Engineers to foster learning, growth and success at Doximity.</em></li> </ul> <p><strong><em>About Us</em></strong></p> <ul> <li><em><a href="https://technology.doximity.com/technology-stack">Explore our stack</a></em></li> <li><em>We have over 500 private repositories in Github containing our pipelines, our own internal multi-functional tools, and<a href="https://github.com/doximity"> open-source projects</a></em></li> <li><em>We have worked as a distributed team for a long time;<a href="https://blog.brunomiranda.com/building-a-distributed-engineering-team-85d281b9b1c"> we're currently about 65% distributed</a></em></li> <li><em>Find out more information </em><em>on the<a href="https://engineering.doximity.com/"> Doximity engineering blog</a></em></li> <li><em>Our<a href="https://work.doximity.com/"> company core values</a></em></li> <li><em>Our<a href="https://engineering.doximity.com/articles/engineering-recruitment-process-doximity"> recruiting process</a></em></li> <li><em>Our<a href="https://engineering.doximity.com/articles/mofo-driven-product-development"> product development cycle</a></em></li> <li><em>Our<a href="https://engineering.doximity.com/articles/software-engineering-on-boarding-at-doximity"> on-boarding &amp; mentorship process</a></em></li> </ul> <p><strong><em>Compensation</em></strong></p> <p><em>The US total compensation range for this full-time position is $172,000 - $254,000 (i</em><em>nclusive of salary + equity). Our ranges are determined by role and level. The range displayed on each job posting reflects the approximate total target compensation for the position across the US. Within the range, individual pay is determined by factors including relevant skills, experience, and education/training. Please note that the compensation listed does not include benefits.</em></p> <p><strong><em>More on /Benefits/Perks&nbsp;</em></strong></p> <p><em>Doximity is proud to offer industry-leading benefits to our full time employees. Some of our offerings include:&nbsp;</em></p> <ul> <li><em>Medical, dental, vision offerings for you and your family</em></li> <li><em>4</em><em>01k with matching program</em></li> <li><em>Employee stock purchase plan</em></li> <li><em>Family planning support, Childcare FSA, and parental leave</em></li> <li><em>Life, AD&amp;D, and Disability</em></li> <li><em>Generous time off, holidays and paid company trips</em></li> <li><em>Wellness benefits…plus many more!</em></li> </ul> <p><strong><em>More About Doximity…</em></strong></p> <p><em>For the past decade, it’s been our mission to help every physician be more productive so they can provide better care for their patients. We believe that when doctors are connected, the healthcare system works better and patients benefit. Doximity enables our verified clinician members to collaborate with colleagues, stay up-to-date with the latest medical news and research, manage their careers, and conduct virtual patient visits. Today, Doximity is the leading digital platform for U.S. medical professionals, with over 85% of physicians, 50% of all nurse practitioners and physician assistants, and 90% of graduating medical students as members.&nbsp;</em></p> <p><em>Joining Doximity means being part of an incredibly talented and humble team passionate about improving inefficiencies in our $4.3 trillion U.S. healthcare system. We are a team of doers who solve problems everyday by treating obstacles like an adventure, and we love creating technology that has a real, meaningful impact on people’s lives. Doxers are committed to working towards a more equitable world both within and beyond our office walls. This starts by fostering an inclusive and diverse work environment where differences are valued and all employees are encouraged to bring their full, authentic selves to work daily. To learn more about our team, culture, and users, check out our</em><a href="https://workat.doximity.com/"><em> </em><em>careers page</em></a><em>,</em><a href="https://blog.doximity.com/"><em> </em><em>company blog</em></a><em>, and</em><a href="https://engineering.doximity.com/"><em> </em><em>engineering blog</em></a><em>. We’re growing fast, and there’s plenty of opportunity for you to make an impact—join us! For more information, visit</em><a href="http://www.doximity.com/"><em> Doximity.com</em></a><em>.</em></p> <p><em>____________________________________________</em></p> <p><strong><em>EEOC Statement</em></strong></p> <p><em>Doximity is proud to be an equal opportunity employer, and committed to providing employment opportunities regardless of race, religious creed, color, national origin, ancestry, physical disability, mental disability, medical condition, genetic information, marital status, sex, gender, gender identity, gender expression, pregnancy, childbirth and breastfeeding, age, sexual orientation, military or veteran status, or any other protected classification. We also consider qualified applicants with criminal histories, consistent with applicable federal, state and local law.</em></p> <p>&nbsp;</p>

title: Data Engineering Manager
full_description: <div class="content-intro"><p>SmarterDx is transforming how health systems use clinical AI to capture the full value of patient care delivered. Built by physician-data scientists and trained on clinically-validated EHR data, our clinical AI platform interprets the nuances behind every patient story and makes clinically-sound recommendations for revenue cycle teams — helping hospitals recover earned revenue, improve quality metrics, reduce denials, and streamline revenue cycle operations. As a Smartian, you’ll help build technology that makes healthcare more accurate, sustainable, and effective for everyone. Learn more at <span><a href="http://smarterdx.com/careers" target="_blank">smarterdx.com/careers</a></span>.</p></div><h3>Role</h3> <p>SmarterDx is seeking a Manager of Data Engineering, Integrations to lead and grow a high‑performing data engineering team responsible for ingesting, harmonizing, and operationalizing healthcare data (EHR, clinical, and claims) across our products. You will scale new‑client onboarding, raise the bar on data quality and governance, and ensure resilient, observable, and cost‑effective ELT pipelines. You will set clear objectives, mentor engineers, and partner closely with Product, Clinical, Data Science, and Engineering to deliver durable outcomes.</p> <p>SmarterDx builds clinical AI that empowers hospitals to analyze the complete record of every patient to fully capture the value of care delivered. Founded by physicians in 2020, its proprietary AI platform understands the nuances of clinical reasoning, enabling hospitals to true the patient record for every discharge. By doing so, hospitals can recover millions in earned revenue, enhance care quality metrics, and optimize healthcare operations. Become a Smartian and help optimize the way the healthcare system works for everyone. Learn more at smarterdx.com/careers.</p> <p><em>**This role is fully remote within the US**</em></p> <p><strong>What You'll Do</strong></p> <ul> <li>Lead, coach, and develop a team of analytics engineers and/or data engineers with a focus on technical excellence, delivery velocity, and collaborative culture.</li> <li>Ensure on-time delivery of client data integrations by owning enterprise data model standards and maintaining consistent, governed data definitions.</li> <li>Oversee client data pipelines using modern tooling (dbt, Airflow, Snowflake, AWS, Python) to ensure reliable operation and uptime.</li> <li>Drive client integration delivery speed by implementing automation, reusable patterns, and clear runbooks to directly optimize client onboarding timelines.</li> <li>Establish and enforce SLOs/SLAs, instrument critical paths, and drive incident reduction via proactive monitoring, alerting, and post‑incident reviews.</li> <li>Implement comprehensive data quality and testing frameworks (schema, nullability, referential integrity, reconciliation) and lineage observability.</li> <li>Collaborate cross‑functionally (Product, Clinical, Data Science, Engineering) to prioritize roadmaps, land measurable outcomes, and manage tradeoffs.</li> <li>Manage high-volume client integration requests, triaging and prioritizing tickets based on client impact, urgency, and business value while maintaining clear communication on resolution timelines.</li> <li>Own daily operations rhythm including ticket review, resource allocation, escalation management, and stakeholder updates to ensure client integration commitments are met.</li> <li>Drive operational metrics and reporting on integration delivery performance, ticket resolution times, and team capacity to identify bottlenecks and improve client outcomes</li> </ul> <p><strong>What You Bring</strong></p> <ul> <li>7+ years of data engineering experience, including leadership of engineers and outcomes (hiring, mentoring, performance, delivery).</li> <li>Deep experience with healthcare datasets: clinical, and/or billing/claims; familiarity with HL7/FHIR and interoperability patterns.</li> <li>Strong SQL and data modeling expertise; proven track record building reliable ELT in dbt, Airflow, Snowflake, and Python.</li> <li>An Operational excellence mindset that integrates SLOs/SLAs, on‑call/incident management, root‑cause analysis, and preventive improvements.</li> <li>Demonstrated ability to accelerate client data onboarding through automation, standardization, and repeatable integration patterns.</li> <li>Excellent communication and stakeholder management; comfortable driving clarity and alignment across Product, Clinical, and technical partners.</li> <li>Willingness to roll up sleeves and contribute hands‑on to unblock the team.</li> </ul> <p><strong>Nice To Haves</strong></p> <ul> <li>Experience at a scale‑up or rapid‑growth technology company.</li> <li>Experience leading product implementations and complex client data integrations.</li> <li>Hands‑on interoperability experience (HL7, FHIR, CCD/C-CDA), EHR extract patterns, and healthcare data normalization.</li> <li>Certifications: Snowflake (SnowPro), AWS (Data Analytics or Solutions Architect), dbt, or healthcare data (CHDA/RHIA).</li> </ul> <p><strong>Our Tech Stack</strong></p> <ul> <li>Databases: Snowflake (primary), Postgres</li> <li>Cloud Infrastructure: AWS (CDK, S3, MWAA, EventBridge, Batch, Lambda, Step Functions), FiveTran</li> <li>Technologies: SQL, dbt, Python</li> </ul> <h3><strong>Compensation</strong></h3> <p>$190,000 - $210,000</p> <p>#LI-Remote</p><div class="content-conclusion"><h3>Benefits</h3> <ul class="p-rich_text_list p-rich_text_list__bullet
