title: Director, Data Engineering (AI Native)
full_description: <h3>About Life360</h3> <p>Life360’s mission is to keep people close to the ones they love. Our category-leading mobile app,Tile tracking devices, and Pet GPS tracker empower members to protect the people, pets, and things they care about most with a range of services, including location sharing, safe driver reports, and crash detection with emergency dispatch. Life360 serves approximately 97.8 million monthly active users (MAU), as of March 31, 2026, across more than 180 countries.&nbsp;</p> <p>Life360 delivers peace of mind and enhances everyday family life with seamless coordination for all the moments that matter, big and small. By continuing to innovate and deliver for our customers, we have become a household name and the must-have mobile-based membership for families (and those friends who are basically family).</p> <p>Life360 has more than 500 (and growing!) remote-first employees. For more information, please visit <a href="http://life360.com/">life360.com</a>.</p> <p><strong><em>Life360 is a Remote First company, which means a remote work environment will be the primary experience for all employees. All positions, unless otherwise specified, can be performed remotely (within the US and Canada) regardless of any specified location above.&nbsp;</em></strong></p> <h4>We are AI Native</h4> <p>We are building an AI native company where AI is an integral part of how we build and operate. AI tool usage during interviews varies by role. You may be asked to demonstrate proficiency with AI tools, discuss how you leverage AI, or complete interview exercises without AI assistance. Your Recruiter will provide clear guidance as you move through the interview process.</p> <p>Undisclosed use of AI not previously discussed with or approved by your Recruiter may impact your candidacy.</p> <h3>About The Team</h3> <p>The Data and Analytics (DnA) organization is the backbone of how Life360 understands its members, measures what matters, and makes better decisions. We own the data platform, analytics engineering, data science, and analytics functions that power insights for every operating group across the company. Our work ensures that every team at Life360 has reliable, well-modeled data and the tools to act on it. We’re a team that values clarity, directness, and building things that work.</p> <h3>About the Job</h3> <p>We’re looking for a Director of Data Engineering to lead the engineering half of our Data and Analytics organization. This is a senior leadership role where you’ll own the data from end to end; from the source to consumption by humans and LLMs. You will manage a strong team of data &amp; analytics engineers and managers and the strategic direction of our data, collaborating closely with leaders on data science, engineering, product analytics, and marketing analytics.</p> <p>This role requires someone who is technically credible across both data platform engineering and analytics engineering—not just one or the other. You need to be able to evaluate Databricks architecture decisions, understand how data flows through instrumentation SDKs and event collection pipelines, and challenge your managers' technical proposals on pipeline design just as readily as you can assess dbt project structure, data modeling standards, and semantic layer strategy. You won’t be writing code day-to-day, but you’ll need enough depth to set technical direction, spot when something is off, and earn the trust of strong engineers.</p> <p>Data engineering at Life360 is a strategic partner, not a service org. You’ll be embedded in product and business decisions—shaping roadmaps, pushing back when data needs aren’t accounted for, and designing systems for needs that haven’t been articulated yet. Your primary impact will come from how you lead people, align teams to business priorities, translate technical complexity into language the business can act on, and represent data engineering at the leadership table.</p> <p>We’re an AI native company, and we expect this leader to bring that mindset to data engineering. That means actively leveraging AI tools to accelerate development, exploring AI-powered approaches to data quality and pipeline optimization, and building infrastructure that supports ML and AI workloads alongside traditional analytics.</p> <p>For candidates based in the US, the salary range for this position is $216,000 to $318,000 USD. For candidates based out of Canada, the salary range for this position is $251,000 to $295,000 CAD. We take into consideration an individual's background and experience in determining final salary - therefore, base pay offered may vary considerably depending on geographic location, job-related knowledge, skills, and experience. The compensation package includes a wide range of medical, dental, vision, financial, and other benefits, as well as equity.</p> <h3>What You’ll Do</h3> <p><strong>Technical Strategy &amp; Execution</strong></p> <ul> <li>Define and drive the technical roadmap across data platform, analytics engineering, and ads data infrastructure. Set the architectural vision for how data is ingested, transformed, modeled, and served at Life360.</li> <li>Own the analytics engineering strategy end-to-end: dbt project structure, data modeling standards (dimensional, OBT, and semantic layer), testing and documentation practices, and the development workflow that analytics engineers use daily.</li> <li>Oversee the data platform: Databricks infrastructure, compute optimization, pipeline orchestration, data lake architecture, and the reliability/observability stack that keeps it all running at consumer scale.</li> <li>Drive toward a self-serve data experience where analysts and data scientists can answer their own questions without engineering bottlenecks—this is the outcome that ties platform and analytics engineering together.</li> <li>Make strategic build vs. buy decisions across the data stack and manage vendor relationships (Snowflake, Databricks, Amplitude, and related tooling).</li> <li>Drive data quality, governance, and documentation standards that make data trustworthy and self-service across the company.</li> <li>Bring an AI native approach to data engineering: leverage AI tools to accelerate development cycles, evaluate AI-powered data quality and anomaly detection solutions, and ensure our data infrastructure supports ML/AI workloads and experimentation at scale.</li> <li>Stay current on emerging technologies in the data and AI space and make pragmatic decisions about adoption—knowing when a new tool solves a real problem vs. when it’s a distraction.</li> </ul> <p><strong>Leadership &amp; People Management</strong></p> <ul> <li>Lead and develop three engineering managers and their teams, setting clear expectations, running effective org-level planning, and building a high-performance culture.</li> <li>Coach and grow your managers as leaders—not just executors. Invest in their ability to hire, develop talent, run retrospectives, and make tradeoffs independently.</li> <li>Own workforce planning, headcount allocation, and hiring strategy across all three teams. Run calibrations and drive promotion decisions.</li> <li>Build a cohesive engineering org from three distinct functional teams, establishing shared standards while respecting each team’s unique domain expertise.</li> </ul> <p><strong>Stakeholder &amp; Cross-Functional Partnership</strong></p> <ul> <li>Serve as the primary point of contact for data engineering across Product, Engineering, Finance, Marketing, and executive leadership. Translate business needs into engineering priorities and translate engineering constraints into language the business can act on.</li> <li>Manage intake and prioritization across multiple operating groups, balancing competing demands and making clear, defensible tradeoff decisions.</li> <li>Represent data engineering in company-level planning, roadmap reviews, and leadership forums. Be the person who can explain what’s possible, what’s expensive, and what’s worth doing.</li> <li>Build strong partnerships with Analytics and Data Science to ensure that platform investments and data models actually serve the analysts, decision scientists, data scientists, and business stakeholders downstream.</li> </ul> <p><strong>Business Impact</strong></p> <ul> <li>Understand Life360’s business deeply—MAU growth, retention mechanics, subscription economics, ads monetization—and use that understanding to prioritize engineering work that moves the needle.</li> <li>Partner with product and business teams to ensure data infrastructure supports experimentation, feature measurement, and decision-making at the speed the business requires.</li> <li>Own budget and cost management for data engineering infrastructure, ensuring efficient use of cloud resources and vendor spend.</li> </ul> <h3>What We’re Looking For</h3> <p><strong>Must-Haves</strong></p> <ul> <li>8–10+ years of experience in data engineering, analytics engineering, or data platform roles at technology companies, with at least 5 years in people management.</li> <li>3+ years managing managers; you know how to lead through others, set org-level direction, and scale teams.</li> <li>Define and drive the architectural vision for end-to-end ELT/ETL processes, covering data ingestion, transformation, modeling, and serving at consumer scale.</li> <li>Strong technical credibility across data platform and analytics engineering, combined with solid business acumen, consistently tying technical decisions to business impact.&nbsp;</li> <li>Strong experience with dbt (data build tool): project structure, testing frameworks, documentation standards, CI/CD for data transformations, and how to scale dbt across multiple teams and domains.</li> <li>Production experience with Databricks or equivalent lakehouse platforms (Snowflake, BigQuery) at consumer scale—including pipeline design, orchestration, reliability and compute optimization, cost management, and data lake architecture.</li> <li>Demonstrated experience managing multiple teams or workstreams simultaneously (15+ people across distinct functions) at a technology company.</li> <li>Strong track record of stakeholder management at the director/VP level—you’re comfortable saying no, explaining tradeoffs, and building trust with non-technical leaders.</li> <li>Ability to distill technical complexity into language that non-technical stakeholders can understand and act on. You can run a crisp stakeholder review with a VP of Product as effectively as you can lead an architecture review with your engineers.</li> <li>Proven ability to prioritize ruthlessly across competing demands from multiple business units—a skill sharpened by working in fast-paced tech environments.</li> <li>Strong business acumen—you understand how product metrics, growth loops, and monetization models connect to data infrastructure decisions.</li> <li>AI native mindset: you actively use AI tools in your own work and have a point of view on how AI changes data engineering practices, team productivity, and infrastructure requirements.</li> <li>Bachelor’s or Master’s degree in Computer Science, Engineering, Data Science, or a related field.</li> </ul> <p><strong>Nice-to-Haves</strong></p> <ul> <li>Experience at a consumer mobile, subscription-based, or marketplace technology company.</li> <li>Hands-on experience with our specific stack: Databricks, Amplitude, Statsig, dbt.</li> <li>Experience building or managing ads data infrastructure or ad-tech data pipelines.</li> <li>Experience with real-time data streaming and event-driven architectures at scale.</li> <li>Track record of implementing AI/ML-powered approaches to data quality, pipeline optimization, or infrastructure automation.</li> <li>Experience managing remote-first or distributed engineering teams at tech companies.</li> </ul> <h3>Our Benefits</h3> <ul> <li>Competitive pay and benefits.</li> <li>Medical, dental, vision, life and disability insurance plans (100% paid for US employees). We offer supplemental plans for medical and dental for Canadian employees.</li> <li>401(k) plan with company matching program in the US and RRSP with DPSP plan for Canadian employees.</li> <li>Employee Assistance Program (EAP) for mental wellness.</li> <li>Flexible PTO and 12 company wide days off throughout the year.</li> <li>Learning &amp; Development programs.</li> <li>Equipment, tools, and reimbursement support for a productive remote environment.</li> <li>Free Life360 Platinum Membership for your preferred circle.</li> </ul> <h3>Life360 Values</h3> <p>Our company’s mission driven culture is guided by our shared values to create a trusted work environment where you can bring your authentic self to work and make a positive difference&nbsp;</p> <ul> <li>Be a Good Person - We have a team of high integrity people you can trust.&nbsp;</li> <li>Be Direct With Respect - We communicate directly, even when it’s hard.</li> <li>Members Before Metrics - We focus on building an exceptional experience for families.&nbsp;</li> <li>High Intensity High Impact - We do whatever it takes to get the job done.&nbsp;</li> </ul> <h3>Our Commitment to Diversity</h3> <p>We believe that different ideas, perspectives and backgrounds create a stronger and more creative work environment that delivers better results. Together, we continue to build an inclusive culture that encourages, supports, and celebrates the diverse voices of our employees. It fuels our innovation and connects us closer to our customers and the communities we serve. We strive to create a workplace that reflects the communities we serve and where everyone feels empowered to bring their authentic best selves to work.</p> <p><em>We are an equal opportunity employer and value diversity at Life360. We do not discriminate on the basis of race, religion, color, national origin, gender, sexual orientation, age, marital status, veteran status, disability status or any legally protected status.&nbsp;&nbsp;</em></p> <p><em>We encourage people of all backgrounds to apply. We believe that a diversity of perspectives and experiences create a foundation for the best ideas. Come join us in building something meaningful.</em><strong><em> </em></strong><em>Even if you don’t meet 100% of the below qualifications, you should still seriously consider applying!</em></p> <p>&nbsp;</p> <p><strong>#LI-Remote</strong></p> <p><em>____________________________________________________________________________</em></p> <p><br><br></p>

title: Lead Data Engineer
full_description: <div class="content-intro"><p><strong>About Karbon</strong></p> <p style="text-align: left;">Karbon is the global leader in AI-powered practice management software for accounting firms. We provide an award-winning cloud platform that helps tens of thousands of accounting professionals work more efficiently and collaboratively every day. With customers in 40 countries, we have grown into a globally distributed team across the US, Australia, New Zealand, Canada, the United Kingdom, and the Philippines. We are well-funded, ranked #1 on G2, growing rapidly, and have a people-first culture that is recognized with Great Place To Work® certification and on Fortune magazine's Best Small Workplaces™ List.</p></div><h2>About the Role</h2> <p>Karbon is transforming how modern businesses manage financial and operational data. As Lead Data Engineer, you are the technical owner of our data platform — the person who sets direction, drives architecture, and is ultimately accountable for what the team builds and ships.</p> <p>You'll define how we design, build, and evolve a platform that powers AI-driven insights, real-time analytics, and next-generation data experiences for thousands of customers and millions of transactions. You'll be the primary technical voice in cross-functional conversations with analytics, BI, AI, and product teams — and the person who makes sure your team has the clarity, quality bar, and unblocking they need to deliver.</p> <h2>What You'll Own</h2> <ul> <li><strong>Platform strategy and architecture</strong> — own the end-to-end design of our data platform: medallion architecture, data contracts, lineage, multi-tenancy, and the technical roadmap that keeps us ahead of scale.</li> <li><strong>Cross-functional technical leadership</strong> — be the primary data engineering partner for analytics, BI, AI/ML, and product teams; lead design reviews, drive alignment on data contracts, and represent the platform in broader engineering conversations.</li> <li><strong>Solution design</strong> — run architecture and design for all significant platform initiatives: pipeline infrastructure, semantic layers, graph database integration, streaming, and AI/ML feature engineering.</li> <li><strong>Security and governance ownership</strong> — accountable for the platform's security model end-to-end: RBAC, row-level security, PII handling, data residency, tenant isolation, and compliance with privacy regulations.</li> <li><strong>Engineering quality and standards</strong> — set and enforce the bar for how the team builds: testing frameworks, CI/CD for data transformations, observability, code review, and documentation.</li> <li><strong>Team enablement</strong> — mentor and unblock senior and junior engineers; identify gaps in capability or process before they become delivery risks; ensure the team can consistently produce high-quality work.</li> <li><strong>Operational excellence</strong> — own platform reliability: monitoring, alerting, incident response playbooks, and cost optimisation as data volume scales.</li> </ul> <h2>What Sets You Apart</h2> <p><strong>Required</strong></p> <ul> <li>10+ years as a data engineer, with demonstrated experience in a lead, staff, or principal-level role.</li> <li>Deep expertise with cloud data platforms, Databricks strongly preferred.</li> <li>Proven track record designing and owning large-scale, multi-tenant data architectures — not just contributing to them.</li> <li>Strong command of ELT/ETL tooling (Fivetran, Airbyte, or similar), transformation frameworks (dbt), and dimensional modeling at scale.</li> <li>Experience driving technical direction across teams: leading design reviews, influencing architectural decisions, and communicating trade-offs clearly to technical and non-technical stakeholders.</li> <li>Experience implementing enterprise-grade security and governance: RBAC, RLS, PII masking, data residency, and privacy regulation compliance (GDPR, CCPA, HIPAA).</li> <li>Infrastructure-as-Code (Terraform or equivalent) and CI/CD ownership for data systems.</li> <li>You've mentored engineers, raised team capability, and unblocked delivery — not just individually shipped.</li> </ul> <p><strong>Highly Valued</strong></p> <ul> <li>Graph databases (Neo4j, Amazon Neptune, ArangoDB) and graph query patterns.</li> <li>Real-time/streaming data (Kafka, Spark Streaming, Fivetran CDC).</li> <li>ML infrastructure, feature stores, or MLOps tooling.</li> <li>Experience shaping data platform strategy in a scaling SaaS environment.</li> </ul> <h2>Our Core Data Stack</h2> <ul> <li><strong>Platform:</strong> Databricks (core compute and transformation layer)&nbsp;</li> <li><strong>Ingestion:</strong> Fivetran and custom pipelines&nbsp;</li> <li><strong>Transformation:</strong> DLT/ dbt with medallion architecture&nbsp;</li> <li><strong>Cloud:</strong> Microsoft Azure&nbsp;</li> <li><strong>Graph:</strong> Neo4j / ArangoDB (evolving)&nbsp;</li> <li><strong>Observability:</strong> Datadog</li> <li><strong>IaC:</strong> Terraform</li> </ul> <p>Our architecture continues to evolve toward real-time streaming, graph intelligence, and AI-native data experiences. If you care about platform ownership, engineering excellence, and building a team that ships with confidence — you'll feel right at home here.</p> <h4><strong>Why Work at Karbon?</strong></h4> <ul> <li>Gain global experience across the USA, Australia, New Zealand, UK, Canada and the Philippines</li> <li>4 weeks annual leave plus 5 extra "Karbon Days" off a year</li> <li>Flexible working environment</li> <li>Work with (and learn from) an experienced, high-performing team</li> <li>Be part of a fast-growing company that firmly believes in promoting high performers from within</li> <li>A collaborative, team-oriented culture that embraces diversity, invests in development, and provides consistent feedback</li> <li>Generous parental leave</li> </ul> <h2>Our Engineering Standards</h2> <p><strong>Balance Speed and Quality</strong> Engineers are expected to balance delivery speed with a strong commitment to quality, meeting agreed timelines while producing reliable, maintainable, and well-tested solutions. Sound judgment in making trade-offs between velocity and long-term sustainability is essential.</p> <p><strong>Collaborate Effectively</strong> Engineering is collaborative by default. Team members are expected to contribute constructively in design discussions, reviews, and planning, communicate clearly about progress and risks, and support shared team outcomes in both hybrid and distributed environments.</p> <p><strong>Build and Maintain Systems</strong> Engineers are responsible for building new capabilities while maintaining and improving existing systems. This includes designing scalable solutions, reducing technical debt, supporting operational stability, and contributing to continuous improvement.</p> <p><strong>Operate with Autonomy</strong> A high degree of autonomy is expected. Given clear objectives, engineers should independently translate problems into actionable technical approaches, proactively identify improvements, and continuously expand relevant technical expertise.</p> <p><strong>Ownership and Accountability</strong> Ownership is fundamental. Engineers are accountable for the quality, performance, and customer impact of their work from design through post-release support, and are expected to follow through on commitments.</p> <p><strong>AI-Enabled Engineering</strong> AI is reshaping how software is built, and we are committed to leveraging it as a force multiplier for creativity, impact, and capability. Engineers are expected to confidently apply strong technical fundamentals while embracing AI tools and approaches to enhance productivity, problem-solving, and innovation.</p> <p><strong>Contribute to Team Culture</strong> Engineers contribute positively to a culture of professionalism, transparency, low bureaucracy, and mutual respect, strengthening team performance through authenticity, curiosity, and collaboration.</p> <p>&nbsp;</p><div class="content-conclusion"><p data-type="unstyled" data-id="37024650-610f-4dfe-8f74-6e5b0feb927b"><strong>Please be aware that Karbon will only contact you via email from our domain, karbonhq.com. If you receive an email from any other domain, please do not click any of those links.</strong></p> <p data-type="unstyled" data-id="37024650-610f-4dfe-8f74-6e5b0feb927b">Karbon embraces diversity and inclusion, aligning with our values as a business. Research has shown that women and underrepresented groups are less likely to apply to jobs unless they meet every single criteria. If you've made it this far in the job description but your past experience doesn't perfectly align, we do encourage you to still apply. You could still be the right person for the role!</p> <p data-type="unstyled" data-id="5f9e3aa2-2690-4788-af20-892dbdba6ed2">We recruit and reward people based on capability and performance. We don’t discriminate based on race, gender, sexual orientation, gender identity or expression, lifestyle, age, educational background, national origin, religion, physical or cognitive ability, and other diversity dimensions that may hinder inclusion in the organization.</p> <p data-type="unstyled" data-id="bb4a5afa-071e-49bd-9510-b951e1f125de">Generally, if you are a good person, we want to talk to you. <span data-type="EMOJI" data-emoji-colon="stuck_out_tongue" data-emoji-id="😛" data-id="97488c7e-05ea-4a57-8774-fd75a6a1882c">😛</span></p> <p data-type="unstyled" data-id="a6b8c301-2390-453b-a5eb-e526a94dca3c">If there are any adjustments or accommodations that we can make to assist you during the recruitment process, and your journey at Karbon, contact us at people.support@karbonhq.com for a confidential discussion.</p> <p data-type="unstyled" data-id="a6b8c301-2390-453b-a5eb-e526a94dca3c">&nbsp;</p> <p style="text-align: center;" data-type="unstyled" data-id="a6b8c301-2390-453b-a5eb-e526a94dca3c"><em><strong><span style="color: rgb(42, 46, 69); font-family: arial, helvetica, sans-serif; font-size: 14px; letter-spacing: normal; text-align: start; word-spacing: 0px; white-space: pre-wrap; float: none; display: inline !important;">At this time, we request that agency referrals are not submitted for this position. We appreciate your understanding and encourage direct applications from interested candidates. Thank you!</span></strong></em></p></div>

title: Senior Data Engineer
full_description: <h2 style="text-align: center;"><span style="font-family: tahoma, arial, helvetica, sans-serif;">Senior Data Engineer&nbsp; &nbsp; &nbsp;&nbsp;</span></h2> <p style="text-align: center;"><span style="color: rgb(126, 140, 141); font-family: tahoma, arial, helvetica, sans-serif;"><em>Prolific</em></span></p> <p><span style="font-family: tahoma, arial, helvetica, sans-serif;">Prolific is not just another player in the AI space – we are the architects of the human data infrastructure that's reshaping the landscape of AI development. In a world where foundational AI technologies are increasingly commoditized, it's the quality and diversity of human-generated data that truly differentiates products and models.</span></p> <p style="text-align: center;"><span style="color: rgb(126, 140, 141); font-family: tahoma, arial, helvetica, sans-serif;"><em>The role</em></span></p> <p>We're looking for a Senior Data Engineer to help build scalable data solutions that serve teams across the business – from data scientists and analysts to AI engineers and product teams. You'll contribute to both the underlying platform and the way data is used in our products, working in a modern, cloud native environment where privacy, security, and good data practices are part of how we build.</p> <p style="text-align: center;"><span style="color: rgb(126, 140, 141); font-family: tahoma, arial, helvetica, sans-serif;"><em>What you’ll bring to the role</em></span></p> <ul> <li><strong>Technical Expertise:</strong> 3+ years building and shipping production grade data systems, with strong SQL skills and proficiency in Python or another object-oriented language (Java, Scala etc.)</li> <li><strong>Familiarity with cloud-native infrastructure</strong> - ideally some exposure to Terraform, Kubernetes, or similar IaC and container orchestration tools. We don't expect deep expertise in all of these, but comfort working in this kind of environment matters.</li> <li><strong>Experience designing data APIs or services</strong> that expose data to applications, or a strong interest in working across the analytical/operational boundary.</li> <li><strong>A thoughtful approach to data quality, privacy, and security</strong> - you understand that how data is used is as important as how it's moved.</li> <li><strong>Strong collaboration skills</strong> and comfort working across teams with different priorities — engineers, data scientists, AI engineers, product.</li> <li><strong>A pragmatic, curious mindset</strong> - you enjoy keeping up with the field and know when to reach for a new tool versus when to stick with what works.</li> <li><strong>Pipeline Management:</strong> Hands on experience with data pipeline tools (Airflow, dbt) and strong ability to optimise for performance and reliability.</li> <li><strong>Quality Focus:</strong> Commitment to continuously improving product quality, security, and performance through rigorous testing and code reviews.</li> <li><strong>Documentation:</strong> Meticulous approach to creating and maintaining architecture and systems documentation.</li> <li><strong>Problem-Solving:</strong> Exceptional analytical skills to troubleshoot complex data issues and implement effective solutions.</li> <li><strong>Independence: </strong>Capability to ship medium features independently while contributing to the team's overall objectives.</li> </ul> <p style="text-align: center;"><span style="font-family: tahoma, arial, helvetica, sans-serif;"><em><span style="color: rgb(126, 140, 141);">What you’ll be doing in the role</span>&nbsp;</em></span></p> <ul> <li>Build and maintain data pipelines that power analytics, ML workloads, and product-facing applications - from internal databases, SaaS sources, and streaming systems through to the teams and services that consume them.</li> <li>Evolve our data platform alongside cloud platform engineers, using infrastructure-as-code (Terraform) and Kubernetes-based deployments (Argo) to keep the platform scalable, reliable, and self-serve.</li> <li>System Architecture: Design and implement scalable data infrastructure that accommodates our growing data volume and complexity.</li> <li>Develop data services and APIs that expose trusted data to product applications, bridging analytical and operational systems.</li> <li>Own data quality and observability, putting monitoring, testing, and alerting in place so issues are caught early and trust in the data stays high.</li> <li>Partner with AI engineers, data scientists, analysts, and product teams to understand their data needs and design the right solutions – not just the quickest ones.</li> <li>Uphold strong data privacy, security, and compliance practices in everything you build, particularly where data flows into product-facing contexts.</li> <li>Technical Documentation: Create and maintain comprehensive documentation of data flows, models, and systems for knowledge sharing.</li> <li>Security and Compliance: Ensure all data systems adhere to security best practices and compliance requirements.</li> </ul> <p style="text-align: center;"><span style="color: rgb(126, 140, 141); font-family: tahoma, arial, helvetica, sans-serif;"><em>Why Prolific is a great place to work</em></span></p> <p><span style="font-family: tahoma, arial, helvetica, sans-serif;">We've built a unique platform that connects researchers and companies with a global pool of participants, enabling the collection of high-quality, ethically sourced human behavioral data and feedback. This data is the cornerstone of developing more accurate, nuanced, and aligned AI systems.</span></p> <p><span style="font-family: tahoma, arial, helvetica, sans-serif;">We believe that the next leap in AI capabilities won't come solely from scaling existing models, but from integrating diverse human perspectives and behaviors into AI development. By providing this crucial human data infrastructure, Prolific is positioning itself at the forefront of the next wave of AI innovation – one that reflects the breath and the best of humanity.</span></p> <p><span style="font-family: tahoma, arial, helvetica, sans-serif;">Working for us will place you at the forefront of AI innovation, providing access to our unique human data platform and opportunities for groundbreaking research. Join us to enjoy a competitive salary, benefits, and remote working within our impactful, mission-driven culture.</span></p> <p style="text-align: center;"><span style="color: rgb(126, 140, 141); font-family: tahoma, arial, helvetica, sans-serif;"><em>Links to more information on Prolific</em></span></p> <p style="text-align: center;"><span style="font-family: tahoma, arial, helvetica, sans-serif;"><a href="https://www.notion.so/prolific/dbc97f2b3ff24f1bb6fa834a2c9c3283?v=4f874e88e46a4f54afd8bf62f1db19ed&amp;p=9f2c1de05dfa43338d48c20362bfead1&amp;pm=c">Benefits&nbsp;</a></span></p> <p style="text-align: center;"><span style="font-family: tahoma, arial, helvetica, sans-serif;"><a href="https://www.notion.so/prolific/dbc97f2b3ff24f1bb6fa834a2c9c3283?v=4f874e88e46a4f54afd8bf62f1db19ed">External Handbook</a>&nbsp;</span></p> <p style="text-align: center;"><span style="font-family: tahoma, arial, helvetica, sans-serif;"><a href="https://www.prolific.com/">Website&nbsp;</a></span></p> <p style="text-align: center;"><span style="font-family: tahoma, arial, helvetica, sans-serif;"><a href="https://www.youtube.com/@JoinProlific">Youtube</a>&nbsp;</span></p> <p style="text-align: center;"><span style="color: rgb(126, 140, 141); font-family: tahoma, arial, helvetica, sans-serif;"><em>Privacy Statement</em></span></p> <p><span style="font-family: tahoma, arial, helvetica, sans-serif;">By submitting your application, you agree that Prolific may collect your personal data for recruiting and global organisation planning. Prolific's <a href="https://www.notion.so/prolific/Policy-Privacy-Notice-for-Candidates-f94a136a67544388b4888bc436451b57">Candidate Privacy Notice</a> explains what personal information Prolific may process, where Prolific may process your personal information, its purposes for processing your personal information, and the rights you can exercise over Prolific use of your personal information.</span></p>

title: Senior Data Engineer - Managed Services
full_description: <div class="content-intro"><p>Are you looking for a role that motivates and challenges you? Are you ready for an opportunity for growth? Do you want to work on teams where people roll up their sleeves to take on tough problems together, and regularly blow the doors off our clients with their outstanding teamwork? If you answered yes to those questions, 3Cloud might just be for you!</p> <p>At 3Cloud, we hire people who aren’t afraid to experiment or fail. We hire people who are willing to give direct and candid feedback to their managers, leaders, and team members. We hire people who jump at those opportunities because they care about our collective growth and success. We hire people who challenge and hold each other accountable for living 3Cloud’s core values because they know that it will result in amazing experiences and solutions for our clients and each other.</p></div><p>Senior Data Engineer - Managed Services<br>Responsibilities<br>• Support of client’s Azure Data platform and Power BI Environment, including response to any escalations while helping to analyze and resolve incidents for customers environment<br>• Consult, develop and advise on solutions in Microsoft Azure with tools such as SSIS, SSRS, SSAS, Synapse, Data Factory, Databricks, Data Lake, Data Warehouse<br>• Consult, develop, and advise on Power BI, including report development and modeling<br>• Consistently learn, apply, and refine skills around data engineering and data analytics<br>• Design and deploy data pipelines, models, and data driven solutions for clients in various industries<br>• Understand and work with customer on licensing involving Microsoft data solutions, and understand the limits/advantages of each licensing platform<br>• Preparing Data Analytic solutions for customers including importing data from multiple sources, performant star schema data models to be ingested via Power BI, and report and dashboard development.<br>• Communicate ticket status information to all associated parties and escalate cases as appropriate<br>• Interact with clients to identify issues, troubleshoot expediently, and follow up on issues via e-mail, ticketing system, and phone calls. Communicate technical issues and solutions with clients in business terms<br>• Configure, administer, tune and develop client’s data environment based on contracted services<br>• Create and maintain updated documentation for each client’s environment<br>• Promote best practices for clients and internally to enhance and improve organizational processes<br>• Participate in recurring discussions with clients to review current issues and to propose possible solutions<br>• Collaborate with team members and client stakeholders to ensure our solutions align to our client’s needs</p> <p>Requirements</p> <ul> <li>Bachelor’s degree in Computer Science, Mathematics, or related field, or equivalent certification</li> <li>Experience working in a support role and understanding standard support processes (i.e. ITIL)</li> <li>5+ years architecting, implementing, and supporting solutions on the Microsoft Azure and Fabric tool stacks for Data &amp; AI (ADF, SQL DW, Azure Synapse, Azure Databricks, Microsoft Fabric Data Engineering, Microsoft Power BI)</li> <li>Strong proficiency in Enterprise Data Warehousing, Semantic Models, Data Lake Architecture, and Metadata-driven ETL processes is required</li> <li>Working familiarity with DevOps/ DataOps, for example experience delivering work using git-based source control and automated deployment pipelines is preferred</li> <li>Working familiarity with AI/ Machine learning use cases on the Microsoft tool stacks for Data &amp; AI is a plus</li> <li>Strong verbal and written communication skills</li> <li>A passion for problem solving and learning new technologies</li> <li>Client relationship skills and experience managing vendors</li> <li>Ability to work in a fast paced, rapidly changing environment</li> <li>Strong desire for personal development and learning, including commitment to continuously upgrading your technical skills as the data landscape change</li> <li>Work remote, but not alone – with a team filled top to bottom with data analytics professionals</li> </ul> <p><br><br>Don’t meet every single requirement? At 3Cloud we are dedicated to building a diverse, inclusive and authentic workplace, so if you’re excited about this role but your experience doesn’t align perfectly with every qualification in the job description, we encourage you to apply anyway.<br>At this time, we cannot sponsor applicants for work visas.</p> <p><strong>This Job Posting will expire on August 24th, 2026.</strong></p> <p>&nbsp;</p><div class="content-pay-transparency"><div class="pay-input"><div class="description"><hr> <p><strong>3Cloud Total Rewards Highlights Include:</strong> &nbsp;</p> <ul> <li>Flexible work location with a virtual first approach to work! </li> <li>401(K) with match up to 50% of your 6% contributions of eligible pay </li> <li>Generous PTO providing a minimum of 15 days in addition to 9 paid company holidays and 2 floating personal days &nbsp;</li> <li>Two medical plan options to allow you the choice to elect what works best for you!</li> <li>Option for vision and dental coverage </li> <li>100% employer paid coverage for life and disability insurance</li> <li>Paid leave for birth parents and non-birth parents </li> <li>Option for Healthcare FSA, HSA, and Dependent Care FSA</li> <li>$67.00 monthly tech and home office allowance</li> <li>Utilization and/or discretionary bonus eligibility based on role </li> <li>Employee Assistance Program to help with everyday challenges</li> </ul> <p>3Cloud offers competitive compensation. In addition to base pay employees are eligible to receive an annual discretionary/utilization bonus. If you are hired at 3Cloud your final base salary is based on factors such as skills, education, experience and/or geographic location. Please keep in mind that the range mentioned above includes the full base salary range for the role. It is not typical for offers to be made at or near the top of the range.</p></div><div class="title">Base Salary Range</div><div class="pay-range"><span>$96,500</span><span class="divider">&mdash;</span><span>$144,800 USD</span></div></div></div><div class="content-conclusion"><p>Don’t meet every single requirement?&nbsp;At 3Cloud we are dedicated to building a diverse, inclusive and authentic workplace, so if you’re excited about this role but your past experience doesn’t align perfectly with every qualification in the job description, we encourage you to apply anyway.</p> <p><strong>At this time, we cannot sponsor applicants for work visas.</strong></p></div>

title: Senior Data Engineer
full_description: <div class="content-intro"><p><strong>Welcome to Planet. We believe in using space to help life on Earth.</strong></p> <p>Planet designs, builds, and operates the largest constellation of imaging satellites in history. This constellation delivers an unprecedented dataset of empirical information via a revolutionary cloud-based platform to authoritative figures in commercial, environmental, and humanitarian sectors. We are both a space company and data company all rolled into one.</p> <p>Customers and users across the globe use Planet's data to develop new technologies, drive revenue, power research, and solve our world’s toughest obstacles.</p> <p>As we control every component of hardware design, manufacturing, data processing, and software engineering, our office is a truly inspiring mix of experts from a variety of domains.</p> <p>We have a people-centric approach toward culture and community and we strive to iterate in a way that puts our team members first and prepares our company for growth. Join Planet and be a part of our mission to change the way people see the world.</p> <p>Planet is a global company with employees working remotely world wide and joining us from offices in San Francisco, Washington DC, Germany, Austria, Slovenia, and The Netherlands.</p></div><p><strong>About the Role:</strong></p> <p>As a Senior Data Engineer (DE) at Planet, you will own, architect and build production, enterprise-scale data ecosystems using modern cloud technologies, to power mission-critical, customer-facing products. You will act as a strategic partner to cross-functional stakeholders, translating complex requirements into robust, scalable solutions. You will build and design data solutions end to end: from high-level architecture and implementation to CI/CD, orchestration automated QA, and seamless integration into production systems. You’ll be a senior member of the Data Team within the Software Engineering organization, which is made up of data scientists, engineers and analysts working on similar tasks across Planet domains.&nbsp;</p> <p>Your scope will focus on the architecture and evolution of data systems supporting our global monitoring products. These products are vital offerings designed to provide persistent, high-cadence monitoring and active intelligence, particularly for our Defence and Intelligence customers. You will collaborate closely with leadership across Product and Engineering to ensure some of our most sophisticated customers have the data required for global security and rapid decision-making. This role is ideal for an expert data engineer looking to lead the evolution of complex systems and deliver transformative technology to the defense sector. The ideal candidate is an independent operator who thrives in a dynamic, global environment and is deeply passionate about Planet’s mission and national security solutions.</p> <p>This is a full-time, hybrid role which will require you to work from our Washington DC or Denver CO office 3 days per week.</p> <p><strong>Impact You'll Own:</strong></p> <ul> <li>Architect and oversee robust, high-availability data pipelines while establishing the standards for maintaining, troubleshooting, and optimizing existing ecosystems.</li> <li>Collaborate with global product, commercial and engineering leads and peers to design and execute a long-term data strategy for products in your scope.</li> <li>Drive high-level mentorship, technical design reviews, and architectural integrity across the Data Team while setting standards for code quality.</li> </ul> <p><strong>What You Bring:</strong></p> <ul> <li>10+ years of relevant experience in data engineering with a track record of technical leadership.</li> <li>Bachelor’s degree in a relevant field.</li> <li>Expertise in designing and implementing end-to-end data architectures for global-scale, customer-facing solutions, with a focus on data integrity and efficiency.</li> <li>Expert-level proficiency in Python and SQL for complex ETL/ELT pipeline construction, including event-driven data architectures.</li> <li>Proactive, self-starter mindset with the ability to lead problem-solving initiatives in a dynamic, high-stakes environment.</li> <li>Exceptional communication skills, with the ability to translate between technical execution and high-level business requirements.</li> <li>Deep experience in maintaining enterprise-grade, peer-reviewed codebases and advanced CI/CD workflows using GitLab CI, Docker, and Kubernetes.</li> <li>Significant experience with cloud-native architectures (GCP preferred).</li> <li>Experience tuning storage formats such as Parquet to ensure sub-second query performance for consumers.</li> </ul> <p><strong>What Makes You Stand Out:</strong></p> <ul> <li>Experience using modern data tooling (e.g. dbt) for pipeline inputs to customer-facing production systems.</li> <li>Experience leveraging Infrastructure as Code tooling (e.g. Terraform) to manage cloud architecture.</li> <li>Experience architecting solutions for geospatial datasets.</li> <li>Knowledge of earth observation and/or satellite operation concepts.</li> </ul> <p><strong>Application Deadline:</strong></p> <p>October 1, 2026 by 11:59p / 23:59 CET (Central European Time)</p> <p><strong>EAR/ITAR Requirements:</strong></p> <p><em>This position requires access to export-controlled information, and as such, employment (or hiring of a contractor) is contingent upon the candidate’s ability to access all applicable export-controlled information without additional export licensing being required by the Bureau of Industry and Security and/or the Directorate of Defense Trade Controls.</em></p> <p><strong>Benefits While Working at Planet:</strong></p> <p><em>These offerings are dependent on employment type and geographical location, based upon applicable law or company policy.</em></p> <ul> <li>Comprehensive Medical, Dental, and Vision plans</li> <li>Health Savings Account (HSA) with a company contribution</li> <li>Generous Paid Time Off in addition to holidays and company-wide days off&nbsp;</li> <li>16 Weeks of Paid Parental Leave</li> <li>Wellness Program and Employee Assistance Program (EAP)</li> <li>Home Office Reimbursement</li> <li>Monthly Phone and Internet Reimbursement</li> <li>Tuition Reimbursement and access to LinkedIn Learning</li> <li>Equity</li> <li>Commuter Benefits (if local to an office)</li> <li>Volunteering Paid Time Off</li> </ul> <p><strong>Compensation:</strong></p> <p>The US base salary range for this full-time position at the commencement of employment is listed below. Additionally, this role might be eligible for discretionary short-term and long-term incentives (bonus and equity). The final salary range is determined by job related experience, skills and location. The range displays our typical hiring range for new hire salaries in US locations only. title: Senior Data Engineer, Constellation Services
full_description: <div class="content-intro"><p><strong>Welcome to Planet. We believe in using space to help life on Earth.</strong></p> <p>Planet designs, builds, and operates the largest constellation of imaging satellites in history. This constellation delivers an unprecedented dataset of empirical information via a revolutionary cloud-based platform to authoritative figures in commercial, environmental, and humanitarian sectors. We are both a space company and data company all rolled into one.</p> <p>Customers and users across the globe use Planet's data to develop new technologies, drive revenue, power research, and solve our world’s toughest obstacles.</p> <p>As we control every component of hardware design, manufacturing, data processing, and software engineering, our office is a truly inspiring mix of experts from a variety of domains.</p> <p>We have a people-centric approach toward culture and community and we strive to iterate in a way that puts our team members first and prepares our company for growth. Join Planet and be a part of our mission to change the way people see the world.</p> <p>Planet is a global company with employees working remotely world wide and joining us from offices in San Francisco, Washington DC, Germany, Austria, Slovenia, and The Netherlands.</p></div><p><strong>About the Role:&nbsp;&nbsp;</strong></p> <p>As a Senior Data Engineer (DE) at Planet, you will own, design and build enterprise ETL pipelines and self-service data tooling using modern cloud technologies. You will work closely with cross-functional stakeholders across the company to understand their needs and to deliver robust, flexible solutions and clear insights to meet those needs. You will build data solutions end to end: architecture, implementation, CI/CD and orchestration of pipelines, QA, visualization and effective data communication. You’ll be part of the Data Team within the Software Engineering organization, which is made up of data scientists, engineers and analysts working on similar tasks across Planet domains.&nbsp;</p> <p>You will own the ongoing evolution of data solutions supporting monitoring and analysis of Planet’s Missions team and Constellation Services (CxS) products, a new line of products which support large customers in buying secure access and reserved capacity on our satellites. You will collaborate closely with CxS-focused counterparts on our Product, Engineering, Space Systems and Commercial orgs to ensure they have the data they need to evaluate the success of the products. This role will appeal to candidates who are looking for opportunities to evolve a complex data system end to end and bring needed technology to the defense and intelligence sector.</p> <p>This is a full-time, hybrid role which will require you to work from our Berlin office 3 days per week.</p> <p><strong>Impact You’ll Own:</strong></p> <ul> <li>Develop and own robust, accurate data pipelines as well as maintain, troubleshoot, and improve existing ones&nbsp;</li> <li>Deliver self-service data tooling, visualizations and insights needed to drive decision making and monitoring around CxS products&nbsp;</li> <li>Collaborate with stakeholders, your manager and peers to define and develop the CxS data roadmap</li> <li>Provide mentorship, technical design feedback and code reviews for peers within your team and across the broader org</li> </ul> <p><strong>What You Bring:</strong></p> <ul> <li>6+ years of relevant experience</li> <li>Bachelors’ degree in a relevant field&nbsp;</li> <li>Experience designing and implementing data architecture for data solutions end to end, from ingestion through insight and with an eye for data accuracy</li> <li>Expertise in Python and SQL ETL pipeline buildout&nbsp;</li> <li>Experience with the modern data stack: Fivetran, dbt, Looker/Omni/Sigma, or similar</li> <li>Ability to be a self-starter for solving problems in a dynamic environment</li> <li>Ability to clearly communicate and collaborate with cross-functional teams</li> <li>Experience with maintaining peer-reviewed code and continuous integration and deployment workflows and tooling like GitLab CI and Docker or similar</li> <li>Experience with cloud architectures and services, such as Google Cloud Platform (preferred) or AWS</li> <li>Experience working with geospatial datasets and event-based data architectures</li> </ul> <p><strong>What Makes You Stand Out:</strong></p> <ul> <li>Experience with messaging services (Pub/Sub, RabbitMQ, etc) and real-time streaming</li> <li>Experience with monitoring and observability tools like Datadog, Grafana, or equivalent</li> <li>Knowledge of earth observation and/or satellite operation concepts</li> </ul> <p><strong>Application Deadline:</strong></p> <p>October 8, 2026 by 11:59p / 23:59 CET (Central European Time)</p> <p><strong>Export Control Requirements:</strong></p> <p><em>Planet Labs Germany GmbH is authorized to receive U.S. export-controlled information at Planet under an export license issued by the Bureau of Industry and Security. This position requires access to U.S. and German/EU export-controlled information, and as such, employment (or hiring of a contractor) is contingent upon the candidate’s ability to access all applicable export-controlled information without additional export licensing being required by German, EU, or U.S. authorities.</em></p> <p><strong>Benefits While Working at Planet:</strong></p> <p><em>These offerings are dependent on employment type and geographical location, based upon applicable law or company policy.</em></p> <ul> <li>Paid time off including vacation, holidays and company-wide days off&nbsp;</li> <li>Employee Wellness Program&nbsp;</li> <li>Home Office Reimbursement</li> <li>Monthly Phone and Internet Reimbursement</li> <li>Tuition Reimbursement and access to LinkedIn Learning</li> <li>Equity&nbsp;</li> <li>Volunteering Paid Time Off</li> </ul><div class="content-conclusion"><p><strong>San Francisco Fair Chance Ordinance<br></strong>Pursuant to the San Francisco Fair Chance Ordinance, we will consider for employment qualified applicants with arrest and conviction records.<strong><br></strong></p> <p><strong>Why we care so much about Belonging.&nbsp;<br></strong>We’re dedicated to helping the whole Planet, and to do that we must strive to represent all of it within each of our offices and on all of our teams. reviews, source control management, and testing methodologies.</li> <li>Excellent customer engagement skills, with the ability to communicate complex technical concepts to diverse audiences.</li> title: Senior Analytics Engineer
full_description: <div class="content-intro"><p><span style="font-size: 12pt;">We&nbsp;take play seriously. We’re looking for curious adventurers ready to find their party, fueled by imagination&nbsp;and drive&nbsp;to build what’s never been built before. At Hasbro and Wizards of the Coast, you’ll collaborate with passionate teams to reimagine our iconic brands and create experiences that spark joy, connection, and community through the magic of play. This is your chance to shape legendary play that lasts a lifetime.</span></p></div><p><span style="font-size: 12pt;">Hasbro is a leading games, IP and toy company whose mission is to create joy and community through the magic of play. With 165 years of expertise, Hasbro delivers groundbreaking play experiences and reaches more than 1 billion fans annually around the world, through physical and digital games, video games, toys, licensed consumer products, location-based entertainment, film, TV and more.</span></p> <p>&nbsp;</p> <p><span style="font-size: 12pt;">Through its franchise-first approach, Hasbro unlocks value from both new and legacy IP, including MAGIC: THE GATHERING, DUNGEONS &amp; DRAGONS, MONOPOLY, HASBRO GAMES, NERF, TRANSFORMERS, PLAY-DOH and PEPPA PIG, as well as premier partner brands. Powered by its portfolio of thousands of iconic marks and a diversified network of partners and subsidiary studios, Hasbro brings fans together wherever they are, from tabletop to screen.</span></p> <p>&nbsp;</p> <p><span style="font-size: 12pt;">For more than a decade, Hasbro has been consistently recognized for its corporate citizenship, including being named one of the 100 Best Corporate Citizens by 3BL Media, a 2025 JUST Capital Industry Leader, one of the 50 Most Community-Minded Companies in the U.S. by the Civic 50, and a Brand that Matters by Fast Company. For more information, visit <a href="https://nam11.safelinks.protection.outlook.com/?url=https%3A%2F%2Fcorporate.hasbro.com%2F&amp;data=05%7C02%7CChristian.Porter%40wizards.com%7C1c2f0acd383b4d9e4c5108de85197b24%7C701edd3ec7a84789b1ce8a243620d68f%7C0%7C0%7C639094541651889218%7CUnknown%7CTWFpbGZsb3d8eyJFbXB0eU1hcGkiOnRydWUsIlYiOiIwLjAuMDAwMCIsIlAiOiJXaW4zMiIsIkFOIjoiTWFpbCIsIldUIjoyfQ%3D%3D%7C0%7C%7C%7C&amp;sdata=I%2BUPsw2L44lwcMF%2BB2tJ0pKHlspdHMsqJgCKs9w8oQc%3D&amp;reserved=0">https://corporate.hasbro.com</a> or @Hasbro on LinkedIn.</span></p> <p style="margin: 0.0in 0.0in 8.0pt; line-height: 115%; font-size: 12.0pt; font-family: Aptos, sans-serif;">&nbsp;</p> <p style="margin: 0.0in 0.0in 8.0pt; line-height: 115%; font-size: 12.0pt; font-family: Aptos, sans-serif;"><span style="font-size: 12pt;">We are growing our&nbsp;<strong>Enterprise Data &amp; Engineering</strong> team!</span></p> <p style="margin: 0.0in 0.0in 8.0pt; line-height: 115%; font-size: 12.0pt; font-family: Aptos, sans-serif;">&nbsp;</p> <p style="margin: 0.0in 0.0in 8.0pt; line-height: 115%; font-size: 12.0pt; font-family: Aptos, sans-serif;"><span style="font-size: 12pt;">We're looking for a <strong>Senior Analytics Engineer</strong> to help shape how data is transformed, modeled, and structured to power analytics and business decision-making across the enterprise. Are you energized by turning complex data into scalable, trusted, analytics-ready data products that drive real impact?</span></p> <p style="margin: 0.0in 0.0in 8.0pt; line-height: 115%; font-size: 12.0pt; font-family: Aptos, sans-serif;">&nbsp;</p> <p style="margin: 0.0in 0.0in 8.0pt; line-height: 115%; font-size: 12.0pt; font-family: Aptos, sans-serif;"><span style="font-size: 12pt;">In this high-impact individual contributor role, you will design enterprise data models and semantic layers that define how we measure performance and consume data. Together, we are building production-grade transformation frameworks and curated datasets that ensure reporting and business metrics are accurate, consistent, and performant.</span></p> <p style="margin: 0.0in 0.0in 8.0pt; line-height: 115%; font-size: 12.0pt; font-family: Aptos, sans-serif;">&nbsp;</p> <p style="margin: 0.0in 0.0in 8.0pt; line-height: 115%; font-size: 12.0pt; font-family: Aptos, sans-serif;"><span style="font-size: 12pt;">We value diverse perspectives and welcome candidates who bring different experiences and approaches to solving complex data challenges.</span></p> <p style="margin: 0.0in 0.0in 8.0pt; line-height: 115%; font-size: 12.0pt; font-family: Aptos, sans-serif;">&nbsp;</p> <p style="margin: 0.0in 0.0in 8.0pt; line-height: 115%; font-size: 12.0pt; font-family: Aptos, sans-serif;"><span style="font-size: 12pt;"><strong>What You'll Do</strong></span></p> <ul style="margin-top: 0.0in; margin-bottom: 0.0in;" type="disc"> <li style="margin-top: 0.0in; margin-right: 0.0in; margin-bottom: 8.0pt; line-height: 115%; font-size: 12.0pt; font-family: Aptos, sans-serif;"><span style="font-size: 12pt;">Responsible for the design and evolution of analytics-ready data models, dimensional schemas, and curated datasets.</span></li> <li style="margin-top: 0.0in; margin-right: 0.0in; margin-bottom: 8.0pt; line-height: 115%; font-size: 12.0pt; font-family: Aptos, sans-serif;"><span style="font-size: 12pt;">Own the development of transformation frameworks using DBT, Databricks, and cloud-native technologies.</span></li> <li style="margin-top: 0.0in; margin-right: 0.0in; margin-bottom: 8.0pt; line-height: 115%; font-size: 12.0pt; font-family: Aptos, sans-serif;"><span style="font-size: 12pt;">Translate business requirements into scalable, reusable data models that standardize metric definitions across teams.</span></li> <li style="margin-top: 0.0in; margin-right: 0.0in; margin-bottom: 8.0pt; line-height: 115%; font-size: 12.0pt; font-family: Aptos, sans-serif;"><span style="font-size: 12pt;">Establish and uphold modeling standards, semantic layer governance, naming conventions, documentation practices, and testing frameworks.</span></li> <li style="margin-top: 0.0in; margin-right: 0.0in; margin-bottom: 8.0pt; line-height: 115%; font-size: 12.0pt; font-family: Aptos, sans-serif;"><span style="font-size: 12pt;">Implement and monitor data quality validation, lineage, and transformation-level controls.</span></li> <li style="margin-top: 0.0in; margin-right: 0.0in; margin-bottom: 8.0pt; line-height: 115%; font-size: 12.0pt; font-family: Aptos, sans-serif;"><span style="font-size: 12pt;">Partner closely with data engineers to align upstream pipeline design with downstream modeling and consumption needs.</span></li> <li style="margin-top: 0.0in; margin-right: 0.0in; margin-bottom: 8.0pt; line-height: 115%; font-size: 12.0pt; font-family: Aptos, sans-serif;"><span style="font-size: 12pt;">Collaborate with product managers, analysts, and business partners to ensure data products are intuitive, trusted, and aligned to business definitions.</span></li> <li style="margin-top: 0.0in; margin-right: 0.0in; margin-bottom: 8.0pt; line-height: 115%; font-size: 12.0pt; font-family: Aptos, sans-serif;"><span style="font-size: 12pt;">Lead analytics engineering initiatives from technical design through deployment and optimization.</span></li> <li style="margin-top: 0.0in; margin-right: 0.0in; margin-bottom: 8.0pt; line-height: 115%; font-size: 12.0pt; font-family: Aptos, sans-serif;"><span style="font-size: 12pt;">Recognize opportunities to simplify the analytics data landscape as well as improve performance.</span></li> </ul> <p style="margin: 0.0in 0.0in 8.0pt; line-height: 115%; font-size: 12.0pt; font-family: Aptos, sans-serif;">&nbsp;</p> <p style="margin: 0.0in 0.0in 8.0pt; line-height: 115%; font-size: 12.0pt; font-family: Aptos, sans-serif;"><span style="font-size: 12pt;"><strong>What You'll Bring</strong></span></p> <ul style="margin-top: 0.0in; margin-bottom: 0.0in;" type="disc"> <li style="margin-top: 0.0in; margin-right: 0.0in; margin-bottom: 8.0pt; line-height: 115%; font-size: 12.0pt; font-family: Aptos, sans-serif;"><span style="font-size: 12pt;">Experience developing and delivering enterprise-scale analytics frameworks and data transformation structures in modern data environments.</span></li> <li style="margin-top: 0.0in; margin-right: 0.0in; margin-bottom: 8.0pt; line-height: 115%; font-size: 12.0pt; font-family: Aptos, sans-serif;"><span style="font-size: 12pt;">Strong hands-on expertise with SQL, DBT, and Databricks, as well as cloud data platforms such as Azure and/or AWS.</span></li> <li style="margin-top: 0.0in; margin-right: 0.0in; margin-bottom: 8.0pt; line-height: 115%; font-size: 12.0pt; font-family: Aptos, sans-serif;"><span style="font-size: 12pt;">Proficiency in Python or similar language for transformation logic, automation, and validation workflows.</span></li> <li style="margin-top: 0.0in; margin-right: 0.0in; margin-bottom: 8.0pt; line-height: 115%; font-size: 12.0pt; font-family: Aptos, sans-serif;"><span style="font-size: 12pt;">Deep understanding of dimensional modeling, semantic layer architecture, and data warehousing standard methodologies.</span></li> <li style="margin-top: 0.0in; margin-right: 0.0in; margin-bottom: 8.0pt; line-height: 115%; font-size: 12.0pt; font-family: Aptos, sans-serif;"><span style="font-size: 12pt;">Experience implementing data quality frameworks, automated testing, and documentation standards.</span></li> <li style="margin-top: 0.0in; margin-right: 0.0in; margin-bottom: 8.0pt; line-height: 115%; font-size: 12.0pt; font-family: Aptos, sans-serif;"><span style="font-size: 12pt;">Ability to lead complex workstreams and influence technical decisions without direct authority.</span></li> <li style="margin-top: 0.0in; margin-right: 0.0in; margin-bottom: 8.0pt; line-height: 115%; font-size: 12.0pt; font-family: Aptos, sans-serif;"><span style="font-size: 12pt;">Strong interpersonal skills, with the ability to translate data concepts into clear business-aligned structures.</span></li> <li style="margin-top: 0.0in; margin-right: 0.0in; margin-bottom: 8.0pt; line-height: 115%; font-size: 12.0pt; font-family: Aptos, sans-serif;"><span style="font-size: 12pt;">Experience supporting advanced analytics or AI-enabled use cases is a plus.</span></li> </ul> <p>&nbsp;</p> <p><span style="font-size: 12pt;"><strong><span style="color: black;">We are an Equal Opportunity / Affirmative Action Employer</span></strong></span></p> <p><span style="font-size: 12pt;">Hasbro is committed to equality of opportunity in all aspects of employment. We are committed to making all employment decisions without regard to race, color, religion, sex, sexual orientation, gender identity or expression, national origin, age, marital status, or any other legally protected status.</span></p> <p>&nbsp;</p> <p><span style="font-size: 12pt;">We will ensure that individuals with disabilities are provided reasonable accommodation to participate in the job application or interview process, to perform essential job functions, and to receive other benefits and privileges of employment. If you have a disability and require assistance in this application process and need to request an accommodation, please contact your recruiter or coordinator.</span></p><div class="content-pay-transparency"><div class="pay-input"><div class="description"><p><span style="font-size: 16px;" data-teams="true">The pay transparency range for this role is listed below.
