Benefits and programs may include:</span></p> <ul> <li style="font-size: 12pt;"><span style="font-size: 12pt;">Health &amp; Wellness</span></li> <li style="font-size: 12pt;"><span style="font-size: 12pt;">Time Off to Recharge</span></li> <li style="font-size: 12pt;"><span style="font-size: 12pt;">Financial Well-being</span></li> <li style="font-size: 12pt;"><span style="font-size: 12pt;">Life &amp; Family Support</span></li> <li style="font-size: 12pt;"><span style="font-size: 12pt;">Volunteer and Community Initiatives</span></li> <li style="font-size: 12pt;"><span style="font-size: 12pt;">Learning &amp; Development&nbsp;</span></li> <li style="font-size: 12pt;"><span style="font-size: 12pt;">Exclusive Perks</span></li> </ul> <p><span style="font-size: 12pt;"></span></p> <p><span style="font-size: 12pt;"><span data-olk-copy-source="MailCompose">Please review our </span><a id="OWAf3b48604-66ff-cab5-fa36-85c2d05d437e" class="OWAAutoLink" href="https://nam11.safelinks.protection.outlook.com/?url=http%3A%2F%2Fdocs.hasbro.com%2Fprivacy%2Fjob-applicant&amp;data=05%7C02%7CMaria.Hughes%40wizards.com%7C73f450b2476e4441be3308dec7006d14%7C701edd3ec7a84789b1ce8a243620d68f%7C0%7C0%7C639167001791388387%7CUnknown%7CTWFpbGZsb3d8eyJFbXB0eU1hcGkiOnRydWUsIlYiOiIwLjAuMDAwMCIsIlAiOiJXaW4zMiIsIkFOIjoiTWFpbCIsIldUIjoyfQ%3D%3D%7C0%7C%7C%7C&amp;sdata=QzsYnPcynaB0OC2yYMSHab2TyW8jOlZQWyr87FBwDt8%3D&amp;reserved=0" data-auth="NotApplicable">Applicant Privacy Notice</a> to learn how we collect, use, and protect your personal information in connection with the application process.&nbsp;</span></p> <p><span style="font-size: 12pt;"><br></span></p> <p>&nbsp;</p> <p><span style="font-size: 12pt;"></span></p></div>

title: Senior Data Engineer
full_description: <div class="content-intro"><p><img src="https://hi-contentful.imgix.net/4xN7JhmwCRuz3y9M8L8CF7/5c244c52cfc982bd1a348a80dc35d43b/hi-logo-new.svg?auto=format&amp;ixlib=react-9.0.3&amp;h=40&amp;w=132&amp;q=50&amp;dpr=2" alt="hi-logo" width="132" style="max-width: 100%;"></p></div><p><span style="font-size: 10pt;"><strong>Human Interest is on a mission to ensure that people in all lines of work have access to retirement benefits.</strong>&nbsp;</span></p> <p><span style="font-size: 10pt;">More than half of all working Americans are not saving enough for their future. Too often, it’s because they are employed by a company that doesn’t offer a retirement plan. Human Interest is changing that by making it affordable and accessible for small and medium-sized businesses to offer employees a path to financial independence through retirement savings.</span></p> <p><span style="font-size: 10pt;">We’re a high-growth fintech company changing the retirement industry. We are backed by a number of investors. This includes funding from Marshall Wace and Baillie Gifford, as well as top investors such as BlackRock, TPG (The Rise Fund), SoftBank, Glynn Capital, NewView Capital, USVP, Wing, Uncork, and more.</span></p> <p><span style="font-size: 10pt;"><strong>About the role</strong></span></p> <p><span style="font-size: 10pt;">As a Senior Data Engineer on the Data Access team, you will be a technical anchor for Human Interest’s data platform at a pivotal moment in Human Interest's growth. This role exists to build and own the infrastructure that ensures the data powering our products, our customer reporting, and our business metrics are reliable and well governed. You will drive the evolution of our data platform toward an architecture that scales for AI consumption and for Human Interest's next growth stage. This is a high-ownership role where your architectural decisions will have a direct and visible impact on Human Interest's capabilities.</span></p> <p><span style="font-size: 10pt;">The Data Access team is Human Interest's internal data platform team. We own the full data stack from ingestion and orchestration through transformation, governance, and delivery to BI tools. Our stack includes Snowflake, dbt, Airflow, Meltano, and Terraform on AWS. We're a small, highly collaborative team embedded within the HI Tech organization, working closely with Analysts, Finance, Product, and engineering teams to ensure that the data people rely on is trustworthy and accessible.&nbsp;</span></p> <p><span style="font-size: 10pt;">You'll have the opportunity to shape how we evolve our architecture towards AI-consumption and to set the standard for how production data assets are built, certified, and maintained at Human Interest. The team actively uses AI tools as part of our development workflow, and we expect this role to help drive and expand that practice across the team.</span></p> <p><span style="font-size: 10pt;"><strong>What you get to do every day</strong></span></p> <ul> <li style="font-size: 10pt;"><span style="font-size: 10pt;">Define and maintain data governance standards including access controls, data lineage, and contracts between data producers and consumers</span></li> <li style="font-size: 10pt;"><span style="font-size: 10pt;">Lead the technical direction and evolution of our data platform as we move toward an AI-first data infrastructure. You will design for AI consumption, unstructured data access, and integration with AI tooling from the ground up.</span></li> <li style="font-size: 10pt;"><span style="font-size: 10pt;">Build and scale data pipelines and architectures that make data accessible and useful to both human analysts and AI systems.</span></li> <li style="font-size: 10pt;"><span style="font-size: 10pt;">Own end-to-end design and development of scalable data pipelines, from ingestion and orchestration to transformation and delivery, using tools including AWS, Terraform, Airflow, Snowflake, dbt, Meltano, Python.</span></li> <li style="font-size: 10pt;"><span style="font-size: 10pt;">Drive data platform reliability through performance optimization, data quality monitoring, and SLA-based prioritization of our most critical data assets.</span></li> <li style="font-size: 10pt;"><span style="font-size: 10pt;">Leverage and champion AI-assisted development tools, including Claude Code, to accelerate development velocity across the team.</span></li> <li style="font-size: 10pt;"><span style="font-size: 10pt;">Mentor data engineers and analysts, raising the technical bar across the team.</span></li> </ul> <p><span style="font-size: 10pt;"><strong>What you bring to the role</strong></span></p> <ul> <li style="font-size: 10pt;"><span style="font-size: 10pt;">5+ years of experience as a Data Engineer with a strong focus on production data pipelines and data infrastructure development.</span></li> <li style="font-size: 10pt;"><span style="font-size: 10pt;">Strong experience with AWS data services in a production context, including storage, compute, and pipeline tooling.</span></li> <li style="font-size: 10pt;"><span style="font-size: 10pt;">Hands-on experience with managing cloud data warehouse technologies, including Snowflake or equivalent, covering access control, performance tuning, and cost management.</span></li> <li style="font-size: 10pt;"><span style="font-size: 10pt;">Strong Python skills, with the ability to independently own and improve complex production data pipelines.</span></li> <li style="font-size: 10pt;"><span style="font-size: 10pt;">Experience with workflow orchestration at scale, including Airflow or equivalent tools.</span></li> <li style="font-size: 10pt;"><span style="font-size: 10pt;">Familiarity with data governance, observability, and data quality practices.</span></li> <li style="font-size: 10pt;"><span style="font-size: 10pt;">Familiarity with event sourcing or change data capture-based data patterns.</span></li> <li style="font-size: 10pt;"><span style="font-size: 10pt;">A strong desire to leverage AI tools and workflow automation to improve team productivity.</span></li> </ul> <p><span style="font-size: 10pt;"><strong>Nice to haves:</strong></span></p> <ul> <li style="font-size: 10pt;"><span style="font-size: 10pt;">Experience with data lakehouse architectures and open table formats such as Apache Iceberg or Delta Lake.</span></li> <li style="font-size: 10pt;"><span style="font-size: 10pt;">Experience with dbt-core in a production repository.</span></li> <li style="font-size: 10pt;"><span style="font-size: 10pt;">Experience building data infrastructure to support AI-driven data access such as exposing data assets via MCP, implementing governance frameworks for AI data access, and curating, monitoring and evaluating the quality of AI-generated query responses.</span></li> <li style="font-size: 10pt;"><span style="font-size: 10pt;">Background in fintech, financial services, or another highly regulated or compliance-driven industry.</span></li> </ul> <p><span style="font-size: 10pt;">Please feel free to apply to this position even if you do not meet 100% of the requirements listed above.</span></p> <p><span style="font-size: 10pt;"><strong>Why you will love working at Human Interest</strong></span></p> <p><span style="font-size: 10pt;">Human Interest is tackling one of our country's biggest challenges - closing the retirement gap. You'll be instrumental in architecting and scaling solutions that bring financial security to employees at small and medium-sized businesses nationwide. We’ve made significant progress, but there is still growth ahead, offering you a unique opportunity to solve complex problems, drive innovation, and advance your career alongside a dedicated, mission-driven team. We value hard work and recognize that our team's contributions are key to our continued success.</span></p> <p><span style="font-size: 10pt;">Join Human Interest and make a lasting impact by shaping the future of retirement.</span></p> <p><span style="font-size: 10pt;">Our operating principles define how we work together as a team. They reflect Human Interest’s unique view on what’s important and what’s right. Documenting this core aspect of our culture helps employees make good decisions on their own. It also helps candidates considering career opportunities critically evaluate whether they will thrive at Human Interest.&nbsp;</span></p> <ul> <li style="font-size: 10pt;"><span style="font-size: 10pt;"><strong>Customer obsession</strong>: We're all about creating amazing experiences for our customers. We put their needs first and go the extra mile to make them smile.</span></li> <li style="font-size: 10pt;"><span style="font-size: 10pt;"><strong>Long-Term Orientation</strong>: We're not just playing for today; we're building a legacy. We think big, plan strategically, and invest in our future.</span></li> <li style="font-size: 10pt;"><span style="font-size: 10pt;"><strong>Autonomous and Accountable Teams</strong>: We trust our team members to take ownership and make smart decisions. We empower you to be your best self!</span></li> <li style="font-size: 10pt;"><span style="font-size: 10pt;"><strong>An Escalating Bar for Talent and Performance</strong>: We're constantly raising the bar and challenging ourselves to be better. We believe in growth and continuous improvement.</span></li> <li style="font-size: 10pt;"><span style="font-size: 10pt;"><strong>Fundamental Optimism</strong>: We see the glass as half full (and then we fill it up with more amazing ideas!). We believe in the power of positivity and the potential for greatness.</span></li> </ul> <p><span style="font-size: 10pt;"><strong>Compensation</strong> - At Human Interest, there are a number of factors that are used to determine the appropriate pay range for each position. We take into account the cost of labor for each position in various markets across the U.S. The base salary for this position spans <strong>$171,000 - $207,000 </strong>and represents the minimum in our lowest geographic region to a maximum<strong> </strong>in our highest geographic region. The salary we offer to a new employee within this range is based on their location within the U.S., their relevant job-related skills, and experience. At Human Interest, base salary is one component of the overall total rewards package. Depending on the position, additional compensation components such as bonuses, commissions, and equity may be offered. All of our employees are offered a robust suite of physical, financial, and mental wellness benefits. In compliance with applicable laws, we do not inquire about salary history, or about criminal history prior to a conditional offer of employment.</span></p> <p><span style="font-size: 10pt;"><strong>Benefits</strong> -&nbsp;</span></p> <ul> <li style="font-size: 10pt;"><span style="font-size: 10pt;">A great 401(k) plan: Our own! Our 401(k) includes a dollar-for-dollar employer match up to 4% of compensation (immediately vested) and $0 plan fees</span></li> <li style="font-size: 10pt;"><span style="font-size: 10pt;">Top-of-the-line health plans, as well as dental and vision insurance</span></li> <li style="font-size: 10pt;"><span style="font-size: 10pt;">Competitive time off and parental leave</span></li> <li style="font-size: 10pt;"><span style="font-size: 10pt;">Addition Wealth:&nbsp; Unlimited access to digital tools, financial professionals, and a knowledge center to help you understand your equity and support your financial wellness</span></li> <li style="font-size: 10pt;"><span style="font-size: 10pt;">Lyra: Enhanced Mental Health Support for Employees and dependents&nbsp;&nbsp;</span></li> <li style="font-size: 10pt;"><span style="font-size: 10pt;">Carrot: Fertility healthcare and family forming benefits</span></li> <li style="font-size: 10pt;"><span style="font-size: 10pt;">Candidly:&nbsp; Student loan resource to help you and your family plan, borrow, and repay student debt</span></li> <li style="font-size: 10pt;"><span style="font-size: 10pt;">Monthly work-from-home stipend; quarterly lifestyle stipend</span></li> <li style="font-size: 10pt;"><span style="font-size: 10pt;">Engaging team-building experiences, ranging from virtual social events to team offsites, promoting collaboration and camaraderie.</span></li> </ul> <p><span style="font-size: 10pt;"><strong>We’re a great place to work</strong> (but don’t take our word for it)&nbsp;</span></p> <p><span style="font-size: 10pt;">Here’s a list of our awards and accolades:</span></p> <ul> <li style="font-size: 10pt;"><span style="font-size: 10pt;">Certified as a Great Place To Work® (2023-2025)</span></li> <li style="font-size: 10pt;"><span style="font-size: 10pt;">Fortune Best Place to Work in the Bay Area (2024)</span></li> <li style="font-size: 10pt;"><span style="font-size: 10pt;">Best Places to Work by Built In (2023-2024)</span></li> <li style="font-size: 10pt;"><span style="font-size: 10pt;">America’s Best Startup Employers by Forbes (2020-2022, 2024)&nbsp;</span></li> <li style="font-size: 10pt;"><span style="font-size: 10pt;">A Top Company by Y Combinator (2020-2023)</span></li> <li style="font-size: 10pt;"><span style="font-size: 10pt;">Inc. Fastest Growing Companies (2021)</span></li> </ul> <p><span style="font-size: 10pt;">Human Interest is an equal opportunity employer. All applicants will receive consideration for employment without regard to race, color, religion, sex, gender, gender identity or expression, sexual orientation, national origin, genetics, disability, age, veteran or military status, pregnancy, or any other characteristics protected under federal, state, or local laws. Employment with Human Interest is at-will, meaning either you or the company may terminate employment at any time, with or without cause or notice, except as otherwise provided by law or written agreement.</span></p> <p><span style="font-size: 10pt;">We are committed to making every stage of our application process fully accessible to all individuals. If you need a reasonable accommodation at any point in the process, please let us know at applicantaccommodations@humaninterest.com.</span><br><br><span style="font-size: 10pt;"><strong>Protect yourself against fraud and identity theft</strong>. Apply to our open positions directly via our careers page on Greenhouse. Human Interest will never ask applicants for their financial or banking information as part of our application process. All legitimate communication will come from a @humaninterest.com email address. If you have questions, please reach out to us directly at careers@humaninterest.com</span></p> <p><span style="font-size: 10pt;">Please note Human Interest does not accept unsolicited resumes from any source other than directly from candidates. We will not consider resumes from vendors, including and without limitation search firms, staffing agencies, fee-based referral services, and recruiting agencies.&nbsp;</span></p> <p><span style="font-size: 10pt;">Pursuant to the San Francisco Fair Chance Ordinance, we will consider employment-qualified applicants with arrest and conviction records. We comply with <strong>CCPA</strong><strong> </strong>guidelines.</span></p> <p><span style="font-size: 10pt;">See more: https://humaninterest.com/disclosures</span></p>

title: Senior Data Engineer
full_description: <p data-renderer-start-pos="1"><strong data-renderer-mark="true">About us:</strong></p> <p data-renderer-start-pos="12">Branch is on a mission to empower workers with financial freedom. We do this by helping companies accelerate payments and providing working Americans with accessible, free financial services. We’re committed to building and delivering more inclusive, transparent, and frictionless financial products.</p> <p data-renderer-start-pos="314">Our goal of empowerment extends to our own employees, too. Have a great idea? Share it today and it might just get implemented tomorrow. As a member of our team, your voice and creativity matter—and they can directly impact our products, company, and culture.&nbsp;</p> <p data-renderer-start-pos="576">We not only focus on attracting great talent from across the country, but also on building teams that help that talent thrive. That means valuing a diversity of opinions and working styles, while creating a shared belief in innovation, initiative, and winning together.</p> <p data-renderer-start-pos="847">Come join our team as we develop new ways to improve the lives of working Americans.</p> <p data-renderer-start-pos="1"><strong data-renderer-mark="true">About the role:</strong></p> <p>Branch helps millions of workers get paid faster and manage their money better. Our team is responsible for the data that drives many critical processes at Branch, including money movement, automated fraud response, and client reporting.&nbsp;</p> <p>We're looking for a Senior Data Engineer to join our Data Engineering team. In this role, you’ll work with other engineers and stakeholders to build and maintain the pipelines that power the rest of Branch. You'll drive design decisions, build reusable patterns and components other engineers adopt, and make the tradeoffs across cost, performance, and maintainability that keep our platform fast and trustworthy as we scale.</p> <p>This is a great role for an experienced data engineer who wants to own systems end-to-end and shape how the team builds going forward. Our team is small, high-trust, and values clarity, maintainability, and getting the details right.</p> <p><strong>What You’ll Do</strong></p> <ul> <li>Own end-to-end data systems and pipelines, that other teams and customers rely on</li> <li>Design and implement data integrations across multiple backend business systems, ensuring efficient and reliable data flows</li> <li>Work on streaming data processing using frameworks like Apache Beam to handle real-time data ingestion and transformation</li> <li>Build and maintain APIs to enable seamless data access and sharing across internal teams and platforms</li> <li>Manage orchestration and automation of workflows using Airflow on Kubernetes</li> <li>Define what "correct" means for the data you own, and back it with monitoring, alerting, and integration tests</li> <li>Build reusable patterns and components (hooks, plugins, alerting utilities) that are easily adoptable by other engineers and processes</li> <li>Break down ambiguous work into actionable tasks, surface edge cases and failure modes early, and clarify requirements with stakeholders</li> <li>Communicate tradeoffs, assumptions, and risks clearly in design reviews and PRs</li> </ul> <p><strong>Qualifications:</strong></p> <ul> <li>5+ years of Data Engineering experience</li> <li>3+ years of Airflow experience</li> <li>Expert in Python and SQL</li> <li>Strong working knowledge of our existing tech stack: Snowflake, Airflow, DBT, GCP</li> <li>BS in Computer Science or related field</li> </ul> <p><strong>Nice to Have</strong></p> <ul> <li>Previous work experience in Fintech, Payments, or related industries</li> <li>Experience with managing Airflow on Kubernetes</li> <li>Experience with data streaming (Apache Beam, Dataflow)</li> <li>Experience with automated ETL (Fivetran, HighTouch)</li> </ul> <p data-renderer-start-pos="1033"><strong data-renderer-mark="true">Compensation:</strong></p> <p data-renderer-start-pos="1048">The base salary range for this role is&nbsp;<span class="fabric-background-color-mark" data-renderer-mark="true" data-background-custom-color="#fedec8">$160-175k. </span>The salary range displayed reflects an average base salary range for the position across all the U.S. The base salary offered to an applicant could be higher or lower based on each applicant's specific skill set, depth of experience, relevant education or training, etc.&nbsp;</p> <p data-renderer-start-pos="1365"><strong data-renderer-mark="true">Location:</strong></p> <p data-renderer-start-pos="1376">This position is classified as REMOTE<strong data-renderer-mark="true"> </strong>within the United States of America.</p> <p data-renderer-start-pos="1452"><em data-renderer-mark="true">We are unable to hire candidates located outside of the domestic U.S.</em></p> <p data-renderer-start-pos="1523"><strong data-renderer-mark="true">Benefits:&nbsp;</strong></p> <ul> <li data-renderer-start-pos="1537">Market-leading medical, dental, and vision insurance&nbsp;</li> <li data-renderer-start-pos="1594">Stock options</li> <li data-renderer-start-pos="1611">Free Premium-Tier Origin Financial Wellness subscription</li> <li data-renderer-start-pos="1671">Monthly home-office stipend</li> <li data-renderer-start-pos="1702">401k (TransAmerica)</li> <li data-renderer-start-pos="1725">12-weeks paid parental leave for birthing and non-birthing parents</li> <li data-renderer-start-pos="1795">Flexible time off + sick and safe time</li> <li data-renderer-start-pos="1837">11 paid company holidays</li> <li data-renderer-start-pos="1837">Branch@Branch Same Day Pay Option</li> </ul> <p data-renderer-start-pos="1865"><strong data-renderer-mark="true">Working at Branch</strong></p> <p data-renderer-start-pos="1884">A remote-first company with employees located throughout the U.S., Branch emphasizes transparency, accountability, and trust to create a collaborative environment where title: Data Engineer
full_description: <div class="content-intro"><p><strong><span data-contrast="none">About SRS Acquiom&nbsp;&nbsp;</span></strong><span data-contrast="none">&nbsp;</span><span data-ccp-props="{&quot;134233117&quot;:false,&quot;134233118&quot;:false,&quot;335557856&quot;:16777215,&quot;335559738&quot;:240,&quot;335559739&quot;:240}">&nbsp;</span></p> <p>SRS Acquiom delivers the smartest way to run a deal™ through a platform and services designed to help deal parties manage complex M&amp;A and loan agency transactions more efficiently.</p> <p>Based in Denver, Colorado, with offices across the United States and in London and Amsterdam,&nbsp;SRS Acquiom supports transactions across North America and Europe. Our M&amp;A services include professional shareholder representation, paying and escrow agent services, and online document solicitation and reporting. For loan and credit transactions, we provide independent Administrative Agent, Collateral Agent, Sub-Agent, and Successor Agent services.</p> <p>Since 2007, SRS Acquiom has supported more than 11,500 transactions globally. By bringing efficiency, expertise, and purpose-built technology to complex financial transactions, we help sophisticated deal parties focus on building great businesses and maximizing value.</p> Summary</span></strong></p> <p>We are growing our Platform Engineering team and are looking to add a Data Engineer who will be able to help build our internal data platform. This data platform is central to ensuring that our software development and data analytics teams have governed access to the data they need. As a Data Engineer on the Platform Team, you will be a part of driving the design, development, and implementation of our data platforms. Your responsibilities include partnering with the architect and engineering teams in developing our platform, as well as providing best practices and approaches to ensure security, resiliency, and availability of solutions. You will be challenged to create strategies that ensure the secure consumption of the platform through continuous innovation, simplification, and self-service automation.</p> <p><strong>Location:&nbsp;</strong>This position is fully remote within the Continental United States. In-person visits to the Denver headquarters are required, on average three times per year.&nbsp;</p> <p><strong>Compensation: </strong>The salary range for this position is between $125k and $140k, depending on experience level.</p> <p><strong><span data-contrast="none">Primary Responsibilities&nbsp;&nbsp;</span></strong><span data-contrast="none">&nbsp;</span><span all initiatives keeping all compliance, security and regulatory needs satisfied</li> <li>Be a thought leader by staying abreast of current and emerging technologies and industry trends.</li> <li>Work with your peers to design and build data platform capabilities and incorporate the necessary automations and tool configurations that ensure agile delivery and secure consumption</li> <li>Design and implement processes and tools that enable product engineering and BI/Analytics teams to consume an extensible and scalable data platform, which enforces all needed governance and consistent operational models</li> <li>Be a trusted advisor for initiatives by providing objective, practical, and relevant ideas, insights, and advice while also building organizational partnerships and networks to ensure comprehensive capabilities are developed with input from appropriate business and Engineering resources</li> <li>Directly support the use and delivery of data platform services in the organization</li> <li>Ensure that all data platform solutions follow established security and compliance controls</li> <li>Maintain existing and future software services code and configuration</li> <li>Make recommendations for improvements to existing architecture</li> <li>Help implement new technologies for future deployment</li> <li>Provide technical guidance, knowledge transfers, and mentorship to clients on their data platform adoption</li> <li>Help implement and improve the development of data pipelines</li> <li>Other duties as assigned</li> </ul> <p><span data-contrast="none">&nbsp;</span><strong><span data-contrast="none">Required Qualifications &amp; Skills</span></strong><span data-contrast="none">&nbsp;</span><span Computer Science or equivalent experience</li> <li>4+ years of experience with and strong working knowledge of SQL required, and understanding of data models</li> <li>4+ years of experience with or strong knowledge of Postgresql, MySQL, or other relational database design and programming: stored procedures, functions, PL/pgSQL</li> <li>Experience with or working knowledge of managing AWS Data Infrastructure: S3, RDS, Managed Streaming Kafka, Lake Formation, Glue, Glue Data Catalog, Athena, Quicksight, DataZone</li> <li>Experience with systems engineering and/or software development: Java, Javascript, Python, PHP</li> <li>Experience with modern API platform design, security practices, and data architectures: event-driven architecture, change data capture, pub/sub</li> </ul> <p><strong>Preferred <span data-contrast="none">Qualifications &amp; Skills</span><span data-contrast="none">&nbsp;</span><span integrations from third-party platforms: NetSuite, Salesforce, Workday, Pendo</li> <li>Experience with reporting and analytic tools: Grafana, Kibana, Elasticsearch, Open Telemetry</li> <li>Experience with ETL processes, projects, and tooling: Boomi</li> <li>Understanding of systems hardening and secure systems configuration</li> <li>Experience with regulatory compliance standards: HIPAA, PCI, SEC, Summary</span></strong></p> <p>We are growing our Platform Engineering team and are looking to add a Senior Data Engineer who will be able to help build our internal data platform. This data platform is central to ensuring that our software development and data analytics teams have governed access to the data they need. As a Senior Data Engineer on the Platform Team, you will be a part of driving the design, development, and implementation of our data platforms. Your responsibilities include partnering with the architect and engineering teams in developing our platform, as well as providing best practices and approaches to ensure security, resiliency, and availability of solutions. You will be challenged to create strategies that ensure the secure consumption of the platform through continuous innovation, simplification, and self-service automation.</p> <p><strong>Location:&nbsp;</strong>This position is fully remote within the Continental United States. In-person visits to the Denver headquarters are required, on average three times per year.&nbsp;</p> <p><strong>Compensation: </strong>The salary range for this position is between $145k and $160k, depending on experience level.</p> <p><strong><span data-contrast="none">Primary Responsibilities&nbsp;&nbsp;</span></strong><span data-contrast="none">&nbsp;</span><span data-ccp-props="{&quot;134233117&quot;:false,&quot;134233118&quot;:false,&quot;335557856&quot;:16777215,&quot;335559738&quot;:240,&quot;335559739&quot;:240}">&nbsp;</span></p> <ul> <li>Participate in all initiatives keeping all compliance, security and regulatory needs satisfied</li> <li>Be a thought leader by staying abreast of current and emerging technologies and industry trends.</li> <li>Work with your peers to design and build data platform capabilities and incorporate the necessary automations and tool configurations that ensure agile delivery and secure consumption</li> <li>Design and implement processes and tools that enable product engineering and BI/Analytics teams to consume an extensible and scalable data platform, which enforces all needed governance and consistent operational models</li> <li>Be a trusted advisor for initiatives by providing objective, practical, and relevant ideas, insights, and advice while also building organizational partnerships and networks to ensure comprehensive capabilities are developed with input from appropriate business and Engineering resources</li> <li>Directly support the use and delivery of data platform services in the organization</li> <li>Ensure that all data platform solutions follow established security and compliance controls</li> <li>Maintain existing and future software services code and configuration</li> <li>Make recommendations for improvements to existing architecture</li> <li>Help implement new technologies for future deployment</li> <li>Provide technical guidance, knowledge transfers, and mentorship to clients on their data platform adoption</li> <li>Help implement and improve the development of data pipelines</li> <li>Other duties as assigned</li> </ul> <p><span data-contrast="none">&nbsp;</span><strong><span data-contrast="none">Required Qualifications &amp; Skills</span></strong><span data-contrast="none">&nbsp;</span><span data-ccp-props="{&quot;134233117&quot;:false,&quot;134233118&quot;:false,&quot;335557856&quot;:16777215,&quot;335559738&quot;:240,&quot;335559739&quot;:240}">&nbsp;</span></p> <ul> <li>B.S. in Computer Science or equivalent experience, 6+ years of experience with and strong working knowledge of SQL required, and understanding of data models is preferred</li> <li>6+ years of experience with or strong knowledge of Postgresql, MySQL, or other relational database design and programming: stored procedures, functions, PL/pgSQL</li> <li>Experience with or working knowledge of managing AWS Data Infrastructure: S3, RDS, Managed Streaming Kafka, Lake Formation, Glue, Glue Data Catalog, Athena, Quicksight, DataZone</li> <li>Experience with systems engineering and/or software development: Java, Javascript, Python</li> <li>Experience with modern API platform design, security practices, and data architectures: event-driven architecture, change data capture, pub/sub</li> </ul> <p><strong>Preferred <span data-contrast="none">Qualifications &amp; Skills</span><span data-contrast="none">&nbsp;</span><span data-ccp-props="{&quot;134233117&quot;:false,&quot;134233118&quot;:false,&quot;335557856&quot;:16777215,&quot;335559738&quot;:240,&quot;335559739&quot;:240}">&nbsp;</span></strong></p> <ul> <li>Experience with cloud solution design patterns: CI/CD, Docker, Kubernetes, containers, microservices, distributed caching</li> <li>Experience with Terraform scripting</li> <li>Experience with data integrations from third-party platforms: NetSuite, Salesforce, Workday, Pendo</li> <li>Experience with reporting and analytic tools: Grafana, Kibana, Elasticsearch, Open Telemetry</li> <li>Experience with ETL processes, projects, and tooling: Boomi</li> <li>Understanding of systems hardening and secure systems configuration</li> <li>Experience with regulatory compliance standards: HIPAA, PCI, SEC, FINRA</li> <li>Excellent written and verbal communication skills, with the ability to present complex technical information in a clear and concise manner to a variety of audiences</li> <li>Understanding of Agile/Kanban methodologies</li> </ul> <p><strong><span data-contrast="none">Desired Characteristics&nbsp; </span></strong><span data-contrast="none">&nbsp;</span><span data-ccp-props="{&quot;134233117&quot;:false,&quot;134233118&quot;:false,&quot;335557856&quot;:16777215,&quot;335559738&quot;:240,&quot;335559739&quot;:240}">&nbsp;</span></p> <ul> <li><span data-ccp-props="{&quot;134233117&quot;:false,&quot;134233118&quot;:false,&quot;335557856&quot;:16777215,&quot;335559738&quot;:240,&quot;335559739&quot;:240}">Self-motivated</span></li> <li><span data-ccp-props="{&quot;134233117&quot;:false,&quot;134233118&quot;:false,&quot;335557856&quot;:16777215,&quot;335559738&quot;:240,&quot;335559739&quot;:240}">Intellectually curious</span></li> <li><span data-ccp-props="{&quot;134233117&quot;:false,&quot;134233118&quot;:false,&quot;335557856&quot;:16777215,&quot;335559738&quot;:240,&quot;335559739&quot;:240}">Amiable</span></li> <li><span Duties, responsibilities, and activities may change, or new ones may be assigned at any time with or without advanced notice.&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;</p> <p>With respect to its programs, services, activities, and employment practices, SRS Acquiom Inc. assesses qualified individuals without regard to their race, color, religion, sex (including pregnancy, sexual orientation, or gender identity), age, national origin, disability, veteran status, genetic information, or other protected status.&nbsp; Requests for reasonable accommodation or the provision of auxiliary aids should be directed to Human Resources.</p> <h5><span data-ccp-props="{}">&nbsp;</span><sup> </sup></h5>

title: Staff Data Engineer
full_description: <div class="content-intro"><p><strong>Exactera </strong>has offices in<strong> New York City, Tarrytown NY, San Diego, CA, London,&nbsp;</strong>and <strong>Argentina.&nbsp;</strong></p></div><p>&nbsp;</p> <h3><strong>The Role</strong></h3> <p>As Staff Data Engineer, you will provide senior onshore technical leadership for the data engineering team. You will own a defined slice of our centralized Databricks data platform with full accountability for decisions and delivery, serve as a technical counterpart to the Principal Data Platform Engineer, and drive architectural judgment and independent problem-solving as platform complexity scales post-migration.</p> <p>This is a hands-on data engineering role focused on building and maintaining production pipelines, exercising architectural judgment on data modeling and pipeline design, and serving as the onshore escalation point and institutional knowledge backup for platform decisions.</p> <p>&nbsp;</p> <h3><strong>The Business Challenge</strong></h3> <p>We operate multiple product lines (Transfer Pricing, R&amp;D Services, RoyaltyStat, Provisioning), each with distinct databases containing enterprise financial data—journal entries, general ledgers, and financial statements. Our immediate challenge is migrating multi-terabyte datasets from legacy systems to a unified Databricks lakehouse while establishing governance patterns that enable multi-product operations at scale. As the platform matures, the data engineering team needs senior onshore technical presence to drive architecture ownership and maintain platform quality.</p> <p>&nbsp;</p> <h3><strong>What You'll Build</strong></h3> <ul> <li>Production Data Pipelines: Build and maintain production data pipelines within the patterns and governance established by the Lead Data Platform Engineer, ensuring reliability and performance at multi-terabyte scale.</li> <li>Data Modeling &amp; Architecture: Exercise architectural judgment on data modeling, pipeline design, and platform usage—translating complex business requirements into scalable data solutions across our product portfolio.</li> <li>Stakeholder Engagement: Engage proactively with product and engineering stakeholders to translate requirements into data solutions, serving as the primary onshore technical point of contact for data engineering needs.</li> <li>Platform Quality: Drive platform quality through code reviews, testing practices, and engineering standards that ensure the team delivers reliable, maintainable data infrastructure.</li> <li>Knowledge &amp; Continuity: Serve as onshore escalation point and institutional knowledge backup for platform decisions, reducing single-point-of-failure risk and building onshore technical depth as the platform scales.</li> </ul> <p>&nbsp;</p> <h3><strong>Business Problems You'll Solve</strong></h3> <ul> <li>Multi-Product Data Delivery: Implement data pipelines that serve multiple product lines (Transfer Pricing, R&amp;D Services, RoyaltyStat, Provisioning) with distinct data requirements, ensuring each product gets the data it needs reliably and on schedule.</li> <li>Legacy Migration Execution: Lead pipeline implementation for migrating multi-terabyte datasets from legacy systems to Databricks, working within the architecture defined by the Lead Data Platform Engineer.</li> <li>Onshore Technical Leadership: Provide the senior judgment layer the current nearshore team cannot—owning problems end-to-end, making independent architectural decisions, and mentoring engineers to raise the quality bar across the team.</li> <li>Cross-Team Coordination: Bridge the gap between product teams and data infrastructure, translating business requirements into data solutions and ensuring the data platform delivers on product commitments.</li> </ul> <p>&nbsp;</p> <h3><strong>Required Experience</strong></h3> <p><strong>Core Data Engineering</strong></p> <ul> <li>SQL, Python, and PySpark—production pipeline implementation and performance optimization</li> <li>Databricks experience—Delta Lake, Workflows, and Databricks SQL; Unity Catalog familiarity preferred</li> <li>5+ years in data engineering with demonstrated ability to own problems end-to-end without close direction</li> <li>Experience building and maintaining ETL/ELT pipelines at scale, including error handling, monitoring, and data quality validation</li> <li>Strong data modeling skills across structured and semi-structured data sources</li> </ul> <p>&nbsp;</p> <p><strong>Platform &amp; Infrastructure</strong></p> <ul> <li>AWS experience (S3, IAM, VPC) with ability to collaborate on infrastructure decisions</li> <li>Infrastructure-as-code experience (Terraform preferred)</li> <li>Familiarity with data governance patterns (Unity Catalog, data lineage, access controls)</li> </ul> <p>&nbsp;</p> <p><strong>Technical Leadership</strong></p> <ul> <li>Demonstrated ability to exercise independent architectural judgment—not just ticket execution</li> <li>Experience mentoring or guiding junior and mid-level data engineers</li> <li>Strong written and verbal communication—able to document architecture decisions and engage directly with both technical and business stakeholders</li> <li>Onshore (US-based)—role requires timezone overlap, async-light communication, and direct stakeholder engagement</li> </ul> <p>&nbsp;</p> <p><strong>Preferred But Not Required</strong></p> <ul> <li>Experience with financial data, accounting systems (NetSuite), or enterprise ERP platforms</li> <li>Background building pipelines that serve AI/ML workloads (preparing data for downstream ML consumption, RAG, and LLMs)</li> <li>Familiarity with data governance frameworks and compliance requirements for regulated industries</li> <li>Experience working alongside or transitioning from nearshore engineering teams</li> </ul><div class="content-conclusion"><h3>What We Offer:<br><em>(The following only applies to US-based positions)</em></h3> <ul> <li> <div>A collaborative team culture with opportunities for career development.&nbsp;</div> </li> <li> <div>Ample opportunities to be recognized, build valuable skills, and grow your career.&nbsp;</div> </li> <li> <div>Generous vacation policy, including paid parental leave.&nbsp;</div> </li> <li> <div>Comprehensive health plans with FSA and HSA options.&nbsp;</div> </li> <li> <div>401(k) retirement plan.&nbsp;</div> </li> <li> <div>Life and disability insurance coverage.&nbsp;</div> </li> <li> <div>Supplemental benefits like a dependent care savings plan, pet insurance, will preparation, and an employee assistance program.&nbsp;</div> </li> </ul> <p>&nbsp;</p> <h3>About Us:</h3> <div data-olk-copy-source="MessageBody">At Exactera, a FinTech SaaS start-up founded in 2016, we stand at the intersection of human and machine intelligence. Our corporate tax solutions are powered by AI and cloud-based technologies, serving customers worldwide. With over $100 million in funding from Savant Venture Fund and Insight Partners, we are poised for growth. We are committed to diversity, inclusion, and equal opportunities for all.&nbsp;</div> <p>&nbsp;</p></div>

title: Data Engineer (Databricks)
full_description: <div>At Livefront, we help companies design and build world-class digital products that command attention and inspire joy. We’ve helped household names like CVS, Samsung, General Mills, and Optum create experiences that have reached millions of people, and startups like HomeSpotter and Credly build entirely new businesses that challenge their industries’ status quo.</div> <div>&nbsp;</div> <div>We're looking for an outstanding Databricks Data Engineer to join our growing data practice — someone who builds the data and AI foundations that digital products and intelligent experiences run on. This is a senior-level position with the opportunity to work remotely in<strong>&nbsp;</strong>Peru.</div> <div> <div class="section page-centered" data-qa="job-description"> <div>&nbsp;</div> <h2><strong>Who you are</strong></h2> <p>You are a Databricks-focused Data Engineer who understands that great data platforms are only as valuable as the products, AI workflows, and experiences they enable. You bring deep, production-grade expertise across the Databricks platform and know how to connect platform capabilities to real business outcomes.</p> <p>You thrive in ambiguity and can quickly assess a client's data landscape to recommend and implement the right solutions. You understand that in consulting, your Databricks depth is most valuable when it connects platform capabilities to the products and experiences clients actually use — and you're as comfortable in a product design conversation as you are building a DLT pipeline.</p> <p>You excel at translating complex data challenges into clear technical requirements and can confidently navigate conversations with everyone from data scientists to executives. Your engineering principles are mature and grounded in real-world experience across various industries and scales.</p> <p>You have an interest in and a curiosity about data platforms and the latest advances in data technology.</p> <h2><strong>What you will be doing</strong></h2> <ul> <li>Design and build production data pipelines using Lakeflow Declarative Pipelines, Autoloader, and Structured Streaming, with end-to-end ownership of ingestion, transformation, data quality expectations, and CI/CD deployment via Declarative Automation Bundles.</li> <li>Architect and implement Lakehouse solutions on Databricks — medallion architecture, Delta Lake, Unity Catalog — tailored to the client's analytics, AI, and application needs.</li> <li>Build and maintain Databricks transformation layers — DLT pipelines, PySpark notebooks, and dbt — with data quality constraints and SLAs baked in.</li> <li>Design and maintain the data and AI foundations — Unity Catalog, Feature Store, MLflow, and Model Serving — that power production ML, agent workflows, and AI-enabled digital products.</li> <li>Collaborate with product and backend engineers to design data models, APIs, and application data contracts — ensuring the platform serves the product, not just the warehouse.</li> <li>Consult with clients to understand their data challenges, develop data strategies, and implement sustainable solutions.</li> <li>Adapt your approach based on project needs — sometimes leading data architecture discussions with clients, other times supporting internal teams with specialized data expertise.</li> <li>Work within multi-cloud environments — primarily AWS and Azure — anchoring data platform recommendations around Databricks where it fits the client's architecture and goals.</li> <li>Champion data governance through Unity Catalog — access control, lineage, data quality policies, and compliance — as a first-class part of every engagement, not an afterthought.</li> <li>Design data-to-application architectures — including Lakebase-backed services and Databricks Apps — that connect governed data to AI workflows, digital products, and user-facing experiences.</li> <li>Help build Livefront's Databricks practice — contributing to accelerators, internal enablement, certification goals, and Databricks partner go-to-market materials alongside delivery work.</li> </ul> <h2><strong>Why you should apply</strong></h2> <ul> <li>You want to work with passionate and talented people who are always looking for ways to make things better.</li> <li>You desire a work environment where respect, mutual trust, and egoless collaboration are paramount.</li> <li>You want colleagues who take their work seriously but not themselves, and who know how to let loose and have a good time.</li> <li>You like being part of a team with a reputation for excellence that gives back to the community by educating, mentoring, and sponsoring.</li> <li>You want to work on products and accounts that have outsized impact and reach.</li> <li>You believe in sweating the details, giving a damn about quality, and taking pride in going the extra mile.</li> <li>You want to help build a data practice specialization from the ground up — shaping how we go to market with Databricks, what we build as accelerators, and what it means to do this kind of work at a digital product company.</li> </ul> <h2><strong>What you bring to the table</strong></h2> <ul> <li>3-5 years of data engineering experience with at least 2 years in production Databricks environments, preferably in a consulting or client delivery context.</li> <li>Solid working knowledge of AWS and Azure cloud services relevant to Databricks deployments — storage, networking, IAM, and compute — with GCP familiarity a plus.</li> <li>Deep, production-grade Databricks expertise: Lakeflow Declarative Pipelines, Autoloader, Structured Streaming, Lakeflow Jobs, Unity Catalog (including fine-grained access control and lineage) — demonstrated through shipped production workloads, not prototypes.</li> <li>Proven experience designing Lakehouse architectures — medallion patterns, Delta Lake table design, partitioning, Z-ordering, and query optimization — at production scale.</li> <li>Hands-on experience with data pipeline testing, observability, and CI/CD for data — including unit testing, data quality frameworks, and version-controlled deployments via Git and Declarative Automation Bundles.</li> <li>Strong proficiency in SQL and Python, with the ability to write clean, performant, and maintainable code.</li> <li>Understanding of data modeling, schema design, and query optimization.</li> <li>Excellent communication skills with the ability to explain complex data concepts to both technical and non-technical stakeholders.</li> <li>Strong problem-solving skills with the ability to navigate ambiguous requirements and deliver pragmatic solutions.</li> <li>Above-average discipline and personal organization skills.</li> <li>Obvious comfort with critique and peer review in the context of an iterative development process.</li> <li>A demonstrated hunger for personal and professional growth.</li> <li>A self-evident love and care for the craft of data engineering.</li> </ul> <h2><strong>Bonus points if you…</strong></h2> <ul> <li>Have worked with real-time streaming technologies (Kafka, Kinesis, etc.).</li> <li>Have hands-on experience with alternative cloud data platforms — useful context for migrations and competitive assessments, though Databricks is our primary platform focus.</li> <li>Have experience in healthcare or fintech domains.</li> <li>Have hands-on experience with MLOps or LLMOps on Databricks — MLflow experiment tracking, model registry, Model Serving endpoints, or Vector Search for RAG pipelines.</li> <li>Have experience with Java, Go, or Scala.</li> <li>Have strong illustration skills for technical diagramming and data architecture documentation.</li> <li>Speak, write, and/or educate publicly about data engineering topics.</li> <li>Have contributed to open-source data projects.</li> <li>Hold or are actively pursuing a Databricks certification (Data Engineer Associate or Professional, or Apache Spark Developer) — we treat these as meaningful signals of platform depth, and they directly support our Databricks partner growth goals.</li> <li>Have experience with Databricks Apps, or Lakebase — early familiarity with where the Databricks platform is heading is a strong differentiator.</li> </ul> <h2>What to expect</h2> </div> <div class="section page-centered" data-qa="closing-description"> <div>When applying, please include a short note about yourself, a summary of your work experience, and a link to any public profiles you actively maintain (e.g., GitHub, LinkedIn, etc).</div> <div>&nbsp;</div> <div>Our hiring process moves quickly and consists of several stages for candidates who capture our attention with their initial submission, sometimes including but not limited to a short preliminary phone interview, a series of video interviews, and a short take-home exercise, which you'll have up to a week to complete.</div> <div>&nbsp;</div> <h2>Additional information</h2> <div>We go out of our way to evaluate all employees and job applicants equally based on merit, competence, and qualifications. We encourage candidates from all backgrounds to apply and consider all qualified applicants. Don't worry, every application will be reviewed by a human.</div> </div> </div>

title: Data Engineer (Databricks)
full_description: <div>At Livefront, we help companies design and build world-class digital products that command attention and inspire joy. We’ve helped household names like CVS, Samsung, General Mills, and Optum create experiences that have reached millions of people, and startups like HomeSpotter and Credly build entirely new businesses that challenge their industries’ status quo.</div> <div>&nbsp;</div> <div>We're looking for an outstanding Databricks Data Engineer to join our growing data practice — someone who builds the data and AI foundations that digital products and intelligent experiences run on.&nbsp;</div> <div> <div class="section page-centered" data-qa="job-description"> <div>&nbsp;</div> <h2><strong>Who you are</strong></h2> <p>You are a Databricks-focused Data Engineer who understands that great data platforms are only as valuable as the products, AI workflows, and experiences they enable. You bring deep, production-grade expertise across the Databricks platform and know how to connect platform capabilities to real business outcomes.</p> <p>You thrive in ambiguity and can quickly assess a client's data landscape to recommend and implement the right solutions. You understand that in consulting, your Databricks depth is most valuable when it connects platform capabilities to the products and experiences clients actually use — and you're as comfortable in a product design conversation as you are building a DLT pipeline.</p> <p>You excel at translating complex data challenges into clear technical requirements and can confidently navigate conversations with everyone from data scientists to executives. Your engineering principles are mature and grounded in real-world experience across various industries and scales.</p> <p>You have an interest in and a curiosity about data platforms and the latest advances in data technology.</p> <h2><strong>What you will be doing</strong></h2> <ul> <li>Design and build production data pipelines using Lakeflow Declarative Pipelines, Autoloader, and Structured Streaming, with end-to-end ownership of ingestion, transformation, data quality expectations, and CI/CD deployment via Declarative Automation Bundles.</li> <li>Architect and implement Lakehouse solutions on Databricks — medallion architecture, Delta Lake, Unity Catalog — tailored to the client's analytics, AI, and application needs.</li> <li>Build and maintain Databricks transformation layers — DLT pipelines, PySpark notebooks, and dbt — with data quality constraints and SLAs baked in.</li> <li>Design and maintain the data and AI foundations — Unity Catalog, Feature Store, MLflow, and Model Serving — that power production ML, agent workflows, and AI-enabled digital products.</li> <li>Collaborate with product and backend engineers to design data models, APIs, and application data contracts — ensuring the platform serves the product, not just the warehouse.</li> <li>Consult with clients to understand their data challenges, develop data strategies, and implement sustainable solutions.</li> <li>Adapt your approach based on project needs — sometimes leading data architecture discussions with clients, other times supporting internal teams with specialized data expertise.</li> <li>Work within multi-cloud environments — primarily AWS and Azure — anchoring data platform recommendations around Databricks where it fits the client's architecture and goals.</li> <li>Champion data governance through Unity Catalog — access control, lineage, data quality policies, and compliance — as a first-class part of every engagement, not an afterthought.</li> <li>Design data-to-application architectures — including Lakebase-backed services and Databricks Apps — that connect governed data to AI workflows, digital products, and user-facing experiences.</li> <li>Help build Livefront's Databricks practice — contributing to accelerators, internal enablement, certification goals, and Databricks partner go-to-market materials alongside delivery work.</li> </ul> <h2><strong>Why you should apply</strong></h2> <ul> <li>You want to work with passionate and talented people who are always looking for ways to make things better.</li> <li>You desire a work environment where respect, mutual trust, and egoless collaboration are paramount.</li> <li>You want colleagues who take their work seriously but not themselves, and who know how to let loose and have a good time.</li> <li>You like being part of a team with a reputation for excellence that gives back to the community by educating, mentoring, and sponsoring.</li> <li>You want to work on products and accounts that have outsized impact and reach.</li> <li>You believe in sweating the details, giving a damn about quality, and taking pride in going the extra mile.</li> <li>You want to help build a data practice specialization from the ground up — shaping how we go to market with Databricks, what we build as accelerators, and what it means to do this kind of work at a digital product company.</li> </ul> <h2><strong>What you bring to the table</strong></h2> <ul> <li>3-5 years of data engineering experience with at least 2 years in production Databricks environments, preferably in a consulting or client delivery context.</li> <li>Solid working knowledge of AWS and Azure cloud services relevant to Databricks deployments — storage, networking, IAM, and compute — with GCP familiarity a plus.</li> <li>Deep, production-grade Databricks expertise: Lakeflow Declarative Pipelines, Autoloader, Structured Streaming, Lakeflow Jobs, Unity Catalog (including fine-grained access control and lineage) — demonstrated through shipped production workloads, not prototypes.</li> <li>Proven experience designing Lakehouse architectures — medallion patterns, Delta Lake table design, partitioning, Z-ordering, and query optimization — at production scale.</li> <li>Hands-on experience with data pipeline testing, observability, and CI/CD for data — including unit testing, data quality frameworks, and version-controlled deployments via Git and Declarative Automation Bundles.</li> <li>Strong proficiency in SQL and Python, with the ability to write clean, performant, and maintainable code.</li> <li>Understanding of data modeling, schema design, and query optimization.</li> <li>Excellent communication skills with the ability to explain complex data concepts to both technical and non-technical stakeholders.</li> <li>Strong problem-solving skills with the ability to navigate ambiguous requirements and deliver pragmatic solutions.</li> <li>Above-average discipline and personal organization skills.</li> <li>Obvious comfort with critique and peer review in the context of an iterative development process.</li> <li>A demonstrated hunger for personal and professional growth.</li> <li>A self-evident love and care for the craft of data engineering.</li> </ul> <h2><strong>Bonus points if you…</strong></h2> <ul> <li>Have worked with real-time streaming technologies (Kafka, Kinesis, etc.).</li> <li>Have hands-on experience with alternative cloud data platforms — useful context for migrations and competitive assessments, though Databricks is our primary platform focus.</li> <li>Have experience in healthcare or fintech domains.</li> <li>Have hands-on experience with MLOps or LLMOps on Databricks — MLflow experiment tracking, model registry, Model Serving endpoints, or Vector Search for RAG pipelines.</li> <li>Have experience with Java, Go, or Scala.</li> <li>Have strong illustration skills for technical diagramming and data architecture documentation.</li> <li>Speak, write, and/or educate publicly about data engineering topics.</li> <li>Have contributed to open-source data projects.</li> <li>Hold or are actively pursuing a Databricks certification (Data Engineer Associate or Professional, or Apache Spark Developer) — we treat these as meaningful signals of platform depth, and they directly support our Databricks partner growth goals.</li> <li>Have experience with Databricks Apps, or Lakebase — early familiarity with where the Databricks platform is heading is a strong differentiator.</li> </ul> <h2>What to expect</h2> </div> <div class="section page-centered" data-qa="closing-description"> <div>When applying, please include a short note about yourself, a summary of your work experience, and a link to any public profiles you actively maintain (e.g., GitHub, LinkedIn, etc).</div> <div>&nbsp;</div> <div>Our hiring process moves quickly and consists of several stages for candidates who capture our attention with their initial submission, sometimes including but not limited to a short preliminary phone interview, a series of video interviews, and a short take-home exercise, which you'll have up to a week to complete.</div> <div>&nbsp;</div> <div>Compensation is $120,000 - $145,000</div> <div>&nbsp;</div> <h2>Additional information</h2> <div>We go out of our way to evaluate all employees and job applicants equally based on merit, competence, and qualifications. We encourage candidates from all backgrounds to apply and consider all qualified applicants. Don't worry, every application will be reviewed by a human.</div> </div> </div>

title: Associate Forward Deployed Product Manager
full_description: <div class="content-intro"><p>Cresta unlocks the true potential of the customer experience, turning every conversation into a competitive advantage. Cresta’s unified AI platform combines conversational AI agents, real-time human agent augmentation, and comprehensive conversation intelligence to drive revenue and efficiency gains across every channel. The world’s leading companies, including United Airlines, Cox Communications, and Marriott, use Cresta to power world-class customer experiences every day.&nbsp;</p> <p>Born from the Stanford AI Lab, Cresta has raised more than $270 million from the world’s leading investors, including a16z, Greylock, and Sequoia. Cresta’s leadership includes some of the leading minds in AI today. Our CEO, <a href="https://www.linkedin.com/in/pingwu/">Ping Wu</a>, founded and led Google's Contact Center AI and Vertex AI platforms before joining Cresta to build the future of AI-driven customer experiences.</p> <p>Over the next few years, AI is going to redefine how people all over the world interact with businesses every day. Come build that future at Cresta.</p></div><div>&nbsp;</div> <p>Cresta is on a mission to turn every customer conversation into a competitive advantage. Our unified platform combines both AI and human intelligence to enable 1. automating conversations with AI Agents and 2. transform their contact centers to discover customer insights.</p> <p>Born from the Stanford AI lab, Cresta's co-founder and chairman is<a href="https://www.linkedin.com/in/sebastian-thrun-59a0b273/"> Sebastian Thrun</a>, the genius behind Google X, Waymo, and Udacity. Our leadership includes CEO,<a href="https://www.linkedin.com/in/pingwu/"> Ping Wu</a>, the co-founder of Google Contact Center AI &amp; Vertex AI platform,<a href="https://www.linkedin.com/in/tianlinshi/"> and co-founder, Tim Shi</a>, an early member of Open AI in 2017. Our investors include Andreessen Horowitz, Greylock Partners, Sequoia, and former AT&amp;T CEO John Donovan.&nbsp;</p> <p>Our valued customers include brands like Airbnb, United Airlines, Hilton, Porsche, United Healthcare, Intuit, Cox Communications, and Carmax and we’ve been recognized by Forbes, Forrestor, and Bain Consulting as one of the top private AI companies in the world.</p> <p>&nbsp;</p> <p><strong>About the Role:</strong></p> <p>As an Associate Forward-Deployed Product Manager on the AI Agents team, you will create, deploy, and optimize AI Agents that transform our customers’ businesses and delight their customers. This customer-facing role blends product thinking, prompt engineering, data-driven insights, and project leadership with Strategic &amp; Fortune 500 companies.</p> <p>In this role, you’ll collaborate with senior Forward-deployed Product Managers and Forward-deployed Engineers (FDEs) to support the build of AI Agent products.&nbsp;</p> <p>What You’ll Do:</p> <ul> <li>Drive AI Agent deployments for customers across the lifecycle — from pre-sales to post-launch optimization</li> <li>Define AI roadmaps for the customer’s executive team by blending both quantitative analysis and qualitative insights; leverage automated conversation discovery tools in addition to traditional data analysis</li> <li>Iterate prompt &amp; context engineering to achieve customer’s use case and intended business outcomes</li> <li>Own designing and running evaluations and A/B tests of AI Agents with Forward-Deployed Engineers (FDEs) using Cresta’s agent builder and beta internal tools</li> <li>Improve our playbooks for agent development and customer deployment</li> </ul> <p>Why join as an Associate FDPM at Cresta?&nbsp;</p> <ul> <li>Differentiated career development:</li> <ul> <li>Get hands-on AI product experience building Agentic solutions using a cutting-edge tech stack</li> <li>Access to an accelerated timeline of customer-facing opportunities as an AI thought partner to executives &amp; leadership</li> </ul> <li>Rotational opportunities:</li> <ul> <li>Launching solutions for mid-market to Fortune 500 companies across different verticals, gaining breadth &amp; depth in industry knowledge and domain expertise</li> <li>Develop multiple functional strengths as a super-generalist: work on projects that give experience across multiple teams, including go-to-market, engineering, product, customer success, and more</li> </ul> <li>Investment &amp; mentorship:</li> <ul> <li>Mentorship from PMs who have been building GenAI solutions since GPT-2, as well as from Microsoft, TikTok, consulting firms, and start-ups</li> <li>Launching pad for your learning and career, we take that seriously as a company in scoping opportunities</li> </ul> </ul> <p><strong>What We’re Looking For:</strong></p> <p>You are a pragmatic, results-oriented problem solver who thrives in fast-paced environments and excels at turning ambiguity into execution.</p> <p>What you’ll bring:</p> <ul> <li>2+ years of relevant experience (e.g., Product Management, Consulting, Implementations, Customer / Sales Engineer, Revenue &amp; Sales Analyst, Investment Banking, Founder)</li> <li>Hands-on work style – curiosity with a bias for action. You deliver with urgency and care</li> <li>Comfortable working with ambiguity and learning quickly in a dynamic startup environment</li> <li>Willingness to travel</li> </ul> <p>Bonus Points:</p> <ul> <li>Previous exposure via clients or projects to the travel &amp; hospitality, healthcare, insurance, and financial industry domains</li> <li>Experience in a startup or high-growth SaaS environment</li> <li>Experience working closely with AI or LLM-first products</li> <li>Technical understanding, systems thinking and exposure to building Agents (not necessarily conversational, personal projects count!)</li> </ul> <p><strong>Perks &amp; Benefits:</strong></p> <p>We offer a comprehensive and people-first benefits package to support you at work and in life:</p> <ul> <li>Comprehensive medical, dental, and vision coverage with plans to fit you and your family</li> <li>Flexible PTO to take the time you need, when you need it</li> <li>Paid parental leave for all new parents welcoming a new child</li> <li>Retirement savings plan to help you plan for the future</li> <li>Remote work setup budget to help you create a productive home office</li> <li>Monthly wellness and communication stipend to keep you connected and balanced</li> <li>In-office meal program and commuter benefits provided for onsite employees</li> </ul> <p><strong>Compensation at Cresta&nbsp;</strong></p> <p>Cresta’s approach to compensation is simple: recognize impact, reward excellence, and invest in our people. We offer competitive, location-based pay that reflects the market and what each individual brings to the table.</p> <p>The posted base salary range represents what we expect to pay for this role in a given location. Final offers are shaped by factors like experience, skills, education, and geography. In addition to base pay, total compensation includes equity and a comprehensive benefits package for you and your family.</p> <p><strong>OTE Range</strong>: $130,000–$170,000 + Offers Equity</p> <p>We have noticed a rise in recruiting impersonations across the industry, where scammers attempt to access candidates' personal and financial information through fake interviews and offers. All Cresta recruiting email communications will always come from the @cresta.ai domain. Any outreach claiming to be from Cresta via other sources should be ignored.&nbsp; If you are uncertain whether you have been contacted by an official Cresta employee, reach out to recruiting@cresta.ai</p>

The future of work is here, and it's at Cresta.</p> <h4>About the role:</h4> <p>At Cresta, the AI Agent team is on a mission to create state-of-the-art AI Agents that solve practical problems for our customers. We are focused on leveraging the latest technologies in Large Language Models (LLMs) and AI Agent systems, while ensuring that the solutions we develop are cost-effective, secure, and data-end="1395">Support performance tuning and troubleshooting of AI agent prototypes.</li> <li data-start="1278" data-end="1395">Work with customers and internal engineering teams to understand requirements and iterate on solutions.</li> <li data-start="1278" data-end="1395">Participate in team demos, feedback sessions, and learning opportunities.</li> <li data-start="1278" data-end="1395">This role provides mentorship and exposure to customer-facing technical problem solving in a fast-moving AI/Product environment.</li> </ul> <h4><strong>What We're Looking For:</strong></h4> <ul> <li>Enrolled in a Bachelor’s or Master’s degree program in Computer Science, Engineering, or a related field.</li> <li>Experience writing code in&nbsp;<strong data-start="2375" data-end="2385">Python</strong> (or another general-purpose language).</li> <li>Basic understanding of programming fundamentals and software development best practices.</li> <li>Strong interest in artificial intelligence, machine learning, or software engineering.</li> <li>Excellent communication skills and willingness to collaborate with teammates and customers.</li> </ul> <p data-start="2862" data-end="2879"><strong data-start="2862" data-end="2879">Nice-to-Haves</strong></p> <ul> <li data-start="2882" data-end="2982">Experience with APIs, web development, or cloud platforms.</li> <li data-start="2882" data-end="2982">Prior coursework or projects involving AI/ML concepts or LLMs.</li> <li data-start="2882" data-end="2982">Exposure to prompt engineering or similar experimentation.</li> </ul> <p><strong>Compensation:&nbsp;</strong>CAD $30-50/hour<br><strong>Perks:</strong></p> <ul> <li>Lunch and dinner can be expensed (up to $25 each) while working in the office</li> <li>Well-stocked office with snacks and drinks</li> <li>Convenient downtown location near Union Station (public transit accessible)</li> <li>PTO: 4 days</li> <li>Exposure to cutting-edge AI systems in production</li> </ul> <p data-pm-slice="1 1 []">This posting will be used to fill a newly-created role.</p> <p>We have noticed a rise in recruiting impersonations across the industry, where scammers attempt to access candidates' personal and financial information through fake interviews and offers. All Cresta recruiting email communications will always come from the @cresta.ai domain.&nbsp;Any outreach claiming to be from Cresta via other sources should be ignored.&nbsp; If you are uncertain whether you have been contacted by an official Cresta employee, reach out to <a href="mailto:recruiting@cresra.ai">recruiting@cresta.ai</a></p>

You anticipate needs and bottlenecks and deliver with urgency and care.</li> <li>Experience leading complex deployments, owning customer relationships (including senior customer stakeholders), and working cross-functionally</li> <li>Strong technical understanding, systems thinking and exposure to building Agents (not necessarily conversational, personal projects count!)</li> <li>Comfortable working with ambiguity and learning quickly in a dynamic startup environment</li> <li>Passion for AI, automation, and shaping the future of customer experience</li> </ul> <p><strong>Bonus Points:</strong></p> <ul> <li>Background in product management, conversation design or forward deployed engineering roles</li> <li>Experience in a startup or high-growth SaaS environment</li> <li>Familiarity with customer support and contact center operations</li> </ul> <p><strong>Perks &amp; Benefits:</strong></p> <p>We offer a comprehensive and people-first benefits package to support you at work and in life:</p> <ul> <li>Comprehensive medical, dental, and vision coverage with plans to fit you and your family</li> <li>Flexible PTO to take the time you need, when you need it</li> <li>Paid parental leave for all new parents welcoming a new child</li> <li>Retirement savings plan to help you plan for the future</li> <li>Remote work setup budget to help you create a productive home office</li> <li>Monthly wellness and communication stipend to keep you connected and balanced</li> <li>In-office meal program and commuter benefits provided for onsite employees</li> </ul> <p><strong>Compensation at Cresta&nbsp;</strong></p> <p>Cresta’s approach to compensation is simple: recognize impact, reward excellence, and invest in our people.
