title: Analytics Engineer
full_description: <div class="content-intro"><p><strong>Who We Are:</strong></p> <p>Enhesa is the leading provider of regulatory and sustainability intelligence worldwide. As a trusted partner, we empower the global business community with the insight to act today and prepare for tomorrow to create a more sustainable future - positively impacting our environment, our health, our safety, and our future. Navigating the fast-changing compliance and sustainability landscapes, we help them understand not just what they should do (first) but also how to do it. Both in their unique business and anywhere in the world. Now and in the future.</p> <p style="line-height: 1;"><strong>Our Mission:</strong></p> <ul> <li>Identify EHS requirements for the industry</li> <li>Provide EHS compliance tools to companies</li> <li>Advise companies in developing and implementing corporate EHS strategies</li> </ul> <p style="line-height: 1;">Enhesa’s core clients include Fortune 500 multinational companies. For more information, visit&nbsp;<a href="https://www.enhesa.com">www.enhesa.com</a></p> <p style="line-height: 1;">As part of our highly dynamic team, we offer:</p> <ul> <li>A competitive salary package &amp; benefits with a flexible home-working policy</li> <li>Work/life balance and a fast-paced and driven environment</li> <li>Accountability and pride for your projects</li> </ul></div><p><strong>Overview of the position</strong></p> <p><em>As an Analytics Engineer at Enhesa, you will own the curated (Gold) analytics layer in Microsoft Fabric - turning raw and semi processed data into trusted, well documented dimensional models and metrics for Power Power BI, self service analytics, and AI enabled use cases. This role bridges data engineering and business intelligence by translating ambiguous business needs into scalable analytical data products.</em></p> <p><strong>Core responsibilities</strong></p> <ul> <li>Own the end-to-end Silver-to-Gold transformation layer—clarify requirements, define grains and KPIs, implement business logic, and deliver curated datasets to production.</li> <li>Develop performant SQL and PySpark transformations (CTEs, window functions, MERGE/upserts) with incremental processing, idempotency, and recovery patterns.</li> <li>Design dimensional models (facts/dimensions, SCD Type 1/2, conformed dimensions) with clearly defined semantics for consistent reporting across domains.</li> <li>Optimize Gold schemas for Power BI semantic models and ad hoc analytics—reducing downstream DAX/SQL complexity and enabling scalable self-service.&nbsp;</li> <li>Implement quality and trust controls: validation and reconciliation checks, automated tests, documentation and lineage, and monitoring for data freshness and breaking changes.</li> <li>Partner with Data Engineers and BI Engineers to align ingestion with consumption; maintain medallion-layer hygiene (partitioning, file sizing, OPTIMIZE/VORDER, schema evolution) in Microsoft Fabric.&nbsp;</li> <li>Apply strong engineering practices and governance: Git branching, CI/CD checks, environment promotions, runbooks; secure access patterns (RLS/OLS), least privilege, and data classification.</li> <li>Manage stakeholders proactively—surface risks, negotiate scope/timelines, and communicate trade offs and impact clearly.</li> </ul> <p><strong>Education Level</strong></p> <p>Bachelor’s degree in Engineering, Computer Science, Information Technology, or a related field (or equivalent practical experience).</p> <p><strong>Experience</strong></p> <p>3+ years in Analytics Engineering, Data Engineering, or Business Intelligence, with hands-on delivery of production analytical data models and curated datasets consumed by reporting and/or self-service analytics.</p> <p><strong>Required Technical Skills</strong></p> <ul> <li>Advanced SQL: CTEs, window functions, query performance tuning, and reusable transformation logic.</li> <li>Dimensional modeling: star schemas, OBTs, fact grain definition, SCD Type 1/2, conformed dimensions, and analytics-ready denormalized patterns experience.</li> <li>Spark &amp; Delta Lake: performant transformations (joins, partitioning, skew handling); lakehouse and medallion architecture; Delta features (MERGE, OPTIMIZE, ZORDER, time travel, schema evolution).</li> <li>Semantic layer awareness (Power BI): models tables and measures for performant semantic models; collaborates to reduce downstream complexity and align KPI definitions.</li> <li>Analytics mindset: translates business questions into metrics and data models; strong understanding of KPI definitions, edge cases, and how definitions impact decisions.</li> <li>Data quality &amp; observability: defines checks (completeness/validity/reconciliation), monitors freshness, and troubleshoots data issues through root-cause analysis.</li> <li>Data access &amp; governance: implements least-privilege access patterns, RLS/OLS concepts, sensitivity/classification expectations, and safe handling of confidential/PII data.</li> </ul> <p><strong>Nice-to-have Technical Skills:</strong></p> <ul> <li>Transformation frameworks: dbt (models, tests, documentation) or equivalent patterns.</li> <li>Orchestration: experience with Fabric or Azure Data Factory pipelines and dependency management.</li> <li>Engineering practices: Git and CI/CD workflows, automated testing and documentation standards.</li> <li>Microsoft Fabric: Fabric artifacts, capacities, and Fabric-specific optimizations (VORDER).</li> <li>Python: scripting for data utilities, profiling, and automation.</li> </ul> <p><strong>Other Required Skills</strong>:&nbsp;</p> <ul> <li>Communication: explains data semantics to non-technical audiences; surfaces scope/timeline/tech-debt risks early.</li> <li>Stakeholder partnership: negotiates constructively; balances competing requests; educates business users without condescension.</li> <li>Ownership &amp; autonomy: you build, you own it; anticipates downstream impact on consumers.</li> <li>Problem solving depth: decomposes complexity; weighs trade offs; digs for root cause rather than patching symptoms.</li> <li>Champion of continuous improvement;</li> <li>Language: fluent in English.</li> </ul> <p>&nbsp;</p><div class="content-conclusion"><p>If you are ready to join our journey, please apply!</p> <p>&nbsp;</p> <p><span data-teams="true"><strong>Equal Opportunity Employer</strong><br>Enhesa is an Equal Opportunity Employer. We celebrate diversity and are committed to creating an inclusive environment for all employees. We do not discriminate on the basis of race, religion, color, national origin, gender, sexual orientation, age, marital status, veteran status, disability status, or any other legally protected characteristic.</span></p> <p>&nbsp;</p></div>

title: AI Data Engineer
full_description: <div class="content-intro"><p><strong>About Us</strong></p> <p>UJET leads the way in AI-powered contact center innovation, delivering a future-proof, cloud platform that redefines the customer experience with cutting-edge AI, true multimodality, and a mobile-first approach. We infuse AI across every aspect of your customer journey and contact center operations, to drive automation and efficiency. UJET's AI solutions empower agents, optimize customer journeys, and transform contact center operations for elevated experiences and actionable insights. Built on a cloud-native architecture with a unique CRM-first approach, UJET ensures unmatched security, scalability, and prioritized data insights (without storing PII). Designed for effortless use, UJET partners with businesses to deliver exceptional interactions, smarter decision-making, and accelerated growth in the AI-driven world.</p> <p>Learn more at <a class="c-link" href="http://www.ujet.cx/" target="_blank" data-stringify-link="http://www.ujet.cx" data-sk="tooltip_parent" data-remove-tab-index="true">www.ujet.cx</a>.</p></div><p><strong>Opportunity</strong></p> <p>UJET’s conversation-analytics product, Spiral, is helping product, support, and CX leaders see what their customers are actually saying at scale. We ingest voice recordings, chat transcripts, surveys, and ticket data from every CCaaS, CRM, and storage system our customers can hand us, transcribe and PII-redact at scale, and use LLMs to surface user stories, taxonomies, and the trends that drive product and operational decisions.&nbsp;</p> <p><strong>We are hiring an AI Data Engineer</strong> to own the seam between "customer says yes" and "data is flowing into Spiral." You will run technical discovery with new customers, design the integration that brings their data into our pipeline, and then build it end-to-end,&nbsp; writing the code that adapts their data, wiring the cloud infrastructure that hosts the connection, and shipping the result into production. This is a hybrid role that combines pre-sale technical scoping with hands-on data-pipeline engineering. You will be the customer's technical counterpart from the first discovery call through steady-state ingest. You will be a member of the Spiral engineering team in every operational sense - working in a shared codebase, opening pull requests, participating in code review, debugging in production, and shipping changes through CI alongside the rest of engineering. This role is engineering-team membership with a customer-facing dimension; it is not a customer-success or pre-sales role that occasionally touches code.</p> <p><strong>Responsibilities</strong></p> <ul> <li>Run technical discovery with new customers to scope what data is available, where it lives, and how it will reach Spiral</li> <li>Design the integration approach for each customer: whether that's cloud-to-cloud bucket access, vendor push uploads, scheduled API pulls, file drops, or auth-protected exports and explain the tradeoffs to their engineering counterpart</li> <li>Shepherd credential and access exchange between the customer and Spiral - cloud access policies, OAuth or token-based authentication setup, key handoff - and stay on the thread until access is verified end-to-end</li> <li>Write the code that adapts each customer's data shape into our ingestion contract, and ship it to production through code review</li> <li>Make infrastructure-as-code changes to provision the cloud resources, identity grants, and secrets each new customer requires</li> <li>Configure and operate the orchestration that runs each customer's recurring ingest; validate end-to-end through run history, logs, and metrics</li> <li>Reconcile ingested data against source-system counts; diagnose and backfill historical gaps</li> <li>Own customer relationships post-go-live: schema changes, new feed requests, data-format drift, and surfacing upsell opportunities back to the account team</li> <li>Partner with Sales, Customer Success, and Spiral Engineering to compress time-to-first-data on every new deal</li> </ul> <p>&nbsp;<strong>Requirements</strong></p> <ul> <li>Bachelor's degree in Computer Science, Computer Engineering, Data Science, or Data Analytics with a CS/computing focus - or an equivalent rigorous technical degree</li> <li>Prior production engineering experience with data pipelines, integrations, or analytics infrastructure - internship, contract, or full-time all qualify</li> <li>Daily fluency with a standard engineering workflow: working in a shared codebase via git, authoring pull requests, giving and receiving substantive code review, debugging from logs, and shipping through CI - this is everyday work in this role, not a once-a-quarter activity</li> <li>Comfort using AI as part of the everyday engineering workflow - LLM APIs, AI-assisted coding tools, prompt design, and a clear-eyed view of where AI multiplies the work and where its first pass needs to be overridden</li> <li>Comfort writing production code in a modern typed or scripting language (years of experience scaling with level - new grads with strong fundamentals welcome)</li> <li>Working knowledge of a major cloud provider - identity and access management, object storage, secrets handling, and the security model that governs cross-organization access</li> <li>Experience with at least one data orchestration or ETL system, whether durable workflow, batch scheduler, or stream processor</li> <li>Strong SQL chops; comfortable querying data to validate and reconcile what landed against what was sent</li> <li>Customer-facing communication skills - able to hold a technical conversation with a customer's engineering counterpart and leave with a decision</li> <li>Clear written communication, async-first, as most coordination happens over email, chat, and tickets across multiple timezones</li> <li>A bias toward decisions over open questions, and a habit of writing the recommendation, not just the options</li> </ul> <p>&nbsp;<strong>Stand-out Qualifications</strong></p> <ul> <li>Hands-on experience with Temporal or another durable workflow engine</li> <li>OAuth integration experience (Salesforce JWT Bearer or Client Credentials especially)</li> <li>Infrastructure-as-code experience with AWS CDK or Terraform</li> <li>Experience with a columnar analytics database (ClickHouse, BigQuery, Snowflake, Redshift, DuckDB)</li> <li>Background in contact center, CCaaS, or CX domains (UJET, Genesys, Five9, Twilio, Aircall, Talkdesk, NICE)</li> <li>Comfort estimating cost and runtime of transcription or LLM pipelines at six-figure-file scale</li> <li>Experience building with LLM APIs (Anthropic, OpenAI, AWS Bedrock, or equivalent) - evaluation, prompt design, cost and latency tradeoffs</li> <li>Has used AI coding agents on a non-trivial engineering project and can articulate where the agent multiplied them and where they had to override its first pass</li> <li>Prior experience as a Forward-Deployed Engineer, Solutions Engineer, Implementation Engineer, or Technical Account Manager</li> </ul> <p>Annual US Hiring Range: $80,000 - $100,000</p> <p><em>*A candidate’s actual placement within this range will depend on geographic location, work experience, education, and/or skill level.<br><br></em>Applicants must be authorized to work for ANY employer in the U.S. We are unable to sponsor or take over sponsorship of an employment visa at this time.</p> <p>This is hybrid role with in-office collaboration required in Santa Monica, California.&nbsp;Applicants must currently reside in the Greater Los Angeles area to be considered; no relocation will be provided.&nbsp;</p> <p><em>#LI-Hybrid</em></p><div class="content-conclusion"><p><strong>UJET is an Equal Opportunity Employer</strong></p> <p>UJET provides equal employment opportunities to all employees and applicants for employment and prohibits discrimination and harassment of any type without regard to race, color, religion, age, sex, national origin, disability status, genetics, protected veteran status, sexual orientation, gender identity or expression, or any other characteristic protected by federal, state or local laws.</p> <p><strong>Compliance Responsibilities</strong></p> <p>Security, data protection and compliance (SDPC) are paramount to the success of our partnerships. All roles at UJET require compliance with legal and regulatory requirements and acceptance and adherence to all policies and standards within UJET. Personnel acknowledges they are personally responsible for reporting any suspected violations or abuse and are required to complete SDPC training and fulfill role-specific SDPC responsibilities.</p> <p><strong>Why UJET?</strong></p> <ul> <li><strong>Impactful Work:</strong> Be at the forefront of innovation, directly shaping the future of customer experience.&nbsp;</li> <li><strong>Dynamic Culture:</strong> Join a collaborative, inclusive team that values big ideas, creative solutions, and powerful relationships.</li> <li><strong>Comprehensive Benefits:</strong> Medical, dental, vision, 401(k) plan, wellness benefits, and more.</li> </ul></div>

title: Lead Forward Deployed Engineer (FDE)
full_description: <p>&nbsp;</p> <p><strong><span class="TextRun SCXW202736664 BCX8" lang="EN-US" data-contrast="auto"><span class="NormalTextRun SCXW202736664 BCX8" data-ccp-charstyle="Strong">Location</span><span class="NormalTextRun SCXW202736664 BCX8" data-ccp-charstyle="Strong">:</span></span><span class="TextRun SCXW202736664 BCX8" lang="EN-US" data-contrast="auto"><span class="NormalTextRun SCXW202736664 BCX8">&nbsp;</span></span><span class="TextRun SCXW202736664 BCX8" lang="EN-US" data-contrast="auto"><span class="NormalTextRun SCXW202736664 BCX8">Remote (U.S.)</span></span></strong></p> <p><strong>Other Considerations: U.S. Citizen or Permanent Resident required</strong></p> <p>&nbsp;</p> <p>&nbsp;</p> <p><strong>LTS</strong> is seeking a <strong>Lead Forward Deployed Engineer (FDE)</strong> to provide technical leadership for the design, development, and deployment of AI-powered software solutions that address complex business and mission challenges. This highly customer-facing role combines AI engineering, software architecture, solution delivery, and technical program leadership while remaining hands-on throughout the development lifecycle.</p> <p>The ideal candidate is an experienced engineering leader who can guide multidisciplinary teams, influence executive stakeholders, and deliver enterprise-scale AI solutions in fast-paced environments. As a Lead Forward Deployed Engineer, you will serve as the primary technical advisor to customers, establish engineering strategy, oversee solution delivery, mentor engineering teams, and help shape LTS's AI engineering capabilities.</p> <p><strong>What You’ll Do:</strong><br>Executive Customer Engagement</p> <ul> <li>Serve as the primary technical advisor for customer engagements, building trusted relationships with executive leadership, technical stakeholders, and business owners.</li> <li>Lead technical discovery sessions, solution workshops, and architecture discussions to define customer objectives and implementation strategies.</li> <li>Translate business priorities into scalable AI-enabled software solutions and technical roadmaps.</li> <li>Advise customers on AI adoption strategies, solution architecture, governance, and operational readiness.</li> <li>Communicate complex technical concepts, trade-offs, and implementation risks to both technical and non-technical audiences.</li> <li>Support business development activities through technical presentations, demonstrations, proof-of-concepts (POCs), proposals, and solution briefings.</li> </ul> <p>Technical Leadership &amp; Delivery</p> <ul> <li>Lead cross-functional engineering teams responsible for delivering enterprise AI and software solutions.</li> <li>Provide technical direction throughout the full software development lifecycle, from architecture through deployment and operational support.</li> <li>Guide engineering teams in designing secure, scalable, maintainable, and high-performing software systems.</li> <li>Lead architecture reviews, design discussions, sprint planning, technical decision-making, and engineering governance.</li> <li>Establish engineering standards, development practices, quality metrics, and delivery processes across engagements.</li> <li>Remove technical blockers, manage delivery risks, and ensure successful execution across multiple projects or workstreams.</li> <li>Mentor engineers through architecture guidance, code reviews, technical coaching, and career development.</li> </ul> <p>AI Solution Architecture &amp; Engineering</p> <ul> <li>Architect and oversee the development of AI-enabled applications, intelligent automation solutions, and modern software platforms.</li> <li>Lead the design and implementation of solutions leveraging large language models (LLMs), retrieval-augmented generation (RAG), agentic AI, machine learning, and other emerging AI technologies.</li> <li>Guide the design of scalable data pipelines, knowledge retrieval systems, APIs, microservices, and enterprise integrations.</li> <li>Establish best practices for prompt engineering, AI evaluation, model governance, human-in-the-loop workflows, and responsible AI.</li> <li>Define engineering approaches that balance scalability, performance, security, reliability, governance, and cost.</li> <li>Review production-quality code and ensure adherence to software engineering best practices.</li> <li>Drive implementation of CI/CD pipelines, automated testing, monitoring, logging, observability, and operational excellence.</li> <li>Develop reusable frameworks, technical accelerators, documentation, and engineering standards that improve delivery across programs.</li> </ul> <p>Program &amp; Practice Leadership</p> <ul> <li>Lead multiple engineering initiatives while ensuring consistent delivery quality across teams.</li> <li>Coordinate cross-functional and geographically distributed engineering teams, including hybrid onshore/offshore delivery models.</li> <li>Establish delivery governance, resource planning, risk management, stakeholder communications, and project health reporting.</li> <li>Contribute to organizational engineering strategy, AI capability development, and technical innovation.</li> <li>Promote continuous improvement through engineering best practices, reusable assets, and knowledge sharing.</li> <li>Stay current on emerging AI technologies, cloud platforms, software engineering trends, and industry best practices.</li> </ul> <p><strong>What We’re Looking For:</strong></p> <ul> <li>Bachelor's degree in Computer Science, Software Engineering, Data Science, Information Systems, or a related technical discipline (or equivalent experience).</li> <li>7+ years of experience in software engineering, AI engineering, machine learning, data engineering, solution architecture, or related technical roles.</li> <li>Demonstrated experience leading engineering teams delivering enterprise software solutions.</li> <li>Experience designing, building, and deploying AI/ML or Generative AI solutions in production environments.</li> <li>Experience architecting distributed systems, APIs, microservices, cloud-native applications, or enterprise integrations.</li> <li>Strong experience with cloud platforms such as AWS, Microsoft Azure, Google Cloud Platform, or hybrid cloud environments.</li> <li>Experience leading technical workstreams and translating business objectives into scalable technology solutions.</li> <li>Experience implementing CI/CD pipelines, DevOps practices, automated testing, and production monitoring.</li> <li>Excellent communication, leadership, consulting, and stakeholder management skills.</li> <li>Proven ability to lead engineering teams in agile environments while remaining hands-on technically.</li> <li>Ability to travel as required by project needs.</li> </ul> <p><strong>Nice to Have:</strong></p> <ul> <li>Experience architecting Generative AI applications utilizing LLMs, Retrieval-Augmented Generation (RAG), agentic AI, intelligent automation, or multi-agent systems.</li> <li>Experience establishing AI governance, evaluation frameworks, prompt engineering standards, model monitoring, and responsible AI practices.</li> <li>Experience implementing MLOps or LLMOps capabilities, including model deployment, monitoring, lifecycle management, and performance evaluation.</li> <li>Experience with modern data engineering platforms including Spark, Airflow, Kafka, streaming technologies, or enterprise data architectures.</li> <li>Experience with containerization and orchestration technologies such as Docker and Kubernetes.</li> <li>Experience integrating enterprise systems using APIs, event-driven architectures, and messaging platforms.</li> <li>Familiarity with cybersecurity, privacy, governance, and regulatory compliance requirements.</li> <li>Experience leading distributed engineering teams across hybrid or global delivery models.</li> <li>Experience supporting federal, healthcare, defense, or other regulated industry customers.</li> <li>Experience contributing to business development, technical strategy, solution architecture, and organizational engineering leadership.</li> </ul> <p>&nbsp;</p> <p><strong>What’s in it for you?</strong></p> <ul> <li>The opportunity to support high visibility federal missions in IT and healthcare</li> <li>A culture that values innovation, growth, collaboration, and quality</li> <li>Access to cutting-edge tools and technologies</li> <li>Comprehensive benefits for you and your family</li> <li>A career path that rewards ambition and performance</li> </ul> <p>&nbsp;</p> <p>If you’re ready to push boundaries, sharpen your skills, and join a team that is passionate about building what’s next, we’d love to meet you. Apply today and let’s build a future together!</p> <p>&nbsp;</p><div class="content-pay-transparency"><div class="pay-input"><div class="title">Pay Range</div><div class="pay-range"><span>$256,000</span><span class="divider">&mdash;</span><span>$350,000 USD</span></div></div></div><div class="content-conclusion"><p><strong>LTS </strong>shares salary ranges to promote transparency. Compensation ranges are provided for informational purposes, and final compensation may vary based on experience, skills, location, and role requirements.</p> <p><strong>LTS&nbsp;</strong>is committed to offering eligible employees comprehensive benefits that will provide them with options intended to meet their needs and the needs of their family.</p></div>

title: Data Engineer, Web Scraping
full_description: <div class="content-intro"><p><strong>About 10a Labs: </strong>10a Labs is the safety and threat-intelligence layer trusted by frontier AI labs, AI unicorns, Fortune 10 companies, and leading global technology platforms. Our adversarial red teaming, model evaluations, and intelligence collection enable engineering, safety, and security teams to stay ahead of evolving threats and deploy AI systems safely.</p></div><p><strong>In this role, you will:</strong></p> <ul> <li>Design, implement, and optimize end-to-end data pipelines for scraping and processing structured and unstructured data using Google Cloud Platform (or similar) and best practices;&nbsp;</li> <li>Conduct ad hoc web scraping and data collection to support research and intelligence initiatives;</li> <li>Prepare data for further analysis, including data cleaning, transformation, anonymization, and masking;</li> <li>Contribute to the development of internal and external APIs, following best practices;&nbsp;</li> <li>Collaborate with ML engineers, other data engineers, and software developers to deliver actionable insights and functional tools, including internal and external dashboards, APIs, and data dumps; and&nbsp;</li> <li>Drive other critical initiatives.&nbsp;</li> </ul> <p><strong>Requirements:</strong></p> <ul> <li>Degree (or equivalent work experience) in Computer Science, Engineering, Information Science, Data Science or a related field (graduate degree preferred)</li> <li>2+ years of professional experience in data engineering or a closely related field</li> <li>Ability to communicate complex technical ideas clearly to non-technical audiences</li> <li>Proficiency in Python, SQL</li> <li>Experience with web scraping/crawling (e.g., Beautiful Soup, Selenium, Scrapy)</li> <li>Experience with Google Cloud Platform (or similar), including storage and database services (e.g., Cloud Storage, CloudSQL, Cloud Spanner) and workflow orchestration (e.g., Cloud Composer/Airflow, Cloud Run, Pub/Sub)</li> <li>Experience building and managing data pipelines, especially for text data</li> <li>Comfort working in fast-moving, high-impact environments, such as startups, AI research labs, or security-focused teams</li> </ul> <p><strong>Compensation &amp; Benefits:</strong></p> <ul> <li><strong>Salary Range:</strong> $105K–$125K, depending on experience and location</li> <li><strong>Bonus:</strong> Performance-based annual bonus</li> <li><strong>Professional Development:</strong> Support for conferences, continuing education, or leadership training</li> <li><strong>Work Environment:</strong> Fully remote, U.S.-based</li> <li><strong>Health Benefits:</strong> Comprehensive health, dental, and vision coverage</li> <li><strong>Time Off:</strong> Generous PTO and paid holiday schedule</li> </ul> <p><strong>Retirement: 401(k) plan</strong></p>

title: Data Engineer
full_description: <div class="content-intro"><p><a href="alt.xyz" target="_blank">Alt</a> is unlocking the value of alternative assets, starting with the $5 B trading-card market. We let collectors buy, sell, vault, and finance their cards in one place and we are backed by leaders at Stripe, Coinbase, Seven Seven Six, and pro athletes like Tom Brady and Giannis Antetokounmpo. Our next frontier is real-time pricing at scale—the Alt Value that powers every trade, loan, and product on the platform.</p></div><h3>The Role</h3> <p>Are you a data engineer who thrives on building robust pipelines and solving complex data challenges? In this role, you'll own Alt's critical data infrastructure that powers our pricing model and marketplace insights by ingesting transaction and listing data from dozens of external marketplaces. You'll be responsible for ensuring our data pipelines deliver fresh, accurate information that drives pricing decisions, market analytics, and business intelligence across the entire platform.</p> <h3>What you'll do here</h3> <ul> <li>Design, optimize and own data pipelines that scrape, process and ingest transaction and listing data from major auction houses and marketplaces.</li> <li>Build comprehensive monitoring and alerting systems to track latency, uptime, and coverage metrics across all data sources.</li> <li>Continuously improve our data infrastructure by modernizing storage and processing technologies, reducing manual interventions, and optimizing for cost, performance, and reliability.</li> <li>Partner with internal teams to understand data usage patterns and ensure pipelines deliver clean, standardized data that meets product requirements.</li> </ul> <h3>This is a perfect fit if you...</h3> <ul> <li>Care deeply about data quality and understand that extraction is only the beginning - the real value comes from delivering clean and usable data.</li> <li>Thrive in a startup environment where you can make immediate impact and own critical systems end-to-end.</li> <li>Take an incremental approach to improvements, preferring evidence-based decisions over wholesale replacements.</li> <li>Are intellectually curious about how data flows through systems and passionate about automation opportunities.</li> <li>Want to work at the intersection of data engineering and product, understanding how your pipelines directly impact business outcomes.</li> </ul> <h3>What you bring to the table</h3> <p>Must-haves:</p> <ul> <li>3-4 years of experience in data engineering or related fields</li> <li>Strong Python proficiency with at least 3 years of hands-on experience</li> <li>Proven experience with large-scale data processing using dataframe technologies (Pandas, Polars, PySpark, or similar)</li> <li>Hands-on experience with pipeline orchestration tools (Airflow, Dagster, or similar DAG-based systems)</li> <li>Track record of owning at least one data pipeline end-to-end within the past 2 years</li> <li>Solid SQL skills for data analysis and transformation</li> <li>Previous startup experience - you understand the pace and adaptability required in a fast-moving environment</li> <li>A pragmatic mindset focused on delivering value incrementally rather than pursuing perfection</li> </ul> <p>Nice-to-haves:</p> <ul> <li>Experience with web scraping technologies (Selenium, Puppeteer, Beautiful Soup)</li> <li>Familiarity with data infrastructure and cloud services (AWS preferred)</li> <li>Interest in or knowledge of trading cards, collectibles, or alternative asset markets</li> <li>Experience with LLM-based automation tools for data extraction and processing</li> </ul> <h3 data-start="2597" data-end="2629"><strong data-start="2601" data-end="2629">What You’ll Get From Us</strong></h3> <ul> <li>A seat at the table to help shape the future of Alt and the alternative asset space</li> <li data-start="2741" data-end="2787">Autonomy and ownership on projects that matter</li> <li data-start="2790" data-end="2825">$100/month work-from-home stipend</li> <li data-start="2828" data-end="2857">$200/month wellness stipend</li> <li data-start="2860" data-end="2883">WeWork office stipend</li> <li data-start="2886" data-end="2914">401(k) retirement benefits</li> <li data-start="2917" data-end="2943">Flexible vacation policy</li> <li data-start="2946" data-end="2976">Generous paid parental leave</li> <li data-start="2979" data-end="3056">Competitive healthcare benefits, including HSA, for you and your dependent(s)</li> </ul> <p data-start="3058" data-end="3178"><strong data-start="3058" data-end="3080">Base salary range:</strong>&nbsp;$155,000–$165,000, plus equity. Offers may vary based on experience, location, and other factors.</p>

title: Senior Data Engineer
full_description: <h2><strong>About Us</strong></h2> <p>Hungryroot is using AI to build the most consumer-centric food and wellness company to ever exist. We act as your personal assistant for healthy living—getting to know your goals, lifestyle, and budget, and recommending and delivering healthy groceries, easy recipes, and essential supplements for you and your family.</p> <p>It’s the easiest way to eat healthy, achieve your goals, save time, and discover new foods. We believe food is the foundation of health, convenience should not mean compromise, and that everyone is unique in how they eat and live. That’s why we’re building a future in which healthy living is both easy and enjoyable.</p> <p>Hungryroot is a distributed team of top talent across 28+ U.S. states. While we have a headquarters in New York City, our remote-first culture emphasizes collaboration, team-building, and flexibility. Expect regular virtual team events, strong ownership and accountability, and an annual company retreat.</p> <p><strong>About the Role</strong></p> <p>We’re looking for a Senior Data Engineer to be a part of our growing Data team. You’ll be responsible for working directly on the data pipelines and systems that create our Hungryroot Analytics data model used to inform financial decision making, product development, inventory management and more.&nbsp;</p> <p>As a member of the team, you will work collaboratively with Analysts across many departments and your Data Engineering peers to build, deploy, and maintain data pipelines that power our Analytics data model. You will be responsible for data model design, developing and deploying pipelines, and setting the example of code quality and value across the Data Engineering team.&nbsp;&nbsp;</p> <p>This role will join Hungryroot’s Data Engineering team and you’ll report directly to our Senior Director, Data Platform &amp; Governance. In this role, you will work closely with Analysts at Hungryroot to improve our Analytical data model and unlock our Analyst’s potential to make broad impacts at Hungryroot. You will set an example in the Data Engineering team as a strong pipeline developer, communicator, and teacher for your Data Engineering peers.&nbsp;</p> <p>Come join our team, apply your passion for data and have fun!</p> <h2><strong>Responsibilities</strong></h2> <ul> <li>Develop pipelines in Spark (Python) in the Databricks Platform</li> <li>Build cross-functional working relationships with business partners in Food Analytics, Operations, Marketing, and Web/App Development teams to power pipeline development for the business</li> <li>Ensure system reliability and performance</li> <li>Deploy and maintain data pipelines in production</li> <li>Set an example of code quality, data quality, and best practices&nbsp;</li> <li>Work with Analysts and Data Engineers to enable high quality self-service analytics for all of Hungryroot</li> <li>Investigate datasets to answer business questions, ensuring data quality and business assumptions are understood before deploying a pipeline</li> </ul> <h2><strong>Qualifications</strong></h2> <ul> <li>5+ years of experience in ETL development and data modeling</li> <li>5+ years of experience in Python</li> <li>2+ years of experience working with the Databricks Platform&nbsp;</li> <li>Experience with Spark</li> <li>Excellent problem-solving skills and the ability to translate business problems into practical solutions</li> </ul> <h2><strong>Perks &amp; Benefits&nbsp;</strong></h2> <ul> <li>Remote-first: work from home, work from our NYC office, work from anywhere in the U.S. - you decide!</li> <li>Equity&nbsp;</li> <li>Unlimited vacation policy</li> <li>Universal paid parental leave</li> <li>Monthly Hungryroot credit for delicious, healthy groceries</li> <li>Comprehensive health, vision, dental, and life insurance</li> <li>401k with Company Match</li> <li>A work from home stipend to support your initial home-office setup</li> </ul> <p><em>Expected Pay Range<br>$151,000-$189,000<br></em></p> <p><span style="font-weight: 400;">#LI-REMOTE</span></p> <p>The employer will not sponsor applicants for work visas.</p> <p><em>Our mission to help make healthy eating easy, accessible, and joyful is better served by a diverse workplace. We are a proud Equal Opportunity Employer committed to building an inclusive workplace. We have zero-tolerance for harassment or discrimination. We do not discriminate on the basis of any protected class.&nbsp;</em></p> <p>&nbsp;</p>

title: Senior Data Engineer
full_description: <p><strong>Correlation One</strong>&nbsp;is the largest provider of AI and data workforce development programs globally, having trained over 500,000 professionals across 11 countries. As the largest training provider for Amazon Career Choice, and a growing partner to state governments building registered apprenticeship programs, Correlation One sits at the intersection of employer talent needs and scalable workforce training.</p> <p>We work with Fortune 500 enterprises, federal and state government agencies, and leading employers to close skills gaps in AI, data analytics, cybersecurity, and operations leadership. Our programs produce job-ready graduates who are prepared to contribute from day one.</p> <h4><strong>Your impact:</strong></h4> <p>As a Senior Data Engineer, you will play a critical role in building and operating reliable, analytics-ready data systems that power decision-making across the organization. You’ll meaningfully contribute to key data pipelines, partner closely with analytics stakeholders, and help evolve our data platform to be more scalable, trustworthy, and easier to work with.</p> <p>This is a hands-on role for someone who cares deeply about production data quality, is comfortable making decisions in ambiguous environments, and is motivated by building systems that other teams depend on every day.</p> <h4><strong>A day in the life:</strong></h4> <ul> <li>Develop long-term technical vision for the team</li> <li>Design technical strategy to develop components, frameworks and libraries to create seamless applications for our customers</li> <li>Hands-on development of scalable components and applications</li> <li>Mentor engineers including senior and junior engineers on their team</li> <li>Improve and uphold standards for engineering and operational excellence</li> <li>Create and encourage comprehensive documentation for code, libraries, and components to facilitate collaboration and knowledge sharing within the team</li> </ul> <p><strong>Your expertise:</strong></p> <ul> <li>4+ years of experience in data engineering or closely related roles</li> <li>Strong experience building and maintaining production data pipelines using Python</li> <li>Hands-on experience integrating data from external APIs</li> <li>Experience operating batch ELT pipelines and working with data warehouses</li> <li>Advanced SQL skills and experience modeling data for analytics and reporting use cases</li> <li>Experience working with orchestration tools such as Airflow</li> <li>Familiarity with dbt or similar transformation tools, and comfort working closely with analytics teams</li> <li>Demonstrated ownership of production systems, including monitoring, debugging, and incident response</li> <li>Comfort working in ambiguous environments and making pragmatic technical decisions</li> <li>Experience working in a cloud environment such as GCP (or equivalent)</li> <li>Familiarity with CI/CD practices and source control (e.g., GitHub)</li> <li>Experience working in collaborative, iterative development environments</li> <li>Additional preferred experience:</li> <ul> <li>Designing or migrating pipelines toward incremental loading patterns</li> <li>Working with infrastructure-as-code tools (e.g., Terraform)</li> <li>Experience with data quality tooling, testing, or observability</li> <li>Exposure to BI tools or supporting analytics workflows</li> <li>Experience learning and adopting new tools or technologies quickly</li> </ul> </ul> <p><strong>Where you are</strong></p> <ul> <li>This role is remote and should be located in <strong>Latin America</strong> (LatAm) and compatible with EST time zone.</li> </ul> <p><strong>How we support our people&nbsp;</strong></p> <ul> <li>Insurance or subsidies based on country</li> <li>Unlimited Time Off, with a minimum time off recommendation&nbsp;</li> <li>Company-paid holidays</li> <li>Official company-wide holiday for the last week of the calendar year&nbsp;</li> <li>Access to free data skills training through our programs</li> <li>A company culture that empowers individuals and embraces diversity through its core mission</li> </ul> <p>The compensation range for this position is specific to location and takes into account the wide range of factors that are considered in making compensation decisions. These factors include (but are not limited to) location, experience, education and skill sets.</p> <p><strong>Correlation One’s Commitment&nbsp;</strong></p> <p>Correlation One is proud to be an Equal Opportunity Employer and is committed to providing equal opportunity for all employees and applicants. Correlation One provides a work environment free of discrimination and harassment. Employment decisions at Correlation One are based solely on business needs, job requirements and individual qualifications, without regard to race, color, religion or belief, national, social or ethnic origin, sex (including pregnancy), age, sexual orientation, gender identity and/or expression, marital, civil union or domestic partnership status, past or present military service, or any other status protected by the laws or regulations in the locations where we operate. We encourage applicants to bring their unique skills, experiences, and outlook to our work environment.&nbsp;</p> <p>Correlation One is committed to the full inclusion of all qualified individuals. In keeping with our commitment, Correlation One strives to provide reasonable accommodations for persons with disabilities to enable them to access the hiring process. If you need an accommodation to access the job application or interview process, please contact <a href="mailto:candidates@correlation-one.com">candidates@correlation-one.com</a>.</p> <p>&nbsp;</p> <p><span style="color: rgb(206, 212, 217);">#noad<br><br>#LI-DNI</span></p>

title: Forward Deployed Engineer
full_description: <div class="content-intro"><p><strong>Warp: We're Building the Platform for Agentic Development</strong></p> <p>Warp began with the vision of reimagining one of the fundamental dev tools — the terminal — to make it more usable and powerful for all developers. As AI has advanced, Warp has evolved beyond its terminal roots into a full agentic development environment: a workbench for dispatching agents to code, deploy, and debug production software.&nbsp;</p> <p>Today, we have two products: Warp, the agentic development environment born out of the terminal, and Oz, our orchestration platform for running hundreds of cloud agents in parallel. With both products, we’re creating the scaffolding for developers to build their own workflows for working with agents.&nbsp;</p> <p>In Warp, seamlessly switch between running commands, launching agents, and iterating on code with agents— eliminating the need to jump between a terminal and an IDE. It all works with our built-in SOTA agent or top agents like Claude Code, Codex, Gemini CLI, and OpenCode. In Oz, build custom agents that are triggered programmatically, tracked centrally, and are shared across full teams to run agents across organizations without compromising security or reliability. Continue cloud agent runs that were triggered by Oz locally in Warp or on the web with one click.</p> <p>With over 750k active developers at companies including Docker, Ramp, Peloton, and over half of the Fortune 500 and revenue that grew over 44x last year, Warp is one of the fastest-growing startups in the AI development space.</p> <p>Our mission has remained the same even as AI has advanced: to empower developers to ship better software more quickly, freeing them to focus on the creative and rewarding aspects of their work. If you want to help define what development looks like in the agentic era at a company where your work reaches hundreds of thousands of engineers every day, we'd love to have you.</p></div><h4><strong>Why This Role?&nbsp;</strong></h4> <p>Warp is scaling its enterprise motion around Oz — Warp's programmable agent platform for running and coordinating agents at scale. Oz Cloud Agents are cloud-connected background agents that run from events, schedules, or integrations, giving engineering teams scalable automation with shared observability. Enterprise customers can choose from Warp-hosted execution (where Oz handles everything from sandboxed environments to integrations) to self-hosted execution (where agents run on the customer's own infrastructure while Oz provides orchestration, session management, and visibility).</p> <p>Every enterprise has different security requirements, internal tooling, and use cases and we need someone to bridge the gap between our platform and their unique environments. As enterprises adopt Oz, they need hands-on technical leadership to deploy production agent workflows: architecting solutions, configuring environments, building integrations, guiding self-hosted deployments, and iterating on prompts and skills until the agents deliver real business value.</p> <p>As our <strong>Founding Forward Deployed Engineer</strong>, you'll be the first hire dedicated to making enterprise implementations successful, turning promising pilots into production deployments that drive real results. This role sits at the intersection of engineering, product strategy, and go-to-market — you'll build alongside customers, not just advise them. You'll report to leadership and have direct exposure across every part of the business.</p> <h4><strong>As a Founding Forward Deployed Engineer, you will...</strong></h4> <ul> <li><strong>Own end-to-end enterprise implementations.</strong> Lead engagements from first discovery call through production deployment. Run deep discovery on customer engineering environments, define success criteria, architect agent workflows tailored to their stack, and own the outcome end-to-end.&nbsp;</li> <li><strong>Architect agent workflows with customers.</strong> Help enterprise engineering teams design agent architectures for their specific use cases: selecting triggers (Slack, Linear, GitHub Actions, webhooks, cron schedules, API calls), configuring sandboxed environments, structuring multi-step agent workflows, and designing integration-driven automation that fits into their existing development lifecycle.</li> <li><strong>Build production-grade solutions alongside customers.</strong> Write and iterate on agent prompts, skills, and configurations. Set up environments, secrets, MCP servers, and integrations. Debug agent runs using session sharing and observability tools. Ship a working version in days, then harden it over weeks with proper monitoring and iteration based on real usage.&nbsp;</li> <li><strong>Guide self-hosted deployments.</strong> For enterprises that need execution on their own infrastructure, help configure managed workers (Docker, Kubernetes, or Direct backend), set up task routing, connect private registries, and validate network connectivity, ensuring they get the same orchestration and observability benefits as Warp-hosted execution.</li> <li><strong>Accelerate deals through technical execution.</strong> Join customer calls from the very first discovery meeting to understand their technical environment and identify opportunities for Oz cloud agents.</li> <li><strong>Develop reusable implementation patterns.</strong> Turn what you build into reusable patterns that make the next implementation faster. Document architecture patterns, reference implementations, agent skills, and templates.</li> <li><strong>Be the voice of the customer to Product and Engineering.</strong> You'll be closer to customer needs than almost anyone else on the team. Surface implementation friction, feature gaps, and common pain points. Translate what you're learning in the field – which integrations should become first-party, what's blocking faster deployments, where the platform needs to evolve – into actionable feedback that shapes the Oz platform roadmap.&nbsp;</li> </ul> <p><strong>You may be a good fit if...</strong></p> <ul> <li><strong>You have FDE-equivalent experience. </strong>Ideally, most recently a Forward Deployed Engineer, or you might be an early engineer or founder at a startup where you worked closely with GTM and did pilot implementations for enterprises. The key signal: you've built custom technical solutions for customers, not just internal products.</li> <li><strong>You have strong infrastructure fundamentals.</strong> You're comfortable with Docker, CI/CD pipelines, cloud infrastructure (AWS, GCP, Azure), container orchestration, and Linux. You understand how enterprise engineering environments work and can navigate them confidently.</li> <li><strong>You're familiar with AI/LLM concepts.</strong> You understand prompt engineering, agent architectures, tool use, and how to evaluate non-deterministic systems. You've built or deployed AI-powered workflows, not just prototypes, but systems that run in production.</li> <li><strong>You're an excellent communicator who can articulate the vision.</strong> You can hold your own with senior engineers and executives alike. You need to articulate Warp's vision for Oz cloud agents in a way that gets enterprise engineering teams and leadership excited.</li> <li><strong>You have high EQ and can navigate enterprise relationships. </strong>Working with other companies' engineers requires a different approach than internal collaboration. You know how to be amenable, patient, and constructive even when things aren't set up the way you'd prefer.</li> </ul> <p><strong>Bonus...&nbsp;</strong></p> <ul> <li>Experience building or deploying AI agents in production (LangChain, custom agent frameworks, or similar)</li> <li>Experience with GitHub Actions, webhooks, and event-driven automation</li> <li>Familiarity with enterprise security and compliance requirements (SOC 2, self-hosted deployments, network boundary constraints)</li> <li>Experience with APIs/SDKs in a customer-facing capacity (building integrations, writing client libraries, or doing technical enablement)</li> <li>Experience with terminal applications, shell scripting, or command-line tooling</li> <li>Experience with Rust or Go</li> </ul> <p><span style="font-weight: 400;">At Warp, we are dedicated to building a diverse, inclusive, and authentic workplace. So if you’re excited about this role, but your past experience doesn’t align perfectly with every qualification in this job description – we encourage you to apply anyways! We are a community of curious learners, and most of us are learning some skills for the first time (like our engineers learning to program Warp in Rust). You might be just the right candidate for this or other roles.</span></p> <p><span style="font-weight: 400;">If you're feeling playful, try out our optional hiring challenge and submit your answers with your application: <a href="https://github.com/warpdotdev/hiring-challenge">https://github.com/warpdotdev/hiring-challenge</a></span></p> <p><strong>Salary Transparency</strong></p> <p>Warp operates from a place of high trust and transparency around compensation. We state the pay range that best aligns with our understanding of the market and the needs for the open role.</p> <p>When we find the right person, we try to put our best foot forward with an offer that excites you. We consider your skills, the level of experience you bring, and what similar jobs in the market pay, all while ensuring equal pay for equal work at Warp.</p> <p>Total compensation at Warp consists of: (1) a market-competitive base salary, (2) a generous benefits package, and (3) meaningful equity.</p> <p><strong>The budgeted compensation amount for this role is targeted at </strong><strong>$200,000 to $250,000 (inclusive of an 80% base / 20% variable split)</strong><strong>.</strong></p> <p><em>Exact compensation will vary based on skills and experience.</em></p> <p>In addition to salary, all employees receive further compensation in the form of equity in the company. This is a meaningful stock option grant with a four-year vesting period and one-year cliff. Your equity is where most of the significant upside potential is. Comparing startup equity is always a bit tricky, so we’re happy to walk you through different valuation scenarios at the offer stage in order to help paint a clearer picture of the upside.</p> <p><strong>What we offer</strong></p> <ul> <li>Competitive salary, generous benefits, and meaningful equity</li> <li>100% coverage on medical, dental, and vision benefits for employees (80% coverage for dependents)</li> <li>A flexible remote-first culture, with optional office spaces in NYC and SF for folks who want to work together IRL</li> <li>Pre-tax FSA and Health Savings Plans</li> <li>Pre-tax Commuter Benefit</li> <li>12 company holidays, 20 days of Paid Time Off, and unlimited sick time</li> <li>16 weeks of paid Parental Leave for both birthing and non-birthing parents</li> <li>Twice-a-year all company retreats in awesome locations</li> <li>A monthly gym and internet stipend</li> <li>A company-sponsored 401(k) through Vestwell</li> <li>A complimentary OneMedical membership</li> </ul> <p><em>Individuals seeking employment at Warp are considered without regard to race, color, religion, national origin, age, sex, marital status, ancestry, physical or mental disability, veteran status, gender identity, or sexual orientation.</em></p><div class="content-conclusion"><p><strong>About Warp</strong></p> <p>We are a company run by product-first builders, building a core product for all developers. We are committed to understanding our users deeply. We will ultimately build the best product and business if that team includes developers and designers from a wide range of backgrounds. The early team comes from Google, Dropbox, Gem, LinkedIn, and Facebook. We are looking for passionate individuals to join us and help bring Warp to the world.</p> <p><span style="font-weight: 400;">We value honesty, humility, and pragmatism, and our core product principle is focusing on the user. If you’re interested in learning more about our company values and&nbsp;</span><span style="font-weight: 400;"><span style="margin: 0px; padding: 0px;">the culture of our engineering team, please take a look at our internal&nbsp;<a href="http://warp.dev/how-we-work" target="_blank">'How We Work'</a></span>&nbsp;guide.</span></p> <p><span style="font-weight: 400;">We’re very fortunate to be backed by a great group of venture capital firms. In August 2023, we announced a $50M Series B funding round ($73M total raised), led by Sequoia Capital.&nbsp;</span><span style="font-weight: 400;">Our other investors include Google Ventures, Neo, and Box Group. We are also backed by a network of passionate angels, including Dylan Field (Co-Founder and CEO,&nbsp;<a href="https://www.linkedin.com/pulse/why-im-leading-warps-series-dylan-field/">Figma</a>), Elad Gil (early investor in Airbnb, Pinterest, Stripe, and Square), Jeff Weiner (Executive Chairman and Ex-CEO, LinkedIn), Marc Benioff (Founder and CEO, Salesforce), and Sam Altman (Co-Founder &amp; CEO, OpenAI).</span></p> <p><strong>The Product</strong></p> <p><span style="font-weight: 400;">Here's our </span><a href="https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=k9BRhSbVSpI">latest demo</a><span style="font-weight: 400;"> showing some of our current features…</span></p> <p><iframe src="https://www.youtube.com/embed/k9BRhSbVSpI" width="560" height="314"></iframe></p></div>

title: Forward Deployed Engineer
full_description: <div class="content-intro"><p>Airtable is the no-code app platform that empowers people closest to the work to accelerate their most critical business processes. More than 500,000 organizations, including 80% of the Fortune 100, rely on Airtable to transform how work gets done.</p></div><p>Join Airtable as a Forward Deployed Engineer and become a trusted advisor to our most strategic customers. In this high-impact, customer-facing role, you’ll embed yourself within leading organizations to drive digital transformation, deliver innovative technical solutions, and champion the adoption of Airtable’s platform. You’ll work directly with senior executives, influence product direction, and help shape the future of how businesses operate—all while leveraging your technical expertise, business acumen, and passion for storytelling.</p> <h1><strong>What you'll do</strong></h1> <ul> <li>Embed within strategic customer organizations to understand their business processes, operations, and goals</li> <li>Identify and deliver high-impact technical solutions that drive organizational transformation and measurable results</li> <li>Act as a thought leader and trusted advisor to senior executives, translating Airtable’s value into customer-specific language</li> <li>Rapidly prototype and deploy solutions to accelerate time to value and drive adoption</li> <li>Lead innovation and solution ideation workshops with key customers</li> <li>Guide clients in building custom solutions, driving adoption, and ensuring successful change management</li> <li>Provide real-world product feedback to influence Airtable’s roadmap</li> <li>Evangelize and expand use cases, driving account growth and AI adoption</li> <li>Reduce risk of churn and contraction by establishing trust and technical expertise</li> <li>Leverage creative storytelling to communicate complex technical concepts</li> <li>Travel to customer sites as needed</li> </ul> <h1><strong>Who you are</strong></h1> <ul> <li>5-7 years in SaaS pre-sales, implementation, or customer-facing roles in enterprise environments</li> <li>7-10 years of technology and sales experience, ideally with large, flexible platforms</li> <li>Experienced in managing sales cycles and technical evaluations</li> <li>Strong business acumen with knowledge of business operations across multiple industries</li> <li>Hands-on technical builder with experience architecting, prototyping, and developing custom solutions</li> <li>Deep understanding of relational database architecture and enterprise integration methodologies</li> <li>Active AI user and implementer, familiar with LLMs, vibe coding, and Airtable</li> <li>Skilled communicator and presenter, comfortable with public speaking and storytelling</li> <li>Passionate about low-code/no-code applications and emerging technology trends</li> <li>Familiar with programming languages and process/persona mapping tools (e.g., Lucid, Figma, Mural, Miro)</li> <li>Willing to travel and thrive in dynamic, customer-centric environments</li> </ul> <p><span style="font-weight: 400;">Airtable is an equal opportunity employer. We embrace diversity and strive to create a workplace where everyone has an equal opportunity to thrive. We welcome people of different backgrounds, experiences, abilities, and perspectives. All qualified applicants will receive consideration for employment without regard to race, color, religion, sex, sexual orientation, gender identity, national origin, disability, protected veteran status or any characteristic protected by applicable federal and state laws, regulations and ordinances. Learn more about your </span><a href="https://drive.google.com/file/d/1jAj2by1bto5g8Xqn9hWNAwPOMGNd_fM6/view?usp=sharing"><span style="font-weight: 400;">EEO rights as an applicant</span></a><strong>.&nbsp;</strong></p> <p><span style="font-weight: 400;">VEVRAA-Federal Contractor</span></p> <p><span style="font-weight: 400;">If you have a medical condition, disability, or religious belief/practice which inhibits your ability to participate in any part of the application or interview process, please complete our </span><a href="https://airtable.com/shrq13fCV8h1sx2t8"><span style="font-weight: 400;">Accommodations Request Form</span></a><span style="font-weight: 400;"> </span><span style="font-weight: 400;">and let us know how we may assist you. Airtable is committed to participating in the interactive process and providing reasonable accommodations to qualified applicants.</span></p><div class="content-pay-transparency"><div class="pay-input"><div class="description"><p>Compensation awarded to successful candidates will vary based on their work location, relevant skills, and experience.</p> <p><span data-slate-node="text"><span data-slate-leaf="true"><span data-slate-string="true">Our total compensation package also includes the opportunity to receive benefits, restricted stock units, and may include incentive compensation.<span class="Apple-converted-space">&nbsp;</span></span></span></span><span data-slate-node="text"><span data-slate-leaf="true"><span data-slate-string="true">To learn Airtable</span></span></span></a><span data-slate-node="text" data-slate-fragment=""><span data-slate-leaf="true"><span data-slate-string="true">.</span></span></span></p></div><div class="title">For title: Staff Data Engineer (Coupang Pay)
full_description: <p><span data-olk-copy-source="MessageBody">쿠팡의 핀테크 사업은 소비자와 판매자 모두가 확장된 금융 서비스 솔루션을 누릴 수 있도록 새로운 방식을 선보이고 있습니다. 쿠팡페이는 coupang.com 및 쿠팡이츠에서 상품을 구매하는 고객부터 쿠팡의 오픈마켓을 이용하는 판매자 그리고 쿠팡이츠 가맹점까지 쿠팡을 이용하는 모든 이들에게 혁신적인 결제 및 금융 서비스 솔루션을 제공하는 데 중점을 두고 있습니다. 쿠팡페이는 최신 기술을 통해 한국과 대만의 쿠팡 고객의 니즈에 부응하기 위해 원터치 결제 서비스와 같은 금융 솔루션을 개발합니다. 대출 분야에서는 업계 최고 수준의 리워드를 제공하는 와우 신용 카드 (제휴)를 빠르게 확장시키고 있습니다. 쿠팡 입점 사업자(셀러)의 사업 성장을 위해 쿠팡페이는 한국 최초의 baas (banking as a service) 기능을 사용하여 다양한 유동성 옵션을 구축하고 있습니다. 금융과 기술을 결합한 핀테크 분야 내에서 광범위한 경험을 쌓으며 업무하시길 원한다면, 쿠팡 페이와 함께 하세요. 우리는 고객이 계산하는 시간을 줄여서 고객이 좋아하는 것을 쇼핑하는 데 더 많은 시간을 보낼 수 있도록 돕습니다.</span></p> <p>&nbsp;</p> <p><strong>Job Overview</strong><strong>:&nbsp;</strong></p> <p>The Fintech Data Platform team at Coupang Pay analyzes approximately millions orders and payment data generated daily by Coupay in real-time through big data processing technologies like Spark, Flink, etc.</p> <p>Our Fintech.FDS, built on real-time analysis, is already hailed as a top-tier system in processing capacity and detection technology, ensuring utmost stability for transactions processed by Coupang Pay.</p> <p>Furthermore, our Fintech Data Platform team enables comprehensive analysis of various source data from Coupang, including orders, payments, Coupay sign-ups, and authentications, by establishing a seamless data flow for data collection, processing, analysis, and reporting. This paves the way for data-driven decision making.</p> <p>To drive continuous improvement and foster the development of diverse data-driven services, we are now opening a job position for Staff Data Engineer within the Fintech Data Platform team.</p> <p>If you possess a strong passion for solving issues for customers and have a keen interest in developing data-driven services, we encourage you to apply today.</p> <p>&nbsp;</p> <p>&nbsp;</p> <p><strong>Responsibilities:</strong></p> <ul> <li>Serve as a technical expert and influencer, increasing the productivity and effectiveness of the team by sharing your deep knowledge and experience.&nbsp;</li> <li>Work closely with other teams, identify their data needs, identify core KPI's to drive business growth, Influence source data producers to log key attributes.&nbsp;</li> <li>Design and build Coupang's next generation Data Lake, Data Warehouse and Systems.&nbsp;</li> <li>Design and build reliable data pipelines using modern distributed processing technologies such as Spark, Flink, Redshift, Iceberg, Presto, Hudi, Doris and ClickHouse with the ability to scale very large data volumes.&nbsp;&nbsp;</li> <li>Design and build tableau dashboards for Fintech Tech units and specific Business units.</li> <li>Foster, mentor, and enforce industry best practices in data architecture, design patterns, big data platform, storage, distributed compute and coding standard.&nbsp;&nbsp;</li> </ul> <p>&nbsp;</p> <p><strong>Qualifications:</strong></p> <ul> <li>Bachelor's degree and/or Master's degree in Computer Science or equivalent.&nbsp;</li> <li>10+ years of experience working on Bigdata with deep understanding of data modeling, data architecture, and data warehousing.&nbsp;</li> <li>Strong hands-on experience in distributed processing using Spark, and Hive.</li> <li>Extensive experience Bigdata Platform, Data Warehousing concepts and languages (SQL, NoSQL) and MPP databases.&nbsp;</li> <li>Problem solver, Pragmatic and Open-minded.</li> <li>Experience with handling large data volumes in a distributed environment.&nbsp;</li> <li>Hands on experience with Cloud Based Computing platforms such as AWS and understanding of scalability and reliability issues.&nbsp;</li> <li>Hands-on experience in one or more programming languages such as Java, Scala, Python or similar languages.&nbsp;</li> <li>Experience in collaborating with cross-functional teams to define mission, vision, and roadmaps.</li> <li>Experience in financial services industry is a plus.</li> <li>English speaker is a plus.</li> </ul> <p>&nbsp;</p> <p>&nbsp;</p> <p><strong>전형 절차 및</strong><strong>&nbsp;</strong><strong>안내</strong><strong>&nbsp;</strong><strong>사항</strong></p> <ul> <li><strong>전형</strong><strong>절차</strong> <ul> <li>서류전형 - 전화면접 - 대면(화상)면접 – 최종 합격</li> <li>전형절차는 직무별로 다르게 운영될 수 있으며, 일정 및 상황에 따라 변동될 수 있습니다.</li> <li>전형 일정 및 결과는 지원서에 등록하신 이메일로 개별 안내 드립니다.</li> </ul> </li> <li><strong>참고</strong><strong>사항</strong> <ul> <li>본 공고는 모집 완료 시 조기 마감될 수 있습니다.</li> <li>지원서 내용 중 허위사실이 있는 경우에는 합격이 취소될 수 있습니다.</li> <li>취업 보호 대상자(보훈대상자, 장애인 등)는 관련 법률에 따라 채용우대를 받을 수 있습니다.</li> <li>직급과 담당 업무 범위는 후보자의 전반적인 경력과 경험 등 제반사정을 고려하여 변경될 수 있습니다. 이러한 변경이 필요할 경우, 최종 합격 통지 전 적절한 시기에 후보자와 커뮤니케이션 될 예정입니다.</li> <li>채용 및 업무 수행과 관련하여 요구되는 법령상 자격이 갖추어지지 않은 경우 채용이 제한될 수 있습니다.</li> </ul> </li> </ul> <p><strong>개인정보 처리방침</strong>&nbsp;</p> <ul> <li>쿠팡 그룹은 입사지원자 개인정보 처리방침(아래 링크)에 따라 귀하의 개인정보를 수집하여 처리합니다.&nbsp;<a href="https://www.coupang.jobs/kr/privacy-policy/">https://www.coupang.jobs/kr/privacy-policy/</a></li> </ul> <p><strong>서류</strong><strong>&nbsp;</strong><strong>반환 정책</strong>&nbsp;</p> <ol> <li>본 고지는 『채용절차의공정화에관한법률』 제11조제6항에 따른 것 입니다.</li> <li>당사 채용에 응시한 구직자 중 최종 합격이 되지 못한 구직자는 『채용절차의 공정화에 관한 법률』에 따라 제출한 채용서류의 반환을 청구할 수 있음을 알려 드립니다. 다만, 홈페이지 또는 전자우편으로 제출된 경우나 구직자가 당사의 요구 없이 자발적으로 제출한 경우에는 그러하지 아니하며, 천재지변이나 그 밖에 당사에게 책임 없는 사유로 채용서류가 멸실된 경우에는 반환한 것으로 봅니다.</li> <li>위2항 본문에 따라 채용 서류 반환 청구를 하는 구직자는 채용 서류 반환 청구서 [채용절차의 공정화에 관한 법률 시행규칙 별지 제 3 호 서식]를 작성하여 이메일 (<a href="mailto:recruitingops@coupang.com">recruitingops@coupangpay.com</a>) 로 제출하면, 제출이 확인된 날로부터 14 일 이내에 지정한 주소지로 등기우편을 통하여 발송해 드립니다. 이 경우 등기우편요금은 수신자 부담으로 하게 되오니 유념하시기 바랍니다.&nbsp;</li> <li>당사는 위2항 본문에 따른 구직자의 반환 청구에 대비하여 채용 여부가 확정된 날로부터 180 일간 구직자가 제출한 채용서류 원본을 보관하게 되며, 그때까지 채용서류의 반환을 청구하지 아니할 경우에는 『개인정보 보호법』에 따라 지체 없이 채용서류 일체를 파기할 예정입니다.</li> <li>단, 위 1항 내지 4항의 내용은 대한민국의 노동 관계 법령이 적용되는 경우에만 적용됩니다. 그 이외의 경우에는 적용되지 않습니다.</li> </ol>

title: Mortgage Forward Deployed Engineer
full_description: <div class="content-intro"><p>Come build at the intersection of AI and fintech. At Ocrolus, we’re on a mission to help lenders automate workflows with confidence—streamlining how financial institutions evaluate borrowers and enabling faster, more accurate lending decisions.</p> <p>Our AI workflow and analytics platform for lenders is trusted at scale, processing nearly one million credit applications every month across small business, mortgage, and consumer lending. By integrating state-of-the-art open- and closed-source AI models with our human-in-the-loop verification engine, Ocrolus captures data from financial documents with over 99% accuracy. Thanks to our advanced fraud detection and comprehensive cash flow and income analytics, our customers achieve greater efficiency in risk management, and provide expanded access to credit—ultimately creating a more inclusive financial system.</p> <p>Trusted by more than 400 customers—including industry leaders like Better Mortgage, Brex, Enova, Nova Credit, PayPal, Plaid, SoFi, and Square—Ocrolus stands at the forefront of AI innovation in fintech. Join us, and help redefine how the world’s most innovative lenders do business.</p></div><p>We’re looking for a <strong>Mortgage Forward-Deployed Engineer (FDE)</strong> to join our team and be the bridge between our product and our customers. This is not your standard engineering role, you’ll be out in the field, working directly with clients, writing production code, and helping them reimagine how they run their business with our platform.&nbsp; You will be working directly with our Account Management team, implementation team and onboarding team.&nbsp; This role reports to the VP of Account Management.</p> <p>As an FDE, you’ll wear different hats: engineer, architect, and trusted advisor. One day you might be prototyping a custom integration; the next, you’re running a proof-of-value with a C-suite exec; the next, you’re feeding back product insights that shape the roadmap. If you like variety, ownership, and making real-world impact, you’ll love this role. This role will report to our Lead Forward-Deployed Engineer and be a part of the Account Management team.</p> <h4>&nbsp;</h4> <p><strong>What you'll do:</strong></p> <ul> <li>Act as a <strong>technical expert and trusted advisor</strong> during the post-sales process, embed with customer teams to understand their workflows and demonstrate how Ocrolus’ technology improves efficiency in mortgage origination.</li> <li>Review and consult on technical solutions, APIs, microservices, data pipelines, and integrations that solve real business problems.</li> <li>Build and support technical solutions and integrations for mortgage clients using Ocrolus, Encompass, and other POS/LOS mortgage platforms.</li> <li>Run proof-of-concepts and proof-of-value engagements that demonstrate ROI fast.</li> <li>Troubleshoot gnarly technical issues in live environments and get things back on track.</li> <li>Partner with the Account Management and Product teams to deliver value, and influence the roadmap.</li> <li>Travel occasionally (~30–50%) to customer sites and industry events</li> </ul> <h4>&nbsp;</h4> <p><strong>Who we're looking for: (Skill Sets and Qualifications)</strong></p> <ul> <li>3+ years of experience in software engineering, solutions engineering, or technical consulting.</li> <li>3+ years of a background in mortgage, or lending workflows.</li> <li>Strong understanding and experience with Encompass</li> <li>Strong consultative sales/account management approach—able to map technology to business challenges and drive change management conversations.</li> <li>Excellent communication and presentation skills, with experience engaging senior mortgage executives.</li> <li>Backend: Python; APIs; Encompass Developer Connect; SQL.</li> <li>Frontend: React/TypeScript; JavaScript with ICE Secure Scripting Framework (SSF) for Encompass custom form and workflow logic.</li> <li>Supporting: Familiarity with mortgage LOS/POS platforms (Encompass, Blend, SimpleNexus, Floify); Git; cloud basics (AWS/Azure).</li> <li>Thrive in fast-paced, ambiguous environments where you figure it out and get it done.</li> </ul> <h4>&nbsp;</h4> <p><strong>Nice to Have:</strong></p> <ul> <li>Experience with SaaS integrations or loan origination systems.</li> <li>Additional mortgage LOS/POS experience&nbsp;</li> <li>Exposure to AI/ML or generative AI.</li> </ul> <p>&nbsp;</p> <h3><strong>Why You’ll Love It Here</strong></h3> <ul> <li>You’ll have <strong>ownership from day one</strong>, our FDEs lead projects end-to-end.</li> <li>You’ll work directly with customers and see the immediate impact of what you build.</li> <li>You’ll shape the product by bringing the voice of the customer straight to engineering.</li> <li>You’ll work directly with a team focused on creating an excellent client experience</li> <li>You’ll have fun working with a highly proactive, collaborative team</li> </ul> <h4>&nbsp;</h4> <p><strong>Note:</strong></p> <p>The full-time salary range for this role is between <span data-sheets-value="{&quot;1&quot;:3,&quot;3&quot;:150000}" data-sheets-userformat="{&quot;2&quot;:12291,&quot;3&quot;:{&quot;1&quot;:4,&quot;2&quot;:&quot;\&quot;$\&quot;#,##0.00&quot;},&quot;4&quot;:{&quot;1&quot;:2,&quot;2&quot;:15597032},&quot;15&quot;:&quot;Calibri&quot;,&quot;16&quot;:12}">$150,000 </span>- $170,000 + commission + equity + benefits. Base pay offered may vary depending on job-related knowledge, skills, experience, and market location.</p> <p>Disclosure as required by N.Y.C. Admin. Code §§ 8-102 and 8-107(32) of the full time salary compensation range for this role when being hired into our offices in New York City.</p> <h4>&nbsp;</h4><div class="content-conclusion"><p><strong>Life at Ocrolus</strong></p> <p>We’re a team of builders, thinkers, and problem solvers who care deeply about our mission — and each other. As a fast-growing, remote-first company, we offer an environment where you can grow your skills, take ownership of your work, and make a meaningful impact.</p> <p><strong>Our culture is grounded in four core values:</strong><strong><br></strong><strong> Empathy – </strong>Understand and serve with compassion<br><strong> Curiosity – </strong>Explore new ideas and question the status quo<strong><br></strong><strong> Humility – </strong>Listen, be grounded, and remain open-minded<br><strong> Ownership – </strong>Love what you do, work hard, and deliver excellence</p> <p>We believe diverse perspectives drive better outcomes. That’s why we’re committed to fostering an inclusive workplace where everyone has a seat at the table, regardless of race, gender, gender identity, age, disability, national origin, or any other protected characteristic.</p> <p>We look forward to building the future of lending together.</p></div>

title: Accounting Operations Data Engineer

Bottomline is a global leader in business payments and cash management, with over 35 years of experience and moving more than $16 trillion in payments annually. We're looking for passionate individuals to join our team and help drive impactful results for our customers.
