title: Data Engineer
full_description: <p>.</p> <table width="682"> <tbody> <tr> <td width="682"> <p><strong>About Nscale</strong></p> </td> </tr> <tr> <td width="682"> <p>Nscale is the GPU cloud engineered for AI. We provide cost-effective, high-performance infrastructure for AI start-ups and large enterprise customers.&nbsp; Nscale enables AI-focused companies to achieve superior results by reducing the complexity of AI development. Our GPU cloud bolsters technical capabilities and directly supports strategic business outcomes, including cost management, rapid innovation, and environmental responsibility.</p> <p>&nbsp;</p> <p>&nbsp;</p> <p>We thrive on a culture of relentless innovation, ownership, and accountability, where every team member takes pride in their work and drives it with excellence and urgency. As an Nscaler, you’ll build trust through openness and transparency, where everyone is inspired to do their best work. If you join our team, you’ll be contributing to building the technology that powers the future.</p> </td> </tr> </tbody> </table> <p>&nbsp;</p> <p>&nbsp;</p> <p>&nbsp;</p> <table width="682"> <tbody> <tr> <td width="682"> <p><strong>About the Role (Job Purpose)</strong></p> </td> </tr> <tr> <td width="682"> <p>We’re looking for a <strong>Data Engineer</strong> to help design, build, and operate the data foundations that underpin Nscale’s platform, internal operations, and customer-facing capabilities.</p> <p>This is a <strong>high-impact, early-stage role</strong>. You’ll work closely with Operations, Infrastructure, Platform Engineering, Product, and Commercial teams to turn raw operational signals — from GPUs, clusters, customers, and internal systems — into reliable, scalable data products that ensure delivery at a previously unseen pace. You’ll help define <em>how data is collected, modelled, served, and trusted</em> across the company, and have the opportunity to build on Palantir Foundry, as well as other products.</p> <p>This role is ideal for someone who enjoys building data systems from first principles, thrives in ambiguous environments, and wants to see their work directly influence product decisions, platform reliability, and customer outcomes.</p> </td> </tr> </tbody> </table> <p>&nbsp;</p> <p>&nbsp;</p> <table width="681"> <tbody> <tr> <td width="681"> <p><strong>What You’ll be Doing (Responsibilities)</strong></p> </td> </tr> <tr> <td width="681"> <h4><strong>Data Platform &amp; Architecture</strong></h4> <p>●&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp; Design and build scalable, reliable data pipelines that ingest data from infrastructure, platform services, and business systems.<br><br></p> <p>●&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp; Define data models and schemas that support operational workflows and use cases across the business, monitoring, and analytics.<br><br></p> <p>●&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp; Clean, transform and structure the data to create a digital twin of Nscale.<br><br></p> <p>●&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp; Implement permissioning and manage access and security of the Foundry implementation.<br><br></p> <h4><strong>Data Products &amp; Enablement</strong></h4> <p>●&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp; Create trusted datasets and metrics that power workflows and processes, internal tools, and customer-facing insights.<br><br></p> <p>●&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp; Enable self-serve analytics by establishing clear data contracts, documentation, and semantic layers.<br><br></p> <p>●&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp; Build use cases including but not limited to capacity planning, cost optimisation, reliability analysis, and customer reporting to drive our business forward.<br><br></p> <p>●&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp; Collaborate with Product and Commercial teams to translate real-world questions into robust data solutions.<br><br></p> <h4><strong>Reliability, Quality &amp; Governance</strong></h4> <p>●&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp; Implement data quality checks, monitoring, and alerting to ensure data correctness and availability.<br><br></p> <p>●&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp; Codify data lineage, freshness, and consistency across systems.<br><br></p> <p>●&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp; Establish best practices around data versioning, access control, and governance appropriate for a fast-scaling company.<br><br></p> <p>●&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp; Continuously improve system resilience and observability.<br><br></p> <h4><strong>Early-Stage Ownership &amp; Growth</strong></h4> <p>●&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp; Take end-to-end ownership of projects, from design through to production and iteration.<br><br></p> <p>●&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp; Help define standards, tooling, and ways of working for data at Nscale.<br><br></p> <p>●&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp; Contribute to technical decision-making as the company scales its platform and customer base.<br><br></p> <p>●&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp; Act as a thought partner to engineers and operators, not just a service function.</p> </td> </tr> </tbody> </table> <p>&nbsp;</p> <table width="679"> <thead> <tr> <td width="679"> <p><strong>About You (Skills / Qualifications)</strong></p> </td> </tr> </thead> <tbody> <tr> <td width="679"> <p>●&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp; Deep, hands-on experience building in <strong>Palantir Foundry</strong>, including ontology modelling, pipeline development, API integration, and large-scale data platform design.</p> <p>●&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp; Strong proficiency in <strong>Python</strong>, with experience applying data engineering libraries and frameworks (e.g. Spark, PySpark, Dask, pandas) to work with large, complex datasets.</p> <p>●&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp; Familiarity with <strong>API-driven data integration</strong>, including REST, GraphQL, and Foundry Action APIs.</p> <p>●&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp; Practical experience working in <strong>Git-based development workflows</strong>, including code reviews, version control, and CI/CD pipelines.</p> <p>●&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp; Comfort working in ambiguous, early-stage environments where requirements evolve quickly.</p> <p>●&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp; Strong communication skills — able to explain data concepts clearly to technical and non-technical stakeholders.</p> <p>●&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp; A bias toward ownership, pragmatism, and shipping useful solutions.</p> <p><strong>Nice to Have</strong></p> <p>●&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp; Experience with cloud platforms (AWS, GCP, Azure) and infrastructure telemetry.</p> <p>●&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp; Familiarity with distributed systems, monitoring data, or usage-based billing data.</p> <p>●&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp; Experience supporting customer-facing data products or platforms.</p> </td> </tr> </tbody> </table> <p>&nbsp;</p> <p>&nbsp;</p> <table width="679"> <thead> <tr> <td width="679"> <p><strong>What We Can Offer You</strong></p> </td> </tr> </thead> <tbody> <tr> <td width="679"> <p>At Nscale, you'll find a <strong>collaborative, supportive, and innovative environment</strong> where your contributions spark real impact. We're building something extraordinary, and we want you at the core.</p> <p>●&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp; Highly <strong>competitive package</strong> (base + equity) with <strong>reviews every 12 months</strong>. 🚀</p> <p>●&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp; Join the <strong>fastest-growing tech startup</strong>, your chance to push boundaries, collaborate with brilliant minds, and make your mark on cutting-edge AI. ✨</p> <p>●&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp; Expect a <strong>dynamic progression plan</strong> tailored to your ambitions. Grow by trying new things, leading, challenging the status quo, and owning your impact, always with our full support.</p> <p>●&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp; <strong>Human-First Flexibility:</strong> We treat you as <strong>humans first</strong>. 🫶🏽 Our flexible workplace trusts Nscalers to deliver, giving you the autonomy to shape your day around life's moments.</p> <p>●&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp; Join our <strong>thriving remote-first team</strong>. Geography is no barrier to impact or connection. We build seamless virtual collaboration, empowering you, wherever you work.</p> </td> </tr> </tbody> </table> <p>&nbsp;</p> <table width="680"> <tbody> <tr> <td width="680"> <p><strong>Interview Process</strong></p> </td> </tr> <tr> <td width="680"> <p>&nbsp;</p> <p>●&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp; Phone screen</p> <p>●&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp; Coding interviews</p> <p>●&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp; Hiring Manager interview</p> </td> </tr> </tbody> </table> <p>&nbsp;</p> <p>&nbsp;</p> <table width="671"> <tbody> <tr> <td width="671"> <p><strong>Equal Opportunities Statement</strong></p> </td> </tr> <tr> <td width="671"> <p>We strongly encourage applications from people of colour, the LGBTQ+ community, people with disabilities, neurodivergent people, parents, carers, and people from lower socio-economic backgrounds.</p> <p>If there’s anything we can do to accommodate your specific situation, please let us know.</p> <p>The responsibilities outlined in this job description are not exhaustive and are intended to provide a general overview of the position. The employee may be required to perform additional duties, tasks, and responsibilities as assigned by management, consistent with the skills and qualifications required for the role.</p> </td> </tr> </tbody> </table> <p>&nbsp;</p> <p>&nbsp;</p><div class="content-conclusion"><p><em>For information on how Nscale handles candidate personal data, please see our Employee &amp; Candidate Privacy Notice:&nbsp;<a href="https://drive.google.com/file/d/1QK5Yg04WHD9K9IAtJgQWubJZC9oLvatK/view?usp=sharing" target="_blank" data-saferedirecturl="https://www.google.com/url?q=https://drive.google.com/file/d/1QK5Yg04WHD9K9IAtJgQWubJZC9oLvatK/view?usp%3Dsharing&amp;source=gmail&amp;ust=1765375172804000&amp;usg=AOvVaw2Ncte4rmlGl8OKuFuDgDtx">Here.</a></em></p></div>

title: Data Engineer (Forward Deployed)
full_description: <p>&nbsp;</p> <div> <p><strong>About Nscale</strong></p> <p>Nscale is the GPU cloud engineered for AI. We provide cost-effective, high-performance infrastructure for AI start-ups and large enterprise customers.&nbsp; Nscale enables AI-focused companies to achieve superior results by reducing the complexity of AI development. Our GPU cloud bolsters technical capabilities and directly supports strategic business outcomes, including cost management, rapid innovation, and environmental responsibility.</p> <p>We thrive on a culture of relentless innovation, ownership, and accountability, where every team member takes pride in their work and drives it with excellence and urgency. As an Nscaler, you’ll build trust through openness and transparency, where everyone is inspired to do their best work. If you join our team, you’ll be contributing to building the technology that powers the future.</p> <p>&nbsp;</p> <p><strong>About the Role</strong></p> <p>We’re looking for a Data Engineer to help design, build, and operate the data foundations that underpin Nscale’s platform, internal operations, and customer-facing capabilities.</p> <p>This is a high-impact, early-stage role. You’ll work closely with Operations, Infrastructure, Platform Engineering, Product, and Commercial teams to turn raw operational signals — from GPUs, clusters, customers, and internal systems — into reliable, scalable data products that ensure delivery at a previously unseen pace. You’ll help define&nbsp;<em>how data is collected, modelled, served, and trusted</em>&nbsp;across the company, and have the opportunity to build on Palantir Foundry, as well as other products.</p> <p>This role is ideal for someone who enjoys building data systems from first principles, thrives in ambiguous environments, and wants to see their work directly influence product decisions, platform reliability, and customer outcomes.</p> <p>&nbsp;</p> <p><strong>What you'll be doing</strong></p> <h4><strong>Data Platform &amp; Architecture</strong></h4> <ul> <li>Design and build scalable, reliable data pipelines that ingest data from infrastructure, platform services, and business systems.</li> <li>Define data models and schemas that support operational workflows and use cases across the business, monitoring, and analytics.</li> <li>Clean, transform and structure the data to create a digital twin of Nscale.</li> <li>Implement permissioning and manage access and security of the Foundry implementation.</li> </ul> <h4><strong>Data Products &amp; Governance</strong></h4> <ul> <li>Implement data quality checks, monitoring, and alerting to ensure data correctness and availability.</li> <li>Codify data lineage, freshness, and consistency across systems.</li> <li>Establish best practices around data versioning, access control, and governance appropriate for a fast-scaling company.</li> <li>Continuously improve system resilience and observability.</li> </ul> <h4><strong>Early-Stage Ownership &amp; Growth</strong></h4> <ul> <li>Take end-to-end ownership of projects, from design through to production and iteration.</li> <li>Help define standards, tooling, and ways of working for data at Nscale.</li> <li>Contribute to technical decision-making as the company scales its platform and customer base.</li> <li>Act as a thought partner to engineers and operators, not just a service function.</li> </ul> <p>&nbsp;</p> <p><strong>About You&nbsp;</strong></p> <ul> <li>Deep, hands-on experience building in Palantir Foundry, including ontology modelling, pipeline development, API integration, and large-scale data platform design.</li> <li>Strong proficiency in Python, with experience applying data engineering libraries and frameworks (e.g. Spark, PySpark, Dask, pandas) to work with large, complex datasets.</li> <li>Familiarity with API-driven data integration, including REST, GraphQL, and Foundry Action APIs.</li> <li>Practical experience working in Git-based development workflows, including code reviews, version control, and CI/CD pipelines.</li> <li>Comfort working in ambiguous, early-stage environments where requirements evolve quickly.</li> <li>Strong communication skills — able to explain data concepts clearly to technical and non-technical stakeholders.</li> <li>A bias toward ownership, pragmatism, and shipping useful solutions.</li> </ul> <p><strong>Nice to Have</strong></p> <ul> <li>Experience with cloud platforms (AWS, GCP, Azure) and infrastructure telemetry.</li> <li>Familiarity with distributed systems, monitoring data, or usage-based billing data.</li> <li>Experience supporting customer-facing data products or platforms.</li> </ul> <p>&nbsp;</p> <p><strong>What we can offer you&nbsp;</strong></p> <p>At Nscale, you'll find a collaborative, supportive, and innovative environment where your contributions spark real impact. We're building something extraordinary, and we want you at the core.</p> <ul> <li>Highly competitive package (base + equity) with reviews every 12 months. 🚀</li> <li>Join the fastest-growing tech startup, your chance to push boundaries, collaborate with brilliant minds, and make your mark on cutting-edge AI. ✨</li> <li>Expect a dynamic progression plan tailored to your ambitions. Grow by trying new things, leading, challenging the status quo, and owning your impact, always with our full support.</li> <li>Human-First Flexibility: We treat you as humans first. 🫶🏽 Our flexible workplace trusts Nscalers to deliver, giving you the autonomy to shape your day around life's moments.</li> </ul> <p>&nbsp;</p> <p><strong>Equal Opportunities Statement</strong></p> <p>We strongly encourage applications from people of colour, the LGBTQ+ community, people with disabilities, neurodivergent people, parents, carers, and people from lower socio-economic backgrounds.</p> <p>If there’s anything we can do to accommodate your specific situation, please let us know.</p> <p>The responsibilities outlined in this job description are not exhaustive and are intended to provide a general overview of the position. The employee may be required to perform additional duties, tasks, and responsibilities as assigned by management, consistent with the skills and qualifications required for the role.</p> </div><div class="content-conclusion"><p><em>For information on how Nscale handles candidate personal data, please see our Employee &amp; Candidate Privacy Notice:&nbsp;<a href="https://drive.google.com/file/d/1QK5Yg04WHD9K9IAtJgQWubJZC9oLvatK/view?usp=sharing" target="_blank" data-saferedirecturl="https://www.google.com/url?q=https://drive.google.com/file/d/1QK5Yg04WHD9K9IAtJgQWubJZC9oLvatK/view?usp%3Dsharing&amp;source=gmail&amp;ust=1765375172804000&amp;usg=AOvVaw2Ncte4rmlGl8OKuFuDgDtx">Here.</a></em></p></div>

title: Google Cloud Platform Data Engineer
full_description: <div style="font-size: 10pt; font-family: 'Tahoma';"> <p>(ID: 2026-2740)</p> <br> <p><strong>Axle Informatics</strong> is a bioscience and information technology company advancing translational research through biomedical informatics, data science, artificial intelligence, and intelligent automation. We partner with research centers, healthcare organizations, and government agencies around the globe to transform complex data into actionable insights that accelerate scientific discovery and improve decision-making. Our multidisciplinary teams of experts in biomedical science, software engineering, data engineering, AI, automation, and program management develop innovative technologies, modernize data ecosystems, and create scalable solutions that enable the next generation of research and healthcare innovation.</p> <p>&nbsp;</p> <div class="ck-content" style="font-family: Tahoma; font-size: 10pt;" data-wrapper="true"> <div style="font-family: Tahoma; font-size: 10pt;"> <p style="border-style: none; margin: 15.0pt 0in 5.0pt; padding: 0in;"><span style="color: #000000; font-family: Tahoma; font-size: 10pt;"><strong>Role Overview</strong></span></p> </div> <p style="margin: 4.0pt 0in; text-align: start;"><span style="color: #000000; font-family: Tahoma; font-size: 10pt;">We are seeking an experienced GCP Data Engineer to own the technical pipeline infrastructure for a Finance Transformation program. The successful candidate will design and implement the shared pipeline architecture that underpins the program's data product delivery, building the Workday and Oracle Finance connectors and owning the unified data model, lineage graph, and real time event capture infrastructure.</span></p> <p style="margin: 4.0pt 0in; text-align: start;"><span style="color: #000000; font-family: Tahoma; font-size: 10pt;">This is a senior, hands on engineering role requiring deep expertise in GCP pipeline services, Workday and Oracle API extraction, and data lineage architecture. The candidate will establish the GCP native pipeline pattern used by both domain connectors, pair with each domain analyst on their source specific extraction builds, and ensure the unified data model in Cloud SQL serves all three reporting lenses -- Function, Supervisory Organization, and Job Family -- from a single join layer.</span></p> <p style="margin: 4.0pt 0in; text-align: start;"><span style="color: #000000; font-family: Tahoma; font-size: 10pt;">The successful candidate will combine engineering rigor with practical delivery focus, able to move between architectural design and production build, and provide clear technical guidance to domain analysts who own the domain specific extraction logic.</span></p> <div style="font-family: Tahoma; font-size: 10pt;"> <p style="border-style: none; margin: 15.0pt 0in 5.0pt; padding: 0in;"><span style="color: #000000; font-family: Tahoma; font-size: 10pt;"><strong>Key Responsibilities</strong></span></p> </div> <ul> <li class="ck-list-marker-font-size ck-list-marker-color ck-list-marker-font-family" data-list-item-id="e108efed1e6cd9f4b39f449bf367e3bbc"> <p style="margin: 2.5pt 0in 2.5pt 0; text-align: start;"><span style="color: #000000; font-family: Tahoma; font-size: 10pt;">Design and implement the shared GCP native pipeline architecture, establishing the Cloud Dataflow and Cloud Composer pattern that both HR and Finance connectors follow, with pipeline health monitoring, alerting, and audit trail via Cloud Monitoring and Cloud Logging.</span></p> </li> <li class="ck-list-marker-font-size ck-list-marker-color ck-list-marker-font-family" data-list-item-id="e0207a20eaa6577fc9af70c192640d1d9"> <p style="margin: 2.5pt 0in 2.5pt 0; text-align: start;"><span style="color: #000000; font-family: Tahoma; font-size: 10pt;">Build the Workday connector using RaaS for bulk extraction and REST APIs for real time lifecycle event capture, and configure Firebase Realtime Database to reflect classification and cost changes within minutes of each lifecycle event.</span></p> </li> <li class="ck-list-marker-font-size ck-list-marker-color ck-list-marker-font-family" data-list-item-id="e36c8fd60a584b511088ee57728a0d731"> <p style="margin: 2.5pt 0in 2.5pt 0; text-align: start;"><span style="color: #000000; font-family: Tahoma; font-size: 10pt;">Build the Oracle Finance connector using REST APIs with SOAP fallback, applying basis tagging at ingestion so the same pipeline serves statutory, management, Lloyd's, and regulatory reporting without rerunning the extract.</span></p> </li> <li class="ck-list-marker-font-size ck-list-marker-color ck-list-marker-font-family" data-list-item-id="e20f778cae742f824c195f53f103090c2"> <p style="margin: 2.5pt 0in 2.5pt 0; text-align: start;"><span style="color: #000000; font-family: Tahoma; font-size: 10pt;">Pair with each domain analyst on their source specific extraction builds, providing GCP architectural guidance while the analyst owns domain specific field mapping, validation, and sign off.</span></p> </li> <li class="ck-list-marker-font-size ck-list-marker-color ck-list-marker-font-family" data-list-item-id="e89c72870a8c9a3bca1d03b12d217caeb"> <p style="margin: 2.5pt 0in 2.5pt 0; text-align: start;"><span style="color: #000000; font-family: Tahoma; font-size: 10pt;">Design and maintain the Cloud SQL unified data model, including the HR to Finance mapping table and the join layer that serves all three reporting lenses -- Function, Supervisory Organization, and Job Family -- from a single data model.</span></p> </li> <li class="ck-list-marker-font-size ck-list-marker-color ck-list-marker-font-family" data-list-item-id="ed5dc58f563f031ff5082d5837c0312d3"> <p style="margin: 2.5pt 0in 2.5pt 0; text-align: start;"><span style="color: #000000; font-family: Tahoma; font-size: 10pt;">Implement the lineage graph in Cloud SQL, publish definitions and lineage via REST API to downstream components, and configure Firebase to stream audit events continuously, logging every access, change, and approval.</span></p> </li> <li class="ck-list-marker-font-size ck-list-marker-color ck-list-marker-font-family" data-list-item-id="ed38553445cf86b117c06673c889bb128"> <p style="margin: 2.5pt 0in 2.5pt 0; text-align: start;"><span style="color: #000000; font-family: Tahoma; font-size: 10pt;">Ensure the data model supports DirectQuery from Power BI Embedded and that all pipeline solutions comply with client governance, security, and segregation of duties requirements.</span></p> </li> </ul> <div style="font-family: Tahoma; font-size: 10pt;"> <p style="border-style: none; margin: 15.0pt 0in 5.0pt; padding: 0in;"><span style="color: #000000; font-family: Tahoma; font-size: 10pt;"><strong>Required Qualifications</strong></span></p> </div> <ul> <li class="ck-list-marker-font-size ck-list-marker-color ck-list-marker-font-family" data-list-item-id="e568292465a74a573aa69db3003d9f7d0"> <p style="margin: 2.5pt 0in 2.5pt 0; text-align: start;"><span style="color: #000000; font-family: Tahoma; font-size: 10pt;">5+ years of GCP engineering experience, with deep expertise in Cloud Dataflow, Cloud Composer, and Cloud SQL, including designing and operating production data pipelines in these services.</span></p> </li> <li class="ck-list-marker-font-size ck-list-marker-color ck-list-marker-font-family" data-list-item-id="ea08a799e2c678405e4010f87bd78f045"> <p style="margin: 2.5pt 0in 2.5pt 0; text-align: start;"><span style="color: #000000; font-family: Tahoma; font-size: 10pt;">Demonstrable experience extracting data from Workday using RaaS bulk export and REST APIs for real time event capture, with an understanding of Workday supervisory org and worker classification data structures.</span></p> </li> <li class="ck-list-marker-font-size ck-list-marker-color ck-list-marker-font-family" data-list-item-id="e4c335d6557c56bc539768be08eb80c3d"> <p style="margin: 2.5pt 0in 2.5pt 0; text-align: start;"><span style="color: #000000; font-family: Tahoma; font-size: 10pt;">Demonstrable experience extracting data from Oracle Fusion Cloud using REST APIs, with SOAP API awareness as a fallback pattern.</span></p> </li> <li class="ck-list-marker-font-size ck-list-marker-color ck-list-marker-font-family" data-list-item-id="efb9a38fa50f26f39eb9931e2177a9623"> <p style="margin: 2.5pt 0in 2.5pt 0; text-align: start;"><span style="color: #000000; font-family: Tahoma; font-size: 10pt;">Experience designing relational data models in Cloud SQL that serve multiple reporting lenses from a single join layer.</span></p> </li> <li class="ck-list-marker-font-size ck-list-marker-color ck-list-marker-font-family" data-list-item-id="e562b9ea355540b81935ac9a62a48a9ec"> <p style="margin: 2.5pt 0in 2.5pt 0; text-align: start;"><span style="color: #000000; font-family: Tahoma; font-size: 10pt;">Strong understanding of data lineage implementation -- specifically building lineage graphs in a relational store and surfacing them via REST API to downstream applications.</span></p> </li> <li class="ck-list-marker-font-size ck-list-marker-color ck-list-marker-font-family" data-list-item-id="e562fa4e65291ade73f7c40ba60594f47"> <p style="margin: 2.5pt 0in 2.5pt 0; text-align: start;"><span style="color: #000000; font-family: Tahoma; font-size: 10pt;">Strong troubleshooting, root cause analysis, and production support skills.</span></p> </li> <li class="ck-list-marker-font-size ck-list-marker-color ck-list-marker-font-family" data-list-item-id="e56ab2cddaa440721eba883e8cdfe59c3"> <p style="margin: 2.5pt 0in 2.5pt 0; text-align: start;"><span style="color: #000000; font-family: Tahoma; font-size: 10pt;">Excellent communication skills and ability to guide domain SMEs on technical architecture decisions.</span></p> </li> </ul> <div style="font-family: Tahoma; font-size: 10pt;"> <p style="border-style: none; margin: 15.0pt 0in 5.0pt; padding: 0in;"><span style="color: #000000; font-family: Tahoma; font-size: 10pt;"><strong>Required Technical Skills</strong></span></p> </div> <ul> <li class="ck-list-marker-font-size ck-list-marker-color ck-list-marker-font-family" data-list-item-id="e79de97de9ffd9508f8bc6cbcf7317ee5"> <p style="margin: 2.5pt 0in 2.5pt 0; title: Data Engineer (Starlink)
full_description: <div class="content-intro"><p>SpaceX was founded under the belief that a future where humanity is out exploring the stars is fundamentally more exciting than one where we are not. Today SpaceX is actively developing the technologies to make this possible, with the ultimate goal of&nbsp;enabling human life on Mars.</p></div><h1 style="line-height: 1.2;"><span style="font-size: 10pt;"><strong>DATA ENGINEER (STARLINK)</strong></span></h1> <p style="line-height: 1.2;">At SpaceX, we’re leveraging our experience building rockets and spacecraft to deploy Starlink, the world’s most advanced broadband internet system. Starlink is the world’s largest satellite constellation, delivering fast, reliable internet to more than 10 million users worldwide. We design, build, test, and operate the full stack — satellites, user terminals, and the software that ties it all together.</p> <p style="line-height: 1.2;">As a Data Engineer on the Starlink Enterprise team, you will improve the quality of data flowing into our commercial systems and build the integrations and AI agents that turn that cleaner data into better decisions and faster execution — from pipeline generation through closed revenue and ongoing enablement. You will partner with Enterprise sales, channel and reseller operations, sales ops, and enablement to fix messy inputs, reduce manual cleanup, and ship automations that make go-to-market workflows more reliable.</p> <h2 style="line-height: 1.2;"><span style="font-size: 10pt;"><strong>RESPONSIBILITIES:</strong></span></h2> <ul> <li>Improve data hygiene and quality across go-to-market systems — identify bad, missing, duplicate, and stale records; implement validation, enrichment, and remediation so higher-quality data enters our system of record, opportunity, partner/reseller, and enablement workflows</li> <li>Raise the quality of inputs to AI agents and analytics so outputs (recommendations, deal status, enablement answers, reporting) are more accurate, trustworthy, and actionable</li> <li>Build integrations across internal tools and external systems so deal, customer, and partner context flows cleanly between sales, channel ops, and operational platforms — reducing manual re-entry and preventing quality issues at the source</li> <li>Develop Model Context Protocol (MCP) servers and related interfaces that connect AI assistants (including Grok) to internal and external go-to-market systems, with an emphasis on reliable, well-scoped access to the right data and tools</li> <li>Design, ship, and iterate AI agents that power Starlink Enterprise go-to-market workflows</li> <li>Contribute to AI-powered applications and automations that accelerate lead-to-revenue cycles, working within the additional datasets and systems architecture owned by the team</li> <li>Instrument data quality and agent performance with monitoring and feedback loops — catching regressions early and tying improvements to outcomes (e.g., cleaner pipeline, faster cycle times, higher adoption and trust)</li> <li>Partner closely with sales, sales ops, channel/reseller ops, and enablement to turn recurring data-quality problems and manual processes into durable automations and standards</li> <li>Create reusable tools, checks, and documentation that help the team maintain data quality and extend agents over time</li> </ul> <h2 style="line-height: 1.2;"><span style="font-size: 10pt;"><strong>BASIC QUALIFICATIONS:</strong></span></h2> <ul> <li>Bachelor’s degree in computer science, data science, physics, mathematics, or another STEM discipline</li> <li>1+ years of professional data engineering or software engineering experience (internship experience applicable)</li> <li>1+ years of development experience in Python or another modern programming language (SQL, etc.)</li> </ul> <h2 style="line-height: 1.2;"><span style="font-size: 10pt;"><strong>PREFERRED SKILLS AND EXPERIENCE:</strong></span></h2> <ul> <li style="line-height: 1.2;">Experience improving CRM data quality (deduping, enrichment, validation rules, field completeness, source-of-truth discipline)</li> <li style="line-height: 1.2;">Experience building AI agents, LLM-powered applications, or tool-using assistants that integrate with real business systems (APIs, CRMs, ticketing, data warehouses)</li> <li style="line-height: 1.2;">Experience designing APIs, webhooks, or integration layers that connect heterogeneous systems and keep data consistent across them</li> <li style="line-height: 1.2;">Familiarity with Model Context Protocol (MCP) or similar patterns for exposing tools and data sources to AI systems</li> <li style="line-height: 1.2;">Experience with CRM and go-to-market tooling; familiarity with Clay, HubSpot, Apollo, or similar sales engagement / enrichment platforms is a plus</li> <li style="line-height: 1.2;">Professional experience supporting sales, sales ops, channel/reseller, or revenue operations workflows with data and automation</li> <li style="line-height: 1.2;">Experience with SQL and modern data tooling; exposure to Grok, internal AI platforms, or comparable LLM developer tooling is a plus</li> <li style="line-height: 1.2;">Experience with agent evaluation, guardrails, permissions, and human-in-the-loop patterns for production AI systems</li> <li style="line-height: 1.2;">Ability to thrive in a fast-changing environment, take initiative on ambiguous problems, and develop new expertise as the product and stack evolve</li> </ul> <p><strong>ADDITIONAL REQUIREMENTS:</strong></p> <ul> <li data-uuid="2f22e95e-0037-4c59-860a-40599b2d0422">Must be available to work extended hours and weekends as needed</li> <li data-uuid="db0c2e6c-7390-4107-a4c3-dc07d01726b7">Willingness to travel to customer sites, other SpaceX locations, and events as needed</li> </ul><div class="content-conclusion"><p><strong>ITAR REQUIREMENTS:</strong></p> <ul> <li>To conform to U.S. Government export regulations, applicant must be a (i) U.S. citizen or national, (ii) U.S. lawful, permanent resident (aka green card holder), (iii) Refugee under 8 U.S.C. § 1157, or (iv) Asylee under 8 U.S.C. § 1158, or be eligible to obtain the required authorizations from the U.S. Department of State. Learn more about the ITAR <a href="https://www.pmddtc.state.gov/?id=ddtc_kb_article_page&amp;sys_id=24d528fddbfc930044f9ff621f961987">here</a>. &nbsp;</li> </ul> <p>SpaceX is an Equal Opportunity Employer; employment with SpaceX is governed on the basis of merit, competence and qualifications and will not be influenced in any manner by race, color, religion, gender, national origin/ethnicity, veteran status, disability status, age, sexual orientation, gender identity, marital status, mental or physical disability or any other legally protected status.</p> <p>Applicants wishing to view a copy of SpaceX’s Affirmative Action Plan for veterans and individuals with disabilities, or applicants requiring reasonable accommodation to the application/interview process should reach out to&nbsp;<a href="mailto:EEOCompliance@spacex.com">EEOCompliance@spacex.com</a><em>.&nbsp;</em></p></div>

title: Data Engineer (Starlink Go-To-Market)
full_description: <div class="content-intro"><p>SpaceX was founded under the belief that a future where humanity is out exploring the stars is fundamentally more exciting than one where we are not. Today SpaceX is actively developing the technologies to make this possible, with the ultimate goal of&nbsp;enabling human life on Mars.</p></div><p><strong>DATA ENGINEER (STARLINK GO-TO-MARKET)</strong></p> <p>Starlink is the world’s largest satellite constellation delivering high-speed, low-latency broadband to customers around the globe. As the Data Engineer on the Starlink Enterprise team, you will design and build the commercial operating system that enables the business to scale. You will identify operational bottlenecks across the lead-to-cash lifecycle, architect scalable systems and AI-powered workflows, and serve as the connective tissue between business strategy and technical execution. Your work ensures commercial systems operate as one connected platform rather than a collection of independent tools, driving higher productivity, cleaner execution, and measurable revenue impact.</p> <p><strong>RESPONSIBILITIES:</strong></p> <p><strong>Commercial Strategy &amp; Systems Architecture</strong></p> <ul> <li>Identify systemic operational bottlenecks that limit growth, reseller productivity, customer experience, or execution velocity across the lead-to-cash lifecycle</li> <li>Translate business strategy into scalable operational capabilities, systems, and cross-functional roadmaps</li> <li>Design and evolve the long-term architecture for Starlink’s commercial operating system across Customer Relationship Management platforms, data platforms, fulfillment, billing, support systems, AI, and reporting</li> <li>Design how commercial systems work together as one connected operating model rather than independent applications</li> <li>Prioritize systems, automation, and process initiatives based on business impact, scalability, and execution velocity</li> <li>Own administration, configuration, and continuous improvement of core go-to-market tools including HubSpot, Clay, Apollo, and related commercial platforms</li> </ul> <p><strong>AI &amp; Intelligent Automation</strong></p> <ul> <li>Design and deploy AI agents and workflow automation that eliminate repetitive work, improve decision quality, and increase execution speed across Sales, Account Management, Channel, and Operations</li> <li>Embed AI throughout commercial workflows where it meaningfully improves execution speed, operational leverage, or customer experience</li> <li>Establish best practices for scalable AI adoption across commercial teams</li> <li>Continuously evaluate emerging AI capabilities and identify high-return opportunities to improve how the organization operates</li> <li>Partner with the Operations Engineer on integrations, Model Context Protocol servers, and data foundations so AI agents operate on clean, trusted context</li> </ul> <p><strong>Commercial Intelligence &amp; Decision Support</strong></p> <ul> <li>Partner with Data Engineering to improve commercial data quality, system integrations, and operational visibility</li> <li>Build executive and team visibility into the commercial funnel, reseller health, operational efficiency, and revenue performance</li> <li>Enable data-driven decision making by ensuring commercial teams operate from a trusted, connected source of truth</li> <li>Drive tool adoption, measure usage and business value, and continuously retire low-value tools in favor of what actually moves revenue metrics</li> <li>Collaborate closely with Sales Operations, Channel Operations, Sales Enablement, and leadership to ensure systems and automation support faster ramp, higher win rates, and scalable channel execution</li> </ul> <p><strong>BASIC QUALIFICATIONS:</strong></p> <ul> <li>Bachelor’s degree in a technical, analytical, or business-related field</li> <li>4+ years of experience in go-to-market engineering, revenue operations, sales systems, business systems, or a related technical operations role</li> <li>1+ years of hands-on experience building workflow automation, system integrations, or AI-powered tools in a commercial environment</li> </ul> <p><strong>PREFERRED SKILLS AND EXPERIENCE:</strong></p> <ul> <li>Experience designing and owning commercial technology stacks (Customer Relationship Management, enrichment, sequencing, billing, and support systems)</li> <li>Strong proficiency with HubSpot, Clay, Apollo, or similar modern go-to-market platforms</li> <li>Demonstrated experience building and deploying AI agents or intelligent automation that drive measurable productivity gains</li> <li>Experience architecting how multiple commercial systems work together as a unified operating model</li> <li>Familiarity with application programming interfaces, data pipelines, Model Context Protocol servers, or integration platforms</li> <li>Proven ability to identify operational bottlenecks and translate them into scalable technical solutions</li> <li>Experience partnering with Sales, Account Management, Channel, Product, and Data teams in a high-growth environment</li> <li>Strong systems thinking with the ability to balance short-term execution needs against long-term architectural health</li> <li>Excellent communication skills with the ability to translate technical concepts into clear business impact for non-technical stakeholders</li> <li>Experience in Enterprise, Software-as-a-Service, telecom, or complex business-to-business go-to-market motions preferred</li> </ul> <p><strong>ADDITIONAL REQUIREMENTS:</strong></p> <ul> <li>Available to work extended hours and/or weekends as needed to support major system launches, critical automation rollouts, or high-priority commercial initiatives</li> <li>Willingness to travel as needed to support cross-functional alignment and key team events</li> </ul> <p><strong>COMPENSATION AND BENEFITS:</strong><br><br>Pay Range:<br>Level 1: $125,000.00 - $150,000.00<br>Level 2: $145,000.00 - $175,000.00<br><br></p> <p>Your actual level and base salary will be determined on a case-by-case basis and may vary based on the following considerations: job-related knowledge and skills, education, and experience.</p> <p>Base salary is just one part of your total rewards package at SpaceX. title: Data Engineer (Starlink Network Analytics, Wi-Fi)
full_description: <div class="content-intro"><p>SpaceX was founded under the belief that a future where humanity is out exploring the stars is fundamentally more exciting than one where we are not. Today SpaceX is actively developing the technologies to make this possible, with the ultimate goal of&nbsp;enabling human life on Mars.</p></div><p><strong>DATA ENGINEER (STARLINK NETWORK ANALYTICS, WIFI)</strong></p> <p>At SpaceX, we’re leveraging our experience in building rockets and spacecraft to deploy Starlink, the world’s most advanced broadband internet system. Starlink is the world’s largest satellite constellation and is providing fast, reliable internet to millions of users worldwide. We design, build, test, and operate all parts of the system – thousands of satellites, consumer receivers that allow users to connect within minutes of unboxing, and the software that brings it all together. We’ve only begun to scratch the surface of Starlink’s potential global impact and are looking for best-in-class engineers to help maximize Starlink’s utility for communities and businesses around the globe.&nbsp;</p> <p>As a Data Engineer, you will&nbsp;be responsible for&nbsp;developing the strategy, key metrics, tools, software services, and processes for assessing how well key aspects of Starlink are scaling and how effective the Starlink Network is itself, in serving millions of users around the globe. You will work with operators, subsystem responsible engineers, software engineers, and network engineers inside the Starlink organization as well as key contacts with various major external partners to help ensure the growth of this program.&nbsp;</p> <p><strong>RESPONSIBILITIES:</strong></p> <ul> <li>Build and maintain mission-critical infrastructure, tools, processes, and custom software to objectively assess growth areas for the Starlink program</li> <li>Automate the aggregation of metrics and detection of widespread application issues across Starlink network, including WiFi&nbsp;</li> <li>Lead technical investigations about chronic application-level issues</li> <li>Build ground-based software systems that ingest, transform, and store data</li> <li>Apply data analytics, models, and techniques to data products created by space vehicles</li> <li>Fuse data from multiple sources to create usable information</li> </ul> <p><strong>BASIC QUALIFICATIONS:</strong></p> <ul> <li>Bachelor’s degree in computer science, data science, physics, mathematics, or a STEM discipline</li> <li>1+ years of data/software engineering experience (internship experience is applicable)</li> <li>Development experience in an object-oriented programming language (i.e. C, C++, Python)&nbsp;</li> </ul> <p><strong>PREFERRED SKILLS AND EXPERIENCE:</strong></p> <ul> <li>Experience in networking technologies and WiFi protocols (TCP/UDP, 802.11, etc)&nbsp;</li> <li>Experience building predictive models and machine learning pipelines on real world data (clustering analysis, prediction, anomaly detection) &nbsp;</li> <li>Experience in custom ETL design, implementation, and maintenance &nbsp;</li> <li>Experience handling large (TB+) datasets &nbsp;</li> <li>Experience with developing and deploying tools used for data analysis&nbsp;</li> <li>Ability to work effectively in a dynamic environment that includes working with changing needs and requirements&nbsp;</li> <li>Ability to take on projects that require taking initiative and developing new expertise&nbsp;</li> </ul> <p><strong>ADDITIONAL REQUIREMENTS:</strong></p> <ul> <li>Must be willing to work extended hours and weekends as needed</li> </ul> <strong>COMPENSATION AND BENEFITS:</strong><br><br> Pay Range:<br> Level 1: $125,000 - $150,000<br>Level 2: $145,000 - $175,000<br><br> <p>Your actual level and base salary will be determined on a case-by-case basis and may vary based on the following considerations: job-related knowledge and skills, education, and experience.</p> <p>Base salary is just one part of your total rewards package at SpaceX. You may also be eligible for long-term incentives, in the form of company stock or long-term cash awards, as well as potential discretionary bonuses and the ability to purchase additional stock at a discount through an Employee Stock Purchase Plan. You will also receive access to comprehensive medical, vision, and dental coverage, access to a 401(k) retirement plan, short and long-term disability insurance, life insurance, paid parental leave, and various other discounts and perks. You may also accrue 3 weeks of paid vacation and will be eligible for 10 or more paid holidays per year. Employees in Washington State accrue paid sick time in compliance with state and federal law. Company shuttles are offered to employees for roundtrip travel from select Seattle locations to the SpaceX Redmond office Monday to Friday.</p><div class="content-conclusion"><p><strong>ITAR REQUIREMENTS:</strong></p> <ul> <li>To conform to U.S. Government export regulations, applicant must be a (i) U.S. citizen or national, (ii) U.S. lawful, permanent resident (aka green card holder), (iii) Refugee under 8 U.S.C. § 1157, or (iv) Asylee under 8 U.S.C. § 1158, or be eligible to obtain the required authorizations from the U.S. Department of State. Learn more about the ITAR <a href="https://www.pmddtc.state.gov/?id=ddtc_kb_article_page&amp;sys_id=24d528fddbfc930044f9ff621f961987">here</a>. &nbsp;</li> </ul> <p>SpaceX is an Equal Opportunity Employer; employment with SpaceX is governed on the basis of merit, competence and qualifications and will not be influenced in any manner by race, color, religion, gender, national origin/ethnicity, veteran status, disability status, age, sexual orientation, gender identity, marital status, mental or physical disability or any other legally protected status.</p> <p>Applicants wishing to view a copy of SpaceX’s Affirmative Action Plan for veterans and individuals with disabilities, or applicants requiring reasonable accommodation to the application/interview process should reach out to&nbsp;<a href="mailto:EEOCompliance@spacex.com">EEOCompliance@spacex.com</a><em>.&nbsp;</em></p></div>

title: Data Engineer (Starship)
full_description: <div class="content-intro"><p>SpaceX was founded under the belief that a future where humanity is out exploring the stars is fundamentally more exciting than one where we are not. title: Sr. Data Engineer, Ground Network Engineering (Business Analytics)
full_description: <div class="content-intro"><p>SpaceX was founded under the belief that a future where humanity is out exploring the stars is fundamentally more exciting than one where we are not. Today SpaceX is actively developing the technologies to make this possible, with the ultimate goal of&nbsp;enabling human life on Mars.</p></div><p><strong>SR. DATA ENGINEER, GROUND NETWORK ENGINEERING (BUSINESS ANALYTICS)</strong></p> <p>At SpaceX, we are leveraging our experience in building rockets and spacecraft to deploy Starlink, the world’s most advanced broadband internet system. Starlink is the world’s largest satellite constellation and is providing fast, reliable internet to millions of users worldwide. We design, build, test, and operate all parts of the system – thousands of satellites, consumer receivers that allow users to connect within minutes of unboxing, and the software that brings it all together. We’ve only begun to scratch the surface of Starlink’s potential global impact and are looking for best-in-class engineers and analysts to rapidly accelerate our growth around the globe.</p> <p>As a Sr. Data Engineer for the Ground Network, you will play a critical role in building the framework that allow us to make decisions for our critical infrastructure. You will be defining and measuring infrastructure KPIs, analyzing data to extract key insights and identifying opportunities to improve spending in the ground network.</p> <p><strong>RESPONSIBILITIES</strong>&nbsp;</p> <ul> <li>Define and develop KPIs and associated metrics&nbsp;that quantify the&nbsp;cost&nbsp;of every asset of the Starlink Ground Network globally including gateway sites, points of presence, data centers, construction, fiber, power, land, hardware, etc.</li> <li>Conduct deep-dive analyses of Starlink ground infrastructure data to identify trends, uncover opportunities for improving&nbsp;cost.</li> <li>Regularly prepare reports and presentations that summarize findings, provide actionable insights, and track the effectiveness of implemented strategies.</li> <li>Build and maintain forecasting engines to predict cost of the Starlink Ground Network.</li> <li>Support&nbsp;cost&nbsp;analysis for launch of future markets.</li> <li>Refactor and scale data ingestion and delivery of cost systems for geographic expansion.</li> <li>Maintain and enhance the integrity and reliability of internal and external data sources. Collaborate with internal and external stakeholders including engineering, and external processors to capture additional data and to ensure data accuracy and consistency.</li> <li>Collaborate closely with software engineering and other cross functional teams.</li> <li>Architect backend services, APIs, and workflows to integrate planning, forecasting, deployments, and cost.</li> </ul> <p><strong>BASIC QUALIFICATIONS</strong>&nbsp;</p> <ul> <li>Bachelor’s degree in engineering, computer science, or related field</li> <li>5+ years of professional experience in reporting and analytics in network infrastructure.</li> <li>3+ years of experience with SQL.</li> </ul> <p><strong>PREFERRED SKILLS AND EXPERIENCE</strong>&nbsp;</p> <ul> <li>Experience with inventory systems, logistics workflows, and/or supply chain tooling.</li> <li>Proficient in C#, C++, or Angular</li> <li>Basic understanding of Kubernetes concepts, including pods, services, deployments, and namespaces</li> <li>Prior success in delivering strong data visualizations using tools such as PowerBI, Grafana, Metabase or other real-time metrics systems</li> <li>Experience building backend services using APIs (Celery, FastAPI, Django, or similar frameworks) and system-to-system data exchange in logistics and supply chain environments</li> <li>Proven track record developing custom forecasting tools and planning logic</li> <li>Knowledge of MRP/ERP, WMS, or other supply chain systems</li> <li>Strong problem-solving skills and comfort working in ambiguous environments with evolving requirements</li> <li>Excellent communication and cross-functional program management skills&nbsp;</li> </ul> <p><strong>ADDITIONAL REQUIREMENTS</strong>&nbsp;</p> <ul> <li>Must be able to work extended hours or weekends</li> </ul> <p><strong>COMPENSATION AND BENEFITS:&nbsp;&nbsp; &nbsp;</strong></p> <p>Pay Range: &nbsp; &nbsp;<br>Network Engineer/Senior: $160,000.00 - $225,000.00/per year &nbsp; &nbsp;&nbsp;</p> <p>Your actual level and base salary will be determined on a case-by-case basis and may vary based on the following considerations: job-related knowledge and skills, education, and experience.</p> <p>Base salary is just one part of your total rewards package at SpaceX. You may also be eligible for long-term incentives, in the form of company stock, stock options, or long-term cash awards, as well as potential discretionary bonuses and the ability to purchase additional stock at a discount through an Employee Stock Purchase Plan. You will also receive access to comprehensive medical, vision, and dental coverage, access to a 401(k) retirement plan, short and long-term disability insurance, life insurance, paid parental leave, and various other discounts and perks. You may also accrue 3 weeks of paid vacation and will be eligible for 10 or more paid holidays per year. Employees in Washington State accrue paid sick time in compliance with state and federal law. Company shuttles are offered to employees for roundtrip travel from select Seattle locations to the SpaceX Redmond office Monday to Friday.</p><div class="content-conclusion"><p><strong>ITAR REQUIREMENTS:</strong></p> <ul> <li>To conform to U.S. Government export regulations, applicant must be a (i) U.S. citizen or national, (ii) U.S. lawful, permanent resident (aka green card holder), (iii) Refugee under 8 U.S.C. § 1157, or (iv) Asylee under 8 U.S.C. § 1158, or be eligible to obtain the required authorizations from the U.S. Department of State. Learn more about the ITAR <a href="https://www.pmddtc.state.gov/?id=ddtc_kb_article_page&amp;sys_id=24d528fddbfc930044f9ff621f961987">here</a>. &nbsp;</li> </ul> <p>SpaceX is an Equal Opportunity Employer; employment with SpaceX is governed on the basis of merit, competence and qualifications and will not be influenced in any manner by race, color, religion, gender, national origin/ethnicity, veteran status, disability status, age, sexual orientation, gender identity, marital status, mental or physical disability or any other legally protected status.</p> <p>Applicants wishing to view a copy of SpaceX’s Affirmative Action Plan for veterans and individuals with disabilities, or applicants requiring reasonable accommodation to the application/interview process should reach out to&nbsp;<a href="mailto:EEOCompliance@spacex.com">EEOCompliance@spacex.com</a><em>.&nbsp;</em></p></div>

full_description: <div class="content-intro"><p>SpaceX was founded under the belief that a future where humanity is out exploring the stars is fundamentally more exciting than one where we are not. SOFTWARE ENGINEER (DATA SCIENCE/DATA ENGINEERING)</strong></p> <p>The application software team is the central nervous system of SpaceX – we create mission critical applications that are used throughout SpaceX to accelerate launch vehicle production and flights as well as systems that allow Starlink to grow into a worldwide fast, reliable internet service.<span data-ccp-props="{}">&nbsp;</span>We are looking for engineers who treat fellow teammates with fairness, respect, and support.<span data-ccp-props="{}">&nbsp;</span></p> <p>Our team is creating systems to analyze data from events like rocket launches or factory production workloads to always-on telemetry from the Starlink satellite constellation with point-in-time analysis, historical analysis and machine learning. lieu of a degree</li> <li>3+ years of professional experience in full-stack development</li> </ul> <p><strong>PREFERRED SKILLS AND EXPERIENCE:</strong><span data-ccp-props="{}">&nbsp;</span></p> <ul> <li>3+ years of professional experience in data engineering or data science</li> <li>2+ years of hands-on experience applying ML techniques (ex: CNNs, RNNs) to datasets</li> <li>Programming experience in Python, C#, or similar languages</li> <li>Experience working with data analysis libraries such as Python Pandas</li> <li>Experience with machine learning libraries like SciPi and PyTorch</li> <li>Experience with data exploration tools like Grafana, Jupyter Notebooks, Metabase, PowerBI or similar</li> <li>Experience with relational databases such as PostgreSQL</li> <li>Experience applying computer vision libraries such as OpenCV and Ultralytics to real-world problems</li> <li>Goodunderstanding of&nbsp;machine learning algorithms,&nbsp;linear algebra, calculus, probability, statistics, and optimization methods.<span data-ccp-props="{}">&nbsp;</span></li> <li>Good understanding of version control, testing, continuous integration, build, deployment and monitoring</li> <li>Some front-end experience in Angular, React, or similar JavaScript framework</li> </ul> <p><strong>ADDITIONAL REQUIREMENTS: </strong><span data-ccp-props="{}">&nbsp;</span></p> <ul> <li><span data-contrast="auto"> You may be asked to work extended hours/weekends dependent on launch cadence </span></li> <li>Must be willing to travel when needed</li> </ul><div class="content-conclusion"><p><strong>ITAR REQUIREMENTS:</strong></p> <ul> <li>To conform to U.S. Government export regulations, applicant must be a (i) U.S. citizen or national, (ii) U.S. lawful, permanent resident (aka green card holder), (iii) Refugee under 8 U.S.C. § 1157, or (iv) Asylee under 8 U.S.C. § 1158, or be eligible to obtain the required authorizations from the U.S. Department of State. Learn more about the ITAR <a href="https://www.pmddtc.state.gov/?id=ddtc_kb_article_page&amp;sys_id=24d528fddbfc930044f9ff621f961987">here</a>. &nbsp;</li> </ul> <p>SpaceX is an Equal Opportunity Employer; employment with SpaceX is governed on the basis of merit, competence and qualifications and will not be influenced in any manner by race, color, religion, gender, national origin/ethnicity, veteran status, disability status, age, sexual orientation, gender identity, marital status, mental or physical disability or any other legally protected status.</p> <p>Applicants wishing to view a copy of SpaceX’s Affirmative Action Plan for veterans and individuals with disabilities, or applicants requiring reasonable accommodation to the application/interview process should reach out to&nbsp;<a href="mailto:EEOCompliance@spacex.com">EEOCompliance@spacex.com</a><em>.&nbsp;</em></p></div>

title: Senior Data Engineer - Finance
full_description: <div class="content-intro"><p>Our mission at Oura is to empower every person to own their inner potential. Our award-winning products help our global community gain a deeper knowledge of their readiness, activity, and sleep quality by using their Oura Ring and its connected app. We've helped millions of people understand and improve their health by providing daily insights and practical steps to inspire healthy lifestyles.</p> <p>Empowering the world starts with living <a href="https://ouraring.com/about-us">our values</a> and empowering our team. As a quickly growing company focused on helping people live healthier and happier lives, we ensure that our team members have what they need to do their best work — both in and out of the office.&nbsp;</p></div><p>We are looking for a <strong>Data Engineer – Finance</strong> to join our Data Engineering &amp; Analytics team. Your primary focus will be building and owning the data infrastructure that powers Oura's financial data — including revenue, LTV, Corporate Scorecard, and RevOps — ensuring clean, reliable, and timely data for stakeholders across Finance, Sales, and Leadership. While Finance data engineering is your core domain, we expect and actively support our engineers growing into adjacent areas — such as membership, supply chain, or broader platform work — as business needs and individual interests evolve.</p> <p>This is a hybrid role based in San Francisco, CA, working onsite 1–2 days per week.</p> <p>&nbsp;</p> <p><strong>What you will do:</strong></p> <p><strong>Primary Focus – Finance Data Engineering</strong></p> <p>- Design, build, and maintain data pipelines and models that power Oura's Corporate Scorecard, revenue reporting, LTV analysis, and RevOps workflows.</p> <p>- Partner closely with Finance, Sales Operations, and Business Intelligence to understand reporting requirements and translate them into scalable data models.</p> <p>- Own end-to-end data quality and reliability for financial datasets — from ingestion to consumption-ready tables.</p> <p>- Integrate and rationalize data across finance source systems (e.g., NetSuite, Salesforce, Pigment, e-commerce platforms) into a unified, governed data layer.</p> <p>- Ensure financial data pipelines meet compliance, audit, and privacy requirements.</p> <p>- Reduce manual, spreadsheet-driven reporting processes by automating trusted, self-serve data products.</p> <p><strong>Cross-functional Growth Areas (expected over time)</strong></p> <p>- Contribute to broader lakehouse architecture work including Apache Iceberg, Databricks, and dbt.</p> <p>- Expand into adjacent domains such as Membership, RevOps, or Supply Chain data as the team grows.</p> <p>- Participate in Data Mesh domain ownership design for the Finance domain.</p> <p>&nbsp;</p> <p><strong>We would love to have you on our team if you have:</strong></p> <p>- 5+ years of experience in data engineering or analytics engineering in production environments.</p> <p>- Deep experience with financial or revenue data systems — understanding of metrics like LTV, ARR, churn, and funnel analytics.</p> <p>- Strong SQL and Python skills with a track record of building tested, reliable data pipelines.</p> <p>- Experience integrating data from ERP, CRM, or e-commerce platforms (NetSuite, Salesforce, Shopify, or similar).</p> <p>- Familiarity with dbt, Databricks, Spark, or modern lakehouse tools.</p> <p>- Experience running and monitoring production pipelines on AWS or equivalent cloud.</p> <p>- Ability to work collaboratively with Finance and non-technical stakeholders to define data requirements.</p> <p>- Self-motivated, pragmatic, and able to manage your own work independently.</p> <p>&nbsp;</p> <p><strong>What Makes You Stand Out</strong></p> <p>- Experience building or owning a Corporate Scorecard or executive-facing metrics layer.</p> <p>- Familiarity with Pigment, Looker, or similar financial planning/BI tools.</p> <p>- Experience with Data Mesh and domain-oriented data ownership.</p> <p>- Exposure to SDM (Supply &amp; Demand Management) or inventory data.</p> <p>&nbsp;</p> <p><strong>Benefits&nbsp;</strong></p> <p>At Oura, we care about you and your well-being. Everyone here at Oura has a ring of their own and we are continually looking to improve employee health.</p> <p>What we offer:</p> <ul> <li>Competitive salary and equity packages</li> <li>Health, dental, vision insurance, and mental health resources</li> <li>An Oura Ring of your own plus employee discounts for friends &amp; family</li> <li>20 days of paid time off plus 13 paid holidays plus 8 days of flexible wellness time off</li> <li>Paid sick leave and parental leave</li> </ul> <p>&nbsp;</p> <p>Oura takes a market-based approach to pay, which may vary depending on your location. US locations are categorized into tiers based on a cost of labor index for that geographic area. While most offers will be closer to the starting range, successful candidates' pay will be determined based on job-related skills, experience, qualifications, work location, internal peer equity, and market conditions. These ranges may be modified in the future.</p> <ul> <li>Region 1 $172,550 - $203,000</li> </ul> <p>A recruiter can determine your zones/tiers based on your US location.</p> <p>We are not considering candidates residing in the following states: Alaska (AK), Delaware (DE), Iowa (IA), Mississippi (MS), Nebraska (NE), South Dakota (SD), West Virginia (WV), and Wisconsin (WI)</p><div class="content-conclusion"><div class="styles--2Kv6p" data-ui="careers-page-content"> <p>Oura is proud to be an equal opportunity workplace. title: Senior Data Engineer, Product
full_description: <div class="content-intro"><p>Our mission at Oura is to empower every person to own their inner potential. Our award-winning products help our global community gain a deeper knowledge of their readiness, activity, and sleep quality by using their Oura Ring and its connected app. We've helped millions of people understand and improve their health by providing daily insights and practical steps to inspire healthy lifestyles.</p> <p>Empowering the world starts with living <a href="https://ouraring.com/about-us">our values</a> and empowering our team. As a quickly growing company focused on helping people live healthier and happier lives, we ensure that our team members have what they need to do their best work — both in and out of the office.&nbsp;</p></div><p>We are looking for a <strong>Senior</strong> <strong>Data Engineer – Product</strong> to join our Data Engineering &amp; Analytics team, based in Helsinki. Your primary focus will be the software product data domain — owning the data infrastructure that powers Oura's end-to-end longitudinal member journey, spanning member engagement, health intelligence, and personalized product experiences. A major part of this role is maturing our product analytics stack from its current foundation (Amplitude, Segment, Snowflake, Athena, QuickSight) to a unified, governed Databricks-based platform. While product data engineering is your core domain, we expect and actively support team members growing into adjacent areas — such as health science analytics, platform infrastructure, or data mesh ownership — as the team and business evolve.</p> <p>This role is based in Helsinki, Finland.</p> <h3><strong>What You'll Do:</strong></h3> <p><strong>Primary Focus – Product Data Engineering</strong></p> <ul> <li>Design, build, and maintain data pipelines that unify Oura's product and member behavioural data — including engagement events, feature usage, session data, health intelligence signals, and subscription lifecycle — into a reliable, governed data layer.</li> <li>Lead the migration of product analytics infrastructure from the current stack (Amplitude, Segment, Snowflake, Athena, QuickSight) to Databricks, ensuring continuity of existing analytics while dramatically improving scalability, governance, and cost efficiency.</li> <li>Build and own the end-to-end longitudinal member journey data model — a unified view of how members engage with Oura's app and ring across health intelligence features, wellness journeys, and subscription milestones.</li> <li>Partner closely with Product, Data Science, and Health Intelligence teams to define data requirements and deliver pipelines and models that power product decisions at scale.</li> <li>Own data quality and reliability for the product data domain: schema governance, lineage documentation, data contracts, and monitoring.</li> <li>Ensure product data systems comply with Oura's privacy, data governance, and security requirements — critical given the sensitivity of health and behavioural data.</li> <li>Evaluate and rationalise the existing tool stack — instrument Segment event schemas, manage Amplitude data exports, maintain Snowflake/Athena integrations during transition — and progressively consolidate onto Databricks.</li> <li>Write clean, tested, production-grade code primarily in Python and SQL.</li> </ul> <p><strong>Cross-functional Growth Areas (expected over time)</strong></p> <ul> <li>Contribute to the broader Databricks lakehouse architecture (Apache Iceberg, dbt, Unity Catalog).</li> <li>Expand data engineering coverage to Health Intelligence and Science data domains.</li> <li>Support the Data Mesh initiative by establishing Product as an owned, well-governed data domain.</li> <li>Mentor and collaborate with analysts who consume product data.</li> </ul> <h3><strong>We would love to have you on our team if you have:</strong></h3> <ul> <li>5+ years of experience developing and operating production data pipelines.</li> <li>Hands-on experience with product analytics stacks — specifically Amplitude, Segment, or equivalent event-tracking and CDP platforms — and strong intuition for how product behavioural data is structured.</li> <li>Experience migrating or consolidating data infrastructure; comfort working in a multi-tool environment during a platform transition.</li> <li>Strong SQL and Python skills; track record of building tested, reliable, production-grade pipelines.</li> <li>Experience with cloud data warehousing — Snowflake, Athena, BigQuery, or similar.</li> <li>Good understanding of event-driven architectures, stream processing, and longitudinal / session-level data modelling.</li> <li>Familiarity with dbt, Databricks, Spark, or modern lakehouse tooling.</li> <li>Experience running and monitoring production data systems on AWS or equivalent cloud.</li> <li>Self-motivated, pragmatic, and able to lead your own work in a distributed team.</li> <li>Fluent in English; comfortable collaborating across Finland, the US, and other Oura locations.</li> </ul> <p><strong>What Makes You Stand Out</strong></p> <ul> <li>Real-world Databricks experience, including Unity Catalog or Delta Live Tables.</li> <li>Experience designing member or user journey data models for a consumer subscription or digital health product.</li> <li>Familiarity with health data — wearable telemetry, physiological signals, or clinical/wellness event data.</li> <li>Experience with Data Mesh and domain-oriented data ownership.</li> <li>Broad knowledge of software fundamentals, system design, and multiple programming languages.</li> </ul> <h3><strong>What We Offer:</strong></h3> <p>At Oura, we care about you and your well-being. Everyone here at Oura has a ring of their own and we are continually looking to improve employee health.</p> <p><strong>Benefits:</strong></p> <ul> <li>Competitive salary</li> <li>Lunch benefit</li> <li>Wellness benefit</li> <li>Flexible working hours</li> <li>Wellness Time Off</li> <li>Enhanced occupational health care</li> <li>Personal Oura Ring plus lifetime membership</li> </ul><div class="content-conclusion"><div class="styles--2Kv6p" data-ui="careers-page-content"> <p>Oura is proud to be an equal opportunity workplace.
