title: Senior Data Engineer, Unified Platform
full_description: <p><strong>DRW</strong> is a diversified trading firm with over 3 decades of experience bringing sophisticated technology and exceptional people together to operate in markets around the world. We value autonomy and the ability to quickly pivot to capture opportunities, so we operate using our own capital and trading at our own risk.</p> <p>Headquartered in Chicago with offices throughout the U.S., Canada, Europe, and Asia, we trade a variety of asset classes including Fixed Income, ETFs, Equities, FX, Commodities and Energy across all major global markets. We have also leveraged our expertise and technology to expand into three non-traditional strategies: real estate, venture capital and cryptoassets.</p> <p>We operate with respect, curiosity and open minds. The people who thrive here share our belief that it’s not just what we do that matters–it's how we do it. <strong>DRW</strong> is a place of high expectations, integrity, innovation and a willingness to challenge consensus.<strong></strong></p> <p>As a <strong>Senior Data Engineer</strong> on our Data Experience team, you will play an integral role in bringing vendor datasets into our data platform, governing our centralized data pipelines, consolidating data delivery infrastructure across disparate systems, and working alongside individual Traders, Quantitative Researchers, and Back-Office personnel to best utilize the firm’s data and platform tools.</p> <p><strong>Technical requirements summary:</strong></p> <ul> <li>Have extensive experience designing and building data pipelines</li> <li>Have experience working within modern batch and streaming data ecosystems</li> <li>An expert in SQL and Java or Python</li> <li>Extensive knowledge of data modeling techniques</li> <li>Able to own the delivery of data products, working with analysts and stakeholders to understand requirements and implement solutions</li> <li>Able to contribute to project management and project reporting</li> <li>Have experience driving nontrivial data migrations across platforms</li> <li>Able to establish, follow, and enforce data modeling standards across a large ecosystem.</li> </ul> <p><strong>What you will do in this role:</strong></p> <ul> <li>Model, build, and manage data products built atop DRW’s Unified Data Platform.</li> <li>Work closely with Data Strategists to determine appropriate data sources and implement processes that onboard and manage new datasets for trading, research, and back-office purposes.</li> <li>Design data governance processes that enable discovery, cost-sharing, usage tracking, access controls, and quality control of datasets to address the needs of DRW trading teams and strategies.</li> <li>Continually monitor data ingestion pipelines and data quality to ensure stability, reliability, and quality of the data. Design monitoring and quality control software and processes.</li> <li>Own the technical aspects of vendor ingestion pipelines, coordinating with vendor relationship managers on upcoming changes, performing routine data operations without breaking internal users, and contributing to the team’s on-call rotation to respond to unanticipated changes.</li> <li>Rapidly respond to user requests, identifying platform gaps, and self-service opportunities that make the user experience more efficient.</li> </ul> <p><strong>What you will need in this role:</strong></p> <ul> <li>7+ years of experience working with modern data technologies and building data-first products.</li> <li>Excellent written and verbal communication skills.</li> <li>Proven ability to work in a collaborative, agile, and fast-paced environment, prioritizing multiple tasks and projects, and efficiently handle the demands of a trading environment.</li> <li>Proven ability to deliver rapid results within processes that span multiple stakeholders.</li> <li>Strong technical problem-solving skills.</li> <li>Extensive familiarity with SQL and Java or Python, with a proven ability to develop and deliver maintainable data transformations for production data pipelines.</li> <li>Experience leveraging data modeling techniques and ability to articulate the trade-offs of different approaches.</li> <li>Experience with one or more data processing technologies (e.g. Flink, Spark, Polars, Dask, etc.).</li> <li>Experience with multiple data storage technologies (e.g. S3, RDBMS, NoSQL, Delta/Iceberg, Cassandra, Clickhouse, Kafka, etc.) and knowledge of their associated trade-offs.</li> <li>Experience with multiple data formats and serialization systems (e.g. Arrow, Parquet, Protobuf/gRPC, Avro, Thrift, JSON, etc.).</li> <li>Experience managing data pipeline orchestration systems (e.g. Kubernetes, Argo Workflows, Airflow, Prefect, Dagster, etc.), delving deeply into low-level details.</li> <li>Proven experience in managing the operational aspects of large data pipelines such as backfilling datasets, rerunning batch jobs, monitoring event throughput, and handling dead-letter queues.</li> <li>Prior experience triaging data quality control processes, correcting data gaps and inaccuracies.</li> </ul> <p>The annual base salary range for this position is $200,000 to $250,000 depending on the candidate’s experience, qualifications, and relevant skill set. The position is also eligible for an annual discretionary bonus. In addition, DRW offers a comprehensive suite of employee benefits including group medical, pharmacy, dental and vision insurance, 401k (with discretionary employer match), short and long-term disability, life and AD&amp;D insurance, health savings accounts, and flexible spending accounts.</p> <p><strong>For more information about DRW's processing activities and our use of job applicants' data, please view our Privacy Notice at <a href="https://drw.com/privacy-notice">https://drw.com/privacy-notice</a>.</strong></p> <p><strong> California residents, please review the California Privacy Notice for information about certain legal rights at <a href="https://drw.com/california-privacy-notice">https://drw.com/california-privacy-notice</a>.</strong></p> <p><strong>[#LI-TL1]</strong>&nbsp;</p>

title: Team Lead, Data Engineering
full_description: <p><strong>DRW</strong> is a diversified trading firm with over 3 decades of experience bringing sophisticated technology and exceptional people together to operate in markets around the world. We value autonomy and the ability to quickly pivot to capture opportunities, so we operate using our own capital and trading at our own risk.</p> <p>Headquartered in Chicago with offices throughout the U.S., Canada, Europe, and Asia, we trade a variety of asset classes including Fixed Income, ETFs, Equities, FX, Commodities and Energy across all major global markets. We have also leveraged our expertise and technology to expand into three non-traditional strategies: real estate, venture capital and cryptoassets.</p> <p>We operate with respect, curiosity and open minds. The people who thrive here share our belief that it’s not just what we do that matters–it's how we do it. <strong>DRW</strong> is a place of high expectations, integrity, innovation and a willingness to challenge consensus.</p> <p>The <strong>Data Ecosystem</strong> team is an important part of DRW’s <strong>Unified Platform (UP)</strong> organization, building the platform capabilities, managed infrastructure, and metadata management systems that underpin our common query layer and power thousands of batch and streaming pipelines across DRW.</p> <p>As a <strong>Team Lead</strong> of our Data Ecosystem team, you will lead a team of Data Engineers who craft the architecture of DRW’s data platform, establishing data infrastructure, creating metadata management tools, and scaffolding governance processes that accelerate the time to onboard datasets, curate them, and make them available throughout the firm. This role encompasses people leadership, technical leadership, product ownership, and hands-on development and support activities.</p> <p><strong>Key Responsibilities:</strong></p> <ul> <li>Lead and mentor a team of data engineers with a variety of skill sets, fostering a culture of excellence, innovation, and continuous improvement.</li> <li>Use strong leadership skills in mentoring, coaching, feedback, development, negotiation, and conflict management to enhance team performance and help people have a great experience in their work life.</li> <li>Coordinate closely with stakeholders to grow the userbase and deliver high-impact functionality to the data platform.</li> <li>Engage in hands-on software development and support activities.</li> <li>Drive project ownership, including defining requirements, scheduling, resource allocation, and ensuring timely delivery of projects.</li> <li>Consult with other development teams to understand how they engage with the data platform.</li> <li>Contribute to process discussions, challenging ideas and actively refining the team’s processes to maximize throughput.</li> </ul> <p><strong>Required Qualifications: </strong></p> <ul> <li>5+ years of experience leading engineers in a technical environment, with a strong emphasis on mentoring, development, and team management.</li> <li>5+ years of experience working with modern data technologies and/or building data-first products.</li> <li>Familiarity with the data modeling practices, storage systems, and compute frameworks common to modern data engineering, with a track record of leveraging this knowledge within a fast-moving data ecosystem.</li> <li>Strong communication and interpersonal skills, capable of balancing and negotiating requests across multiple stakeholders.</li> </ul> <p><strong>Desirable Qualifications:</strong></p> <ul> <li>Experience building, scaling, and supporting large data platforms, and familiarity with the use cases, modeling techniques, and ergonomics expected by data platform users.</li> <li>Experience managing technical roadmaps for platforms with a wide variety of users.</li> <li>Familiarity with the common needs of data platform users within financial firms.</li> <li>History of balancing feedback from multiple users, translating technical roadblocks, refining processes, and improving delivery.</li> <li>Ability to own projects, define requirements, and lead development and support initiatives.</li> <li>Excellent problem-solving skills, with the judgment and capacity to deliver data engineering and software engineering artifacts that fit the needs of our users.</li> <li>Track record of fostering collaboration and effective teamwork across global locations.</li> </ul> <p><strong>For more information about DRW's processing activities and our use of job applicants' data, please view our Privacy Notice at <a href="https://drw.com/privacy-notice">https://drw.com/privacy-notice</a>.</strong></p> <p><strong> California residents, please review the California Privacy Notice for information about certain legal rights at <a href="https://drw.com/california-privacy-notice">https://drw.com/california-privacy-notice</a>.</strong></p> <p><strong>[#LI-TL1]</strong>&nbsp;</p>

title: Senior Forward Deployed Engineer I (AI/ML)
full_description: <div class="content-intro"><p>Dive in and do the best work of your career at DigitalOcean. Journey alongside a strong community of top talent who are relentless in their drive to build the simplest scalable cloud. If you have a growth mindset, naturally like to think big and bold, and are energized by the fast-paced environment of a true industry disruptor, you’ll find your place here. &nbsp;We value winning together—while learning, having fun, and making a profound difference for the dreamers and builders in the world.&nbsp;</p></div><h2><strong>Position Overview</strong></h2> <ul> <li>We are looking for a <strong>Senior Forward Deployed Engineer (FDE)</strong> who is passionate about operationalizing production AI-native and agentic workloads at scale. This is a high-impact role designed to serve as the “technical tip of the spear” for DigitalOcean’s most strategic AI-native customers and platform initiatives.</li> <li>As an FDE, you will operate at the intersection of Product Engineering, AI Infrastructure, and Customer Implementation. You will partner deeply with strategic AI-native enterprises (ANEs), startups, infrastructure vendors, and internal engineering teams to deploy, optimize, and scale production AI systems on DigitalOcean’s AI-Native Cloud.</li> <li>This role extends beyond traditional GPU infrastructure deployment. You will work across Inference Engine, runtime systems, orchestration frameworks, and AI-native applications to help customers operationalize production AI and agentic systems with strong focus on scalability, reliability, latency, and workload economics.</li> <li>FDE engineers also act as the “first customer” for new AI-native platform capabilities. You will validate products under real-world workloads, surface operational insights and architectural gaps, and help accelerate product maturity through continuous feedback loops with Product Engineering and Research teams.</li> <li>You will build scalable deployment frameworks, benchmarking systems, automation tooling, and AI starter kits that transform field learning into reusable platform intelligence and repeatable deployment patterns across the DigitalOcean ecosystem.</li> <li>Your mission is to accelerate production adoption of AI-native systems while helping shape the future of DigitalOcean’s AI-Native Cloud for the inference and agentic era.</li> </ul> <h2><strong>What You’ll Do</strong></h2> <ul> <li>Strategic AI Workload Operationalization: Partner with strategic ANEs and AI startups to architect, deploy, optimize, and scale production AI and agentic systems on DigitalOcean’s AI-Native Cloud. Support complex migrations, production-ready PoCs, deployment acceleration, and long-term workload expansion across inference and runtime platforms.</li> <li>AI Performance &amp; Systems Engineering: Optimize distributed inference and runtime performance through benchmarking, GPU efficiency tuning, KV-cache optimization, speculative decoding, prefill/decode disaggregation, multi-node deployments, and latency/cost optimization.Platform Validation &amp; Product Acceleration:</li> <li>Act as the “first customer” for DigitalOcean’s AI-native platform capabilities including Inference Engine, runtimes, orchestration systems, GPU platforms, and deployment workflows. Surface real-world operational insights, architectural gaps, and scaling bottlenecks directly to Product Engineering and Research teams.Platform Intelligence &amp; Automation:</li> <li>Build scalable deployment assets including benchmarking systems, automation tooling, AI starter kits, deployment frameworks, operational playbooks, finetuning workflows, and reference architectures that improve deployment velocity and platform adoption.</li> <li>Ecosystem &amp; Technical Enablement:&nbsp;Collaborate with GPU vendors, model providers, infrastructure partners, and ISVs on co-development, technical validation, optimization, and launch readiness. Enable customer-facing technical teams and partner teams through validated deployment patterns, benchmarking insights, operational playbooks, reference architectures, demos, and technical guidance that help scale adoption of DigitalOcean’s AI-native platform.Travel &amp; Collaboration Requirements: Ability to travel up to 30% for customer engagements, strategic workshops, conferences, and internal collaboration. Ability to consistently overlap with North American business hours, including availability until at least noon Eastern Time, to collaborate effectively with customers, Product, Engineering, and go-to-market teams.</li> </ul> <h2><strong>Key Metrics</strong></h2> <ul> <li>Customer Adoption &amp; Production Success: Measured by high-impact production workloads launched, reduction in time-to-production, pilot-to-production conversion rates, and expansion of AI-native platform adoption across strategic customers.</li> <li>Platform Intelligence &amp; Product Influence: Measured by product improvements, roadmap influence, validated customer hypotheses, and operational insights generated from real-world production deployments.Asset &amp; Tooling Delivery:&nbsp;</li> <li>Measured through adoption of FDE-built frameworks, automation tooling, benchmarking systems, operational playbooks, and reference architectures across customers and internal teams.Field Enablement &amp; Ecosystem Scale:</li> <li>Measured through successful enablement of customer-facing teams, ecosystem collaboration outcomes, and adoption of FDE deployment standards across the AI-native ecosystem.</li> </ul> <h2><strong>What You’ll Add to DigitalOcean</strong></h2> <ul> <li>AI-Native Systems &amp; Architecture Expertise: Experience designing and operationalizing production AI systems including inference workloads, agentic runtimes, orchestration frameworks, and AI-native applications.</li> <li>Strong hands-on experience with inference and serving frameworks such as vLLM, SGLang, Ray Serve, NVIDIA Dynamo, llm-d, or equivalent systems, along with LLM optimization techniques including continuous batching, quantization, KV-cache optimization, and speculative decoding.&nbsp;</li> <li>Distributed Systems &amp; Infrastructure Mastery: Deep expertise with NVIDIA and AMD GPU platforms and their software ecosystems including CUDA, ROCm, TensorRT, Triton, NCCL, RCCL, NVLink, XGMI, and RoCE.</li> <li>Strong proficiency with Kubernetes (K8s), distributed systems, networking, storage systems, Infrastructure as Code, and large-scale AI infrastructure architectures.Runtime &amp; Orchestration Systems: Experience with AI orchestration and agent frameworks such as LangGraph, CrewAI, MCP ecosystems, LlamaIndex, OpenAI Agents SDK, or similar runtime systems.&nbsp;</li> <li>Understanding of workflow orchestration, deployment systems, memory patterns, and AI-native application architectures.Software Engineering &amp; Automation: Strong production coding skills in Python or Go with experience building tooling, automation systems, deployment workflows, benchmarking frameworks, and operational platforms.Performance &amp; Operational Intelligence: Proven ability to benchmark and optimize AI infrastructure with strong focus on scalability, reliability, GPU efficiency, runtime performance, latency optimization, and workload economics.Consultative &amp; Cross-Functional Execution: Ability to establish technical credibility with CTOs, Principal architects, Product Engineering teams, and ecosystem partners while managing high-impact production deployments and strategic technical initiatives.</li> </ul> <h2><strong>Preferred Qualifications</strong></h2> <ul> <li>AI Infrastructure &amp; Forward Deployed Engineering Experience: 4+ years of experience working in Forward Deployed Engineering, ML Engineer, Applied AI Engineer, AI Infrastructure, Technical Consulting, or equivalent customer-facing engineering roles supporting production AI systems.Platform Enablement &amp; Ecosystem Experience:</li> <li>Experience building deployment standards, technical enablement programs, platform adoption frameworks, or ecosystem integration strategies across customer-facing and engineering organizations.Open Source &amp; AI Ecosystem Involvement: Active contributor to open-source AI, infrastructure, orchestration, or developer tooling ecosystems.Vendor &amp; Strategic Partnership Collaboration:</li> <li>Experience collaborating with GPU vendors, infrastructure providers, model vendors, or ecosystem partners on benchmarking, optimization, technical validation, or launch readiness initiatives.</li> </ul> <p class="p1"><span style="color: rgb(0, 0, 0);">*This job is located in Bengaluru, India</span></p> <p class="p1"><span style="color: rgb(0, 0, 0);"><span style="font-weight: 400; color: rgb(0, 0, 0);">JR: 2026-7937</span></span></p> <p class="p1"><span style="color: rgb(0, 0, 0);"><em>#LI-Hybrid</em></span></p> <p class="p1">&nbsp;</p><div class="content-conclusion"><h2 class="p-rich_text_section"><strong data-stringify-type="bold">Why You’ll Like Working for DigitalOcean</strong></h2> <ul class="p-rich_text_list p-rich_text_list__bullet p-rich_text_list--nested" data-stringify-type="unordered-list" data-list-tree="true" data-indent="0" data-border="0"> <li data-stringify-indent="0" data-stringify-border="0"><strong data-stringify-type="bold">We innovate with purpose. </strong>You’ll be a part of a cutting-edge technology company with an upward trajectory, who are proud to simplify cloud and AI so builders can spend more time creating software that changes the world. As a member of the team, you will be a Shark who thinks big, bold, and scrappy, like an owner with a bias for action and a powerful sense of responsibility for customers, products, employees, and decisions.</li> <li data-stringify-indent="0" data-stringify-border="0"><strong data-stringify-type="bold">We prioritize career development.</strong> At DO, you’ll do the best work of your career. You will work with some of the smartest and most interesting people in the industry. We are a high-performance organization that will always challenge you to think big. Our organizational development team will provide you with resources to ensure you keep growing. We provide employees with reimbursement for relevant conferences, training, and education. All employees have access to LinkedIn Learning's 10,000+ courses to support their continued growth and development.</li> <li data-stringify-indent="0" data-stringify-border="0"><strong data-stringify-type="bold">We care about your well-being.&nbsp;</strong>Regardless of your location, we will provide you with a competitive array of benefits to support you from our Employee Assistance Program to Local Employee Meetups to flexible time off policy, to name a few. While the philosophy around our benefits is the same worldwide, specific benefits may vary based on local regulations and preferences.</li> <li data-stringify-indent="0" data-stringify-border="0"><strong data-stringify-type="bold">We reward our employees. </strong>The salary range for this position is based on market data, relevant years of experience, and skills. You may qualify for a bonus in addition to base salary; bonus amounts are determined based on company and individual performance. We also provide equity compensation to eligible employees, including equity grants upon hire and the option to participate in our Employee Stock Purchase Program.</li> <li data-stringify-indent="0" data-stringify-border="0"><strong data-stringify-type="bold">DigitalOcean is an equal-opportunity employer. </strong>We do not discriminate on the basis of race, religion, color, ancestry, national origin, caste, sex, sexual orientation, gender, gender identity or expression, age, disability, medical condition, pregnancy, genetic makeup, marital status, or military service.</li> </ul> <p><strong data-stringify-type="bold">Application Limit:</strong>&nbsp;You may apply to a maximum of 3 positions within any 180-day period. This policy promotes better role-candidate matching and encourages thoughtful applications where your qualifications align most strongly.</p></div>

title: Staff Forward Deployed Engineer, AI/ML
full_description: <div class="content-intro"><p>Dive in and do the best work of your career at DigitalOcean. Journey alongside a strong community of top talent who are relentless in their drive to build the simplest scalable cloud. If you have a growth mindset, naturally like to think big and bold, and are energized by the fast-paced environment of a true industry disruptor, you’ll find your place here. &nbsp;We value winning together—while learning, having fun, and making a profound difference for the dreamers and builders in the world.&nbsp;</p></div><p>We are looking for a <strong>Staff Forward Deployed Engineer (FDE)</strong> who is passionate about operationalizing production AI-native and agentic workloads at scale. This is a high-impact role designed to serve as the “technical tip of the spear” for DigitalOcean’s most strategic AI-native customers and platform initiatives.</p> <p>As an FDE, you will operate at the intersection of Product Engineering, AI Infrastructure, and Customer Implementation. You will partner deeply with strategic AI-native enterprises (ANEs), startups, infrastructure vendors, and internal engineering teams to deploy, optimize, and scale production AI systems on DigitalOcean’s AI-Native Cloud.</p> <p>This role extends beyond traditional GPU infrastructure deployment. You will work across Inference Engine, runtime systems, orchestration frameworks, and AI-native applications to help customers operationalize production AI and agentic systems with strong focus on scalability, reliability, latency, and workload economics.</p> <p>FDE engineers also act as the “first customer” for new AI-native platform capabilities. You will validate products under real-world workloads, surface operational insights and architectural gaps, and help accelerate product maturity through continuous feedback loops with Product Engineering and Research teams.</p> <p>You will build scalable deployment frameworks, benchmarking systems, automation tooling, and AI starter kits that transform field learning into reusable platform intelligence and repeatable deployment patterns across the DigitalOcean ecosystem.</p> <p>Your mission is to accelerate production adoption of AI-native systems while helping shape the future of DigitalOcean’s AI-Native Cloud for the inference and agentic era.</p> <h2><strong>What You’ll Do</strong></h2> <ul> <li><strong>Strategic AI Workload Operationalization: </strong>Partner with strategic ANEs and AI startups to architect, deploy, optimize, and scale production AI and agentic systems on DigitalOcean’s AI-Native Cloud. Support complex migrations, production-ready PoCs, deployment acceleration, and long-term workload expansion across inference and runtime platforms.</li> <li><strong>AI Performance &amp; Systems Engineering: </strong>Optimize distributed inference and runtime performance through benchmarking, GPU efficiency tuning, KV-cache optimization, speculative decoding, prefill/decode disaggregation, multi-node deployments, and latency/cost optimization.</li> <li><strong>Platform Validation &amp; Product Acceleration: </strong>Act as the “first customer” for DigitalOcean’s AI-native platform capabilities including Inference Engine, runtimes, orchestration systems, GPU platforms, and deployment workflows. Surface real-world operational insights, architectural gaps, and scaling bottlenecks directly to Product Engineering and Research teams.</li> <li><strong>Platform Intelligence &amp; Automation: </strong>Build scalable deployment assets including benchmarking systems, automation tooling, AI starter kits, deployment frameworks, operational playbooks, finetuning workflows, and reference architectures that improve deployment velocity and platform adoption.</li> <li><strong>Ecosystem &amp; Technical Enablement: </strong>Collaborate with GPU vendors, model providers, infrastructure partners, and ISVs on co-development, technical validation, optimization, and launch readiness. Enable customer-facing technical teams and partner teams through validated deployment patterns, benchmarking insights, operational playbooks, reference architectures, demos, and technical guidance that help scale adoption of DigitalOcean’s AI-native platform.</li> <li><strong>Travel: </strong>Ability to travel up to 30% for customer engagements, strategic onsite workshops, ecosystem partnerships, conferences, and internal collaboration.</li> </ul> <h2><strong>Key Metrics</strong></h2> <ul> <li><strong>Customer Adoption &amp; Production Success: </strong>Measured by high-impact production workloads launched, reduction in time-to-production, pilot-to-production conversion rates, and expansion of AI-native platform adoption across strategic customers.</li> <li><strong>Platform Intelligence &amp; Product Influence: </strong>Measured by product improvements, roadmap influence, validated customer hypotheses, and operational insights generated from real-world production deployments.</li> <li><strong>Asset &amp; Tooling Delivery: </strong>Measured through adoption of FDE-built frameworks, automation tooling, benchmarking systems, operational playbooks, and reference architectures across customers and internal teams.</li> <li><strong>Field Enablement &amp; Ecosystem Scale: </strong>Measured through successful enablement of customer-facing teams, ecosystem collaboration outcomes, and adoption of FDE deployment standards across the AI-native ecosystem.</li> </ul> <h2><strong>What You’ll Add to DigitalOcean</strong></h2> <ul> <li><strong>AI-Native Systems &amp; Architecture Expertise: </strong>Experience designing and operationalizing production AI systems including inference workloads, agentic runtimes, orchestration frameworks, and AI-native applications. Strong hands-on experience with inference and serving frameworks such as vLLM, SGLang, Ray Serve, NVIDIA Dynamo, llm-d, or equivalent systems, along with LLM optimization techniques including continuous batching, quantization, KV-cache optimization, and speculative decoding.</li> <li><strong>Distributed Systems &amp; Infrastructure Mastery: </strong>Deep expertise with NVIDIA and AMD GPU platforms and their software ecosystems including CUDA, ROCm, TensorRT, Triton, NCCL, RCCL, NVLink, XGMI, and RoCE. Strong proficiency with Kubernetes (K8s), distributed systems, networking, storage systems, Infrastructure as Code, and large-scale AI infrastructure architectures.</li> <li><strong>Runtime &amp; Orchestration Systems: </strong>Experience with AI orchestration and agent frameworks such as LangGraph, CrewAI, MCP ecosystems, LlamaIndex, OpenAI Agents SDK, or similar runtime systems. Understanding of workflow orchestration, deployment systems, memory patterns, and AI-native application architectures.</li> <li><strong>Software Engineering &amp; Automation: </strong>Strong production coding skills in Python or Go with experience building tooling, automation systems, deployment workflows, benchmarking frameworks, and operational platforms.</li> <li><strong>Performance &amp; Operational Intelligence: </strong>Proven ability to benchmark and optimize AI infrastructure with strong focus on scalability, reliability, GPU efficiency, runtime performance, latency optimization, and workload economics.</li> <li><strong>Consultative &amp; Cross-Functional Execution: </strong>Ability to establish technical credibility with CTOs, Principal architects, Product Engineering teams, and ecosystem partners while managing high-impact production deployments and strategic technical initiatives.</li> </ul> <h2><strong>Preferred Qualifications</strong></h2> <ul> <li><strong>AI Infrastructure &amp; Forward Deployed Engineering Experience: </strong>Experience working in Forward Deployed Engineering, AI Infrastructure, Technical Consulting, AI Platform Engineering, or equivalent customer-facing engineering roles supporting production AI systems.</li> <li><strong>Platform Enablement &amp; Ecosystem Experience: </strong>Experience building deployment standards, technical enablement programs, platform adoption frameworks, or ecosystem integration strategies across customer-facing and engineering organizations.</li> <li><strong>Open Source &amp; As a member of the team, you will be a Shark who thinks big, bold, and scrappy, like an owner with a bias for action and a powerful sense of responsibility for customers, products, employees, and decisions.</li> <li data-stringify-indent="0" data-stringify-border="0"><strong data-stringify-type="bold">We prioritize career development.</strong> At DO, you’ll do the best work of your career. title: Senior Analytics Engineer
full_description: <h3>Who we are</h3> <p>The real world is the next frontier, and at Metropolis, we are creating the artificial intelligence to make it responsive. We are pioneering the Recognition Economy — a future where mundane repetition disappears and being known unlocks access, comfort and belonging everywhere you go. From transforming parking into a seamless drive-in, drive-out experience for millions of Members to expanding our intelligence layer across retail and hospitality, we are building a world that feels instinctive and magical. The future isn’t coming; it’s here, and we need builders, innovators and problem solvers to help us create it.</p> <h3>Who you are</h3> <p>Metropolis is seeking a Senior Analytics Engineer to join our Data Engineering and Analytics team. The ideal candidate will possess a passion for creating value using data and a strong foundation in all aspects of analytics engineering. In this pivotal role, you will be instrumental in making data accessible and actionable, facilitating informed decision-making throughout the company. You will partner and lead across multiple product team functions and domains and drive impact through your data vision and architecture.</p> <h3>What you'll do</h3> <ul> <li>Design and implement large-scale data warehouses and data marts using Snowflake, Star, or Data Vault 2.0 schemas</li> <li>Build and maintain data transformation pipelines using SQL frameworks like dbt to abstract model code</li> <li>Write complex SQL including window functions and STRUCT/ARRAY manipulations while adhering to performance best practices</li> <li>Manage and optimize DAGs, including troubleshooting task failures and improving run times</li> <li>Implement metadata management, data lineage, and security principles such as least-privilege access</li> <li>Use a test-driven approach to design systems that trap poor quality data and trigger alerts within the data flow</li> <li>Partner with engineering, data science, and business stakeholders to own project scopes</li> <li>Mentor others on data best practices to drive impact through your data vision</li> <li>Provide architectural guidance to ensure data is accessible and actionable across the company</li> <li>Drive technical excellence within the data engineering and analytics domain</li> </ul> <h3>What we're looking for</h3> <ul> <li>5+ years of experience in data/analytics engineering with proficiency in Python and SQL within Snowflake</li> <li>3+ years of experience with dbt</li> <li>Owned large-scale data projects that drove significant impact through effective cross-functional partnering</li> <li>Led the design, implementation, and monitoring of data warehouses that enable clear product-focused insights</li> <li>Expert at SQL, window functions, and query optimization for complex data manipulations</li> <li>Applied performance best practices, including partitions, sort keys, and incremental modeling</li> <li>Designed systems using test-driven approaches to ensure data quality and flow integrity</li> <li>Familiarity with ELT processes and tools to prepare and clean data for analysis</li> <li>Skill in using tools like dbt to build data transformation pipelines</li> <li>Experience mentoring stakeholders in Data Science, Analytics, PM, and Engineering on data best practices</li> <li>Experience leveraging AI tools to transform static workflows into responsive, high-output processes</li> </ul> <h3>While not required, these are a plus:</h3> <ul> <li>A dbt certification</li> </ul> <hr> <p><strong>4 Days in Office:</strong> Metropolis values in-person collaboration to drive innovation, strengthen culture, and enhance the Member experience. Our corporate team members hold to our office-first model, which requires employees to be on-site at least four days a week, fostering organic interactions that spark creativity and connection.</p> <p><em>When you join Metropolis, you'll join a team of world-class product leaders and engineers, building an ecosystem of technologies at the intersection of parking, mobility, and real estate. Our goal is to build an inclusive culture where everyone has a voice and the best idea wins. You will play a key role in building and maintaining this culture as our organization grows. The anticipated base salary for this position is $130,000.00 USD to $175,000.00 USD annually. The actual base salary offered is determined by a number of variables, including, as appropriate, the applicant's qualifications for the position, years of relevant experience, distinctive skills, level of education attained, certifications or other professional licenses held, and the location of residence and/or place of employment. Base salary is one component of Metropolis's total compensation package, which may also include access to or eligibility for healthcare benefits, a 401(k) plan, short-term and long-term disability coverage, basic life insurance, a lucrative stock option plan, bonus plans and more. </em><span style="color: rgb(255, 255, 255);"><em>#LI-CM1 #LI-Onsite</em></span></p><div class="content-conclusion"><p><em>Metropolis may utilize an automated employment decision tool (AEDT) to assess or evaluate your candidacy for employment or promotion. AEDTs are used to assist in assessing a candidate’s application relative to the required job qualifications and responsibilities listed in the job posting.</em></p> <p><em>As part of this process, Metropolis retains data relevant to your candidacy, including personal information, for a period that is reasonably necessary for the use of the tool. If you are hired for the position, your data may become part of your employee records.</em></p> <p><em>Metropolis Technologies is an equal opportunity employer. We make all hiring decisions based on merit, qualifications, and business needs, without regard to race, color, religion, sex (including gender identity, sexual orientation, or pregnancy), national origin, disability, veteran status, or any other protected characteristic under federal, state, or local law.</em></p></div>

title: Senior Data Engineer
full_description: <h3>Who we are</h3> <p>The real world is the next frontier, and at Metropolis, we are creating the artificial intelligence to make it responsive. We are pioneering the Recognition Economy — a future where mundane repetition disappears and being known unlocks access, comfort, and belonging everywhere you go. From transforming parking into a seamless drive-in, drive-out experience for millions of Members to expanding our intelligence layer across retail and hospitality, we are building a world that feels instinctive and magical. The future isn't coming; it's here, and we need builders, innovators, and problem solvers to help us create it.</p> <h3>Who you are</h3> <p>As a Senior Data Engineer at Metropolis, you will play a key role in shaping data products that align with our mission. Your technical expertise and analytical acumen will contribute to designing and building extensive data sets that impact thousands of internal users and millions of members. Join a world-class data engineering team dedicated to advancing your skills and career in data engineering and beyond.</p> <h3>What you'll do</h3> <ul> <li>Collaborate with cross-functional teams to develop end-to-end data pipelines and foundational data sets</li> <li>Design and own data architecture for large-scale projects while managing operational trade-offs</li> <li>Build and optimize sophisticated data pipelines, models, and visualizations across multiple domains</li> <li>Define and manage Service Level Agreements (SLAs) for all owned data sets</li> <li>Implement data security models, ensure privacy compliance, and evolve data governance processes</li> <li>Solve complex integration challenges using optimal ETL/ELT patterns for structured and unstructured data</li> <li>Maintain production processes by optimizing complex code using advanced algorithmic concepts</li> <li>Streamline data artifact development by optimizing pipelines, dashboards, and frameworks</li> <li>Mentor team members through actionable feedback to foster collective skill growth</li> <li>Maintain flexibility with availability during evening hours</li> </ul> <h3>What we're looking for</h3> <ul> <li>Bachelor's degree in Computer Science, Computer Engineering, or a relevant technical field</li> <li>5+ years of experience in data engineering, database engineering, business intelligence, data warehousing, and ETL tools, working with large data sets in the cloud</li> <li>5+ years of experience with Python, and experience building scalable Big Data solutions and ETL ecosystems</li> <li>Proficiency in SQL, ETL/ELT, and data modeling, with extensive experience in Snowflake and dbt</li> <li>Hands-on experience with RDBMS such as MySQL, MS SQL Server (optional), and Postgres</li> <li>Familiarity with integration tools like Airflow and Automic, along with a working knowledge of CI/CD pipelines</li> <li>Ability to deliver high standards of code quality, system reliability, and performance</li> <li>Deep understanding of the modern IaC ecosystem to drive automated infrastructure deployments</li> <li>Experience with cloud computing services, preferably AWS, including hands-on experience with services like Glue, Airflow (MWAA), DMS, EKS, and SNS</li> <li>Experience leveraging AI tools to transform static workflows into responsive, high-output processes</li> </ul> <h3>While not required, these are a plus:</h3> <ul> <li>Experience with Spark and PySpark</li> <li>Master's degree in Computer Science, Computer Engineering, or a relevant technical field</li> </ul> <hr> <p><strong>4 Days in Office:</strong> Metropolis values in-person collaboration to drive innovation, strengthen culture, and enhance the Member experience. Our corporate team members hold to our office-first model, which requires employees to be on-site at least four days a week, fostering organic interactions that spark creativity and connection.</p> <p><em>When you join Metropolis, you’ll join a team of world-class product leaders and engineers, building an ecosystem of technologies at the intersection of parking, mobility, and real estate. Our goal is to build an inclusive culture where everyone has a voice and the best idea wins. You will play a key role in building and maintaining this culture as our organization grows. The anticipated base salary for this position is $160,000.00 USD to $220,000.00 USD annually. The actual base salary offered is determined by a number of variables, including, as appropriate, the applicant's qualifications for the position, years of relevant experience, distinctive skills, level of education attained, certifications or other professional licenses held, and the location of residence and/or place of employment. Base salary is one component of Metropolis’s total compensation package, which may also include access to or eligibility for healthcare benefits, a 401(k) plan, short-term and long-term disability coverage, basic life insurance, a lucrative stock option plan, bonus plans and more. </em><span style="color: rgb(255, 255, 255);"><em>#LI-CM1 #LI-Onsite</em></span></p><div class="content-conclusion"><p><em>Metropolis may utilize an automated employment decision tool (AEDT) to assess or evaluate your candidacy for employment or promotion.
