title: Senior Data Engineer
full_description: <h3>Who we are</h3> <p>Metropolis is an artificial intelligence company that uses computer vision technology to enable frictionless, checkout-free experiences in the real world. Today, we are reimagining parking to enable millions of consumers to just "drive in and drive out." We envision a future where people transact in the real world with a speed, ease and convenience that is unparalleled, even online. Tomorrow, we will power checkout-free experiences anywhere you go to make the everyday experiences of living, working and playing remarkable - giving us back our most valuable asset, time.</p> <h3>Who you are</h3> <p>As a Senior Data Engineer at Metropolis, you will play a key role in shaping both customer-facing and business-facing data products that align with our mission. Your technical expertise and analytical acumen will contribute to designing and building extensive data sets that impact thousands of internal users and millions of members. Join a world-class data engineering team dedicated to advancing your skills and career in data engineering and beyond.</p> <h3>What you'll do</h3> <ul> <li>Collaborate with engineers, product managers, and data scientists to understand data needs, and develop end-to-end data pipelines, solutions, and foundational data sets</li> <li>Design and own the data architecture for large-scale projects, making informed decisions about design and operational trade-offs</li> <li>Build, launch, and optimize sophisticated data pipelines, data models, and visualizations that support diverse use cases across multiple products or domains</li> <li>Define and manage Service Level Agreements (SLAs) for all data sets within areas of ownership</li> <li>Implement data security models based on privacy requirements, ensure compliance with safeguards, address data quality issues, and evolve data governance processes</li> <li>Solve complex data integration challenges using optimal ETL/ELT patterns, frameworks, and query techniques for both structured and unstructured data sources</li> <li>Maintain and enhance existing production processes by optimizing complex code using advanced algorithmic concepts&nbsp;</li> <li>Optimize pipelines, dashboards, frameworks, and systems to streamline data artifact development</li> <li>Mentor team members, providing and receiving actionable feedback to foster skill growth and collaboration</li> <li>Ensure flexibility with availability during evening hours</li> </ul> <h3>What we're looking for</h3> <ul> <li>Bachelor's degree in computer science, Computer Engineering, or a relevant technical field; a master's degree is preferred</li> <li>5+ years of experience in data engineering, database engineering, business intelligence, data warehousing, and ETL tools, working with large data sets in the cloud</li> <li>Proficiency in SQL, ETL/ELT, and data modeling, with extensive experience in Snowflake and dbt&nbsp;</li> <li>5+ years of experience with Python, and experience building scalable Big Data solutions and ETL ecosystems</li> <li>Hands-on experience with RDBMS such as MySQL, MS SQL Server, and Postgres</li> <li>Familiarity with integration tools like Airflow and Automic, along with a working knowledge of CI/CD pipelines</li> <li>Ability to deliver high standards of code quality, system reliability, and performance</li> <li>Experience with Spark and PySpark to handle large-scale data processing and enhance data pipeline efficiency</li> <li>Strong proficiency in Terraform is a must-have.</li> </ul> <h3>Our Stack</h3> <ul> <li>Languages + Frameworks: TypeScript, React, Scala (principally), Java (limited)</li> <li>Datastores: MySQL, PostgreSQL, Snowflake</li> <li>Cloud: AWS</li> <li>Version control: Git &amp; GitHub</li> <li>AI Tooling: Copilot on GitHub</li> <li>Observability: Datadog</li> </ul> <hr> <p><em>When you join Metropolis, you’ll join a team of world-class product leaders and engineers, building an ecosystem of technologies at the intersection of parking, mobility, and real estate. Our goal is to build an inclusive culture where everyone has a voice and the best idea wins. You will play a key role in building and maintaining this culture as our organization grows. </em><span style="color: rgb(255, 255, 255);"><em>#LI-SR1 #LI-Onsite</em></span></p><div class="content-conclusion"><p><em>Metropolis may utilize an automated employment decision tool (AEDT) to assess or evaluate your candidacy for employment or promotion. AEDTs are used to assist in assessing a candidate’s application relative to the required job qualifications and responsibilities listed in the job posting.</em></p> <p><em>As part of this process, Metropolis retains data relevant to your candidacy, including personal information, for a period that is reasonably necessary for the use of the tool. If you are hired for the position, your data may become part of your employee records.</em></p> <p><em>Metropolis Technologies is an equal opportunity employer. We make all hiring decisions based on merit, qualifications, and business needs, without regard to race, color, religion, sex (including gender identity, sexual orientation, or pregnancy), national origin, disability, veteran status, or any other protected characteristic under federal, state, or local law.</em></p></div>

title: Staff Analytics Engineer
full_description: <div class="content-intro"><p>Join the company that’s building the telemetry infrastructure for the AI era. At Cribl, we partner with IT and Security teams at many of the world’s biggest enterprises, including half of the Fortune 100, to bridge the gap between AI ambition and infrastructure reality. As the AI Platform for Telemetry, we give customers the choice, control, and flexibility to manage and analyze telemetry for both humans and agents, so they can build what’s next.</p> <p>We’re one of the fastest‑growing private companies and a leading player in a massive, fast‑moving market. With a global workforce, we’re remote‑first and grounded in a simple idea: software is a people business. Cribl is the place where curious, collaborative people can do their best work, grow fast, and bring their full selves to the herd.</p></div><p><strong>Why You’ll Love This Role</strong></p> <p>As part of the Data team at Cribl, you will own the evolution of our analytics engineering platform, ensuring our data models are trusted, scalable, and AI-ready.</p> <p>Analytics Engineering transforms raw data into governed, business-ready models that power analytics, executive reporting, and AI-enabled decision making. As Cribl continues to scale, this role will establish the architecture, standards, and technical practices that enable consistent, trusted analytics across Product, Engineering, Customer, Marketing, GTM, and Finance.</p> <p>You will serve as a technical leader for analytics engineering, partnering closely with analysts, data engineers, governance, and business stakeholders to improve the quality, consistency, and reliability of our warehouse. While analysts own business logic within their domains, you will own the technical architecture, modeling standards, and engineering practices that allow our analytics platform to scale.</p> <p>If you enjoy building systems that enable others, care deeply about data quality and developer experience, and want to shape the future of analytics at Cribl, we'd love to talk to you.</p> <p><strong><br>As An Active Member Of Our Team, You Will…</strong></p> <ul> <li>Own the long-term architecture and evolution of Cribl's analytics engineering platform, including foundational model stabilization and AI readiness.</li> <li>Design, build, and maintain certified dbt models as the authoritative source for business-critical metrics.</li> <li>Establish and enforce analytics engineering standards for modeling, testing, documentation, and code review, including review of high-impact dbt changes.</li> <li>Design and maintain semantic and metadata layers that enable reliable AI-powered analytics and self-service.</li> <li>Partner with analysts to migrate high-value business logic from Omni into governed warehouse models.</li> <li>Partner with Data Engineering to improve source reliability, warehouse architecture, and Snowflake performance and cost efficiency.</li> <li>Mentor analysts and analytics engineers on dbt development, data modeling, and analytics engineering best practices.</li> <li>We are a remote-first company and work happens across many time-zones - you may be required to occasionally perform duties outside your standard working hours.</li> </ul> <p><strong><br>If You’ve Got It - We Want It</strong></p> <ul> <li>7+ years of experience in analytics engineering, data engineering, or a related technical field, including at least 3 years of hands-on experience running dbt in a production environment, with expert-level SQL and demonstrated ownership of scalable dimensional models.</li> <li>Deep expertise in modern analytics engineering practices, including version control, testing, CI/CD, documentation, data contracts, lineage, and governance.</li> <li>Experience designing reusable semantic models, certified metrics, and warehouse architectures that enable self-service analytics across multiple business domains.</li> <li>Strong understanding of Snowflake performance optimization and modern cloud data warehouse architecture.</li> <li>Demonstrated ability to establish technical standards, influence engineering practices without formal authority, and improve platform reliability while reducing technical debt.</li> <li>Experience with semantic layers, metadata management, or AI-enabled analytics platforms.</li> <li>Proven ability to partner closely with analysts to translate business requirements into scalable, maintainable warehouse models.</li> <li>Strong communication skills with the ability to explain complex technical concepts and tradeoffs to both technical and business audiences.</li> </ul> <p><br>#LI-JB1<br>#LI-Remote</p><div class="content-pay-transparency"><div class="pay-input"><div class="description"><p>The salary for this role is dependent on geographic location and will be based on the individual candidate's job-related knowledge, skills, and experience.<br><br>In addition to base salary, for sales and some sales-adjacent roles, employees are eligible to earn incentive compensation (commission). For all other roles, employees are eligible to participate in the Cribl Corporate Bonus Program.<br><br>In addition to a competitive salary, Cribl also offers a generous benefits package which includes health, dental, vision, short-term disability, and life insurance, paid holidays and paid time off, a fertility treatment benefit, 401(k), and equity.</p></div><div class="title">Base Salary Range</div><div class="pay-range"><span>$145,000</span><span class="divider">&mdash;</span><span>$190,000 USD</span></div></div></div><div class="content-conclusion"><p><strong>Bring Your Whole Self<br></strong><span style="font-weight: 400;"><br></span><span style="font-weight: 400;">Diversity drives innovation, enables better decisions to support our customers, and inspires change for the better. We’re building a culture where differences are valued and welcomed, and we work together to bring out the best in each other. All qualified applicants will receive consideration for employment without regard to race, color, religion, sex, sexual orientation, gender identity, national origin, or any other applicable legally protected characteristics in the location in which the candidate is applying.</span></p> <p><span style="font-weight: 400;">Interested in joining the Cribl herd? Learn more about the smartest, funniest, most passionate goats you’ll ever meet at </span><a href="https://cribl.io/about-us/"><span style="font-weight: 400;">cribl.io/about-us</span></a><span style="font-weight: 400;">.&nbsp;</span></p></div>

title: Senior Data Engineer
full_description: <div class="content-intro"><p><a href="https://www.federato.ai/">Federato</a> is on a mission to defend the right to efficient, equitable insurance for all. We enable insurers to provide affordable coverage to people and organizations facing the issues of today - the climate crisis, cyber-attacks, social inflation, etc. Our vision is understood and well funded by those behind Salesforce, Veeva, Zoom, Box, etc.<br><br>Federato is the only AI-native platform that spans the full policy lifecycle and changes the way insurance work gets done. Better decisioning is built-in, not bolted on: insurers' unique portfolio goals, strategies, rules, and appetite are part of the workflow so underwriters win the right deals, faster. From the moment a submission hits an underwriter’s inbox, AI is put to work, triaging submissions with a focus on high-appetite business, delivering real-time feedback on the portfolio, and consolidating workflows into a single proven system. Federato drives better business outcomes.</p></div><p><span style="text-decoration: underline;"><strong>Role Overview</strong></span><br>You’ll be joining a small, high-impact data engineering team within Federato’s AI/ML organization. Our focus is on building the infrastructure and internal frameworks that empower machine learning engineers to develop, deploy, and iterate on AI-powered features ranging from prompt-based LLM workflows to more traditional model-driven systems. We collaborate closely with ML, analytics, and product teams to ensure data and tooling are reliable, scalable, and aligned with the needs of our AI-native platform.</p> <p><span style="text-decoration: underline;"><strong>What You'll Be Doing</strong>:</span></p> <ul> <li>Collaborate with Data Science, Product Managers and Software Engineers to build robust ETL pipelines that enable the Product Support team to deliver compelling user-facing features</li> <li>Contribute to architecture decisions, observability tooling, and data quality initiatives that keep our platform robust and maintainable.</li> <li>Contribute to a scalable internal framework for managing prompt engineering pipelines and other AI workflows.</li> <li>Enforce and elevate engineering best practices across the AI/ML org, including code quality, testing, and documentation.</li> </ul> <p><span style="text-decoration: underline;"><strong>Who We Hope You Are:</strong></span></p> <ul> <li>5+ years of experience in data engineering, backend engineering, or related roles with a focus on data infrastructure.</li> <li>Proven experience designing and maintaining scalable data pipelines (e.g., using Airflow, Dagster, or Prefect).</li> <li>Experience with software development practices like version control, CI/CD, or dbt testing strategies.</li> <li>Strong proficiency in SQL and Python, with bonus points for Typescript (or similar) experience</li> <li>Comfort working with version control, CI/CD systems, and cloud infrastructure (e.g., AWS, GCP, Terraform).</li> <li>Comfortable navigating ambiguity and working closely with business stakeholders to understand their data needs.</li> <li>Proven track record of designing high-impact data products and pipelines in fast-paced environments.</li> </ul> <p><span style="text-decoration: underline;"><strong>Bonus Points for:</strong></span></p> <ul> <li>Prior experience working in or adjacent to insurance, fintech, or risk modeling domains.</li> <li>Prior exposure to ML ops or experience supporting AI/ML-driven products</li> <li>Enthusiasm for building internal tools or frameworks to improve team velocity.</li> <li>Contributions to open-source data tools or involvement in the data community.</li> </ul> <p>$160,000 - $210,000 a year<br>Final offer amounts are determined by multiple factors including candidate location, experience and expertise and may vary from the amounts listed above. Total compensation package does include stock options, benefits and additional perks.&nbsp;</p><div class="content-conclusion"><p>Here at Federato, your capabilities are important, but culture fit is essential. We move fast, are eager to listen to our users, take a first principles approach to solving problems, and value learning and the ability to change our minds. Most importantly, we're here to have fun. Our ability to make a difference starts with our people. We would love to work with you!&nbsp;</p> <p><br><em>We are an equal-opportunity employer and value diversity at our company. We do not discriminate on the basis of race, religion, color, national origin, sex, gender expression, sexual orientation, age, marital status, veteran status or disability status. We will provide reasonable accommodation to individuals with disabilities to participate in the job application or interview process, to perform essential job functions, and to receive other benefits and privileges of employment. Please contact us to request accommodation at <a href="mailto:talent@federato.ai">talent@federato.ai</a></em></p></div>

title: Contract: Sr. Analytics Engineer - Internal Data
full_description: <p>Seeking to hire a Senior Analytical Engineer - Contractor based out of Mexico or Argentina for Newsela's Internal Data Team.</p> <ul> <li>We are looking for a Senior Analytics Engineer to join our data team.</li> <li>Reporting to the Hiring Manager, you will be responsible for applying data engineering best practices to analytics code to transform, test, and document data.</li> <li>You will provide clean and organized data sets to end users.</li> <li>You will be provide expert code reviews and mentorship to other engineers in the data space</li> <li>You will be responsible for building and maintaining composable data models, as well as optimizing SQL query performance for the models you build.</li> <li>You will transform raw data into business insights, working closely with stakeholders and developing analyses to answer critical business questions.</li> <li>You will create data visualizations and help stakeholders explore and understand the data visualization tools available to them.</li> </ul> <p><strong>Why you'll love this role:</strong></p> <p><strong>Data Modeling and Transformation</strong></p> <ul> <ul> <li>Build new analyses and support existing ones using SQL and Python.</li> <li>Apply software engineering principles like version control and continuous integration to the analytics codebase.</li> <li>Expand our data warehouse with clean data ready for analysis.</li> </ul> </ul> <p><strong>Data Quality and Testing</strong></p> <ul> <ul> <li>Apply advanced data testing strategies to ensure resulting datastores are aligned with expected business logic.</li> <li>Implement validation checks and automated testing procedures to manage data quality in your ETL/ELT pipelines.</li> </ul> </ul> <p><strong>Collaboration and Communication</strong></p> <ul> <ul> <li>Work with stakeholders to define business logic and data expectations.</li> <li>Help drive a change in the usage of data by actively surfacing insights to stakeholders.</li> <li>Lead initiatives and problem definition, scoping, design, and planning.</li> </ul> </ul> <p><strong>Infrastructure and Automation</strong></p> <ul> <ul> <li>Build tools and automation to run data infrastructure.</li> <li>Manage large-scale data migrations in relational datastores.</li> </ul> </ul> <p><strong>Why you're a great fit:</strong></p> <ul> <li>8+ Years experience working with data in a software environment.&nbsp;</li> <li>Required Skills: Mastered proficiency in SQL and Python; advanced experience managing business semantic layer tooling, data catalog tooling and&nbsp; data integrity testing frameworks. Experience with dbt orchestration and best practices.</li> <li>You have a track record of working autonomously and proactively, with deep domain knowledge of data systems.</li> <li>Required Tech Stack: SQL, Python, relational datastores, DAG tooling (like Dagster or Airflow), dbt and Tableau.</li> <li>Experience with cloud-based infrastructure (AWS, GCP, Terraform) and document, graph, or schema-less datastores.</li> </ul> <p>Please note that given the nature of the contract, this role will not be eligible to participate in company-sponsored benefits</p> <h3><span style="font-size: 10pt;"><strong>About Newsela:</strong></span></h3> <p>Newsela is a leading education technology company dedicated to meaningful classroom learning for every student. We deliver integrated, AI-powered solutions designed to unlock student engagement, empower teachers, and drive meaningful learning outcomes. Our suite of products supports knowledge and skill development, writing practice, daily instruction, assessment, and data-informed decision-making across K–12 classrooms. Grounded in learning science research, Newsela’s solutions integrate content, assessment, and analytics to help educators track progress, understand student outcomes, and deliver high-impact instruction that supports every learner.</p> <p>&nbsp;</p> <p>#LI-Remote</p>

title: Data Engineer
full_description: <p><strong>The Role:</strong></p> <p>We are seeking a skilled Data Engineer to join our dynamic team. In this role, you will be responsible for building and maintaining integrations, automating data workflows, and ensuring data accuracy and availability across educational platforms. You’ll play a key role in supporting school operations.</p> <p><span style="font-family: arial, helvetica, sans-serif; font-size: 12pt;"><strong>Why You'll Love This Role:&nbsp;</strong></span></p> <ul> <li style="font-size: 12pt;"><span style="font-size: 12pt;">Develop and maintain custom integrations and ETL processes between K-12 data and internal/external systems.</span></li> <li style="font-size: 12pt;"><span style="font-size: 12pt;">Design, implement, and optimize data pipelines</span></li> <li style="font-size: 12pt;"><span style="font-size: 12pt;">Collaborate with stakeholders to understand data needs and deliver robust, scalable solutions.</span></li> <li style="font-size: 12pt;"><span style="font-size: 12pt;">Maintain technical documentation for systems, data models, and processes.</span></li> <li style="font-size: 12pt;"><span style="font-size: 12pt;">Monitor and troubleshoot data flow issues and respond to API changes.</span></li> <li style="font-size: 12pt;"><span style="font-size: 12pt;">Participate in cross-functional projects related to school operations, enrollment, attendance, and performance data.</span></li> <li style="font-size: 12pt;"><span style="font-size: 12pt;">Perform quality assurance (QA) on downstream data to ensure accuracy, consistency, and alignment with customer requirements.</span></li> </ul> <p><span style="font-family: arial, helvetica, sans-serif; font-size: 12pt;"><strong>What You'll Be Doing:</strong></span></p> <ul> <li style="font-size: 12pt;"><span style="font-size: 12pt;">Develop and maintain custom integrations and ETL processes between K-12 data and internal/external systems.</span></li> <li style="font-size: 12pt;"><span style="font-size: 12pt;">Design, implement, and optimize data pipelines</span></li> <li style="font-size: 12pt;"><span style="font-size: 12pt;">Collaborate with stakeholders to understand data needs and deliver robust, scalable solutions.</span></li> <li style="font-size: 12pt;"><span style="font-size: 12pt;">Maintain technical documentation for systems, data models, and processes.</span></li> <li style="font-size: 12pt;"><span style="font-size: 12pt;">Monitor and troubleshoot data flow issues and respond to API changes.</span></li> <li style="font-size: 12pt;"><span style="font-size: 12pt;">Participate in cross-functional projects related to school operations, enrollment, attendance, and performance data.</span></li> <li style="font-size: 12pt;"><span style="font-size: 12pt;">Perform quality assurance (QA) on downstream data to ensure accuracy, consistency, and alignment with customer requirements.</span></li> </ul> <p><span style="font-family: arial, helvetica, sans-serif; font-size: 12pt;"><strong>About You:</strong></span></p> <ul> <li style="font-size: 12pt;"><span style="font-size: 12pt;">4+ years of experience in a software engineering, data engineering, or SIS specialist role.</span></li> <li style="font-size: 12pt;"><span style="font-size: 12pt;">Hands-on experience with Student Information Systems or other K-12 platforms, including APIs, data exports/imports, and plugin development.</span></li> <li style="font-size: 12pt;"><span style="font-size: 12pt;">Strong skills in SQL, Python, and ETL/ELT tools.</span></li> <li style="font-size: 12pt;"><span style="font-size: 12pt;">Experience working with RESTful APIs, SFTP, and other data exchange methods.</span></li> <li style="font-size: 12pt;"><span style="font-size: 12pt;">Experience in working with educational data.</span></li> <li style="font-size: 12pt;"><span style="font-size: 12pt;">Familiarity with data warehousing concepts.</span></li> <li style="font-size: 12pt;"><span style="font-size: 12pt;">Experience with data quality assurance practices and tools, including validation and reconciliation of datasets.</span></li> <li style="font-size: 12pt;"><span style="font-size: 12pt;">Strong attention to detail and ability to translate customer data requirements into technical specifications and QA checks.</span></li> <li style="font-size: 12pt;"><span style="font-size: 12pt;">Ability to work independently in a fully remote position.</span></li> </ul> <p><span style="font-size: 12pt; font-family: arial, helvetica, sans-serif;"><strong>Why you’ll love working at Newsela:</strong></span></p> <ul> <li style="font-family: arial, helvetica, sans-serif; font-size: 12pt;"><span style="font-family: arial, helvetica, sans-serif; font-size: 12pt;"><strong>Health &amp; Wellness:</strong> Comprehensive medical benefits with employer contribution to premiums and to HSA accounts. Additional benefits such as gym reimbursement, pet insurance, free access to the Calm app, Rocket Lawyer and more to help you stay healthy: mind, body, and soul.&nbsp;</span></li> <li style="font-family: arial, helvetica, sans-serif; font-size: 12pt;"><span style="font-family: arial, helvetica, sans-serif; font-size: 12pt;"><strong>Work From Home: </strong>We are a fully remote company. We provide a monthly tech stipend to support your WFH needs!</span></li> <li style="font-family: arial, helvetica, sans-serif; font-size: 12pt;"><span style="font-family: arial, helvetica, sans-serif; font-size: 12pt;"><strong>Supporting ALL Families:</strong> Inclusive benefits to support you and your family, including parental leave, fertility support, adoption, and more!&nbsp;</span></li> <li style="font-family: arial, helvetica, sans-serif; font-size: 12pt;"><span style="font-family: arial, helvetica, sans-serif; font-size: 12pt;"><strong>Financial Wellbeing:</strong> Invest in your future with our 401(k) plan, which includes a employer match to help you build long-term financial security.</span></li> <li style="font-family: arial, helvetica, sans-serif; font-size: 12pt;"><span style="font-family: arial, helvetica, sans-serif; font-size: 12pt;"><strong>Time Off: </strong>Flexible PTO, paid sick time off, company holidays plus winter break (Dec 24th - Jan 1st).</span></li> <li style="font-family: arial, helvetica, sans-serif; font-size: 12pt;"><span style="font-family: arial, helvetica, sans-serif; font-size: 12pt;"><strong>Professional Development:</strong><strong> </strong>Newsela offers an annual learning and development allowance to employees to attend external training sessions, classes, workshops, conferences, and educational materials to foster professional growth within their current role and career aspirations at Newsela.</span></li> <li style="font-family: arial, helvetica, sans-serif; font-size: 12pt;"><span style="font-family: arial, helvetica, sans-serif; font-size: 12pt;"><strong>Make A Difference:</strong> No matter your role or department, the work you do each day helps share the future of education and improves the lives of students and teachers.</span></li> </ul> <h3><span style="font-size: 12pt; font-family: arial, helvetica, sans-serif;"><strong>About Newsela:</strong></span></h3> <p><span style="font-family: arial, helvetica, sans-serif; font-size: 12pt;">Newsela is a leading education technology company dedicated to meaningful classroom learning for every student. We deliver integrated, AI-powered solutions designed to unlock student engagement, empower teachers, and drive meaningful learning outcomes. Our suite of products supports knowledge and skill development, writing practice, daily instruction, assessment, and data-informed decision-making across K–12 classrooms. Grounded in learning science research, Newsela’s solutions integrate content, assessment, and analytics to help educators track progress, understand student outcomes, and deliver high-impact instruction that supports every learner.</span></p> <p><span style="font-size: 12pt; font-family: arial, helvetica, sans-serif;">#LI-Remote</span></p><div class="content-pay-transparency"><div class="pay-input"><div class="description"><p><span style="font-size: 16px;">Final compensation will be determined based on the candidate’s individual skills, qualifications, and experience. Total compensation for this role also includes incentive stock options and benefits.&nbsp;</span></p></div><div class="title">Base Compensation Range</div><div class="pay-range"><span>$100,000</span><span class="divider">&mdash;</span><span>$120,800 USD</span></div></div></div>

title: Analytics Engineer
location: Los Angeles, CA, United States; New York, NY, United States
full_description: <div class="content-intro"><h2 class="job-details__description-header text-color-secondary font-family-secondary" data-bind="i18n: 'job-details.employer-description-title'">About Us</h2> <div class="job-details__description-content basic-formatter" data-bind="html: pageData().job.corporateDescription"> <div>Fanatics is building a leading global digital sports platform. We ignite the passions of global sports fans and maximize the presence and reach for our hundreds of sports partners globally by offering products and services across Fanatics Commerce, Fanatics Collectibles, and Fanatics Betting &amp; Gaming, allowing sports fans to Buy, Collect, and Bet. Through the Fanatics platform, sports fans can buy licensed fan gear, jerseys, lifestyle and streetwear products, headwear, and hardgoods; collect physical and digital trading cards, sports memorabilia, and other digital assets; and bet as the company builds its Sportsbook and iGaming platform. Fanatics has an established database of over 100 million global sports fans; a global partner network with approximately 900 sports properties, including major national and international professional sports leagues, players associations, teams, colleges, college conferences and retail partners, 2,500 athletes and celebrities, and 200 exclusive athletes; and over 2,000 retail locations, including its Lids retail stores. Our more than 22,000 employees are committed to relentlessly enhancing the fan experience and delighting sports fans globally.&nbsp;</div> </div> <p>&nbsp;</p></div><p><strong>About the Team</strong></p> <p>We're looking for an Analytics Engineer to join the Sales &amp; Finance Engineering team at Fanatics Collectibles. This team is the technical backbone for our Commercial, Sales, Strategy, and Finance organizations, and this role sits at the intersection of those business relationships and the data infrastructure that serves them via our internal applications.</p> <p>You'll spend roughly equal time understanding what our stakeholders need and building the systems that deliver it. That means partnering directly with product managers, finance analysts, and sales ops leaders to translate fuzzy business questions into well-defined data products and then owning the pipeline, transformation, and visualization work that makes those products real.</p> <p>This isn't a pure platform engineering role or a pure analytics role. We're looking for someone who is comfortable moving between engineering, data and product thinking, curious enough to learn what they don't know, and confident enough to push back when requirements don't make sense yet.</p> <h4>&nbsp;</h4> <h4>What You'll Do</h4> <ul> <li>Build and maintain data pipelines that bring data from source systems into Snowflake, with a focus on reliability, observability, and maintainability</li> <li>Implement and manage orchestration flows using Airflow or Dagster</li> <li>Build transformation layers using dbt, including data modeling, testing, and documentation standards</li> <li>Design and maintain Sigma dashboards that serve as the primary reporting surface for our business stakeholders</li> <li>Ensure data quality and consistency across reporting surfaces, establishing validation practices and source-of-truth definitions</li> <li>Partner with Commercial, Sales, Strategy, and Finance stakeholders, product manager and engineering leads to understand data and reporting needs, surface gaps, and build the tools necessary to support</li> <li>Translate business requirements into clear data specs and prioritized work, often without a fully formed brief to start from</li> <li>Communicate proactively with partners explaining tradeoffs, setting expectations, and making complex data concepts accessible</li> <li>Tag into broader engineering work on the Sales &amp; Finance team as needed this could mean contributing to data-oriented product features, reviewing code, or supporting integrations that touch our data layer</li> </ul> <h4>&nbsp;</h4> <h4>What You'll Bring</h4> <ul> <li>3–5 years of experience in a analytics engineering, data engineering or software engineering, or a hybrid (preferred)</li> <li>Hands-on experience with Snowflake. SQL and Python</li> <li>Strong working knowledge of dbt for transformation, testing, and documentation</li> <li>Experience building and managing pipeline orchestration with Airflow or Dagster</li> <li>Experience building dashboards and data visualizations, ideally with Sigma or a comparable BI tool</li> <li>Exposure to engineering work beyond data, APIs, backend services, or product feature development</li> <li>Demonstrated ability to work directly with business stakeholders, not just receiving tickets, but helping shape what gets built and why</li> <li>Strong written and verbal communication skills; comfortable in rooms with both engineers and executives</li> <li>A genuine curiosity and willingness to stretch, this role will evolve and we want someone who evolves with it</li> </ul> <h4>&nbsp;</h4> <h4>Nice to Have</h4> <ul> <li>Familiarity with AWS data services (Glue, S3, Firehose, Kinesis)</li> <li>Experience in a commerce, collectibles, or marketplace environment</li> <li>Experience in an environment where you had to define your own scope and priorities</li> </ul> <h4>&nbsp;</h4> <h4>Why This Role</h4> <p>You'll have real relationships with the business leaders whose decisions depend on the work you do, and you'll have the latitude to shape how data is used across Sales and Finance at Fanatics Collectibles.</p> <p>&nbsp;</p> <p><em>Ensure your Fanatics job offer is legitimate and don’t fall victim to fraud.&nbsp; Fanatics never seeks payment from job applicants.&nbsp;Feel free to ask your recruiter for a phone call or other type of communication for interview, and ensure your communication is coming from a Fanatics email address (including @collectfanatics.com).&nbsp; For added security, where possible, apply through our company website at&nbsp;<a href="http://www.fanaticsinc.com/careers">www.fanaticsinc.com/careers</a></em></p><div class="content-pay-transparency"><div class="pay-input"><div class="description"><p>The salary range represents base pay only and does not include short-term or long-term incentive compensation. &nbsp;This salary range is specific to Los Angeles and may not be applicable to other locations. &nbsp;When determining base pay, as part of a final compensation package, we consider several factors such as location, experience, qualifications, and training. For information about our benefits, please visit&nbsp;<a href="https://benefitsatfanatics.com/">https://benefitsatfanatics.com/</a></p></div><div class="title">Los Angeles Salary Range</div><div class="pay-range"><span>$90,400</span><span class="divider">&mdash;</span><span>$113,000 USD</span></div></div><div class="pay-input"><div class="description"><p>The salary range represents base pay only and does not include short-term or long-term incentive compensation. &nbsp;This salary range is specific to New York City and may not be applicable to other locations. &nbsp;When determining base pay, as part of a final compensation package, we consider several factors such as location, experience, qualifications, and training. For information about our benefits, please visit&nbsp;<a href="https://benefitsatfanatics.com/">https://benefitsatfanatics.com/</a></p></div><div class="title">NYC Salary Range</div><div class="pay-range"><span>$100,000</span><span class="divider">&mdash;</span><span>$125,000 USD</span></div></div></div><div class="content-conclusion"><p>By submitting your application, you agree to our terms of service and acknowledge you have read our&nbsp;<a href="https://static1.squarespace.com/static/5ff494b9931735466ce28d5b/t/69de53bde2c02c25e9c38758/1776178110037/Candidate+Privacy+Policy+April+2026.pdf" target="_blank">Candidate Privacy Policy.</a></p></div>

title: Senior Analytics Engineer
full_description: <div class="content-intro"><p><span style="font-weight: 400;">Kard is transforming customer loyalty with our rewards-as-a-service API platform. We empower our partners, from neobanks to financial institutions and beyond, to offer tailored rewards that celebrate customers' daily transactions. Backed by $30M from industry-leading investors and seasoned experts, we are reshaping the future of customer engagement.</span></p></div><h3>About the Role</h3> <p><span class="discussion-id-2efb55c6-420f-80b7-8626-001c917224cf notion-enable-hover" data-token-index="0">As a Senior Analytics Engineer at Kard, you will be the go-to expert for turning data into impactful business intelligence tools and visualizations. You'll leverage your strong SQL, dbt, and visualization skills to build dashboards, reports, and analytics solutions that empower our go-to-market teams and external merchant partners to make data-driven decisions. You'll spend most of your time on BI work—designing intuitive visualizations and translating complex data into clear, actionable insights—while collaboratively maintaining and growing an existing foundation of well-structured, reusable data models. This position is ideal for someone who loves the craft of building useful, beautiful data products with an eye toward long-term sustainability, all while embodying Kard's values of initiative, humility, and openness.</span></p> <h3>Responsibilities</h3> <ul> <li><strong>Data Infrastructure and Analytics Engineering</strong>: Build and maintain robust data models using dbt, ensuring data accuracy and accessibility across the organization through automated processes and improved data quality checks.</li> <li><strong>Business Intelligence and Reporting</strong>: Design and migrate dashboards and reports to modern BI tools like Count, translating complex data requirements into clear visualizations that support merchant and issuer success.</li> <li><strong>Merchant and Issuer Analytics</strong>: Develop analytical frameworks for merchant segmentation, performance metrics, and campaign measurement, providing insights that drive strategic decisions and optimize partner relationships.</li> <li><strong>Cross-Functional Collaboration</strong>: Partner with sales, account management, and product teams to understand data needs, scope technical solutions, and deliver analytics that directly support revenue growth and operational efficiency.</li> <li><strong>Technical Leadership and Process Improvement</strong>: Lead pitch development and technical investigations, identifying opportunities to improve data workflows, reduce costs, and enhance the scalability of analytics infrastructure.</li> </ul> <h3>Desired Skills</h3> <ul> <li>Strong proficiency in SQL, with a preference for Databricks experience.</li> <li>dbt familiarity</li> <li>Demonstrated expertise in BI visualization tools</li> <li>Proficiency in Python, with a preference for PySpark experience.</li> <li>Exceptional communication skills with the ability to translate complex data insights into compelling narratives for non-technical stakeholders.</li> <li>A strategic thinker with a product mindset, capable of understanding and aligning data insights with broader business objectives, while proactively addressing the details.</li> <li>Humility and openness, with the ability to seek out, learn from, and adapt to diverse perspectives, achieving the best outcomes.</li> </ul><div class="content-pay-transparency"><div class="pay-input"><div class="title">Remote - USA Pay Range</div><div class="pay-range"><span>$160,000</span><span class="divider">&mdash;</span><span>$180,000 USD</span></div></div></div><div class="content-conclusion"><div><strong>Why Join:</strong></div> <div> <p><span style="font-weight: 400;">Kard recognizes the unique value each of us brings to our collective success. As part of our remote-first team, you'll have the autonomy to influence our trajectory, actively contributing to the growth of our business and the evolution of our company culture. By embodying our core values of Initiative, Openness, and Humility, you will not only witness but directly foster an inclusive and dynamic work environment, from the executive level down.</span></p> <span style="font-weight: 400;">Our competitive benefit plan includes:</span> <ul> <li style="font-weight: 400;"><span style="font-weight: 400;">Flexible PTO, with a minimum requirement to take one day off a quarter, ten days a year, promoting mental well-being</span></li> <li style="font-weight: 400;"><span style="font-weight: 400;">Observance of 11 Federal Holidays, plus an additional day each for Black Friday and Christmas Eve (US employees only)</span></li> <li style="font-weight: 400;"><span style="font-weight: 400;">Health, Dental and Vision Insurance</span></li> <li style="font-weight: 400;"><span style="font-weight: 400;">401k with employer match (US employees only)</span></li> <li style="font-weight: 400;"><span style="font-weight: 400;">Coworking space and WFH setup reimbursement</span></li> <li style="font-weight: 400;"><span style="font-weight: 400;">Company Offsites two times per year for planning, team-building, and networking</span></li> </ul> <span style="font-weight: 400;">Kard is an equal opportunity employer and we actively encourage applicants of all genders, backgrounds, and experiences to apply. We believe that diverse perspectives enrich our workplace and drive our success.</span></div></div>

title: Principal Data Engineer
full_description: <p>At Mitratech, we are a team of technocrats focused on building world-class products that simplify operations in the Legal, Risk, Compliance, and HR functions. We are a close-knit, globally dispersed team that thrives in an ecosystem that supports individual excellence and takes pride in its diverse and inclusive work culture centered around great people practices, learning opportunities, and having fun! Our culture is the ideal blend of entrepreneurial spirit and enterprise investment, enabling the chance to move at a rapid pace with some of the most complex, leading-edge technologies available.</p> <p>For over 35 years, the experts at Mitratech have been focused on solving the complex needs. Today, we serve 20,000 client companies of all sizes globally, representing 30% of the Fortune 500 and over 500,000 users in over 160 countries.</p> <p>As we continue to grow, we’re always looking for resourceful, enthusiastic, and fresh perspectives. Join our global team and see what makes Mitratech a truly exceptional place to work!</p> <p><strong><span data-contrast="none">About Engineering at Mitratech Legal Solutions</span></strong><span data-ccp-props="{&quot;335559738&quot;:280,&quot;335559739&quot;:80}">&nbsp;</span></p> <p><span data-contrast="none">Mitratech's engineering&nbsp;organization&nbsp;is a collaborative and dynamic environment where engineers are empowered to drive technical direction and innovation. title: Forward Deployed Engineer (AI Practice)
full_description: <p>Greenlight helps organizations solve complex business challenges through intelligent automation, agentic AI, and custom technology solutions.</p> <p>&nbsp;</p> <p>Our teams work directly with clients to understand their operations, identify opportunities, and rapidly build solutions that create measurable business value. We combine deep consulting expertise with hands-on engineering to bridge the gap between strategy and execution.</p> <p>&nbsp;</p> <p>We’re building a future where consultants and engineers work alongside AI to deliver faster outcomes, stronger businesses, and transformative customer experiences. Anthropic’s Claude is embedded across how we design, build, and validate solutions - and this role is at the technical frontier of that capability.</p> <h3>&nbsp;</h3> <h3><strong>What makes a star at Greenlight?</strong></h3> <h3>·&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp; Builder's instinct, architect's discipline</h3> <h3>·&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp; The most technically credible person in the room</h3> <h3>·&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp; AI-native at depth - you know how LLMs fail and build around it</h3> <h3>·&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp; Client-embedded, not arm's length</h3> <h3>·&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp; Commercially aware - scope, economics, outcomes</h3> <p><strong>The Role</strong></p> <p>The Forward Deployed Engineer is Greenlight’s most senior technical delivery role. You design, build, and operationalize AI agent workflows and intelligent automation solutions - embedded directly in client environments, working alongside their teams to deliver production-grade solutions that create measurable business outcomes.</p> <p>&nbsp;</p> <p>This is not a back-office engineering role. You will be in client discovery sessions, presenting architectures to IT leadership, deploying AI agents against real enterprise systems, and building the reusable skills and accelerators that make Greenlight faster and more differentiated on every subsequent engagement. You are expected to be the most technically credible person in the room - combining deep AI fluency with the consulting presence of a senior practitioner.</p> <p>&nbsp;</p> <p>You work at the frontier of agentic AI - designing and building Claude-powered solutions, connecting AI agents to enterprise systems through MCP integrations, and producing the technical documentation that the delivery team and the client can both stand behind. The platform is Anthropic Claude. The problem space is complex, regulated, and high-stakes. The bar is production-grade.</p> <p><strong>At a Glance</strong></p> <table width="624"> <tbody> <tr> <td width="142"> <p><strong>Reports To</strong></p> </td> <td width="482"> <p>AI Practice Lead</p> </td> </tr> <tr> <td width="142"> <p><strong>Works Closely With</strong></p> </td> <td width="482"> <p>Pre-Sales SE, Automation Business Consultant, AI Delivery Engagement Manager</p> </td> </tr> <tr> <td width="142"> <p><strong>Client Interaction</strong></p> </td> <td width="482"> <p>Yes — C-suite, IT architects, operations leaders, technical teams</p> </td> </tr> <tr> <td width="142"> <p><strong>Travel Requirement</strong></p> </td> <td width="482"> <p>Regular client travel required — discovery, workshops, POC delivery, go-live</p> </td> </tr> <tr> <td width="142"> <p><strong>Platform Focus</strong></p> </td> <td width="482"> <p>Anthropic Claude (Cowork + Skills), MCP, Python / Node.js, REST APIs</p> </td> </tr> <tr> <td width="142"> <p><strong>Seniority</strong></p> </td> <td width="482"> <p>Intermediate 3-5 year’s experience</p> </td> </tr> <tr> <td width="142"> <p><strong>Location</strong></p> </td> <td width="482"> <p>Onshore Canada — Toronto preferred</p> </td> </tr> <tr> <td width="142"> <p><strong>Engagement Type</strong></p> </td> <td width="482"> <p>Hybrid — onsite client-facing with remote delivery phases</p> </td> </tr> </tbody> </table> <p>&nbsp;</p> <p><strong>What You’ll Do</strong></p> <p><strong>Solution Architecture &amp; Technical Discovery</strong></p> <ul> <li>Lead technical discovery workshops with C-suite, IT architects, and operations leaders - identifying high-value AI automation opportunities and translating complex operational workflows into executable solution architectures</li> <li>Design end-to-end AI agent architectures using Anthropic’s Claude platform (Cowork and Skills framework), aligned to client infrastructure, security requirements, and compliance constraints</li> <li>Translate business requirements into agent-based automation blueprints: what the AI owns, what stays human, how exceptions route, and how the two coordinate</li> <li>Assess the right solution architecture for each use case - knowing when a Claude agent is the answer, when a procode integration is cleaner, and when a simpler rules-based approach is more appropriate than AI</li> <li>Present technical architectures to executive and non-technical audiences with clarity, confidence, and the credibility that comes from having built things like this before</li> </ul> <p><strong>AI Agent Build &amp; Delivery</strong></p> <ul> <li>Engineer production-ready AI agent workflows using Claude Cowork and the Skills framework - built to a standard that holds up in regulated, enterprise production environments</li> <li>Develop and configure Claude Skills: structured instruction sets, templates, validation rules, and reference documents that encode client-specific processes and quality standards into repeatable AI agent behaviour</li> <li>Build and maintain MCP connectors that integrate AI agents with enterprise systems - CRMs, ERPs, document management platforms, core banking systems, and custom APIs - handling authentication, data mapping, error handling, and audit requirements</li> <li>Implement advanced LLM patterns where required: retrieval-augmented generation (RAG), chain-of-thought reasoning, tool use, multi-agent orchestration, and structured output validation</li> <li>Conduct output validation, performance tuning, and safety testing of deployed AI agents before client go-live - quality is non-negotiable when outputs go to regulators or clients</li> </ul> <p><strong>Procode &amp; Integration Engineering</strong></p> <ul> <li>Write production-quality Python or Node.js to extend platform capability - data pipelines, transformation logic, webhook handlers, API wrappers, and back-end components that sit outside the automation itself</li> <li>Build, test, and maintain integrations with enterprise platforms (Salesforce, Jira, DocuSign, SharePoint, ServiceNow, and others) using REST, GraphQL, OAuth 2.0, and enterprise authentication patterns</li> <li>Design and implement monitoring, logging, and observability into deployed solutions so operations teams can diagnose issues without requiring an engineer in the room</li> <li>Apply cloud platform fundamentals (AWS, Azure, or GCP) to deployment, scaling, and infrastructure decisions where the engagement requires it</li> </ul> <p><strong>Accelerators &amp; Practice Development</strong></p> <ul> <li>Build and continuously refine reusable Skills libraries, automation templates, and reference architectures that make every subsequent Greenlight engagement faster and more defensible</li> <li>Contribute to pre-sales activities: technical demos, POC builds, RFP responses, and solution estimations - your hands-on credibility is a direct input to Greenlight’s win rate</li> <li>Produce high-quality technical documentation - solution design documents, architecture diagrams, runbooks, and post-deployment reports - written to a standard the client’s IT team can maintain</li> <li>Identify account expansion opportunities within active engagements - additional use cases, adjacent processes, and follow-on automation candidates - and surface them to the AI Delivery Engagement Manager</li> <li>Mentor Automation Engineers and client technical teams on AI agent patterns, LLM integration best practices, and platform capabilities</li> </ul> <p><strong>Client Engagement &amp; Consulting Delivery</strong></p> <ul> <li>Operate with high autonomy inside client environments - navigating ambiguity, defining structure where none exists, and delivering without requiring hand-holding from the practice lead</li> <li>Manage scope, timelines, and deliverables across concurrent client engagements - you know when to flag a risk and when to absorb it</li> <li>Build trusted-advisor relationships with client technical teams - the goal is to be the person they call when they have an AI or automation problem, not just the vendor who delivered the last project</li> <li>Deliver working solutions within 2–4 week sprint cycles - you build and show, you don’t slide and tell</li> </ul> <p>&nbsp;</p> <p><strong>What We’re Looking For</strong></p> <p><strong>Skills &amp; Competencies</strong></p> <ul> <li>Technical depth with consulting presence - credible with a CTO and a COO in the same meeting</li> <li>AI-native - you understand how LLMs behave under pressure, where they fail, and how to build reliable systems around them</li> <li>Builder's bias - working software over slide decks, every time</li> <li>High autonomy - you define structure where none exists without needing direction</li> <li>Commercially aware - scope, delivery economics, and what it costs when technical decisions go sideways</li> <li>Clear communicator - architecture decisions and trade-offs explained in language both business and IT can act on</li> </ul> <p>&nbsp;</p> <p><strong>Technical Familiarity</strong></p> <p>&nbsp;</p> <ul> <li>LLM platforms - Anthropic Claude (primary), Microsoft Copilot, Google Gemini, OpenAI; hands-on with APIs, agent frameworks, and production deployment across at least one</li> <li>Claude Skills framework and Cowork - structured instruction design, output validation, skill packaging</li> <li>MCP (Model Context Protocol) - connector design, enterprise system integration, authentication flows</li> <li>Python and/or Node.js - production-grade, testable, maintainable code</li> <li>REST and GraphQL APIs - authentication, error handling, rate limiting, webhooks</li> <li>Cloud platforms - AWS, Azure, or GCP; deployment, containers, infrastructure basics</li> <li>LLM evaluation and observability - LangSmith, Weights &amp; Biases, or equivalent</li> </ul> <p><strong>What Great Looks Like in This Role</strong></p> <table width="624"> <tbody> <tr> <td width="624"> <p><strong>The standard we are hiring to:</strong></p> <p>The best FDEs we have worked with walk into a client environment and within a week they know more about the client’s process than the client’s own team has documented. They build a working POC in a few weeks that makes the client stop asking whether it’s possible and start asking when it can go to production. They write code that the next engineer can understand without a two-hour briefing. They know when the LLM is going to fail before it does, and they’ve already built the fallback. And they leave every engagement with a client technical team that is more capable than when they arrived - because that is what makes clients come back.</p> </td> </tr> </tbody> </table> <p>&nbsp;</p> <p>&nbsp;</p> <table width="624"> <tbody> <tr> <td width="624"> <p><strong>AI in Our Hiring Process</strong></p> <p>We use AI tools to support parts of our recruitment - organizing applications and flagging relevant experience. These tools inform our process, they don’t drive it. Every hiring decision is made by our team, full stop.</p> <p><strong>Compensation</strong></p> <p>The expected salary range for this role is $90,000 – $130,000 CAD annually, based on experience and qualifications.</p> </td> </tr> </tbody> </table> <p>&nbsp;</p> <p>&nbsp;</p>

title: Data Engineer
full_description: <div class="content-intro"><h2><strong>Who Are We?</strong></h2> <p>Postman is the world’s leading API platform, used by more than 45 million+ developers and 500,000 organizations, including 98% of the Fortune 500. Postman is helping developers and professionals across the globe build the API-first world by simplifying each step of the API lifecycle and streamlining collaboration—enabling users to create better APIs, faster.</p> <p>The company is headquartered in San Francisco and has offices in Boston, New York, Austin, Tokyo, London, and Bangalore - where Postman was founded. Postman is privately held, with funding from Battery Ventures, BOND, Coatue, CRV, Insight Partners, and Nexus Venture Partners. title: Senior Forward Deployed Engineer
full_description: <div class="content-intro"><h2><strong>Who Are We?</strong></h2> <p>Postman is the world’s leading API platform, used by more than 45 million+ developers and 500,000 organizations, including 98% of the Fortune 500. Postman is helping developers and professionals across the globe build the API-first world by simplifying each step of the API lifecycle and streamlining collaboration—enabling users to create better APIs, faster.</p> <p>The company is headquartered in San Francisco and has offices in Boston, New York, Austin, Tokyo, London, and Bangalore - where Postman was founded. Postman is privately held, with funding from Battery Ventures, BOND, Coatue, CRV, Insight Partners, and Nexus Venture Partners. Learn more at postman.com or connect with Postman on X via @getpostman.</p> <p>P.S: We highly recommend reading <a href="https://api-first-world.com/">The "API-First World" graphic novel</a> to understand the bigger picture and our vision at Postman.</p></div><h2><strong>The Opportunity</strong></h2> <p>This team will build 0-to-1 products from scratch — innovative, strategic initiatives designed to unlock hundreds of millions of dollars in new revenue. As a Sr Forward Deployed Engineer (FDE), you will be deeply embedded with customers and partners, working at the intersection of product, engineering, and GTM to build high-leverage solutions that solve real-world problems at scale.</p> <h2><strong>What You’ll Do</strong></h2> <ul> <li>Work closely with strategic customers and internal teams to deeply understand high-value problems</li> <li>Design and build full-stack applications, integrations, and tools that solve real-world use cases with APIs at the center</li> <li>Rapidly prototype solutions, validate impact, and iterate quickly based on user feedback</li> <li>Own end-to-end technical execution — from architecture and infrastructure to frontend and backend development</li> <li>Act as a trusted technical advisor and partner to customers, helping them unlock the full power of Postman</li> <li>Collaborate with Postman’s product and engineering teams to influence roadmap based on field learnings</li> <li>Help define and scale the Forward Deployed Engineering function from the ground up</li> <li>Travel Requirement: 20–30%, based on customer engagement needs. This may include on-site visits for strategy, discovery, deployment, support meetings</li> </ul> <h2><strong>About You</strong></h2> <ul> <li>Have <strong>6+ years of professional experience</strong>&nbsp;as a full-stack or backend engineer; startup or founding team experience is a big plus</li> <li>Are&nbsp;<strong>customer-obsessed</strong>&nbsp;and energized by solving real problems with high business impact</li> <li>Experience with AI Agents, API Platforms is a plus</li> <li>Have experience working directly with customers or in consultative roles (e.g., solutions engineering, CTO-as-a-service, technical consulting)</li> <li>Are a strong generalist with proficiency in&nbsp;<strong>Node.js, Python, or similar</strong>&nbsp;backend languages, and&nbsp;<strong>React/Next.js</strong>&nbsp;or similar for the frontend</li> <li>Have experience building and shipping cloud-based applications (AWS/GCP/Azure)</li> <li>Enjoy ambiguity and thrive in fast-paced, 0→1 environments</li> <li>Are excited about building systems and practices that can scale with a rapidly growing company</li> </ul> <p>The reasonably estimated base salary for this role ranges from $220,000 to $240,000, plus a competitive equity package. Actual compensation is based on the candidate's skills, qualifications, and experience.&nbsp;</p><div class="content-conclusion"><h2 data-renderer-start-pos="4036"><strong data-renderer-mark="true">What Else?</strong></h2> <p data-renderer-start-pos="4048">In addition to Postman's pay-on-performance philosophy, and a flexible schedule working with a fun, collaborative team, Postman offers a comprehensive set of benefits, including full medical coverage, flexible PTO, wellness reimbursement, and a monthly lunch stipend. Along with that, our wellness programs will help you stay in the best of your physical and mental health. Our frequent and fascinating team-building events will keep you connected, while our donation-matching program can support the causes you care about. We’re building a long-term company with an inclusive culture where everyone can be the best version of themselves.&nbsp;</p> <p data-renderer-start-pos="4048">At Postman we value in person collaboration. We are in office 5 days a week for all roles based out of our hubs in San Francisco Bay Area, Boston, Austin, New York City, Tokyo and London. For roles based in Bangalore, employees currently work in the office three days a week and will transition to five days per week by the end of the year. We were thoughtful in our approach which is based on collaboration and grounded in feedback from our workforce, leadership team, and peers. The benefits of our in office model will be shared knowledge, brainstorming sessions, communication, and building trust in-person that cannot be replicated via zoom.</p> <h2 data-renderer-start-pos="3620"><strong data-renderer-mark="true">Our Values</strong></h2> <p data-renderer-start-pos="3633">At Postman, we create with the same curiosity that we see in our users. We value transparency and honest communication about not only successes, but also failures. In our work, we focus on specific goals that add up to a larger vision. Our inclusive work culture ensures that everyone is valued equally as important pieces of our final product. We are dedicated to delivering the best products we can.</p> <h2><strong>Equal opportunity</strong></h2> <p data-renderer-start-pos="4048">Postman is an Equal Employment Opportunity and Affirmative Action Employer. title: Manager, Analytics Engineering
full_description: <div class="content-intro"><h1><strong>Who We Are</strong></h1> <p><span style="font-weight: 400;">At Justworks, you’ll enjoy a welcoming and casual environment, great benefits, wellness program offerings, company retreats, and the ability to interact with and learn from leaders in the startup community. We work hard and care about our most prized asset - our people.</span></p> <p><span style="font-weight: 400;">We’re helping businesses get off the ground by enabling them to focus on running their business. We solve HR issues. We’re data-driven and never stop iterating. If you’d like to work in a supportive, entrepreneurial environment, are interested in building something meaningful and having fun while doing it, we’d love to hear from you.</span></p> <p><span style="font-weight: 400;">We're united by shared goals and shared motivations at Justworks. These are best summed up in our company values, which are reflected in our product and in our team.</span></p> <p><a href="https://www.justworks.com/careers#values"><span style="font-weight: 400;">Our Values</span></a></p> <p><span style="font-weight: 400;">If this sounds like you, you’ll fit right in.</span></p></div><h1><strong>Department</strong></h1> <p>Data &amp; Analytics</p> <h1><strong>Who You Are</strong></h1> <p>You are a data leader with a curious mindset, a passion for building, and an eye for optimization. You thrive when enabling the strategic and effective use of data within and outside of a data team. You are adept at understanding business challenges and framing them as enablement opportunities. You possess strong empathy, interpersonal skills, and attention to detail, enabling you to work effectively with your team and cross-functional partners. You can clearly translate complex data concepts into assets that enable data-informed strategy, and have a growing track record of guiding projects to successful outcomes.</p> <p>We are seeking a highly skilled and motivated <strong>Manager of Analytics Engineering</strong> with people management experience and hands-on development skills. In this "player-coach" role, you will be responsible for managing a team of analytics engineers, while also working closely with cross-functional teams to design, build, and maintain scalable data pipelines and robust data models. Your work will directly contribute to business growth by providing a reliable and accessible data foundation for analysis and reporting. This role requires a strong analytical &amp; data modeling background, a deep understanding of the modern data stack, and a passion for mentoring and growing teams.</p> <h1><strong>Your Success Profile</strong></h1> <h2><strong>What You Will Work On</strong></h2> <ul> <li>Lead and develop a team of <strong>analytics engineers</strong>, fostering their professional growth through goal-setting, coaching, feedback, and recognition.</li> <li>Guide your team in <strong>designing, implementing, and maintaining scalable data models</strong> and <strong>ELT pipelines</strong>, a star schema architecture, and reporting solutions that power business intelligence, reduce time to insights, and elevate our company-wide data literacy.</li> <li>Manage project priorities and timelines for your team, ensuring the timely delivery of rigorous, impactful solutions while upholding <strong>high standards for data quality and integrity</strong>.</li> <li>Collaborate with Data &amp; Analytics leadership and cross-functional teams (including Engineering, Operations, and business teams) to understand requirements and translate them into executable <strong>analytics engineering</strong> projects and <strong>data products</strong>.</li> <li>Contribute to the <strong>data strategy and roadmap</strong> by identifying opportunities and executing projects leveraging <strong>data modeling, data warehouse architecture, and other analytics engineering best practices</strong>.</li> <li>Uphold and contribute to best practices for <strong>data model development, testing, and documentation</strong> within your team to deliver iteratively and efficiently.</li> <li>Build relationships with cross-functional leads to promote the adoption of new methods, technologies, and <strong>self-service data tools</strong> that help the organization scale.</li> </ul> <h2><strong>How You Will Do Your Work</strong></h2> <p>In the Manager, Analytics Engineering role, <em>how</em> results are achieved is paramount for your success and ultimately result in our success as an organization. Your foundational knowledge, skills, abilities, and personal attributes are anchored in the following competencies:</p> <ul> <li><strong>Plans &amp; Aligns</strong> - Ensures individual actions support company mission; communicates effectively; understands urgency and plans accordingly</li> <li><strong>Balances Stakeholders</strong> - Understands internal and external stakeholder requirements, expectations, and needs and acts fairly despite conflicting demands of stakeholders.</li> <li><strong>Influence and leadership&nbsp;</strong>- fostering a community of knowledge-sharing, collaboration, mentorship, and forward-thinking across the organization.</li> <li><strong>Manages Complexity&nbsp;</strong>- Making sense of complex, high quantity, and sometimes contradictory information to effectively solve problems.</li> <li><strong>Good Judgment&nbsp;</strong>- Pauses before acting; asks questions to fully understand context; applies critical thinking and takes ownership of outcomes</li> <li><strong>Teamwork &amp; Communication&nbsp;</strong>- Honors diverse thoughts and opinions; leverages unique skills to maximize efficiency</li> <li><strong>Skills and Knowledge&nbsp;</strong>- the capacity to actively learn and apply specific domain knowledge, know-how, and best practices to continually enhance and improve.</li> </ul> <p>In addition, all Justworkers focus on aligning their behaviors to our core values known as <strong>COGIS</strong>. It stands for:</p> <ul> <li><strong>Camaraderie</strong> - Day to day you can be seen working together toward a higher purpose. You like to have fun. You’re an active listener, treat people respectfully, and have a strong desire to know and help others.</li> <li><strong>Openness</strong> - Your default is to be open. You're willing to share information, understand other perspectives, and consider new possibilities. You’re curious, ask open questions, and are receptive to thoughts and feedback from others.</li> <li><strong>Grit </strong>- You demonstrate grit by having the courage to commit and persevere. You’re committed, earnest, and dive in to get the job done well with a positive attitude.</li> <li><strong>Integrity</strong> - Simply put, do what you say and say what you'll do. You’re honest and forthright, have a strong moral compass, and strive to match your words with your actions while leading by example.&nbsp;</li> <li><strong>Simplicity</strong> - Be like Einstein: “Everything should be made as simple as possible, but no simpler.”</li> </ul> <h2><strong>Qualifications</strong></h2> <ul> <li>7+ years of experience in a data role such as analytics engineering, data engineering, or data warehousing, with hands-on experience designing and building scalable data models and ETL/ELT pipelines.</li> <li>2+ years in people management, with a proven track record of developing and managing high-performing teams.</li> <li>Proficiency in SQL and Python.</li> <li>Deep expertise in data warehouse architecture and dimensional modeling (e.g., Kimball).</li> <li>Demonstrated experience with a modern data stack, preferably Snowflake, Airflow, and dbt.</li> <li>Strong analytical and problem-solving skills, with the ability to work with complex datasets to transform and model them for analytical use.</li> <li>Excellent oral &amp; written communication skills and a strong ability to collaborate with both technical and non-technical stakeholders.</li> <li>Strong project management skills, with the ability to manage multiple projects, prioritize effectively, and meet deadlines.</li> </ul> <p>The base wage range for this position based in our New York City office is targeted at <span class="collapsed-field-text">$166,000.00 - $214,000.00</span>&nbsp;per year.</p> <p><span data-sheets-root="1">#LI-DG2 #LI-Hybrid</span></p><div class="content-conclusion"><p><span style="font-weight: 400;">Actual compensation is based on multiple factors that are unique to each candidate, including and not limited to skill set, level of relevant experience, and specific work location.&nbsp; </span><span style="font-weight: 400;">Salary ranges for positions based in other locations may differ based on the cost of labor in that location.&nbsp;</span></p> <p><span style="font-weight: 400;">For more information about Justworks’ Total Reward Philosophy, including all of the perks and benefits we are proud to offer our team members, please visit </span><a href="https://www.justworks.com/careers#total-reward-philosophy">Total Rewards @ Justworks</a><span style="font-weight: 400;">.&nbsp;</span></p> <h1><strong>Diversity At Justworks</strong></h1> <p><span style="font-weight: 400;">Justworks is committed to maintaining a workplace where diversity of identity, culture, and life experience is the norm and is celebrated authentically and respected consistently. Diversity in our work, our people, and our product drives creativity and innovation, entrepreneurial leadership and integrity, competitiveness, and collaboration throughout our business and in the market. We depend on our differences to make our team stronger, our workplace more dynamic, and our product accessible to all of our customers.</span></p> <p>We’re proud to be an equal opportunity employer open to all qualified applicants regardless of race, color, ancestry, religion, sex, national origin, sexual orientation, age, citizenship, marital or familial status, disability, pregnancy, gender identity or expression, veteran status, genetic information, or any other legally protected status. Justworks is fully dedicated to providing necessary support to candidates with disabilities who may require reasonable accommodations. We also provide reasonable accommodations to employees based on their sincerely held religious beliefs, as well as for other covered reasons consistent with applicable federal, state, and local laws. If you're in need of a reasonable accommodation, please reach out to us at <a href="mailto:accommodations@justworks.com">accommodations@justworks.com</a>. Your comfort and success matter to us, and we're here to ensure an inclusive experience.</p> <p><a href="https://www.justworks.com/press/company-news/justworks-impact-report-2024" target="_blank"><span style="font-weight: 400;">Our DEIB Report&nbsp;</span></a></p></div>

title: Senior Analytics Engineer
full_description: <div class="content-intro"><h1><strong>Who We Are</strong></h1> <p><span style="font-weight: 400;">At Justworks, you’ll enjoy a welcoming and casual environment, great benefits, wellness program offerings, company retreats, and the ability to interact with and learn from leaders in the startup community. We work hard and care about our most prized asset - our people.</span></p> <p><span style="font-weight: 400;">We’re helping businesses get off the ground by enabling them to focus on running their business. We solve HR issues. We’re data-driven and never stop iterating. If you’d like to work in a supportive, entrepreneurial environment, are interested in building something meaningful and having fun while doing it, we’d love to hear from you.</span></p> <p><span style="font-weight: 400;">We're united by shared goals and shared motivations at Justworks. These are best summed up in our company values, which are reflected in our product and in our team.</span></p> <p><a href="https://www.justworks.com/careers#values"><span style="font-weight: 400;">Our Values</span></a></p> <p><span style="font-weight: 400;">If this sounds like you, you’ll fit right in.</span></p></div><h1><strong>Department</strong></h1> <p>Technology, Data &amp; Analytics</p> <h1><strong>About The Team</strong></h1> <p>The Data &amp; Analytics team is Justworks' data and AI center of excellence — a connected set of functions spanning business intelligence, data science, analytics engineering, platform data/ML engineering and AI builders. We've grown significantly over the last several years and continue to build momentum across the business, leveling up maturity in how we deliver insights, govern data, and enable the company to make better decisions. You'll find a highly collaborative &amp; diverse group motivated by craft, shared learning, and a genuine commitment to growing our impact for customers while evolving how we work together for our people. Come build with us!</p> <h2><strong>Who You Are</strong></h2> <p>You're a data practitioner with a strong track record for designing, building, and deploying models within a medallion architecture to accelerate reporting, insights, and self-service capabilities. You have a strong understanding of how to use data to answer business questions, and are passionate about unlocking that capability for data teams and business stakeholders across the organization. You enjoy partnering closely with stakeholders, translating their questions into trustworthy data products, and you display an enthusiasm for working through complex problems together. You have a keen sense of the big picture and can effectively drive projects toward realistic, yet rewarding business outcomes.</p> <p>As a Senior Analytics Engineer, you will be a key contributor in helping the organization derive more value from its datasets through scalable, modular design of our enterprise data models and semantic layer. You will work closely with data engineers, analysts, and business stakeholders to develop &amp; iterate on data requirements, put data models into production, and surface them through governed BI. Your work will directly support enhanced operations and decision-making as we improve the quality of our data assets and scale of our data practice.</p> <h2><strong>Your Success Profile</strong></h2> <h3><strong>What You Will Work On</strong></h3> <ul> <li>Transform raw data into high-quality data models that are easily queried and understood by various business units (e.g., Sales, Marketing, Product, Engineering, Operations)</li> <li>Design, build, and maintain our semantic layer using Snowflake semantic views — defining metrics, entities, and business logic so stakeholders can self-serve trustworthy, consistently-defined data without re-deriving logic</li> <li>Partner with analysts and business teams to build and govern BI content in Tableau and Redash — curated data sources, certified dashboards, and reusable metrics — and reduce dashboard sprawl through consolidation and clear standards</li> <li>Partner directly with stakeholders to translate business questions into data requirements, co-develop metric definitions, and drive adoption of governed models and dashboards</li> <li>Proactively identify opportunities to close gaps in reporting and insights generation by architecting new business facts and dimensions</li> <li>Apply effective data testing and sensitive data management strategies to ensure the quality and security of information provided to the business</li> <li>Level up our information design, data governance, and analytics engineering practices by defining our internal standards for lineage, maintainability, performance, and cost management</li> <li>Provide technical leadership and mentorship to junior and mid-level team members, fostering best practices in data modeling, orchestration, data warehouse architecture, data privacy, and analytics enablement</li> <li>Perform other related duties as assigned</li> </ul> <h3><strong>How You Will Do Your Work</strong></h3> <p>In the Senior Analytics Engineer, <em>how</em> results are achieved is paramount for your success and ultimately result in our success as an organization.
