title: Analytics Engineer I
location: New York, New York, United States
full_description: <p>Hi, we're Oscar. We're hiring an Analytics Engineer I to join our Data Team.</p> <p>Oscar is the first health insurance company built around a full stack technology platform and a relentless focus on serving our members. We started Oscar in 2012 to create the kind of health insurance company we would want for ourselves—one that behaves like a doctor in the family.</p> <p><strong>About the role:</strong></p> <p>The Analytics Engineer I designs, builds, and maintains dimensional models and data transformations that convert raw operational data into reliable, well-structured analytical assets. You’ll work within Oscar’s unified data foundation in dbt, partnering with domain experts and stakeholders to capture business logic and translate it into dimensional models that serve analytics use cases across the organization. Under the guidance of more senior Analytics Engineers, you’ll build and maintain models, implement automated tests to ensure long-term data product health, and contribute to the semantic layer that powers enterprise business intelligence. This is a hands-on role focused on growing your craft in dimensional modeling, SQL, and analytics engineering within a healthcare environment.</p> <p>You will report into the Associate Director, Data Science.&nbsp;</p> <p><strong>Work Location: </strong>This position is based in our New York City office, requiring a hybrid work schedule with 3 days of in-office work per week. Thursdays are a required in-office day for team meetings and events, while your other two office days are flexible to suit your schedule.</p> <p><strong>Pay Transparency: </strong>The base pay for this role is: $106,812 - $140,190.75 per year. You are also eligible for employee benefits, participation in Oscar's unlimited vacation program, company equity grants, and annual performance bonuses.</p> <p><strong>Responsibilities:</strong></p> <ul> <li>Demonstrates a strong working knowledge of Oscar's analytics technologies and tools as well as the breadth of content contained within.</li> <li>Contribute to the development and documentation of best practices for engineering and analytics development, consistent with industry standards and internal guidelines.</li> <li>Under the guidance of more senior Analytics Engineers, partner with Subject Matter Experts and Business stakeholders to explore data, define business requirements, design and implement solutions for one or more data transformation and/or downstream semantic layer systems.</li> <li>Advance the Analytics Engineering roadmap by executing on small-to-medium projects with minimal oversight</li> <li>Contribute to the administration of the analytics platform, including but not limited to active participation in support channels and actioning&nbsp; access requests.</li> <li>Leverage a working knowledge of existing data quality checks as well as monitoring and alerting pipelines to perform Root Cause Analyses of data defects as needed and escalate to the appropriate team when warranted.</li> </ul> <p><strong>Requirements:</strong></p> <ul> <li>1+ years of experience in Analytics Engineering, Data Science, or a related field.</li> <li>1+ years of experience working with SQL.</li> <li>1+ years of experience developing in a semantic modeling language (e.g. LookML).</li> <li>1+ years of experience developing within a modern data transformation tool like DBT.</li> <li>1+ years of experience with cloud database tools (GCP, AWS, Azure).</li> </ul> <p><strong>Bonus points:</strong></p> <ul> <li>Experience working with healthcare or insurance industry data, analytics, and reporting.</li> <li>Experience with Google Cloud Platform and dbt.</li> <li>Experience building dimensional models (star schemas, slowly changing dimensions).</li> <li>Strong communication skills, as demonstrated by experience partnering with non-technical stakeholders.</li> </ul><div class="content-conclusion"><p><span style="font-weight: 400;">This is an authentic Oscar Health job opportunity. Learn more about how you can safeguard yourself from recruitment fraud</span><a href="http://hioscar.com/careers/recruitment-fraud-alert"><span style="font-weight: 400;"> </span><span style="font-weight: 400;">here</span></a><span style="font-weight: 400;">.&nbsp;</span></p> <p><span style="font-weight: 400;">At Oscar, being an Equal Opportunity Employer means more than upholding discrimination-free hiring practices. It means that we cultivate an environment where people can be their most authentic selves and find both belonging and support. We're on a mission to change health care -- an experience made whole by our unique backgrounds and perspectives.</span></p> <p><strong>Pay Transparency:&nbsp; </strong><span style="font-weight: 400;">Final offer amounts, within the base pay set forth above, are determined by factors including your relevant skills, education, and experience. </span><span style="font-weight: 400;">Full-time employees are eligible for benefits including: medical, dental, and vision benefits, 11 paid holidays, paid sick time, paid parental leave, 401(k) plan participation, life and disability insurance, and paid wellness time and reimbursements.</span></p> <p><span style="font-weight: 400;"><strong>Artificial Intelligence (AI): </strong>Our <a href="http://hioscar.com/careers/ai-guidelines" target="_blank">AI Guidelines</a> outline the acceptable use of artificial intelligence for candidates and detail how we use AI to support our recruiting efforts.</span></p> <p><strong>Reasonable Accommodation: </strong><span style="font-weight: 400;">Oscar applicants are considered solely based on their qualifications, without regard to applicant’s disability or need for accommodation. Any Oscar applicant who requires reasonable accommodations during the application process should contact the Oscar Benefits Team (accommodations@hioscar.com) to make the need for an accommodation known.</span></p> <p><span style="font-weight: 400;"><strong>California Residents:</strong> For information about our collection, use, and disclosure of applicants’ personal information as well as applicants’ rights over their personal information, please see our <a href="https://www.hioscar.com/legal/privacy/">Privacy Policy</a>.</span></p></div>

title: Sales Data Engineering Manager
full_description: <div class="content-intro"><p><strong>Who we are:</strong></p> <p>Motive empowers the people who run physical operations with tools to make their work safer, more productive, and more profitable. For the first time ever, safety, operations and finance teams can manage their drivers, vehicles, equipment, and fleet related spend in a single system. Combined with industry leading AI, the Motive platform gives you complete visibility and control, and significantly reduces manual workloads by automating and simplifying tasks.</p> <p>Motive serves nearly 100,000 customers – from Fortune 500 enterprises to small businesses – across a wide range of industries, including transportation and logistics, construction, energy, field service, manufacturing, agriculture, food and beverage, retail, and the public sector.</p> <p>Visit <a href="http://gomotive.com">gomotive.com</a> to learn more.</p></div><div>&nbsp;</div> <p><strong>About the Role:&nbsp;</strong></p> <p>As a Sales Data Engineering Manager you will be part of the core data team building out world class sales data products that will drive Motive Sales.&nbsp; You will be working on the intersection of all data streams in the company and support our reps globally.&nbsp; You will partner closely with both the Sales, Product, &amp; Marketing teams. We are seeking strong team players who thrive on innovation and continuous improvement. We pride ourselves on our culture, and ability to work effectively across a highly diversified team.&nbsp;<strong><br></strong></p> <p><strong>Responsibilities</strong><strong>:</strong></p> <p>Build data roadmap for the sales data engineering team building both internal and external data products.&nbsp;</p> <p>Drive innovation in our sales data products with the application of AI in both the processes we work on to the products we deliver.</p> <p>Build teams to scale out our maturing set of analytics products.</p> <p>Be deeply technical and continue to code and drive technical standards within the team.</p> <p>Architect and design data models in collaboration with data and product teams.</p> <p>Communicate effectively and drive strategy across multiple teams and projects.<strong><br></strong></p> <p><strong>Qualifications:</strong></p> <ul> <li>Bachelor's degree or higher in a quantitative field, e.g. Computer Science, Math, Economics, or Statistics</li> <li>Knowledge of Sales Cycles and B2B Sales</li> <li>10+ years experience in Data Engineering, and 3+ years managing a team</li> <li>Expertise with data engineering stack, dBt, Snowflake, airflow, data observability tools, AWS, pyspark.</li> <li>Expertise in SQL and Python</li> <li>Great planning and roadmapping skills</li> <li>Excellent communication, collaboration, and people skills</li> </ul><div class="content-conclusion"><p data-pm-slice="1 1 []"><em>Creating a diverse and inclusive workplace is one of Motive's core values. We are an equal opportunity employer and welcome people of different backgrounds, experiences, abilities and perspectives.&nbsp;</em></p> <p><em>Please review our Candidate Privacy Notice&nbsp;<a class="ProsemirrorEditor-link" href="https://drive.google.com/file/d/1X9AbvQHki8zZCyjDjip1g0NtdZHcykJc/view?usp=drive_link" target="_blank">here</a>.</em></p> <p><em>UK Candidate Privacy Notice <a href="https://drive.google.com/file/d/1nqdWTJ7WJJikhj1Yhlo4jxDRA_6yXZbd/view">here</a>.</em></p> <p><em><span id="m_-8012333603671085849gmail-docs-internal-guid-8821a356-7fff-42cb-93df-e4c910033371">The applicant must be authorized to receive and access those commodities and technologies controlled under U.S. Export Administration Regulations.&nbsp;</span>It is Motive's policy to require that employees be authorized to receive access to Motive products and technology.&nbsp;</em></p> <p><em>All job postings are for existing vacancies. Please note; some interviews or new-hire training sessions may be held in person at one of our global offices.</em></p></div>

title: Data Engineer (Databricks / Snowflake)
full_description: <p>&nbsp;</p> <div id="message-list_1783615196.642129"> <div> <div> <div> <div> <div> <div> <div> <div> <div> <div> <div>Alpha Financial Markets Consulting (Alpha) is a leading global consultancy to the financial services industry. We are a boutique management consulting firm that offers the world’s top industry players a competitive edge through our expertise and industry insight. Our team is of a uniquely high calibre and works across regulated financial markets, bringing deep expertise in insurance, alternative asset markets — including private equity, private credit, infrastructure and real estate — and other specialised financial sectors. This focus enables us to bring relevant, hands-on experience to our clients’ most important challenges.&nbsp; We have our headquarters located the United Kingdom, as well as offices in major global financial centres across the United States, France, Netherlands, Luxembourg, Switzerland, and Asia.</div> </div> </div> </div> </div> </div> </div> </div> </div> </div> </div> </div> <h1>About the role</h1> <p>We’re looking for a Data Engineer with 2+ years’ experience to join our growing data engineering capability. This role is focused on client delivery. You’ll work closely with client teams, alongside their internal engineers and Alpha consultants, to build and support modern data platforms in regulated financial services environments. The work is hands-on and delivery-focused: building pipelines, developing data models, and helping turn business requirements into trusted, production-ready data solutions.</p> <p>This is a strong opportunity for an engineer who wants more than just pipeline delivery. You’ll work directly with clients, solve real platform and data problems, and build experience across a range of delivery challenges in complex regulated environments.</p> <h1>What you'll do</h1> <ul> <li>Build reliable ingestion pipelines that move client data from source systems into the platform efficiently and securely</li> <li>Develop data pipelines to agreed SLAs, with a focus on reliability in production</li> <li>Work with domain SMEs and delivery teams to translate business logic into pipeline logic that produces accurate, trusted outputs Senior Data Engineer.docx</li> <li>Design data models, including Data Vault 2.0 and Kimball dimensional models, that support auditability and trusted reporting</li> <li>Support testing, monitoring, troubleshooting, and documentation across the delivery lifecycle</li> <li>Contribute to engineering standards and delivery best practices as part of a wider team</li> <li>Work with the team to implement LLM-based processing in pipelines where appropriate, alongside deterministic logic</li> <li>Work on delivery teams building AI-enabled solutions on the platform, helping to apply trusted data to real client problems</li> <li>Share learnings from delivery work and contribute to internal assets, tooling, and practices over time.</li> </ul> <h1>What you'll bring</h1> <ul> <li>2+ years in data engineering, including production experience with Databricks <strong>or</strong> Snowflake (one platform is sufficient)</li> <li>Strong SQL and Python; comfort with PySpark or Snowpark</li> <li>Experience with at least one cloud vendor, Azure, AWS, or GCP, including familiarity with core services beyond the data platform itself (networking, IAM, storage)</li> <li>Working knowledge of dimensional modelling (Kimball) and/or Data Vault 2.0</li> <li>Strong attention to quality, documentation, and delivery discipline</li> </ul> <h1>Additional experience we’d value</h1> <ul> <li>Snowflake or Databricks certifications</li> <li>Familiarity with private markets data (fund administration, private equity/real estate, secondaries) or London Market insurance</li> <li>Experience with some supporting tooling across areas such as infrastructure-as-code, transformation, DevOps, data quality, governance, or orchestration would be beneficial.</li> <li>Exposure to regulated environments such as financial services, insurance, or similarly audited sectors would be beneficial.</li> </ul> <h1>What the engagement looks like</h1> <p>You'll work within client teams, alongside their internal engineers and Alpha consultants in a regulated environment where change control, data lineage and documentation matter. Expect a mix of independent build work and collaborative delivery, with regular touchpoints against a delivery plan.</p> <h1>What We Offer</h1> <ul> <li>Competitive base salary&nbsp;</li> <li>Annual performance bonus tied to individual and company outcomes.</li> <li>Comprehensive benefits package including private medical insurance, life assurance, and income protection.</li> <li>Hybrid working model with flexible arrangements to support work-life balance.</li> <li>Generous pension scheme with enhanced employer contributions.</li> <li>Continuous learning budget and access to industry conferences, certifications, and training programmes.</li> <li>25 days annual leave plus bank holidays, with option to buy additional days.</li> <li>Employee assistance programme and wellbeing support.</li> </ul>

title: Senior Data Engineer
full_description: <h1><span style="font-size: 12pt;">About Aiviq</span></h1> <p>Aiviq is a cutting-edge fintech company revolutionising financial services and asset management. We empower the world's leading asset managers with data-driven insights and innovative technology solutions. Our cloud-based platform transforms complex financial data into actionable intelligence, addressing critical challenges in client data quality and insights. Serving global asset managers overseeing trillions in Assets under Management, we're at the forefront of financial technology innovation.</p> <p><strong>Reporting Structure</strong></p> <p><strong>Reports to:</strong> Data Centre of Excellence Team Lead<br><strong>Dotted line to:</strong> Head of Engineering<br><strong>Location:</strong> UK (Hybrid)</p> <p><strong>Job Purpose</strong></p> <p>We're seeking an accomplished Data Engineer to join our Data Centre of Excellence while working closely with our Engineering team on our sophisticated financial data management platform. This role combines the technical depth of enterprise data engineering with the fast-paced delivery demands of product development, requiring someone who is adept at translating business logic into code, can think architecturally while attending to implementation details. You'll be the bridge between our data architecture standards and practical product delivery, ensuring our financial data pipelines are robust, performant, and built on solid engineering principles.</p> <p><strong>Key Responsibilities</strong></p> <p><strong>Data Engineering &amp; Development</strong></p> <ul> <li>Design, build, and optimize data pipelines across Microsoft SQL Server and Azure Synapse Analytics environments</li> <li>Develop and maintain Spark SQL notebooks for complex data transformations and analysis</li> <li>Translate business logic and financial calculation requirements into clear, maintainable code</li> <li>Create data integrity checking scripts and validation frameworks in collaboration with QA teams</li> <li>Implement automated data quality checks and reconciliation processes</li> <li>Assist with the maintenance of a curated, anonymized dataset for system testing that covers all known scenarios and edge cases</li> <li>Analyse production datasets to identify anomalies, debug stored procedures and notebooks, and resolve data quality issues</li> <li>Demonstrate tenacity in investigating root causes, diving deep into complex problems until resolution is achieved</li> </ul> <p><strong>Architecture &amp; Performance</strong></p> <ul> <li>Consult on database architecture decisions, balancing performance, scalability, and maintainability</li> <li>Optimize query performance and data processing workflows for large-scale financial datasets</li> <li>Design and implement solutions using Azure Data Factory, Delta Lake, and related technologies</li> <li>Think end-to-end about data flows while ensuring rigorous attention to implementation details</li> </ul> <p><strong>Documentation &amp; Process</strong></p> <ul> <li>Create and maintain comprehensive documentation of database schemas, processes, and data flows</li> <li>Develop visual process models using tools such as Lucidchart, Visio, dbt, Azure Purview, or similar platforms</li> <li>Document data transformation logic and calculation methodologies for audit and compliance purposes</li> <li>Contribute to data governance standards and best practices across the organization</li> </ul> <p><strong>Production Support &amp; Collaboration</strong></p> <ul> <li>Act as first point of escalation for high-priority data issues in production environments</li> <li>Partner with test automation engineers to develop data-driven testing strategies and create data integrity checking scripts</li> <li>Collaborate across engineering teams using Azure DevOps for CI/CD pipeline development</li> <li>Support both Data CoE initiatives and product engineering priorities through effective stakeholder management</li> </ul> <p><strong>Required Skills &amp; Experience</strong></p> <p><strong>Technical Expertise</strong></p> <ul> <li><strong>Database Technologies:</strong> Strong proficiency in MS SQL Server and Azure Synapse Analytics</li> <li><strong>Big Data Processing:</strong> Hands-on experience with PySpark, Spark SQL, and notebook-based development</li> <li><strong>Cloud Platforms:</strong> Demonstrable experience with Azure ecosystem (Synapse, Data Factory, Delta Lake)</li> <li><strong>Programming:</strong> Solid coding skills in SQL, Python, and/or C#</li> <li><strong>Version Control:</strong> Experience with Git and Azure DevOps or similar CI/CD platforms</li> <li><strong>Testing:</strong> Experience building out unit and integration test frameworks and processes to ensure pipelines and notebooks and other code artefacts are fully automation-tested</li> </ul> <p><strong>Domain Knowledge</strong></p> <ul> <li>Ideally a proven track record working with complex financial data and calculations</li> <li>Understanding of financial data structures, reconciliation processes, and audit requirements</li> <li>Experience handling temporal data, slowly changing dimensions, and historical data management</li> <li>Knowledge of data quality frameworks and validation methodologies</li> </ul> <p><strong>Professional Capabilities</strong></p> <ul> <li>Strong analytical and debugging skills for complex data scenarios across stored procedures and notebooks</li> <li>Relentless problem-solving approach - comfortable digging deep into technical issues and pursuing answers until problems are fully understood and resolved</li> <li>Experience with testing principles and data integrity validation</li> <li>Ability to consult on technical architecture while maintaining pragmatic focus</li> <li>Excellent documentation and process modelling capabilities</li> </ul> <p><strong>Experience Level</strong></p> <ul> <li>5+ years in data engineering roles with increasing responsibility</li> <li>Track record of delivering production data systems at scale</li> <li>Experience working in matrix or cross-functional team structures</li> </ul> <p><strong>Desirable Skills</strong></p> <ul> <li>Knowledge of Azure Purview or data cataloguing solutions</li> <li>Familiarity with Great Expectations or similar data quality frameworks</li> <li>Understanding of behaviour-driven development for data testing</li> <li>Familiarity with data anonymization, masking, and synthetic data generation techniques</li> <li>Experience with GraphQL APIs and modern application data layers</li> <li>Exposure to modern data visualization tools (Power BI, Tableau)</li> <li>Experience with data build tool (dbt) or similar transformation frameworks</li> </ul> <p><strong>Personal Attributes</strong></p> <p><strong>Essential</strong></p> <ul> <li><strong>Collaborative mindset:</strong> Comfortable working across teams with different priorities and technical backgrounds</li> <li><strong>Detail-oriented:</strong> Meticulous about data accuracy while maintaining delivery momentum</li> <li><strong>Investigative nature:</strong> Thrives on troubleshooting complex issues and pursuing problems through multiple layers until fully resolved</li> <li><strong>Intensely curious:</strong> Demonstrates deep curiosity about existing processes and systems, with a natural drive to investigate how things work and independently acquire new knowledge</li> <li><strong>Pragmatic problem-solver:</strong> Balances architectural thinking with practical implementation</li> <li><strong>Clear communicator:</strong> Articulates technical concepts to both specialist and generalist audiences</li> <li><strong>Ownership mentality:</strong> Takes responsibility for production systems and follows through on commitments</li> </ul> <p><strong>Cultural Fit</strong></p> <ul> <li>Thrives in a matrix organization with multiple stakeholders</li> <li>Comfortable with ambiguity and competing priorities</li> <li>Passionate about engineering excellence and continuous improvement</li> <li>Values documentation and knowledge sharing</li> <li>Responds well under pressure during production incidents</li> </ul> <p><strong>What We Offer</strong></p> <p><strong>Professional Development</strong></p> <ul> <li>Exposure to enterprise-scale financial data systems handling complex calculations</li> <li>Opportunity to shape data engineering standards across the organization</li> <li>Work with modern Azure cloud infrastructure and emerging technologies</li> <li>Collaborate with skilled engineers across test automation, software development, and data teams</li> <li>Matrix structure providing diverse learning opportunities from both CoE and product perspectives</li> </ul> <p><strong>Work Environment</strong></p> <ul> <li>Hybrid working arrangement with flexibility</li> <li>Collaborative engineering culture valuing quality and craftsmanship</li> <li>Investment in tools, training, and professional growth</li> <li>Meaningful work on systems that impact financial data integrity and business decisions</li> </ul>

title: Senior Data Engineer (Databricks / Snowflake)
full_description: <p>&nbsp;</p> <div id="message-list_1783615196.642129"> <div> <div> <div> <div> <div> <div> <div> <div> <div> <div> <div>Alpha Financial Markets Consulting (Alpha) is a leading global consultancy to the financial services industry. We are a boutique management consulting firm that offers the world’s top industry players a competitive edge through our expertise and industry insight. Our team is of a uniquely high calibre and works across regulated financial markets, bringing deep expertise in insurance, alternative asset markets — including private equity, private credit, infrastructure and real estate — and other specialised financial sectors. This focus enables us to bring relevant, hands-on experience to our clients’ most important challenges.&nbsp; We have our headquarters located the United Kingdom, as well as offices in major global financial centres across the United States, France, Netherlands, Luxembourg, Switzerland, and Asia.</div> </div> </div> </div> </div> </div> </div> </div> </div> </div> </div> </div> <h1>About the role</h1> <p>Our senior data engineers are hands-on leaders of consultants and client teams, building data platforms for clients in regulated financial services – private equity, real estate, fund administration, and specialist insurance. You'll work directly with client stakeholders, from engineering leads to CTOs, delivering production pipelines on Databricks or Snowflake (strong experience in one is sufficient). This is a hands-on build role with client exposure. You'll write code, review architecture decisions, lead junior engineers and explain trade-offs to people who aren't engineers.</p> <h1>What you'll do</h1> <ul> <li>Lead and mentor junior engineers on your delivery workstream, reviewing their code and data model decisions</li> <li>Build reliable ingestion pipelines that move client data from source systems into the platform efficiently and securely</li> <li>Design data models – Data Vault 2.0, Kimball Dimensional etc. that survive audit and give the business one trusted version of the numbers</li> <li>Build pipelines to agreed SLAs and own their reliability in production</li> <li>Work with domain SMEs to translate business logic into pipeline logic that produces correct numbers</li> <li>Bring LLM-based processing into pipelines where it fits, document parsing, entity extraction, unstructured data classification, alongside deterministic logic</li> <li>Build AI-powered business solutions on the platform, agents, search, and applied use cases on Mosaic AI or Cortex, that solve a client problem with trusted data rather than demo a capability</li> <li>Support pre-sales by sizing effort and cost for prospective engagements, contribute to delivery plans, and present technical approach to prospective clients</li> </ul> <h1>What you'll bring</h1> <ul> <li>4+ years in data engineering, with at least 2 years on Databricks <strong>or</strong> Snowflake in production (one platform is sufficient)</li> <li>Strong SQL and Python; comfort with PySpark or Snowpark</li> <li>Strong experience with at least one cloud vendor, Azure, AWS, or GCP, including core services beyond the data platform itself (networking, IAM, storage)</li> <li>Working knowledge of dimensional modelling (Kimball) and/or Data Vault 2.0</li> <li>Comfortable presenting technical decisions to non-technical stakeholders</li> <li>Track record of setting engineering standards and reviewing others' code for quality, not just correctness</li> <li>Comfortable leading or mentoring junior engineers on a delivery team, and taking ownership of technical decisions</li> </ul> <h1>Additional experience we’d value</h1> <ul> <li>Snowflake or Databricks certifications</li> <li>Familiarity with private markets data (fund administration, private equity/real estate, secondaries) or London Market insurance</li> <li>Experience with supporting tooling such as IaC; Transformation (dbt); DevOps; Data Quality; Data Modelling; Data Glossary/Governance; iPaaS/ELT, Orchestration</li> <li>Some exposure to regulated environments – financial services, insurance, or similarly audited sectors – and awareness of relevant regulatory frameworks including FCA SYSC, DORA, and MiFID II</li> </ul> <h1>What the engagement looks like</h1> <p>You'll work closely with client teams, alongside their internal engineers and Alpha consultants in a regulated environment where change control, data lineage and documentation matter. You'll write code, typically tackling the most complex challenges, review code and data model decisions across the team, and take responsibility for a delivery workstream – planning it, running it, and leading the junior engineers working on it.</p> <h1>What We Offer</h1> <ul> <li>Competitive base salary&nbsp;</li> <li>Annual performance bonus tied to individual and company outcomes.</li> <li>Comprehensive benefits package including private medical insurance, life assurance, and income protection.</li> <li>Hybrid working model with flexible arrangements to support work-life balance.</li> <li>Generous pension scheme with enhanced employer contributions.</li> <li>Continuous learning budget and access to industry conferences, certifications, and training programmes.</li> <li>25 days annual leave plus bank holidays, with option to buy additional days.</li> <li>Employee assistance programme and wellbeing support.</li> </ul>

title: Forward Deployed Software Engineer (Japan Contract)
full_description: <div class="content-intro"><p>We help organizations move AI out of experimentation and into production — safely, reliably, and at scale. From inconsistent performance to limited transparency and challenges with integration and long-term viability, our Applied Intelligence Engine solves the risks that cause most AI initiatives to stall.</p> <p>Built for some of the most highly regulated industries in the world, we enable teams to deploy AI systems that are auditable, explainable, and aligned with real-world constraints. Our solutions introduce structure, visibility, and control into how AI operates, turning advanced capabilities into a dependable, production-grade infrastructure so our customers can move faster and scale with confidence.</p></div><p><span style="text-decoration: underline;"><strong>Contractor Details</strong></span></p> <ul> <li><strong>Duration:</strong> 1 year</li> <li><strong>Working location:</strong> Japan-based</li> <li><strong>Compensation:</strong> Hourly ~6,568 JPY - ~8,490 JPY ($41/hour - $53/hour) or fixed monthly fee, negotiable based on experience and preferred engagement structure</li> <li><strong>Expected Commitment:</strong> Full-time equivalent, 40 hours/week</li> </ul> <p><strong>Job Overview</strong></p> <p>Lazarus is seeking an experienced Forward Deployed Software Engineer to join our team dedicated to our Japanese market for a 1-year contract. As a contractor, you will be an integral part of our Engineering team, working with clients, senior engineers, and data engineers to facilitate the successful and efficient adoption of our solutions.</p> <p><strong>Responsibilities</strong></p> <ul> <li>Work with the engineering and sales teams to gather client requirements and assist in creating implementation plans.</li> <li>Assist in configuring and customizing our products to align with client needs and integrate into their existing systems and solutions.</li> <li>Participate in the end-to-end implementation process, ensuring timely project completion and client satisfaction.</li> <li>Troubleshoot technical issues and collaborate with engineers to provide effective solutions during the implementation phase.</li> <li>Document implementation processes, best practices, and solutions for internal knowledge sharing and client reference.</li> <li>Work closely with cross-functional teams, including prompt-engineering, sales, support, and development, to ensure efficient and successful customer solution delivery.</li> <li>Provide training and technical assistance to clients, guiding them through product functionalities and usage.</li> <li>Work with clients and partners to develop optimal prompts for their use cases.</li> <li>Continuous learning regarding how LLM’s and associated tools may improve existing and new solutions.</li> </ul> <p><strong>Requirements</strong></p> <ul> <li>Must be a native Japanese speaker with full working proficiency in reading and writing.</li> <li>Basic proficiency in spoken and written English.</li> <li>1-2 years of experience in a technical role, such as technical support, systems administration, software development, or related internships.</li> <li>Available for video conferences with customers across North American and Asian time zones, including occasional evening calls as needed.</li> <li>Knowledge of programming languages, scripting, and system configurations.</li> <li>Familiarity with cloud technologies, APIs, and database management.</li> <li>Proven familiarity with LLMs and prompt engineering strategies and tactics.</li> <li>Problem-solving skills and a proactive attitude towards learning and troubleshooting.</li> <li>Excellent communication skills to effectively interact with clients and internal teams.</li> <li>Ability to work collaboratively in a fast-paced environment, adapting to evolving client needs and project requirements.</li> </ul><div class="content-conclusion"><p>Lazarus AI is an equal opportunity employer. We are committed to equal employment opportunity and nondiscrimination for all employees and qualified applicants without regard to a person's race, color, gender, age, religion, national origin, ancestry, disability, veteran status, genetic information, sexual orientation or any characteristic protected under applicable law. We do not tolerate discrimination or harassment of any kind. This applies to every aspect of employment at Lazarus, including, but not limited to, employment, training, promotion, demotion, transfer, leaves of absence and termination.</p></div>

title: Staff Forward Deployed Engineer, Life Sciences
full_description: <p><strong>Your Impact at LILA</strong></p> <div class="notion-selectable notion-text-block" data-block-id="2151a62c-8a83-80e3-b6fe-ffa07bb140fc"> <div class="notranslate" data-content-editable-leaf="true"> <p>We're seeking extraordinarily talented Forward Deployed Engineers across Engineer, Senior Engineer, and Lead Engineer levels to join our growing team. Our FDEs are high-performing generalists with a broad set of skills, in addition to depth in one or more focus areas.&nbsp;</p> <p>As an FDE, you'll embed directly with our internal biotech and science teams as well as external customers, living their problems and rapidly building solutions that transform their research capabilities. You'll leverage our AI and software platform tools while developing bespoke features that address each customer's unique challenges. This isn't traditional software development—it's high-velocity, AI-fueled, high-impact engineering where you'll ship production code in days, not months.</p> </div> </div> <p><strong>What You'll Be Building</strong></p> <ul> <li>Developing, wrapping, and adapting domain-specific tools for molecular modeling, data analysis, process optimization, molecular modeling, and more</li> <li>Deploying custom, agentic scientific workflows that integrate with industrial instruments (PLCs, control software) and data systems (ERP, MES, SCADA)</li> <li>Building real-time data pipelines that transform messy scientific data into actionable insights</li> <li>Creating intuitive interfaces that make complex AI/ML capabilities accessible to scientists</li> <li>Architecting solutions that scale from proof-of-concept to enterprise-wide deployment</li> </ul> <p><strong>What You'll Need to Succeed</strong></p> <ul> <li>Background in biology, molecular science, or related scientific field</li> <li>Outstanding communication skills—you'll interface directly with both executives and the manufacturing line</li> <li>Experience with data engineering, ETL pipelines, and working with complex/messy datasets</li> <li>Strong full-stack development skills with the ability to work across backend, frontend, and data layers</li> <li>Exceptional problem-solving abilities and comfort with ambiguous requirements</li> <li>Knack for product discovery and ability to pass requirements back to the platform</li> <li>Willingness to travel to customer sites for frequent and extended deployments</li> </ul> <p><strong>Bonus Points For</strong></p> <ul> <li>Previous customer-facing or consulting experience</li> <li>Depth in controls engineering and machine learning</li> <li>Experience with scientific computing libraries (NumPy, Pandas, SciPy, RDKit)</li> <li>Familiarity with ML/AI implementation and deployment</li> <li>Familiarity with modern web technologies (TypeScript, React)</li> </ul> <p><strong>What Makes a Great FDE</strong></p> <ul> <li><strong>Customer Obsessed</strong>: You're energized by understanding customer problems deeply and seeing your solutions drive real impact</li> <li><strong>Fast Learner</strong>: You quickly grasp new scientific domains and translate complex requirements into elegant solutions</li> <li><strong>Autonomous</strong>: You thrive with minimal supervision and take extreme ownership of customer success</li> <li><strong>Collaborative</strong>: You work seamlessly with both technical and non-technical stakeholders</li> <li><strong>Technically Versatile</strong>: You're comfortable jumping between technologies and picking up new tools as needed</li> </ul> <p><strong>Ready to Transform Scientific Discovery?</strong></p> <p>If you're excited about embedding with world-class scientists, shipping impactful solutions at startup speed, and building the future of scientific software, we want to hear from you. Join us in our mission to accelerate breakthrough discoveries that will define the next century of human progress.</p><div class="content-pay-transparency"><div class="pay-input"><div class="description"><p><strong>Compensation</strong></p> <p>We offer competitive base compensation with bonus potential and generous early-stage equity. Your final offer will reflect your background, expertise, and expected impact.</p> <p><strong>U.S. Benefits.</strong> Full-time U.S. employees receive a comprehensive benefits program including medical, dental, and vision coverage; employer-paid life and disability insurance; flexible time off with generous company wide holidays; paid parental leave; an educational assistance program; commuter benefits, including bike share memberships for office based employees; and a company subsidized lunch program.</p> <p><strong>International Benefits.</strong> Full-time employees outside the U.S. receive a comprehensive benefits program tailored to their region. USD salary ranges apply only to U.S.-based positions; international salaries are set to local market.</p></div><div class="title">Expected Base Salary Range</div><div class="pay-range"><span>$192,000</span><span class="divider">&mdash;</span><span>$256,000 USD</span></div></div></div><div class="content-conclusion"><p><strong>About LILA</strong></p> <p>Lila Sciences is building Scientific Superintelligence™ to solve humankind's greatest challenges. We believe science is the most inspiring frontier for AI. Rather than hard-coding expert knowledge into tools, LILA builds systems that can learn for themselves.</p> <p>LILA combines advanced AI models with proprietary AI Science Factory™ instruments into an operating system for science that executes the entire scientific method autonomously, accelerating discovery at unprecedented speed, scale, and impact across medicine, materials, and energy. Learn more at www.lila.ai.</p> <p>Guided by our core values of truth, trust, curiosity, grit, and velocity, we move with startup speed while tackling problems of historic importance. If this sounds like an environment you'd love to work in, even if you don't meet every qualification listed above, we encourage you to apply.</p> <p><strong>We’re All In</strong></p> <p>Lila Sciences is&nbsp;committed to equal employment opportunity&nbsp;regardless of race, color, ancestry, religion, sex, national origin, sexual orientation, age, citizenship, marital status, disability, gender identity or Veteran status.</p> <p>Information you provide during your application process will be handled in accordance with our <a href="https://www.lila.ai/candidate-privacy-policy-notice">Candidate Privacy Policy</a>.</p> <p><strong>A Note to Agencies</strong></p> <p><em>Lila Sciences does not accept unsolicited resumes from any source other than candidates. The submission of unsolicited resumes by recruitment or staffing agencies to Lila Sciences or its employees is strictly prohibited unless contacted directly by Lila Science’s internal Talent Acquisition team. Any resume submitted by an agency in the absence of a signed agreement will automatically become the property of Lila Sciences, and Lila Sciences will not owe any referral or other fees with respect thereto.</em></p></div>

title: Data Engineer
full_description: <p><strong>Opportunity Overview:</strong></p> <p>We are looking for a Data Engineer to build and optimize scalable data pipelines and analytical&nbsp;datasets on our AWS-based lakehouse platform.</p> <ul> <li>You will work with Apache Iceberg, AWS Glue, Athena, dbt, and Airflow to support reliable and efficient data processing for analytics and operational use cases.</li> <li>You will collaborate with senior engineers, analytics teams, and platform stakeholders to deliver production-grade data solutions.</li> </ul> <p><strong>What you’ll do:</strong></p> <ul> <li>Develop and maintain batch data pipelines</li> <li>Build reusable ingestion and transformation workflows</li> <li>Build and maintain Apache Iceberg datasets</li> <li>Support schema evolution and partition optimization</li> <li>Optimize Athena queries and troubleshoot performance bottlenecks</li> <li>Implement data validation checks and monitoring</li> <li>Support production issue troubleshooting</li> </ul> <p><strong>Required Qualifications:</strong></p> <ul> <li>4–5 years of experience in data engineering</li> <li>Strong hands-on experience with Python, SQL, PySpark/Spark</li> <li>Experience with AWS S3, Glue, Athena</li> <li>Experience with DBT and Airflow (or equivalent)</li> <li>Familiarity with Apache Iceberg / Hudi / Delta</li> <li>Understanding of partitioning and Parquet optimization</li> </ul> <p><strong>Interview Process*:</strong></p> <ol> <li>Connect with Talent Acquisition&nbsp;</li> <li>Technical Round 1&nbsp;</li> <li>Technical Round 2</li> <li>Final Round&nbsp;</li> </ol> <p>*Subject to change</p> <p><strong>About Cohere Health:</strong></p> <p>Cohere Health’s clinical intelligence platform and agentic AI-powered solutions connect health plans’ strategic goals and providers’ needs, optimizing the speed, cost, and quality of care. With an enterprise approach that streamlines payer-provider decision-making across the care continuum–including policy, prior authorization, payment accuracy, and more–the company improves collaboration and reduces burden, resulting in up to 8x ROI and 94% provider satisfaction.&nbsp;</p> <p>With the acquisition of ZignaAI, we expanded our AI-native platform with a comprehensive Payment Integrity Suite that spans data mining, clinical and coding validation, authorization and claims reconciliation, and end-to-end payment integrity services across pre- and post-pay workflows. By connecting clinical and payment insights, our transparent, AI-powered solutions help health plans proactively improve payment accuracy, reduce waste and vendor dependency, strengthen provider relationships, and build smarter, more efficient payment integrity programs.</p> <p>Cohere Health’s innovations continue to receive industry-wide recognition. We’ve been recognized on TIME’s World Top HealthTech Companies 2025 list, the 2025 Inc. 5000 list, in the Gartner® Hype Cycle™ for U.S. Healthcare Payers (2022-2025), and ranked as a Top 5 LinkedIn™ Startup for 2023 &amp; 2024.</p> <p><strong>We can’t wait to learn more about you and meet you at Cohere Health!</strong></p> <p><strong>Equal Opportunity Statement:&nbsp;</strong></p> <p>Cohere Health is an Equal Opportunity Employer. We are committed to fostering an environment of mutual respect where equal employment opportunities are available to all.&nbsp; To us, it’s personal.</p> <p>&nbsp;</p> <p>&nbsp;</p>

title: Lead Data Engineer
full_description: <p><strong>Opportunity Overview:</strong></p> <p>We are seeking a Lead Data Engineer to drive the design and delivery of our cloud-native healthcare data platform. You will lead the implementation of scalable data solutions built on AWS, Apache Iceberg, Lake Formation, Glue Catalog, Athena, dbt, and modern orchestration frameworks. This role combines deep hands-on engineering with technical leadership and collaboration across platform, analytics, and business teams.</p> <p><strong>What you’ll do:</strong></p> <ul> <li>Lead Data Engineering Initiatives&nbsp;</li> <li>Lead delivery of complex data engineering projects across multiple teams&nbsp;</li> <li>Drive technical execution from design through production deployment&nbsp;</li> <li>Establish scalable implementation patterns Build and Optimize Data Platforms&nbsp;</li> <li>Design and implement batch and near-real-time pipelines&nbsp;</li> <li>Build reusable ingestion, transformation, validation, and publishing frameworks&nbsp;</li> <li>Support modernization of legacy workloads Lakehouse Engineering&nbsp;</li> <li>Lead Apache Iceberg implementation and optimization&nbsp;</li> <li>Define standards for schema evolution, partitioning, compaction, and metadata management&nbsp;</li> <li>Ensure efficient storage and query performance Data Quality and Reliability&nbsp;</li> <li>Implement data quality frameworks&nbsp;</li> <li>Drive observability and monitoring practices&nbsp;</li> <li>Improve operational excellence and reliability Technical Leadership&nbsp;</li> <li>Review architecture and design proposals&nbsp;</li> <li>Conduct code reviews and engineering reviews&nbsp;</li> <li>Mentor engineers and establish best practices</li> </ul> <p><strong>Required Qualifications:</strong></p> <ul> <li>8–12 years of Data Engineering experience.</li> <li>Experience leading enterprise-scale data initiatives Cloud Technologies&nbsp;</li> <li>AWS S3, Glue, Athena, Lake Formation&nbsp;</li> <li>IAM and security controls Modern Data Stack&nbsp;</li> <li>Apache Iceberg (preferred)&nbsp;</li> <li>Delta Lake / Hudi • dbt • Airflow or Step FunctionsProgramming&nbsp;</li> <li>Python&nbsp;</li> <li>SQL&nbsp;</li> <li>PySpark / Spark</li> </ul> <p><strong>Interview Process*:</strong></p> <ol> <li>Connect with Talent Acquisition&nbsp;</li> <li>Meet with the Hiring Manager</li> <li>Behavioral Interview(s)</li> <li>Case Study</li> <li>Interview with Senior Leadership</li> </ol> <p>*Subject to change</p> <p><strong>About Cohere Health:</strong></p> <p>Cohere Health’s clinical intelligence platform and agentic AI-powered solutions connect health plans’ strategic goals and providers’ needs, optimizing the speed, cost, and quality of care. With an enterprise approach that streamlines payer-provider decision-making across the care continuum–including policy, prior authorization, payment accuracy, and more–the company improves collaboration and reduces burden, resulting in up to 8x ROI and 94% provider satisfaction.&nbsp;</p> <p>With the acquisition of ZignaAI, we expanded our AI-native platform with a comprehensive Payment Integrity Suite that spans data mining, clinical and coding validation, authorization and claims reconciliation, and end-to-end payment integrity services across pre- and post-pay workflows. By connecting clinical and payment insights, our transparent, AI-powered solutions help health plans proactively improve payment accuracy, reduce waste and vendor dependency, strengthen provider relationships, and build smarter, more efficient payment integrity programs.</p> <p>Cohere Health’s innovations continue to receive industry-wide recognition. We’ve been recognized on TIME’s World Top HealthTech Companies 2025 list, the 2025 Inc. 5000 list, in the Gartner® Hype Cycle™ for U.S. Healthcare Payers (2022-2025), and ranked as a Top 5 LinkedIn™ Startup for 2023 &amp; 2024.</p> <p><strong>We can’t wait to learn more about you and meet you at Cohere Health!</strong></p> <p><strong>Equal Opportunity Statement:&nbsp;</strong></p> <p>Cohere Health is an Equal Opportunity Employer. We are committed to fostering an environment of mutual respect where equal employment opportunities are available to all.&nbsp; To us, it’s personal.</p> <p><strong>ISMS roles and responsibilities</strong></p> <ul> <li>Good knowledge of Information security</li> <li>Oversee specific business processes within the ISMS.</li> <li>Responsible to manage the ISMS documentation, conduct risk assessments, and implement risk treatment plans.</li> <li>Risk Owners are responsible for identifying, assessing, and managing risks within their areas of responsibility.</li> <li>They are also responsible for implementing risk treatment plans.</li> <li>Conduct the BCP and other test related to information security continuity along with CISO</li> <li>Responsible for monitoring and reporting on the performance of the ISMS.</li> <li>Responsible for implementation of security policies and procedures and report any issues to the CISO.</li> </ul> <p>&nbsp;</p>

title: Sr. Data Engineer
full_description: <p><strong>Opportunity Overview:</strong></p> <p>We are seeking a Sr. Data Engineer to help drive the technical vision of our Data Engineering team as we scale our infrastructure, services, and architecture. This role is ideal for someone who blends deep technical expertise with strategic thinking and cross-functional influence.</p> <p>As a Sr. Data Engineer, you will partner closely with other data and analytics engineers, architects, product leaders, and business stakeholders to evolve our data infrastructure in support of high-trust, scalable, and business-aligned outcomes. You’ll also serve as a force multiplier across teams; raising the bar for data engineering excellence and accelerating the maturity of our data platform capabilities.</p> <p><strong>What you’ll do:</strong></p> <ul> <li>Develop, and maintain robust ETL/ELT pipelines to ingest, transform, and load data from multiple source systems into enterprise data platforms.&nbsp;</li> <li>Ensure pipelines are scalable, reusable, fault-tolerant, and optimized for batch and near real-time processing requirements.</li> <li>Develop interactive dashboards, scorecards, and KPI reporting solutions using BI tools. Deliver executive, operational, and analytical reporting aligned with business goals.</li> <li>Identify repetitive manual reporting processes and automate them using scripting, workflow orchestration, scheduling tools, and dashboard refresh mechanisms to improve efficiency, accuracy, and turnaround time.</li> <li>Collaborate with internal stakeholders and leadership teams to understand data integration and reporting needs, define KPIs, document business rules, and translate requirements into scalable technical solutions.</li> <li>Implement data validation checks, reconciliation controls, lineage documentation, metadata standards, and governance policies to maintain trusted, secure, and compliant data assets across the organization.</li> <li>Provide technical guidance, code reviews, best practices, and mentor junior team members.</li> <li>Investigate and resolve production incidents related to pipelines, delayed reports, data mismatches, access issues, and system bottlenecks. Perform root cause analysis and implement preventive fixes.</li> <li>Continuously improve platform reliability, performance, scalability, observability, security, and cost efficiency through engineering excellence and proactive monitoring.</li> </ul> <p><strong>Required Qualifications:</strong></p> <ul> <li>5+ years of experience in Data Engineering demonstrating expertise in designing scalable data platforms, building robust ETL/ELT pipelines, optimizing data workflows, implementing cloud-based solutions, and delivering reliable, high-performance data systems that support business growth and analytics needs.</li> <li>5+ years of experience in developing automated data ingestion pipelines that integrate data sourced from multiple streams&nbsp; into enterprise platforms through cleansing, transformation, validation, and scheduled batch or near real-time processing workflows.</li> <li>Strong proficiency in writing and tuning advanced SQL queries, views, and data models to improve report performance and reduce processing time by monitoring indexing, partitioning, query plans, and database resource utilization.</li> <li>5+ years of proven expertise in cloud-based, distributed environments (e.g., AWS, EMR, Glue, Iceberg, dbt, Lambda, Athena).</li> <li>Demonstrated experience designing and managing secure client data integrations, including inbound and outbound file exchanges, SFTP transfers, API-based data ingestion, and reliable end-to-end data delivery between client systems and internal platforms.</li> <li>Fluency in modern data architecture patterns, including medallion/lakehouse models, schema evolution, streaming ingestion, and batch orchestration.</li> <li>Familiarity with reporting and dashboarding tools such as AWS QuickSIght or Tableau.</li> <li>Designed and maintained low-latency, high-availability data pipelines to support near real-time processing, timely reporting, and reliable downstream data consumption.</li> <li>Monitored production pipelines and triaged data incidents by performing root cause analysis, resolving failures, fixing data quality issues, and ensuring SLA adherence.</li> <li>Implemented strong data governance, observability, and quality controls including schema validation, lineage tracking, alerting, reconciliation checks, and policy-based access management.</li> <li>Knowledge of CI/CD pipelines, version control, and DevOps practices using GitHub, AWS code pipeline.</li> <li>Bachelor’s or Master’s degree in Computer Science, Engineering, or a related discipline.</li> </ul> <p><strong>Ability to commute/relocate:</strong></p> <ul> <li>Hyderabad, Telangana: Reliably commute or planning to relocate before starting work (Preferred)</li> </ul> <p><strong>Interview Process*:</strong></p> <ol> <li>Connect with Talent Acquisition&nbsp;</li> <li>Meet with the Hiring Manager</li> <li>Behavioral Interview(s)</li> <li>Case Study</li> <li>Interview with Senior Leadership</li> </ol> <p>*Subject to change</p> <p><strong>About Cohere Health:</strong></p> <p>Cohere Health’s clinical intelligence platform and agentic AI-powered solutions connect health plans’ strategic goals and providers’ needs, optimizing the speed, cost, and quality of care. With an enterprise approach that streamlines payer-provider decision-making across the care continuum–including policy, prior authorization, payment accuracy, and more–the company improves collaboration and reduces burden, resulting in up to 8x ROI and 94% provider satisfaction.&nbsp;</p> <p>With the acquisition of ZignaAI, we expanded our AI-native platform with a comprehensive Payment Integrity Suite that spans data mining, clinical and coding validation, authorization and claims reconciliation, and end-to-end payment integrity services across pre- and post-pay workflows. By connecting clinical and payment insights, our transparent, AI-powered solutions help health plans proactively improve payment accuracy, reduce waste and vendor dependency, strengthen provider relationships, and build smarter, more efficient payment integrity programs.</p> <p>Cohere Health’s innovations continue to receive industry-wide recognition. We are committed to fostering an environment of mutual respect where equal employment opportunities are available to all.&nbsp; To us, it’s personal.</p> <p><strong>ISMS roles and responsibilities:</strong></p> <ul> <li>Good knowledge of Information practices.</li> <li>Assist the manager in all the information security activities implementation and maintenance process.</li> <li>Ensuring the&nbsp; team and imparted with Competence related to Information security</li> <li>Responsible for implementation of security policies and procedures and report any issues to the Information Security Manager.</li> </ul>
